gmw_00005245.name: LAE2
gmw_00005245.overview: LAE2 is a compact Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.167 [arXiv:1709.08228]. It lies 90.4 kiloparsecs from a quasar and exhibits strong silicon IV emission, though its exact powering mechanism remains unclear [arXiv:1709.08228].

sources.summary: LAE2 is a compact Ly emitter at z=3.167, 90.4 kpc from the quasar; its strong emission suggests hard ionization, but its line ratios make its nature unclear.

quotes.quote: This approach led to the identification of two \ emitters at a redshift similar to , AGN1 at z=3.179 0.001 (EW _ rest >168 ) at 11.3 ( 85.9 kpc), and LAE2 at z=3.167 0.001 (EW _ rest >29 ) at 11.9 ( 90.4 kpc) (Fig. figsources ).

On the other hand, LAE2 shows strong emission, also requiring a hard-ionizing source (ionization energy of I _ SiIV =33.49 eV; Draine2011 ). However, we don't detect other high ionization lines ( He ii , C iv , N v , ) which typically accompany Si iv in AGN spectra (e.g., McCarthy1993,Humphrey2008,Hainline2011,Lacy2013 ). To our knowledge, there are no sources reported in the literature with only \ and detected. In the population of Lyman break galaxies (LBGs; e.g., shapley03 ) or LAEs characterized by similar strong \ emission as LAE2, is usually observed in absorption (e.g., 4th quartile in shapley03 ).

LAE2 thus seems a clear outlier for both type-2 AGN and LBGs. However, we argue that the strong emission should be a signature of powering mechanisms more similar to an AGN. The nature of LAE2 remains unclear, and follow up studies are needed.

gmw_00005246.name: SDSS J0805 + 2818
gmw_00005246.overview: SDSS J0805 + 2818 is classified as a Seyfert or active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.01846]. Chandra X-ray observations reveal a detected primary nucleus with a soft spectrum, which may be shaped by heavy obscuration [arXiv:2006.01846].

sources.summary: The system remains in the Seyfert/AGN BPT class but only weakly by WHAN; Chandra data show primary detections, secondary upper limits, and a soft X-ray spectrum possibly shaped by obscuration.

quotes.quote: While SDSS J0317 + 0048 and SDSS J0805 + 2818 remain in the Seyfert/AGN classification in the BPT, the equivalent width of H is very low and certainly in the weak AGN regime.

Chandra X-ray observations are available for SDSS J0805 + 2818 Barrows:2016,Hou:2019 , SDSS J0858 + 1822 (Proposal ID: 14700279, PI: Liu, unpublished), and SDSS J1115 + 5423 Barrows:2016 . In all Chandra images the primary nuclei are always detected, while the secondary nuclei are usually undetected. We derived a 5 upper limit on the 2--10 keV flux based on the background noise, which corresponds to <5 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , <2.2 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , and <2.5 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 for the three sources, respectively, adopting a N_H=10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and a power-law index of =1.7 .

The X-ray fluxes of the primary nuclei for SDSS J0805 + 2818 and SDSS J1115 + 5423 are published in Barrows:2016 , but we re-visit the spectra and adjust luminosity estimates based on spectral shape. For SDSS J0858 + 1822 we found that the bulk of X-ray emission is located between the optical nuclei in the SDSS images, which is fully consistent with our LBT spectroscopy. Furthermore, the X-ray spectra of SDSS J0805 + 2818 and SDSS J0858 + 1822 are rather soft, which could be due to a heavy obscuration of the primary nuclear component up to 4--5 keV (and potentially up to the Compton-thick level of 10 keV when the spectrum is dominated by the disc reflected component) and leaving a soft X-ray scattered component dominating at low energies.

gmw_00005247.name: SDSS J0858 + 1822
gmw_00005247.overview: SDSS J0858 + 1822 is an ongoing galaxy merger system that contains two apparent stellar nuclei [arXiv:2006.01846]. While initially suspected to host a dual active galactic nucleus, observations show that the secondary nucleus is instead dominated by star formation [arXiv:2006.01846].

sources.summary: The paper finds the emission peak offset from the two optical nuclei, undermining the dual-AGN interpretation; diagnostics and X-rays indicate a star-formation-dominated, non-AGN secondary.

quotes.quote: Surprisingly, the emission-line peak for SDSS J0858+1822 is clearly offset by 1 \ from the position of the two apparent stellar nuclei of the ongoing merger system. This already calls into question the initial dual AGN nature of this galaxy as the SDSS fibres were positioned at the two continuum nuclei, which means that a high fraction of the flux from the brightest emission-line source is captured by both fibres simultaneously. The emission-line kinematics at the position of the two nuclei are significantly different, which suggest that local emission is dominating even for the secondary nuclei.

SDSS J0858 + 1822 is the system where the peak in the [ O iii ] emission is located between the two continuum nuclei making the dual AGN signature of the SDSS fibre obsolete, and shows that one of the nuclei is dominated by star formation, as confirmed by the BPT diagram, which cannot be properly distinguished in the WHAN diagram in the transition zone.
The X-ray observations of SDSS J0858 + 1822 also confirm the non-AGN nature of the secondary nucleus which appears to be dominated by star formation in the LBT spectra.

gmw_00005248.name: SDSS J0947 + 6339
gmw_00005248.overview: SDSS J0947 + 6339 is an interacting system containing two nuclei with consistent radial velocities and velocity dispersions [arXiv:2006.01846]. The secondary nucleus exhibits lower-ionization emission that is likely ionized by its stellar population rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.01846].

sources.summary: For SDSS J0947 + 6339, the nuclei have consistent radial velocity and dispersion; primary spillover may affect the secondary, whose lower-ionization emission likely comes from stars.

quotes.quote: An exception is SDSS J0947 + 6339, where the radial velocity and velocity dispersion are consistent within the errors. This means that the PSF-smeared emission of the primary may even dominate the emission at the location of the second nucleus. This should be taken into account when interpreting the emission-line ratios.

The secondary nuclei of SDSS J0947 + 6339 and SDSS J1646 + 2411 clearly fall into a lower ionisation regime which are likely ionised by the stellar population instead of an AGN given the low equivalent width of H in the WHAN classification.

gmw_00005249.name: SDSS J1609 + 2830
gmw_00005249.overview: SDSS J1609 + 2830 is an interacting system that retains an unambiguous dual active galactic nucleus classification [arXiv:2006.01846]. Its primary nucleus exhibits very broad emission lines with a velocity dispersion of 600 km/s, suggesting the presence of an outflow [arXiv:2006.01846].

sources.summary: SDSS J1609 + 2830 has coincident bright continuum and emission-line nuclei, broad primary AGN lines indicating an outflow, and the clearest retained dual-AGN classification.

quotes.quote: Only in one of the seven cases (SDSS J1609+2830) does the brighter emission-line source coincide with the brighter continuum nucleus.

The emission lines are very broad for the primary AGN nucleus in SDSS J1609 + 2830 with a velocity dispersion of 600 km/s and a blue shift of more than 600 km/s with respect to the secondary nucleus. This suggests that a powerful outflow is released by the AGN which is interesting by itself, but outside the scope of this paper.

Only SDSS J1609 + 2830 seems to retain an unambiguous dual AGN classification although the BPT diagnostic reveals a significant SF or LINER-like contribution to the emission inconsistent with the previous Seyfert-Seyfert classification based solely on SDSS.

gmw_00005250.name: SDSS J1646 + 2411
gmw_00005250.overview: SDSS J1646 + 2411 is an interacting galaxy system containing a dual active galactic nucleus (AGN) candidate [arXiv:2006.01846]. Spatially resolved spectroscopy reveals that its secondary nucleus falls into a lower ionization regime likely powered by stellar populations rather than an AGN [arXiv:2006.01846].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSS J1646 + 2411 as an interacting dual-AGN candidate whose secondary nucleus falls into a weak, lower-ionization regime likely powered by stellar populations.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert-Seyfert pre-selection from SDSS of the two nuclei is confirmed by the BPT diagram shown in Fig. fig:BPT (upper left panel), although the second nucleus of SDSS J1115 + 5423 and SDSS J1646+2411 is in the weak AGN regime following the WHAN diagram. Hence, all seven systems have been assumed as dual AGN in interacting galaxy systems based on these optical diagnostics. However, the MODS spatially resolved spectroscopy reveals a significantly different picture, as shown in the right panels of Fig. fig:BPT . Except for SDSS J1115+5423, all the fainter secondary nuclei, which were initially classified clearly as Seyfert-like AGN, are changing their apparent emission-line classification. The secondary nuclei of SDSS J0947 + 6339 and SDSS J1646 + 2411 clearly fall into a lower ionisation regime which are likely ionised by the stellar population instead of an AGN given the low equivalent width of H in the WHAN classification.

gmw_00005251.name: Q 2345+007
gmw_00005251.overview: Q 2345+007 is an ambiguous quasar pair consisting of two components [arXiv:astro-ph/9710165]. The A component is a 30 Jy radio source, while the B component has not been detected in the radio band [arXiv:astro-ph/9710165].

sources.summary: The paper treats Q 2345+007 as an ambiguous quasar pair: one component is radio detected, weak-lensing evidence is offset, and spectral differences resemble isolated quasars viewed at different times.

quotes.quote: For example, Patnaik, Schneider Narayan (1996) discovered that Q 2345+007 A is a 30 Jy radio source. The B image was not detected, but the predicted flux was too close to the noise level to test the lens hypothesis.
A weak lensing effect is detected near Q 2345+007 (Bonnet et al. 1993), but its center is too far from the pair to be responsible for the image splitting.

An initial study by Small, Sargent Steidel (1997) showed that the emission line differences in Q 1634+267 and Q 2345+007 were consistent with the differences seen in the spectra of isolated quasars viewed at different times. First, in focusing on Q 1634+267 and Q 2345+007, Small et al. (1997) selected the quasar pairs already known to show smaller spectral differences than a randomly selected quasar pair.

gmw_00005252.name: Q 0151+048
aliases_external: Q0151+048 / PHL 1222 / UM 144 binary quasar system
gmw_00005252.overview: Q 0151+048 is a physical binary quasar pair at a redshift of approximately 1.929 [arXiv:1106.3183, arXiv:astro-ph/9710165]. The brighter member of this pair, Q 0151+048A, is associated with an extended Lyman-alpha blob that exhibits a strong velocity gradient along its major axis [arXiv:1106.3183].

sources.summary: The paper studies S4 as an extended Ly blob near Q 0151+048A, finding a 42 by 28 kpc size, a strong velocity gradient, and an association with qA rather than the DLA.

quotes.quote: The NOT/ALFOSC data On three nights in September 1997 we used the NOT equipped with Andalucia Faint Object Spectrograph and Camera (ALFOSC) to obtain a spectrum of the elongated Ly emission line object discovered by FMW99 (see their Fig. 2) who named it S4. Our slit was centered on qA and aligned with the major axis of S4 at a position angle of 78.5 deg (see Fig.  slitpos ).

We clearly detect extended Ly over a total region of about 5 arcsec (42 kpc proper), corresponding well to the extend of the emission seen in narrowband imaging (Fig.  slitpos , lower panel). It is also clear that the emission is not symmetrically distributed around the QSO (also consistent with Fig.  slitpos ), that it is well separated (in redshift) from the DLA and, somewhat surprisingly, that the emission has a strong velocity gradient along the major axis which has a remarkable resemblance to a rotation curve.

The new redshift of qA means that the Ly blob is now more likely belonging to this QSO. We determine a Black Hole mass of qA of 10^ 9.33 M_ and a corresponding DM halo mass of 10^ 13.74 M_ . The Ly blob is sitting in the potential well of this halo. The blob has a size of 42 by 28 kpc, it has a clear signature of a strong velocity gradient along its major axis which is consistent with the rotation curve of a large disk galaxy in todays universe but may alternatively be caused by in- or out-flow.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q 0151+048A as the brighter member of a physical quasar pair, with a host galaxy, massive black hole, halo, associated Ly blob, and foreground DLA geometry.

quotes.quote: Q 0151+048 is a physical quasar (QSO) pair at z 1.929 with a separation of 3.3 arcsec on the sky. In the spectrum of the brighter member of this pair, Q 0151+048A, a damped Ly absorber (DLA) is observed at a higher redshift. We have previously detected the host galaxies of both QSOs as well as a Ly blob whose emission surrounding Q 0151+048A extends over 5 3.3 arcsec.
We determine a Black Hole mass of qA of 10^ 9.33  M_ and a corresponding DM halo mass of 10^ 13.74  M_ . The Ly blob is sitting in the potential well of this halo.
We detect emission in the trough of the DLA which we interpret as the signature of the quasar host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper classifies Q 0151+048 as a secure binary-quasar case, citing no plausible lens, a redshift difference confirmed by absorption, and flux ratios too large for normal lensing.

quotes.quote: We can be certain a quasar pair is a binary quasar if there is no plausible lens candidate and the optical and radio flux ratios are grossly discrepant (PKS 1145--071, MGC 2214+3550, and Q 1343+2640), or if there is no plausible lens candidate and there is a significant emission line redshift difference confirmed by an absorption feature at the same velocity in the spectrum of the foreground object (Q 0151+048=PHL 1222=UM 144).

MG 0023+171 whose morphology is inconsistent with lensing, and HS 1216+5032 in which only one of the quasars is a BAL quasar, Q 1120+0195 which has a significant velocity difference, and Q 0151+048, Q 1120+0195, LBQS 1429--008, and LBQS 2153-2056 which have highly unlikely flux ratios for gravitational lenses).

Second, bright quasar lenses should rarely show large flux ratios ( 1 - 2 mag depending on the magnitude, see Kochanek 1993) between the components, while four of the O^2 pairs show magnitude differences of more than 2.5 mag (Q 1120+019, Q 1429--008, Q 0151+048, LBQS 2153--2056).

gmw_00005253.name: MG 0023+171
gmw_00005253.overview: MG 0023+171 is a radio-selected quasar candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/9710165]. Although it has been evaluated as a potential gravitational lens, its observed morphology is inconsistent with lensing [arXiv:astro-ph/9710165].

sources.summary: The paper treats MG 0023+171 as a dubious radio-selected candidate: its morphology argues against lensing, while statistical limits depend on whether it is counted as a lens or binary.

quotes.quote: MG 0023+171 whose morphology is inconsistent with lensing, and HS 1216+5032 in which only one of the quasars is a BAL quasar, Q 1120+0195 which has a significant velocity difference, and Q 0151+048, Q 1120+0195, LBQS 1429--008, and LBQS 2153-2056 which have highly unlikely flux ratios for gravitational lenses).

We in fact found only one (dubious) candidate, MG 0023+171, which has a Poisson likelihood 10^ 5 . Thus the expected number of O^2R^2 binaries, P_ R30 P_ pair N_ Rqso 0.5 , is smaller by a factor of P_ R30 and is consistent with the discovery of only one candidate (MG 0023+171).

First, given the 11 optically selected pairs, we expect to find 11 f_L r_s = 16.5 f_L ``dark lenses'' in the radio surveys, while with probability f_L we found one and with probability 1-f_L we found zero for the cases of treating MG 0023+171 as a lens or a binary. Third, given N_ Rqso 5000 radio quasars we expect P_ R30 P_ pair N_ Rqso (1-f_L) 0.5 (1-f_L) O^2R^2 pairs for a flux limit 30 mJy, and we found either zero with probability f_L or one with probability 1-f_L depending on the treatment of MG 0023+171.

gmw_00005254.name: Q 1343+2640
gmw_00005254.overview: Q 1343+2640 is a binary quasar system [arXiv:astro-ph/9710165]. It is classified as an optically selected, radio-bright pair with grossly discrepant optical and radio flux ratios and no plausible gravitational lens candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/9710165].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q 1343+2640 as a binary-quasar case with discrepant optical/radio ratios and no plausible lens, then notes it is the single O^2R pair found in the sample.

quotes.quote: We can be certain a quasar pair is a binary quasar if there is no plausible lens candidate and the optical and radio flux ratios are grossly discrepant (PKS 1145--071, MGC 2214+3550, and Q 1343+2640), or if there is no plausible lens candidate and there is a significant emission line redshift difference confirmed by an absorption feature at the same velocity in the spectrum of the foreground object (Q 0151+048=PHL 1222=UM 144).
We discovered that Q 1343+2640 is in fact an O^2R pair, but that the remaining O^2 pairs are all radio-faint to the NVSS catalog limits of 3 mJy.
Thus for 11 optically selected quasar pairs we would expect 2 O^2R pairs at a 1 mJy flux limit, and we found one (Q 1343+2640), for a Poisson likelihood of 27 .

gmw_00005255.name: IC 4857
gmw_00005255.overview: IC 4857 is a galaxy with a spiral arm to its east that was previously omitted from photometric analyses [arXiv:1802.04043]. It exhibits similar kinematics across its stellar, ionized-gas, and neutral hydrogen components, with no observed metallicity drops in its outskirts [arXiv:2001.07516].

sources.summary: The paper compares IC 4857 with H I-rich survey galaxies, finding no metallicity drops or very metal-poor regions and noting similar stellar, ionized-gas, and H I kinematics.

quotes.quote: There are neither metallicity drops at the outskirts nor particularly metal-poor star forming regions observed in the \ and galaxies or in IC 4857 (for the detailed data see Appendix app:op_spec ). We have presented the analysis of optical spectra of ten \ galaxies as well as a set of control galaxies. In a first step, measurements of the , stellar (where available) and \ recession velocities in the \ galaxies and IC 4857 have been compared. Generally, the kinematics of all three components are similar.

Figure fig:ic4857_appendix shows the detailed results of IC 4857, which is the only control galaxy that was observed with . Figure fig:ic4857_appendix shows the same data as Figs. fig:eso111_appendix to fig:eso290_appendix .

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies IC 4857 after finding its photometry missed an eastern spiral arm, underestimating optical luminosity and overestimating H I richness; its H I disc resembles the control sample.

quotes.quote: For ESO208-G026 and IC 4857 the last paper was inconclusive on whether these two galaxies are true \ galaxies , because in the \ mass fraction vs. stellar mass plane, both galaxies were located within the 1 scatter of the \ parent sample (see below in Fig. fig:mhi_vs_mstar ) . Upon examination of the \ aperture that was used to measure the optical photometry of IC 4857, it became apparent that a spiral arm to the east of the galaxy had not been included in the photometrical analysis. This led to an underestimation of the \ magnitude and subsequently an overestimation of the richness. IC 4857 is therefore reclassified as a \ galaxy. As will be seen in the analysis of this paper, the properties of the \ disc of IC 4857 are furthermore similar to the remaining \ sample and not to the \ sample.

gmw_00005256.name: ESO208-G026
gmw_00005256.overview: ESO208-G026 is a lenticular galaxy located in a relatively sparse environment [arXiv:1802.04043]. It features a regular stellar disc and an exceptionally large neutral hydrogen disc, which may have been influenced by a gas-rich minor merger [arXiv:1802.04043].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO208-G026 as an H I-rich S0 with an H I disc about 7.5 times the stellar disc and a possible gas-rich minor merger despite regular optical starlight.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, the \ disc of ESO208-G026 is approximately 7.5 times larger than the stellar disc, despite the fact that this galaxy is located less than 1 above the running average of \ in Fig. fig:mhi_vs_mstar . In the \ mass--size plane, this galaxies lies right on the Wang2016 relation. In the stellar mass--size plane, this galaxy is located approximately 1 below the running average of . ESO208-G026 has been classified as S0 by the RC.3 catalogue deVaucouleurs1991 and upon inspection of optical images, a bright bulge and a very faint disc can be seen (see App. app:hix ). Therefore the effective radius is small.

ESO208-G026 is located in a relatively sparse environment. Hence, it would be more likely for the arm to be caused by a gas-rich minor merger than by tidal interaction. However, the stellar disc of ESO208-G026 appears regular and no debris of the merger is found in optical images.

gmw_00005257.name: ESO075-G006
gmw_00005257.overview: ESO075-G006 is an extreme, neutral hydrogen-rich galaxy with a massive, undisturbed gas disc [arXiv:1802.04043, arXiv:1701.02447]. This galaxy exhibits highly inefficient star formation due to its high specific baryonic angular momentum, and it has likely built its disc through the constant accretion of pristine gas [arXiv:1802.04043, arXiv:1701.02447].

sources.summary: The paper calls ESO075-G006 the most extreme HIX galaxy, linking its massive H I disc to low star-formation efficiency, high baryonic angular momentum, possible pristine-gas accretion, and potential XUV-disc star formation.

quotes.quote: The most extreme galaxy in the \ sample (ESO075-G006) has built its massive \ disc through a combination of a lower star formation efficiency ( SFE_ HI = SFR / M_ HI ), due to a high specific baryonic angular momentum, and likely some accretion of pristine gas (as probed by gas-phase metallicity gradients).
The two galaxies with the largest \ to stellar diameter ratios and \ diameters above 150 kpc are NGC 289 and ESO075-G006.

In particular in that respect NGC 289 is remarkably similar to ESO075-G006, which also has a nearby dwarf companion without an \ detection (for a more detailed discussion see Lutz2017 ). The \ NUV image of ESO075-G006 has a much shorter exposure time than the one of NGC 289 (207 s compared to 1696 sec) and optical imaging is of lower quality. Hence, a XUV disc or an extend low surface brightness stellar disc can not be excluded in ESO075-G006. However, given the large \ column densities above 1 \ well outside the visible disc of ESO075-G006, star formation in an XUV disc is also a possibility in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on ESO075-G006 as an extreme H I-rich galaxy whose undisturbed kinematics, abundance pattern, and high angular momentum imply inefficient star formation after sustained gas accretion.

quotes.quote: For the most \ massive galaxy in our sample ( , log M _ HI [ M _ ] = (10.8 0.1) ) the kinematic properties are the reason for inefficient star formation and \ excess. Examining the Australian Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) \ imaging and Wide Field Spectrograph ( ) optical spectra of \ reveals an undisturbed galaxy without evidence for recent major, violent accretion events. A tilted-ring fit to the \ disc together with the gas--phase oxygen abundance distribution supports the scenario that gas has been constantly accreted onto \ but the high specific angular momentum makes \ very inefficient in forming stars. Thus a massive \ disc has been built up.
Section sec:focus focuses on the most extreme galaxy in our sample, ESO075-G006, and in sections sec:discuss and sec:conclude we discuss our findings and conclude.

gmw_00005258.name: ESO378-G003
gmw_00005258.overview: ESO378-G003 is a neutral hydrogen-rich galaxy located within the NGC 3783 group [arXiv:1802.04043]. It exhibits one of the strongest known warps, displaying a morphology characterized by tails, arms, and clouds that may have been disturbed by tidal interactions [arXiv:1802.04043].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO378-G003 as one of the strongest-warp H I-rich galaxies, with tails or clouds possibly tied to tidal interaction in the NGC 3783 group near NGC 3706.

quotes.quote: As mentioned above, galaxies ESO208-G026, ESO378-G003 and ESO381-G005 are fitted with the strongest warps. Upon visual inspection, these galaxies also show the most interesting \ morphologies with \ tails, arms and clouds. Images in the top panel of Fig. fig:morph show the moment 0 maps of these galaxies.

ESO378-G003 is part of the NGC 3783 group Kilborn2006 and the elliptical galaxy NGC 3706 is located 22 arcmin = 255 kpc away (projected distance). Furthermore, Kilborn2006 has found an isolated \ cloud (GEMS N3783 2) without apparent optical counter part (see their fig. 6 for a resolved map). They concluded the cloud to be a remnant of tidal interaction. This tidal interaction could have potentially disturbed the \ distribution of ESO378-G003 as well.

gmw_00005259.name: ESO381-G005
gmw_00005259.overview: ESO381-G005 is an H I-rich galaxy that exhibits a strongly warped structure [arXiv:1802.04043]. It features an arm aligned with wide spiral structures pointing toward a southern dwarf companion, suggesting a potential recent minor merger [arXiv:1802.04043].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO381-G005 as a strongly warped, H I-rich galaxy whose arm aligns with spiral structure and a dwarf companion, suggesting minor-merger debris but not enough mass to explain its H I excess.

quotes.quote: As mentioned above, galaxies ESO208-G026, ESO378-G003 and ESO381-G005 are fitted with the strongest warps. Upon visual inspection, these galaxies also show the most interesting \ morphologies with \ tails, arms and clouds. Images in the top panel of Fig. fig:morph show the moment 0 maps of these galaxies.

In ESO381-G005 the arm feature is aligned with wide spiral arms and points towards a dwarf companion (PGC 629239) towards the south, which is also detected in . Additionally the inspection of optical images reveals three more sources aligned within the arm that are not foreground stars (PGC630365, USNO A2 0525-15319577 and USNO A2 0525-15322378), but look more like potential debris. Their position on the \ column density map is marked with black crosses in Fig. fig:morph .

This might indicate a recent minor merger. However, in order for the minor merger to increase the \ content of a galaxy from \ sample levels to \ sample levels, it would have had to bring in almost 10^ 10 M_ without changing the stellar disc a lot. This appears very unlikely to be the only cause why ESO381-G005 is a \ galaxy.

gmw_00005260.name: ESO245-G010
gmw_00005260.overview: ESO245-G010 is an interacting galaxy that displays clear signs of a recent merger in both its stellar and gas disks [arXiv:1802.04043]. These features may be the result of accreting gas-rich dwarf companions, which could eventually drive star formation as the disk settles [arXiv:1802.04043].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO245-G010 as an interacting HIX galaxy with signs of a recent merger; gas-rich dwarf accretion may explain features, but their H I mass cannot fully explain its excess.

quotes.quote: There is one \ galaxy that shows clear signs of interaction both in its stellar and \ disc (ESO245-G010), and three \ galaxies that have some kind of \ arms or clouds attached to their disc (ESO208-G026, ESO378-G003 and ESO381-G005). These features might be due to the accretion of gas rich dwarf companions(ESO245-G010 and ESO381-G005) or tidal interaction with another galaxy (ESO378-G003 with NGC 3706). We find, however, that the \ mass of those features is not large enough to fully explain the high \ masses of \ galaxies. It should be noted that the kinematic properties of the discs of these galaxies still agree with a model of a marginally stable disc.

The \ galaxies with a relatively low halo spin might, also be galaxies in transition. ESO245-G010 for example shows clear signs of a recent merger. Large gas masses at large radii might move further towards the centre of the galaxy and be used for star formation, once the disc settles.

gmw_00005261.name: NGC 3769
gmw_00005261.overview: NGC 3769 is a galaxy located at a distance of approximately 16 Mpc [arXiv:1603.02685]. It has a slowly falling observed rotation curve and is located near the galaxy NGC 3769A, which is positioned at a projected distance of about 0.0108 degrees [arXiv:1603.02685].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3769 has a slowly falling observed rotation curve, while an external field produces enough decline and is compatible with the nearby galaxy NGC 3769A.

quotes.quote: (11) NGC 3769 : This galaxy with the total luminosity of L_V=4.7 10^9 L_ n3521-lu and right ascension and declination of ( 11^h37^m44.1^s, 47^d53^m35^s ) is located at the distance of 16^ +4 _ 6 Mpc n2998-md, n3769-dm . Figure 2 shows that the observational RC falls slowly at large distances but that the MOND RC without an external field is almost constant. A sufficient fall in the RC is achieved by adding an external field equal to a_e=0.085 a_0 with the maximum and minimum values of a_e=0.195 a_0 and zero, respectively. NGC 3769A n3769a-lu is the most considerable nearby galaxy to NGC 3769 which can produce enough gravitational acceleration at the location of NGC 3769. The mean distance of NGC 3769A from us is 17 Mpc n3031-dm with a projected distance of about 0.0108^o to NGC 3769, which is roughly enough to produce an external gravitational field of a_e=1.53 10^ 3 a_0 with the maximum and minimum values of a_e=0.472a_0 and a_e=2.06 10^ 4 a_0 in the MOND regime which is compatible with the value obtained from the MOND fit with an EFE.

gmw_00005262.name: NGC 4100
gmw_00005262.overview: NGC 4100 is a galaxy in the Ursa Major cluster located approximately 21.8 Mpc away [arXiv:1603.02685, arXiv:astro-ph/0409622]. It features a central lens containing tightly wound spiral arms, bright circumnuclear H-alpha emission, and a dynamically decoupled inner region [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622].

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC 4100 as an Ursa Major cluster galaxy with a central lens, bright circumnuclear H emission, and a dynamically decoupled inner region.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is a member of the Ursa Major cluster (Odenwald 1986), although it has no noticeable neighbors -- the distance to the nearest luminous cluster galaxy is no less than 210 kpc (see the map of the cluster in Tully et al. 1996). Both CCD images and surface brightness profiles reveal a central ``lens'' between the radii of 15 \ and 70 containing a pair of tightly wound spiral arms. Beyond the ``lens'', the surface brightness falls abruptly, although some interesting details -- particularly two low-contrast symmetric arms -- can still be seen in the outer parts of the disk. Our IFP data covers only the region of the central ``lens''; here the H emission is confined to the spiral arms and to the very bright circumnuclear region, <12 (Fig. ngc4100 b).

A kinematic study of this galaxy's ionized gas with a long-slit spectrograph (Afanasiev et al. 1988b, 1992) has shown that the circumnuclear region of NGC 4100 is dynamically decoupled from the rest of the disk: its rotation curve demonstrates two local extrema at = 5 , with a rotation velocity gradient of about 300 kpc ^ 1 between them, which may be evidence of a highly concentrated bulge (Afanasiev et al. 1988b).

sources.summary: The paper gives NGC 4100 luminosity, coordinates, and distance, then reports that an external field improves the fit to its outer rotation curve.

quotes.quote: (12) NGC 4100 : The luminosity of this galaxy is about L_B=2.12 0.90 10^ 10 L_ , and it is located at right ascension and declination of ( 12^h06^m08.4^s, 49^d34^m58^s ) n4100-lu . The measured distance to NGC 4100 is about 21.8 Mpc with the minimum and maximum estimation of 15.7 Mpc and 25.5 Mpc ic-md, n3521-md , respectively. As can be seen in Figure 2 the observed rotation curve of this galaxy falls bellow the curve predicted by MOND without an EFE in the outer part. Adding an external field of a_e=0.335 a_0 improves the fit by reducing the ^2 value from 2.15 to 1.87.

gmw_00005263.name: UGC 6446
gmw_00005263.overview: UGC 6446 is a late-type, bulgeless spiral galaxy [arXiv:1703.08096, arXiv:1103.4928] that exhibits a very smooth velocity field [arXiv:1103.4928] and weak hydrogen-alpha emission [arXiv:1304.0447]. Its stellar and gas rotation curves require a substantial dark matter component [arXiv:1703.08096], which is well-modeled by cored dark matter halos [arXiv:1703.08096].

sources.summary: The paper models UGC 6446 as an irregular/late-type galaxy whose stellar and gas rotation curves require dark matter, with Burkert, Einasto, and Stadel halos fitting its HI rotation curve.

quotes.quote: In this work we study the mass distribution of two irregular galaxies, UGC 6446 and UGC 7524, by means of HI rotation curves derived from high resolution HI velocity fields obtained through the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope data archive. We constrain the stellar and gas content of both galaxies with stellar population synthesis models and by deriving the HI+He+metals rotation curves from the total HI surface density maps, respectively. The discrepancy between the circular velocity maxima of the stellar plus the HI+He+metals rotation curves and the observed HI rotation curves of both galaxies requires the inclusion of a substantial amount of dark matter. We explore the Navarro Frenk and White, Burkert, Di Cintio, Einasto and Stadel dark matter halo models. We obtain acceptable fits to the observed HI rotation curves of UGC 6446 and UGC 7524 with the cored Burkert, Einasto and Stadel dark matter halos.

UGC 6446 is morphologically classified in the EFIGI catalogue Baillard2011 as a transition between spiral and irregular with very weak bulge and no obvious sign of an ordered spiral structure. The NED and HYPERLEDA http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ Makarov2014 morphological types of UGC 6446 are respectively Sd and Scd concordant with the EFIGI catalogue. MCD09 hereafter McDonald2009 performed 1D bulge-disc decomposition of UGC 6446 , among other 65 spiral galaxies in the Ursa Major cluster of galaxies, using K ^ band photometry from Tully1996 , finding that UGC 6446 has a very faint bulge fitted by a Sersic profile Sersic1968 .

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 6446 has a rising M/L profile and argues against a massive spherical dark halo, while leaving non-spherical halo possibilities open.

quotes.quote: UGC 6446 The M/L profile of galaxy UGC 6446 grows with radius attaining values much higher than for the other examined galaxies, even after subtraction of HI and He. Such a singular behavior of the M/L profile is expected at the edge of luminous disk, where the luminosity per unit of surface tends to zero, () 0 , while surface mass density remains finite. Then, the local M/L ratio becomes singular, ()/ () . After subtraction of mass contribution of gas, the increasing M/L ratio indicates, that the stars at the outskirts of luminous disk, contain possible admixture of died stars, "black" dwarfs, neutron stars, or stars of too low a mass for nuclear burning (so called brown dwarfs).

The following thought experiment provides a strong argument against the existence of a spherical halo around galaxy UGC 6446. Suppose for a while that this galaxy is dominated by such a halo. Then, the mass distribution could be considered spherical with good accuracy, thus the corresponding mass function could be calculated from M()= rv^2() G (and this can be done, since the rotation curve of this galaxy satisfies the sphericity condition).

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 6446 as a flattened disk-galaxy test case, showing velocity dispersion can strongly alter inferred mass-to-light behavior in its low-density outer stellar disk.

quotes.quote: We studied galaxy UGC 6446 already in 2010MNRAS.406.2805J where we gave some arguments suggesting why this galaxy should be regarded as a flattened object rather than dominated by a spherically symmetric component. On the other hand, the of this galaxy grew rapidly at the edge of the stellar disk, suggesting a domination of unseen masses. Therefore, we find UGC 6446 to be a good candidate for studying possible sensitivity of the model predictions to additional factors -- such as the velocity dispersion -- modifying the mass density-rotation dependence and the resulting mass-to-light ratio behavior.

We tested this method on the example of galaxy UGC 6446 . We already studied this galaxy in the past by neglecting the velocity dispersion and found a peculiarity in the behavior of the at the edge of the stellar disk. In this paper we showed that in a region of low stellar mass density, the velocity dispersion has a substantial influence on the resulting of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 6446 as the Type 1 example, describing a smooth velocity field, marginal receding/approaching-side differences, and near-zero kinematic perturbation.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig_examples , upper row shows as an example the late-type spiral galaxy UGC 6446. Its velocity field (left panel) is very smooth and shows the typical spider pattern. The derived rotation velocities for receding and approaching sides (right panel) only differ marginally.
In UGC 6446, the rotation velocities of receding and approaching sides are in very good agreement at all radii. Not surprisingly, the value of _ kin is close to zero.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 6446 as a bulgeless SAd galaxy with only one faint possible X-ray source, likely a background contaminant, consistent with weak H emission.

quotes.quote: UGC 6446 is an SAd galaxy at a distance of 18.0 Mpc. Our exposure reveals one dim source within the D25 ellipse. We detect only 4.7 net counts, near our detection threshold but statistically significant. If this source is real, it could be a high-mass X-ray binary within UGC 6446 or, more likely, a background contaminant. As this galaxy is weak in H , the lack of notable X-ray sources is not surprising.

sources.summary: The paper says UGC 6446's MOND curve misses the inner and outer rotation curve differently, and that possible Ursa Major membership would make the galaxy less compatible with MOND.

quotes.quote: UGC 6446 -- The MOND curve falls below the observed rotation curve in the central parts, and above the rotation curve in the outer parts. If this galaxy is part of the Ursa Major cluster (e.g., Tully et al. 1996), the increased distance of 18.6 Mpc (Tully Pierce 2000) would make this galaxy less compatible with MOND. However, this galaxy is on the edge of the boundary taken to define the UMa cluster in both velocity and angle on the sky, so it may not be a genuine cluster member. In this paper, we have adopted a distance of 12.8 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper says UGC 6446 is poorly fit by MOND without the EFE, but an external field brings its observed rotation velocities into excellent agreement.

quotes.quote: (17) UGC 6446 : The rotation curve as predicted by Milgromian dynamics without the EFE falls bellow the observed one in the inner parts of the rotation curve (except the first data points in the central region), and above the observed rotation curve in the outer parts. So, an external field would help to make the Milgromian dynamics prediction more compatible with the observed curve. Milgromian dynamics without the EFE reproduces the observed RC poorly (with a reduced ^2 of about 2.43), while the Milgromian dynamics curve with an EFE is in excellent agreement with the observed rotation velocities. A good MOND fit is found for a value of a_e= 0.325^ +0.090 _ 0.090 a_0 .

gmw_00005264.name: UGC 6983
gmw_00005264.overview: UGC 6983 is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by a central bar and faint spiral arms [arXiv:2106.13809]. It is a member of the Ursa Major Cluster, which provides a strong gravitational field that influences the galaxy's rotation curve [arXiv:1603.02685].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 6983 as another Ursa Major Cluster member whose rotation-curve fit improves when the external field is included and agrees with the cluster field.

quotes.quote: (18) UGC 6983 : This galaxy is also a member of the Ursa Major Cluster and has the same behavior in its rotation curve. The Milgromian dynamics fit without the EFE gives us ^2=1.37 but by using an external field as a free parameter in the rotation curve fit the ^2 reduces to 0.79 and a better fit is achieved with an external field of a_e=0.390^ +0.155 _ 0.175 . The Ursa Major Cluster can produce a strong enough gravitational field as a source of the EFE. As we can see in Figure 2 the amount of external field is in good agreement within the 95 confidence interval with the theoretical value implied by the MOND RC fit.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC06983 as a barred galaxy with faint spiral arms, comparing how AUTOPROF, PHOTUTILS, XVISTA, and GALFIT model its light distribution.

quotes.quote: The fifth column shows UGC06983 , which has a central bar and faint spiral arms. In this case, both AUTOPROF and PHOTUTILS obtained accurate models for the light distribution. XVISTA fitted only the bar feature, though the solution is extended to the optical edge of the galaxy. GALFIT (roughly) recovered the bar and accurately modelled the global light distribution using a two-component fit.

gmw_00005265.name: SDSS J124634.65 + 023809.0
gmw_00005265.overview: SDSS J124634.65 + 023809.0 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1801.03519] located at a redshift of z=0.362 [arXiv:1105.0772]. It is a ray source [arXiv:1801.03519] that features an unresolved, compact core [arXiv:1801.03519] and exhibits strong radio variability [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: This ray source has flat spectral indexes, an unresolved core also seen by VLBA, strong variability, high-frequency nondetections, and star formation that is not dominant.

quotes.quote: J1246+0238 According to Foschini11 , SDSS J124634.65+023809.0 is a ray source. The spectral indexes are all flat, as expected from this kind of source, and in agreement with the index already measured by Foschini15 . The source, as shown in Fig. fig:J1246p0238 , appears only as an unresolved core. The same compact core is reported by Gu15 at 5 GHz with VLBA observations. The flux they measured was less than half our value (8.7 mJy against 19.66 mJy in the core), suggesting that this source is strongly variable.

Despite this, it was not detected neither at 22 GHz nor at 37 GHz. The star formation is not negligible (68 M _ yr ^ 1 ), but the q22 parameter of -0.36 suggests that it is not the main contributor to the radio emission.

sources.summary: The paper discusses SDSS J124634.65 + 023809.0 as a faint, farther NLS1 near a bright Fermi blazar in diagnostic plots, with no evident radio variability between NVSS and FIRST.

quotes.quote: The two other sources, FBQS J1102 + 2239 and SDSS J124634.65 + 023809.0, are farther, with z=0.455 and 0.362 , respectively.

It is worth noting a bright Fermi blazar close to the position of SDSS J124634.65 + 023809.0 in the ray vs radio loudness graphic (Fig. fig:sample1 , right panel ), which is OX + 169 ( z=0.21 ), although in term of radio power the difference between the two is about one order of magnitude (see Fig. fig:radio ).

Instead, SDSS J124634.65 + 023809.0 does not show evident indications of variability, although the two measurements were done on much larger timescale (NVSS, 36 1 mJy, 1995 Feb 27; FIRST, 38 1 mJy, 1998 July).

gmw_00005266.name: NGC 5557-E1
gmw_00005266.overview: NGC 5557-E1 is a gas-rich tidal dwarf galaxy with an estimated age of about 4 Gyr [arXiv:1403.0626, arXiv:1506.07534]. It is located along a low surface brightness tidal tail near the massive elliptical galaxy NGC 5557 and exhibits a very low current star-formation rate [arXiv:1403.0626].

sources.summary: NGC 5557-E1 is presented as a solar-metallicity, gas-rich tidal dwarf about 4 Gyr old, lying in NGC 5557 tidal debris with unusually large size.

quotes.quote: One dwarf, referred as NGC 5557-E1, located in the vicinity of the massive elliptical, NGC 5557, has a gas-phase metallicity of about solar; its oxygen abundance is 0.6 dex above that typical of regular dwarf galaxies, or conversely its abundance corresponds to that of a galaxy ten times more massive. This dwarf, together with two fainter companion objects, E2 and E3 (for which no spectra could be extracted), lie along an extended, very low surface brightness tidal tail, most likely formed during a major merger that occurred at least 2 Gyr ago. A burst model fitting the spectral energy distribution of NGC 5557-E1 provides an age for the dwarf of about 4 Gyr, consistent with the age estimate of the merger at the origin of NGC 5557. Object E1, plus most likely its companions located in the same tidal structure, in particular the apparently relaxed object E2, would thus be the oldest confirmed tidal dwarf galaxies so far identified.

Besides, contrary to typical satellites of massive galaxies, NGC 5557-E1/E2 managed to retain a large gas reservoir despite several Gyr of evolution and interaction with their parent galaxy. They may preserve it for several additional Gyr, given their very low current star-formation rate, as measured from the UV and/or \ luminosity, unless stripping mechanisms become important.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5557-E1 as an old tidal dwarf whose surrounding tidal field can mimic a dark sub-halo and may explain its extended survival.

quotes.quote: NGC 5557 Duc2014 found three TDGs around the early-type galaxy NGC5557. SED fitting of the TDG NGC5557-E1 reveals an age of 4 Gyr, which makes it the oldest TDG uniquely identified so far. The work of this paper provides a possible explanation for the long-term survival of this object.

At the edge of TDG NGC 5557-E1 6 kpc Duc2014 the accelerations are comparable to those of an NFW DM sub-halo with a few times 10^9 or to a Burkert sub-halo with 10^9 . Around the effective radius of NGC 5557-E1 ( r_e = 2.3 kpc ), where the baryonic matter is expected to dominate in all cases Toloba2011 , the additional acceleration from the tidal field is still the same as in DM sub-halo with M_ vir,sub 10^ 8 . In addition, TDG NGC 5557-E1 is also well in the regime where the compression in y and z direction dominates the tidal stretching (grey shaded area in Fig. fig:app ).

gmw_00005267.name: PMN J0641 0320
gmw_00005267.overview: PMN J0641 0320 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and MeV blazar located at a redshift of z=1.196 [arXiv:1602.06446]. It exhibits a hard NuSTAR spectrum produced via inverse Compton scattering, with parsec-scale radio emission resolved by very long baseline array observations [arXiv:1602.06446].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PMN J0641 0320 as a z=1.196 flat-spectrum radio quasar and MeV blazar with hard NuSTAR emission and a robust radio/X-ray counterpart association.

quotes.quote: Here, we report on the discovery by the Fermi Large Area Telescope and subsequent follow-up observations with NuSTAR , Swift and GROND of a new member of the MeV blazar family: PMN J0641 0320. Our optical spectroscopy provides confirmation that this is a flat--spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z=1.196 . Its very hard NuSTAR spectrum (power--law photon index of 1 up to 80 keV) indicates that the emission is produced via inverse Compton scattering off photons coming from outside the jet. The overall spectral energy distribution of PMN J0641 0320 is typical of powerful blazars and by reproducing it with a simple one-zone leptonic emission model we find the emission region to be located either inside the broad line region or within the dusty torus.

The prominent flat--spectrum radio source PMN J0641 0320 fomalont03 lies only 2.4 arcsec away from the XRT centroid and well within its error radius. The source was resolved in prior very long baseline array (VLBA) observations at 8.6 GHz showing parsec--scale emission and a total flux of 0.83 Jy. The radio brightness and the small angular separation between the XRT and the radio source make the probability that the radio source is a background unrelated object negligible petrov13 .

gmw_00005268.name: CXO CDFS J033213.9-274526
gmw_00005268.overview: CXO CDFS J033213.9-274526 is a dusty extremely red object located at a redshift of z=1.327 [arXiv:astro-ph/0211305]. Its hard X-ray properties are consistent with a highly absorbed type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0211305].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CXO CDFS J033213.9-274526 as a dusty z=1.327 ERO and the only Chandra-detected sample object, with hard X-ray properties consistent with a highly absorbed type 2 AGN.

quotes.quote: Only one source, a dusty ERO at z=1.327 , was individually detected; the optical position is almost coincident ( <0.3" , within the accuracy of the K--band position) with the Chandra source CXO CDFS J033213.9-274526 in the G02 catalog.

CXO CDFS J033213.9-274526: a type 2 AGN The only ERO individually detected in the 1 Ms exposure is the highest redshift, highest luminous (R=23.81, K_s =18.44) object in the ``dusty'' sample. It is a very hard X--ray source (F _ 2-8 keV = 7.34 10^ 16 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , =1.4 and N _H =Gal; G02) undetected in the soft band (F _ 0.5-2 keV < 5.48 10^ 17 erg cm ^ 2 ) The observed band ratio (H/S > 7.3) implies an intrinsic X--ray column density larger than 4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , for a classic AGN power--law spectrum with =1.8 ). The observed 2--8 keV luminosity is 7 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 which corresponds to an unabsorbed, rest--frame hard X-ray luminosity 4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , typical of Seyfert galaxies. At the high--energy, therefore, CXO CDFS J033213.9-274526 is naturally classified as a Type 2, active object.

gmw_00005269.name: Mrk 883
gmw_00005269.overview: Mrk 883 is a nearby Seyfert active galactic nucleus that exhibits spectral-type variations [arXiv:2401.03046]. The galaxy displays a disturbed merger morphology featuring a gaseous ring, tidal tails, and a double nucleus with a projected separation of 1.7 kpc [arXiv:2401.03046].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 883 as a nearby Seyfert AGN with spectral-type variations, disturbed merger morphology, a gaseous ring, a double nucleus, and MEGARA-resolved nuclei separated by 1.7 kpc.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert galaxy Mrk 883 is a nearby AGN at z = 0.038. Classified as Sy1.9 by Osterbrock+83 , it was identified by Goodrich+95 as an object that shows spectral-type variations. In particular, broad emission line variations were detected in October 1993, producing a change from Sy1.9 to Sy1.8 (or even Sy1.5), with little change in the optical continuum emission.

Mrk 883 also shows disturbed morphology in the optical images of the SDSS Abazajian+2009 . A gaseous ring and tidal tales can be identified, supporting evidence of past merger activity. A double nucleus in its center can be seen---see Figure fig:sloan . Our MEGARA data presented in this work allowed us to establish that the two nuclear sources (see Section sec7 ), one at the center of Mrk 883 and the other one at the southwest, have a projected separation of 2.2 " or 1.7 kpc.

gmw_00005270.name: S1
gmw_00005270.overview: S1 is a resolved background galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 2.21 [arXiv:1002.0521]. It is positioned behind the galaxy cluster MACS J0744.8+3927, where it is gravitationally lensed into an arc [arXiv:1002.0521].

sources.summary: The paper discusses S1 as a resolved background galaxy at z=2.21 behind MACS J0744.8+3927, arguing its arc should be multiply lensed while disfavoring a three-clump counterimage interpretation.

quotes.quote: We did not find record of past analysis yet recently a spectroscopic redshift of a resolved galaxy behind this cluster was published (Jones et al. 2009; z_ s =2.21 , marked as ``S1'' in Figure curves0744 here). Unfortunately, no counter images were reported. As can be seen in Figure curves0744 , this arc lies within the critical curve for a source at z_ s 2.2 according to our model, and therefore should be multiply-lensed and we mark other possible counter images of this galaxy. Another option, which would favour a steeper model, is that the three clumps seen in this arc are counter images of the same galaxy, though we find this option to be less probable according to the velocity map presented in Jones et al. (2009), which shows that the three clumps have different velocities.

gmw_00005271.name: II Zw 70
gmw_00005271.overview: II Zw 70 is a small, gas-rich blue compact dwarf galaxy experiencing an intense starburst [arXiv:1710.03149]. It is currently interacting with its companion galaxy, II Zw 71, with which it shares a common neutral hydrogen envelope and a connecting gaseous bridge [arXiv:0901.2274].

sources.summary: The paper groups II Zw 70 among nearby starbursts with broad absorption, low velocity shifts, high extinction, and likely static dusty gas that destroys photons.

quotes.quote: Four of the eight \ galaxies observed with GHRS/HST by kunth98 show broad \ absorption profiles: II Zw 70, Mrk 36, SBS 0335-052, and \ studied here. As already noted by these authors, these objects clearly differ from those with \ in emission by very low velocity shifts between the interstellar absorption lines and the systemic velocity Two of the objects with \ in absorption, \ and SBS 0335-052, have also been observed with FUSE. with a large aperture. The measurements of grimes08 confirm the earlier finding of low velocity shifts, now also on a much larger aperture. . An essentially static ISM appears therefore as one of the main factors leading to \ absorption, as already concluded by these authors and as supported by our radiation transfer modeling. Furthermore, among the \ absorbers, SBS 0335-052 and II Zw 70 show clearly higher extinction, with E(B-V) = 0.18 and 0.15 respectively (less than 0.02 for Mrk 36, izotov98 ).

Other nearby galaxies with intense star formation, such as II Zw 70, Mrk 36 and SBS 0335-052, also show strong \ absorption kunth98 . They appear to show a nearly static cold ISM and more or less significant amounts of dust. At least two of these objects exhibit very high \ column density (up to 2.4 10 ^ 21 2). We suggest that \ absorption in these objects is due to the same reasons as for : a very large number of scatterings in static, high column density gas leading to an efficient destruction of \ photons by even small amounts of dust.

sources.summary: The paper classifies II Zw 70 as a small, distant blue compact dwarf galaxy whose metal-poor, gas-rich starburst spectrum and high-frequency radio emission are dominated by thermal radiation.

quotes.quote: II Zw 40 and II Zw 70 are classical blue compact dwarf galaxies (BCGDs). BCDGs are also called H ii galaxies because they are dominated by giant H ii regions occupying much of their total volumes. Their emission-line spectra indicate that they are metal-poor, while they are gas-rich and are experiencing intense bursts of star formation.

II Zw 40 was observed in the radio continuum by klein84a , klein91 , and by deeg93 . II Zw 70 is a small and distant BCDG so that existing radio continuum measurements by klein84a, skillman88, deeg93 only provided integrated flux densities. The high-frequency radiation of these two galaxies is purely thermal.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IIZw70 as the blue compact dwarf companion interacting with IIZw71, sharing an H I envelope and gaseous bridge in an ongoing interaction.

quotes.quote: In the case of IIZw71, there are proofs from interferometric observations of interaction with IIZw70, another BCD. In fact, although clearly separated on optical images, both galaxies share a common HI envelope with a gaseous bridge or streamer connecting both structures (see Figure 3 of Cox et al., 2001). This points to an ongoing interaction between the two galaxies.

gmw_00005272.name: Mrk 36
gmw_00005272.overview: Mrk 36 is a compact H II galaxy undergoing a very young starburst [arXiv:1109.2081]. This intense star formation is concentrated in southeastern knots within a dense parental cloud, possibly triggered by a transient encounter with the neighboring spiral galaxy Haro 26 [arXiv:1109.2081].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 36 among nearby starbursts with broad absorption, low velocity shifts, low extinction, and nearly static dusty gas that can destroy photons.

quotes.quote: Four of the eight \ galaxies observed with GHRS/HST by kunth98 show broad \ absorption profiles: II Zw 70, Mrk 36, SBS 0335-052, and \ studied here. As already noted by these authors, these objects clearly differ from those with \ in emission by very low velocity shifts between the interstellar absorption lines and the systemic velocity Two of the objects with \ in absorption, \ and SBS 0335-052, have also been observed with FUSE. with a large aperture. The measurements of grimes08 confirm the earlier finding of low velocity shifts, now also on a much larger aperture. . An essentially static ISM appears therefore as one of the main factors leading to \ absorption, as already concluded by these authors and as supported by our radiation transfer modeling. Furthermore, among the \ absorbers, SBS 0335-052 and II Zw 70 show clearly higher extinction, with E(B-V) = 0.18 and 0.15 respectively (less than 0.02 for Mrk 36, izotov98 ).

Other nearby galaxies with intense star formation, such as II Zw 70, Mrk 36 and SBS 0335-052, also show strong \ absorption kunth98 . They appear to show a nearly static cold ISM and more or less significant amounts of dust. At least two of these objects exhibit very high \ column density (up to 2.4 10 ^ 21 2). We suggest that \ absorption in these objects is due to the same reasons as for : a very large number of scatterings in static, high column density gas leading to an efficient destruction of \ photons by even small amounts of dust.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 36 as a compact H II galaxy with southeastern star-forming knots, dense-cloud star formation, possible encounter-triggering by Haro 26, and a very young starburst.

quotes.quote: Mrk 36 (Haro 4, UGCA 225) is a compact H ii galaxy showing at least two large star-forming knots L07 in the south-eastern part of the galaxy. T83 reports integrated near-IR colors (de-reddened) of J-H=0.38 and H-K=0.42 , while HE06 reports integrated colors of J-H=0.422 0.100 and H-K=0.543 0.310 . The regions of largest H line emission L07 and HI maps BA04 coincide, indicating that the current SF is restricted to the dense region of the parental cloud. Bravo-Alfaro et al. argue that a transient encounter between Mrk 36 and the neighboring spiral Haro 26 could explain both the SF in the former and the pronounced warp in the latter.

Finally, recent radio observations with the VLA in 1.4, 4.9 and 8.4 GHz by RG07 have shown that Mrk 36 has a nearly flat radio spectrum dominated by thermal emission, similar to the regions detected by JK03 in Henize 2-10. This may be an indication that the first contribution of the synchrotron emission to the low frequency emission, due to the first supernova (SN) explosions, has not yet appeared. These integrated properties show definite evidence of a very young starburst.

gmw_00005273.name: PKS B0405 385
gmw_00005273.overview: PKS B0405 385 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z = 1.285 [arXiv:2205.02402]. It exhibits rapid intraday variability [arXiv:2205.02402] caused by interstellar scintillation from a nearby scattering screen [arXiv:astro-ph/0412396], as well as a 2.8-year quasi-periodic oscillation in its gamma-ray and optical emissions [arXiv:2205.02402].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0405-385 as an FSRQ/blazar and finds a 2.8-year quasi-periodic signal in gamma-ray and optical light curves, with jet or binary-SMBH explanations.

quotes.quote: We analyze the quasi-periodic oscillation (QPO) of the historical light curve of FSRQs PKS 0405-385 detected by the Fermi LAT from August 2008 to November 2021. To identify and determine the QPO signal of PKS 0405-385 in the ray light curve, we use four time series analysis techniques based on frequency and time domains, i.e., the Lomb-Scargle periodogram (LSP), the weighted wavelet z-transform (WWZ), the REDFIT and the epoch folding. The results show that PKS 0405-385 has a quasi-periodic behavior of 2.8 yr with the significance of 4.3 in Fermi long-term monitoring. Remarkably, we also performed QPO analysis in the G-band light curve observed from October 2014 to October 2021 using LSP and WWZ technology, and the results ( 4 of significance) are consistent with the periodic detection in ray. This may imply that the optical emission is radiated by an electron population same as the \ emission. In discussing the possible mechanism of quasi-periodic behavior, either the helical motion within a jet or the supermassive black hole binary system provides a viable explanation for the QPO of 2.8 yr, and the relevant parameters have been estimated.

PKS 0405-385 (also known as 4FGL J0407.0-3826) is identified as an FSRQ based on its robust and broad emission lines in the optical spectrum with a redshift z = 1.285 1990A&AS...86..543V,1997ApJ...490L...9K . Its observations commenced in November 1993, as part of the Australia Telescope Compact Array intraday variability (IDV) survey. The source exhibits fluctuations on times of a day or less in the flux density at gigahertz frequency. The interstellar scintillations model can well explain such an IDV behavior, and then the value of the brightness temperature is near 10^ 13 K at 5 GHz 2003MNRAS.341..230P .

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 0405-385's rapid polarized intraday variability as interstellar scintillation, requiring scattering only 3-30 pc away and implying a compact source with ~2e13 K brightness temperature.

quotes.quote: The remarkable rapid variations in radio flux density and polarization of the quasar PKS 0405-385 observed in 1996 are subject to a correlation analysis, from which characteristic time scales and amplitudes are derived. The variations are interpreted as interstellar scintillations. The cm wavelength observations are in the weak scintillation regime for which models for the various auto- and cross-correlations of the Stokes parameters are derived and fitted to the observations. These are well modelled by interstellar scintillation (ISS) of a 30 22 as source, with about 180 degree rotation of the polarization angle along its long dimension. This success in explaining the remarkable intra-day variations (IDV) in polarization confirms that ISS gives rise to the IDV in this quasar.

However, the fit requires the scintillations to be occurring much closer to the Earth than expected according to the standard model for the ionized interstellar medium (IISM). Scattering at distances in the range 3-30 parsec are required to explain the observations. The associated source model has a peak brightness temperature near 2 10^ 13 K, which is about twenty-five times smaller than previously derived for this source.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS B0405 385 as the first large-amplitude, extremely rapid scintillator; time-delay observations showed nearby-screen interstellar scintillation rather than intrinsic extreme source variability.

quotes.quote: PKS B0405 385 was the first of the large amplitude, extremely rapid variables discovered KJW+97 . Here the very rapidity of the variations, if intrinsic to the source, implied brightness temperatures of up to 10 ^ 21 K, nine orders of magnitude in excess of the inverse Compton limit. This forced a major re-appraisal of the intrinsic variability hypothesis which ultimately led to the first intensity pattern time delay observations and the unequivocal demonstration that interstellar scintillation rather than intrinsic variability was the mechanism responsible for the rapid flux density variations JKL+00 .

Subsequent investigations of PKS B0405 385 and the other two rapid scintillators PKS B1257 326 and CLASS J1819+3845, have revealed that the reason for these fast variations is the presence of nearby ISM scattering screens, rather than any extreme properties ( T_B > 10^ 15 K) of the sources. The rapid variability in each has been shown to be the product of scattering by ISM plasma at a distance of no more than a few tens of pc RKJ02 . Brightness temperatures of the order of 10 ^ 12 K have been established, closely overlapping those found in conventional VLBI and space VLBI observations LJB+03 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0405 385 as a southern z=1.285 intrahour-variable quasar whose rapid 5 GHz variations and frequency dependence favored interstellar scintillation rather than intrinsic variability.

quotes.quote: The discovery of hourly variations in the southern z = 1.285 quasar PKS 0405 385 at 4.8 and 8.6 GHz caused serious difficulties to explain the variability as intrinsic to the source ked97 . As shown in Fig. fig-0405 , the variability was so large and rapid, with changes of up to 50 , or 1 Jy, in an hour or less at 5 GHz, that the implied brightness temperature for intrinsic variability was in excess of 10^ 21 K and so the authors were led to consider ISS. The observed frequency dependence of the modulation amplitude and timescales were both consistent with weak scattering at frequencies of 5 GHz and above, and strong scattering at frequencies below 5 GHz, with the largest amplitude variations close to the transition frequency wal98 . Remarkably, the dramatic variations ceased after a few months.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0405 385 as a fast intraday-variable compact extragalactic source whose rapid variations, time delays, and axial ratio support interstellar scintillation and anisotropic scattering.

quotes.quote: The problem was exacerbated by the discovery of 40--60 min. \ variations in PKS 0405 385 for which the variations, if intrinsic, would imply a brightness temperature 10^ 21 K . It is now clear that the varations are not solely intrinsic to the sources themselves but are due at least partially, if not fully, to ISS .

The first is the measurement of significant time delays between widely separated telescopes in the variability patterns of the three fastest IDV sources, PKS 0405 385 , J1819 + 3845 , and PKS 1257 326 .
An axial ratio 4 :1 is derived for PKS 0405 385 and 15:1 for J1819 + 3845 .

gmw_00005274.name: PKS B1257 326
gmw_00005274.overview: PKS B1257 326 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0412396] and intrahour-variable quasar [arXiv:0709.1301]. It is a persistent rapid scintillator whose fast radio variability and annual cycles are caused by interstellar scintillation from nearby plasma [arXiv:0809.1694, arXiv:astro-ph/0412396].

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS1257 326 as a persistent rapid scintillator whose time-scale and delay data are fit by a one-dimensional anisotropic scintillation model attributed to the scattering medium.

quotes.quote: For the persistent rapid scintillators J1819 + 3845 and PKS1257 326 we show that this model offers a good description of the two-station time-delay measurements and the annual cycle in the scintillation time-scale. Generalising the model to finite anisotropy yields a better match to the data but the improvement is not significant and the two additional parameters which are required to describe this model are not justified by the existing data. The extreme anisotropy we infer for the scintillation patterns must be attributed to the scattering medium rather than a highly elongated source.

PKS1257 326 The time-scale and delay data for this source are presented in Bignall et al (2006). We have confined our attention to the 4.8 GHz data as these are expected to be less affected by atmospheric phase and opacity fluctuations than the 8.5 GHz data. As in Bignall et al (2006) we fit simultaneously to the time-scale and delay, in order to determine which model best fits the data overall, but our approach differs in two respects from theirs.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS B1257 326 as a long-lived rapid scintillator whose annual cycle, microarcsecond structure, and fast variability point to nearby ISM scattering rather than extreme intrinsic properties.

quotes.quote: Such an annual cycle has been found for the two long-lived rapid variables PKS B1257 326 BJL+03 and CLASS J1819 + 3845 DD02 , as well as for several of the slower IDV sources : B0917 + 624 RWK+01,JM01 , PKS B1519 273 JJB+03 , and more recently B0059 + 581 (D. L. Jauncey et al., in preparation). These rapid variables have played a crucial role in establishing ISS as the principal mechanism responsible for centimetre wavelength IDV.

This technique has been sucessfully applied to determine the microarcsecond flux density and polarization structure of PKS B0405 385 RKJ02 and the microarcsecond structure of CLASS J1819+3845 DD03 and PKS B1257 326 BJL+03 .

Subsequent investigations of PKS B0405 385 and the other two rapid scintillators PKS B1257 326 and CLASS J1819+3845, have revealed that the reason for these fast variations is the presence of nearby ISM scattering screens, rather than any extreme properties ( T_B > 10^ 15 K) of the sources. The rapid variability in each has been shown to be the product of scattering by ISM plasma at a distance of no more than a few tens of pc RKJ02 . Brightness temperatures of the order of 10 ^ 12 K have been established, closely overlapping those found in conventional VLBI and space VLBI observations LJB+03 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1257 326 as an ATCA-discovered intrahour-variable quasar whose repeated annual variability cycle and long ATCA-VLA delays show rapid variability caused by interstellar scintillation.

quotes.quote: The IHV quasar PKS 1257 326 was discovered serendipitously with the ATCA big2003 . ATCA monitoring of PKS 1257 326 at 4.8 and 8.6 GHz revealed an annual cycle in the timescale of variability which is repeated over several years of observations at both frequencies. Successful time delay measurements have been made for PKS 1257 326 between the ATCA and VLA on three occasions during 2002-03 big2006 . The observed annual cycle and time delays demonstrate conclusively that the rapid, large-amplitude variability of PKS 1257 326 is entirely due to ISS. A striking feature of the time delay measurements for PKS 1257 326 is the length of the delays; for both PKS 0405 385 and J1819+3845 the measured delays were 2-3 minutes at most, whereas for PKS 1257 326 delays as long as 8 minutes were observed, as shown in Fig. fig-1257 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 1257 326 as one of the fastest IDV sources, citing measured inter-telescope time delays, strong rapid variations, and annual scintillation cycles.

quotes.quote: The first is the measurement of significant time delays between widely separated telescopes in the variability patterns of the three fastest IDV sources, PKS 0405 385 , J1819 + 3845 , and PKS 1257 326 . The delay arises because the intensity fluctuations caused by the scattering medium move across the line of sight with a finite speed (which is a vector sum between the Earth and medium's velocities), so that there is a finite delay between the intensity fluctuations observed on 5000 km baselines. The effectiveness of this technique depends on the ability to measure significant flux density changes on timescales shorter than the time delay between the two telescopes, typically 30--600 s. Time delay experiments have so far been limited to only the three strong sources whose variations are sufficiently strong and rapid.

Annual cycles have now been found for five sources, including J1819 + 3845 , B0917 + 624 , and PKS 1257 326 , and there is increasing evidence (e.g., from the MASIV survey ) that it is present in a number of other sources exhibiting centimeter-wavelength IDV .

gmw_00005275.name: CLASS J1819+3845
gmw_00005275.overview: CLASS J1819+3845 is an extremely variable radio quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0201061] that exhibits rapid, intra-hour interstellar scintillation [arXiv:1408.2200, arXiv:astro-ph/0412396]. This fast variability is caused by a nearby interstellar medium scattering screen [arXiv:astro-ph/0412396, arXiv:0709.1301], while its polarized radio structure indicates the presence of a jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0410109].

sources.summary: The paper follows J1819 + 3845, an intra-hour variable quasar, showing that its extreme scintillation ceased as local scattering material moved out of the line of sight.

quotes.quote: The quasar J1819 + 3845 is the most intensely studied of all the intra-day variable sources (Dennett-Thorpe de Bruyn 2000, 2002, 2003; Macquart de Bruyn 2006, 2007). Its variability, exceeding an rms of 30 of the mean flux on timescales less than 20 minutes at a wavelength of 6 cm, makes it the most extreme variable extragalactic radio source known, and the prototype of a small group of sources called intra-hour variable sources. Frequent monitoring observations have established that the source undergoes an annual cycle in the timescale of variability, caused by Earth's motion about the Sun relative to the turbulent ISM (Dennett-Thorpe de Bruyn 2003). Power spectral analysis of the 6 cm lightcurves have revealed information on the micro-arcsecond structure of the source and shown that it is temporally evolving on a timescale of about a year (Macquart de Bruyn 2007). Most importantly, it has also enabled a measurement of the location of the scattering screen, showing that the extremely rapid variations arise in a region only 1-3 pc from Earth (Dennett-Thorpe de Bruyn 2003; Macquart de Bruyn 2007).

The quality and extent of the data on J1819 + 3845 puts us in the uniquely strong position of being able to isolate the cause of this termination. Were the cause intrinsic to the source, it would enable us to constrain the lifetime of compact IDV components, whereas if the cause were extrinsic it would enable us to confidently deduce a number of physical properties of the scattering screen responsible for the IDV. In this paper we determine whether the discontinuity in fast variations is attributable to an intrinsic or extrinsic cause.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1819+3845 as an extremely variable radio quasar whose hour-scale variations arise from interstellar scintillation, requiring a very small, stable source with an unusual rising radio spectrum.

quotes.quote: The most extremely variable extragalactic source known at radio wavelengths is the quasar J1819+3845. This quasar shows variations of factors of 4 or more on a timescale of hours, easiest interpreted as scintillation due to scattering in the interstellar medium (ISM) . One can think of this scattering medium as focusing and defocusing the waves from the source, producing a pattern of dark and bright patches . As we move through these patches, or scintles, we observe a temporal variation in intensity. In order to display this effect, the source must be small: J1819+3845 is only tens of microarcseconds (a few light-months at the source redshift 0.54) .

Our observations prove beyond doubt that the variations in J1819+3845, the most extreme example of an IDV source, are due to scintillation. Yet, as this source must be extremely small, it could easily be intrinsically variable on time scales of a few months (using simple light-travel time arguments). Using relativistic outflow arguments its is not difficult to bring this down to weeks. Intrinsic variations on these time scales would be very hard to separate from scintillation induced variations. We have evidence however that the source is remarkably stable over a period of many months with only a slow increase in brightness over the last two years (Dennett-Thorpe de Bruyn, in prep).
The small size of the quasar J1819+3845 is probably related to the unusual rising radio spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper uses J1819 + 3845 as a fast IDV source with measured scintillation time delays, polarized substructure, and anisotropic scattering linked partly to jet structure.

quotes.quote: The first is the measurement of significant time delays between widely separated telescopes in the variability patterns of the three fastest IDV sources, PKS 0405 385 , J1819 + 3845 , and PKS 1257 326 . The delay arises because the intensity fluctuations caused by the scattering medium move across the line of sight with a finite speed (which is a vector sum between the Earth and medium's velocities), so that there is a finite delay between the intensity fluctuations observed on 5000 km baselines. The effectiveness of this technique depends on the ability to measure significant flux density changes on timescales shorter than the time delay between the two telescopes, typically 30--600 s. Time delay experiments have so far been limited to only the three strong sources whose variations are sufficiently strong and rapid.

Even a rudimentary analysis shows that the linearly polarized structure of J1819 + 3845 at 5 GHz consists of at least two separate sources, separated by roughly 50 as and indicates their location relative to the rest of the unpolarized emission.

An axial ratio 4 :1 is derived for PKS 0405 385 and 15:1 for J1819 + 3845 . In the latter case, this aspect ratio probably reflects not only scattering but intrinsic structure within the source (i.e., jet structure).

sources.summary: The paper describes CLASS J1819+3845 as a rapid scintillator with time-delay, annual-cycle, and microarcsecond-structure evidence showing fast variability produced by a nearby ISM scattering screen.

quotes.quote: A time delay of several minutes has been found for the three most rapidly variable sources PKS B0405 385 JKL+00 , PKS B1257 326 BJL+04 and CLASS J1819+3845 DD02 .

Such an annual cycle has been found for the two long-lived rapid variables PKS B1257 326 BJL+03 and CLASS J1819 + 3845 DD02 , as well as for several of the slower IDV sources : B0917 + 624 RWK+01,JM01 , PKS B1519 273 JJB+03 , and more recently B0059 + 581 (D. L. Jauncey et al., in preparation). These rapid variables have played a crucial role in establishing ISS as the principal mechanism responsible for centimetre wavelength IDV.

Subsequent investigations of PKS B0405 385 and the other two rapid scintillators PKS B1257 326 and CLASS J1819+3845, have revealed that the reason for these fast variations is the presence of nearby ISM scattering screens, rather than any extreme properties ( T_B > 10^ 15 K) of the sources. The rapid variability in each has been shown to be the product of scattering by ISM plasma at a distance of no more than a few tens of pc RKJ02 . Brightness temperatures of the order of 10 ^ 12 K have been established, closely overlapping those found in conventional VLBI and space VLBI observations LJB+03 .

sources.summary: The paper presents J1819+3845 as a fainter intrahour-variable quasar with extreme 5 GHz variability, ongoing rapid scintillation since discovery, and an unusually nearby Galactic scattering screen.

quotes.quote: After PKS 0405 385, the next IHV quasar to be discovered was the fainter quasar J1819+3845, for which the variability was discovered serendipitously with the WSRT dtdb2000 .
