The estimated jet-to-counterjet ratio is R=1.25 , which leads to a maximum viewing angle <87^ , allowing for a largely misaligned jet orientation.

gmw_00005359.name: PKS 1258 321
gmw_00005359.overview: PKS 1258 321 is a radio galaxy that features a faint, one-sided jet extending to the north-west [arXiv:2007.10474]. This jet exhibits a subluminal apparent speed, and no counterjet has been detected at its radio position [arXiv:2007.10474].

sources.summary: The paper reports a faint one-sided jet in PKS 1258 321, subluminal apparent speed, intrinsic jet constraints, no detected counterjet, and no gamma-ray detection at its radio position.

quotes.quote: 1258 321 The source shows a faint one-sided jet extending to the north-west (see Fig. fig:wallpaper ), aligned with the kpc-scale structure 2005ApJS..156...13M .

1258 321 In this source, only one jet component (J1) can be tracked for at least five epochs. The corresponding apparent speed is subluminal. Figure beta_theta shows the limits on the intrinsic jet speed and viewing angle for PKS 1258 321 resulting from our observations. We do not detect a counterjet in this case, therefore we set a lower limit for the jet-to-counterjet flux ratio ( R ).
PKS 1258 321 is not detected (TS=2.36, F_ UL =2.71 10^ 9 ).

gmw_00005360.overview: The radio galaxy 1733 565 is a Fanaroff-Riley II source that exhibits a double-sided jet structure aligned with its large-scale radio orientation [arXiv:2007.10474]. Its highly disturbed optical morphology features tidal tails, irregular arc-like structures, and a complex system of dust lanes indicating interaction or merger activity [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1733-56 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: This BLRG/FRII, with evidence for starburst activity provided by its FIR excess and strong PAH features in its MIR spectrum Dicken09,Dicken10 , lies in a relatively crowded field. Its optical morphology, as revealed by our GMOS-S image (Figure pks1733_online ), is highly disturbed, showing at least two tidal tails: a shorter one pointing to the West and a longer one to the SE both having _V =23.6 mag arcsec ^ 2 . We also detect several arc-like irregular features, including a smooth, inner one (of _V =23.6 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) up to 6.5 kpc in the SE direction and a larger one of _V =24 mag arcsec ^ 2 extended up to 11.6 kpc to the NW. It is likely that at least part of the complex structure visible in the central regions of the galaxy is due to obscuration by a complex system of dust lanes.

sources.summary: The paper presents 1733 565 as an FR II radio galaxy whose TANAMI data reveal double-sided jet structure aligned with the large-scale radio orientation and stable across epochs.

quotes.quote: 1733 565 This FR II radio galaxy shows a double-sided structure, with jets extending in the northeast-southwest direction, up to 5 mas from the core (see Fig. fig:wallpaper ). Our TANAMI data therefore allow us to recover additional structure with respect to previous VLBI images, where the source appeared as a compact core 2004AJ....127.3609O . The jet orientation follows the one of the large scale radio structure 1982PASAu...4..447H . The jet structure appears to be consistent across the epochs.

gmw_00005361.name: PKS 2027 308
gmw_00005361.overview: PKS 2027 308 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.539 [arXiv:2007.10474]. It features an asymmetric double-sided structure with a south-west jet and a bending northern counterjet, though no jet motion is measured [arXiv:2007.10474].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 2027 308 as the most distant sample radio galaxy, with asymmetric double-sided structure, no contemporaneous 22 GHz data, and no significant jet motion measured.

quotes.quote: 2027 308 This is the most distant radio galaxy in the sample, with a redshift z=0.539 (at this scale, 1 mas corresponds to 6 pc). Interestingly, the source shows an asymmetric double-sided structure, with one jet extending to the south-west and a counterjet which seems to bend north (see Fig. fig:wallpaper ). No contemporaneous 22 GHz data are available for this source.

2027 308 In this case, we do not measure any significant jet motion. The uncertainty of the component positions is very large, due to their faint flux density. Therefore, we do not attempt to estimate the jet viewing angle and intrinsic speed for PKS 2027 308, as such estimates would be extremely uncertain.

gmw_00005362.name: J0933+2114
gmw_00005362.overview: J0933+2114 is a dual active galactic nucleus system composed of two Type I active galactic nuclei [arXiv:2111.00635]. These two optical cores have a spatial separation of 12.3 kpc and a velocity offset of 261 +/− 21 km s−1 [arXiv:2111.00635].

sources.summary: The paper resolves J0933+2114 into two Type I AGN cores, measures redshifts and broad-line widths for each, and reports a 12.3 kpc separation and 261 21 km s ^ 1 offset.

quotes.quote: As an example, we briefly discuss the properties of dual AGN J0933+2114. As shown in Fig. slitimages of J0933+2114 , the two spectra of the dual cores, i.e. J0933+2114EN and J0933+2114WS, are spatially well resolved, allowing robust identification and classification of the system. J0933+2114EN has a the redshift of 0.17234 0.00005 and the FWHM of the NLR is 629 46 km s ^ 1 as measured from the [ O iii ] 5007 line.

This value of BLR is much greater than 2200 km s ^ 1 , thus J0933+2114EN is clearly a Type I AGN. J0933+2114WS has a redshift of 0.17321 0.00005 and the FWHM of the NLR is 548 53 km s ^ 1 , again measured from the [ O iii ] 5007 line.

The velocity of the BLR is again greater than 2200 km s ^ 1 , confirming it a Type I AGN. J0933+2114 is thus identified as a dual AGN composed of two Type I AGNs. The two optical cores have a separation of 12.3 kpc and a velocity offset of 261 21 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00005377.name: nircam2-7122
gmw_00005377.overview: The source nircam2-7122 appears in JWST data as two distinct stellar clumps [arXiv:2211.00045]. The northern clump is an already known galaxy at a lower redshift, while the redder southern clump is a newly detected JWST source [arXiv:2211.00045].

sources.summary: The paper uses nircam2-7122 as a JWST example where two stellar clumps separate into an older HST-detected northern galaxy and a redder southern JWST source.

quotes.quote: We show one of the selected galaxies, nircam2-7122, which seems to have 2 stellar clumps in the JWST data. The Northern part, orange in the RGB image, was included in HST catalogs e.g., in 2017ApJS..229...32S . At wavelengths redder than 2 m, another clump starts to be visible to the South, a region that gets brighter at longer wavelengths while the Northern knot gets relatively dimmer (and almost disappears by 4 m ). Our 2D analysis indicates that the Northern (already known) galaxy lies at a lower redshift, as revealed by the photo-z map, which counts with 4-5 resolution elements for the Northern galaxy and 6-7 times more for the new JWST source. SEDs and photo-z fits are shown in Figure fig:2Dphotoz for the centers of the 2 distinct galaxies.

gmw_00005378.name: Q ER 0047-2808
gmw_00005378.overview: Q ER 0047-2808 is a massive early-type elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.485 [arXiv:1503.02074, arXiv:astro-ph/0606714]. It acts as a gravitational lens, forming the first discovered optical Einstein ring from a highly star-forming background source [arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: The paper constrains the 0047-2808 lens galaxy from HST imaging, favoring isothermal-like mass models, a slightly steeper-than-isothermal power law, a Sersic-plus-point-source light profile, and aligned mass and light.

quotes.quote: Using software specifically designed for the analysis of resolved gravitational lens systems, we focus on how the image alone can constrain the mass distribution in the lens galaxy. We find the data are of sufficient quality to strongly constrain the lens model with no a priori assumptions about the source.

An elliptical power-law model ( R^ ) for the surface mass density favours a slope slightly steeper than isothermal with = 1.08 0.03 . Other models including a constant M/L, pure NFW halo and (surprisingly) an isothermal sphere with external shear are ruled out by the data. We find the galaxy light profile can only be fit with a S ersic plus point source model. The resulting total M/L _B contained within the images is 4.7 h_ 65 0.3 . In addition, we find the luminous matter is aligned with the total mass distribution within a few degrees.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q ER 0047-2808 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: Q ER 0047-2808 (herein Q0047) was discovered serendipitously by warren1 as part of a spectroscopic survey of early type galaxies that included the lens: a massive early type galaxy at z_ l =0.485 . Q0047 was the first optical Einstein ring discovered. warren2 confirmed that the source is a z_ s =3.595 highly star-forming galaxy and measured the ring radius to be =1.35 . There is no prior observational work on the environment of the lens galaxy. Based on non-parametric lens models, wayth conclude that the SIS+external shear models are ruled out by the data.

sources.summary: The thesis describes ER 0047-2808 as an HST Einstein Ring whose Bayesian source reconstruction improved resolution and allowed less constrained lens-model parameters tied to the lens galaxy light.

quotes.quote: The Hubble Space Telescope observations of ER 0047-2808 had already been extensively analysed beforehand, so the results could be directly compared to those obtained by other means. The choice of the prior distribution over pixellated sources was also modified slightly to improve the efficiency of the MCMC exploration. The resulting source reconstruction had a significant improvement in resolution (by about 50 per cent) compared to previous studies, and the lens model parameters were more uncertain because some assumptions could be relaxed and the number of unknown source pixels was increased. Particularly, the central position of the lens model was allowed to be a free parameter - although it was found to be consistent with the central position of the lens galaxy's light profile after all.

sources.summary: The paper models Einstein ring 0047-2808, finding the lens mass profile aligned with the light, a likely binary high-redshift source, and a measured enclosed lensing-galaxy mass.

quotes.quote: Here, we apply the method to the well studied optical Einstein ring 0047-2808. Our results are in broad agreement with previous studies, showing that the density profile of the lensing galaxy is aligned within a few degrees of the light profile, and suggesting that the source galaxy (at redshift 3.6) is a binary system, although its size is only of order 1-2 kpc. We also find that the mass of the elliptical lensing galaxy enclosed by the image is (2.91 0.01) 10 ^ 11 M _ .

gmw_00005379.name: Q J0158-4325
gmw_00005379.overview: Q J0158-4325 is a doubly imaged lensed quasar at a redshift of 1.29 [arXiv:astro-ph/0405521, arXiv:1503.02074] that is classified as a lower-luminosity system [arXiv:2306.03472]. The system is strongly microlensed [arXiv:1306.0008] and displays high-frequency variations in its light curves [arXiv:2110.05500].

sources.summary: The paper describes CTQ 414 as a doubly imaged lensed quasar whose field contains a probable galaxy group and a detected lensing galaxy between the quasar images.

quotes.quote: CTQ 414 (also named Q J0158-4325) has been first observed in the Cal an-Tololo Survey (CTS; Maza et al. 1995), and was fully identified as a doubly imaged quasar at redshift z= 1.29 by Morgan et al. (1999). The quasar images are separated by 1.22 . A PSF subtraction of the quasar images reveals an extended object lying in between the quasar images: it is thought to be the lensing galaxy. It is located 0.72 West to image A. The presence of the lensing galaxy has been confirmed by HST NICMOS observations (CASTLES).

A search for clusters and groups of galaxies on the line of sight towards 8 lensed quasars In this paper we present new ESO/VLT FORS1 and ISAAC images of the fields around eight gravitationally lensed quasars: CTQ 414, HE 0230-2130, LBQS 1009-0252, B 1030+074, HE 1104-1805, B 1359+154, H 1413+117 and HE 2149-2745. When available and deep enough, HST/WFPC2 data were also used to infer the photometric redshifts of the galaxies around the quasars. The search of galaxy overdensities in space and redshift, as well as a weak-shear analysis and a mass reconstruction are presented in this paper.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes COSMOGRAIL microlensing light curves of QJ0158-4325, finding high-frequency variations favor smaller accretion-disk sizes and allow a single-band photometric estimate of the BLR size.

quotes.quote: In this new framework, we analyse COSMOGRAIL light curves of the two-image system known to display high-frequency variations. Using exclusively the low frequency part of the power spectrum our constraint on the accretion disk radius agrees with the thin disk model estimate and previous work that fit the microlensing light curves in real space. However, if we also take into account the high-frequency variations, the data favour significantly smaller disk sizes than previous microlensing measurements.

In this case, our results are in agreement with the thin disk model prediction only if we assume very low mean masses for the microlens population, i.e. = 0.01 . At the same time, including the differentially microlensed continuum reverberation by the BLR successfully explains the high frequencies without requiring such low mass microlenses. This allows us to measure, for the first time, the size of the BLR using single-band photometric monitoring, = 1.6^ +1.5 _ 0.8 10^ 17 cm, in good agreement with estimates using the BLR size-luminosity relation.

sources.summary: The paper presents first black-hole mass estimates for QJ0158 4325, identifies it as among the lower-luminosity systems, and compares Mg II, H, and C IV line estimates.

quotes.quote: There are the first M _ BH estimate obtained for the systems QJ0158 4325 (log _ 10 ( M _ BH /M _ ) = 8.05 0.58, 8.51 0.25 and 8.32 0.46 for C IV , Mg II and H , respectively), HE0512 3329 (log _ 10 ( M _ BH /M _ ) = 8.14 0.25) and WFI2026 4536 (log _ 10 ( M _ BH /M _ ) = 8.28 0.25 and 7.83 0.35, for H and H , respectively).

In particular, we were able to obtain estimates for the lower luminosity systems QJ0158 4325, SDSS0924+0219, HE0512 3329 and HE0047 1756 (from 10 ^ 44 to 10 ^ 46.5 ). The systems QJ0158 4325 and SDSS0924+0219 have the lowest luminosities (log _ 10 ( L _ ref ) < 44.60 L _ ), and the latter has the lowest M _ BH , log _ 10 ( M _ BH /M _ ) = 7.43 0.05 (this is the average of the H and H estimates). htb We separately examine the three systems observed with X-Shooter (QJ0158 4325, LBQS13333+0113, and Q1355 2257) because they have multiple M _ BH estimates using different emission lines.

As in the previous case, the C IV emission line for QJ0158 4325 is not consistent with the other estimates. The mean values for Mg II and H are log _ 10 ( M _ BH /M _ ) = 8.51 0.25 and log _ 10 ( M _ BH /M _ ) = 8.32 0.46, respectively.

sources.summary: QJ 0158 4325 is included among the lens galaxies, with prior Chandra observations and later rejection from dust-to-gas analysis because microlensing dominates over absorption.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we analyze \ observations of 10 gravitational lenses, HE 0047 1756 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2004; Ofek et al. \ 2006), QJ 0158 4325 (Maza et al. \ 1995; Morgan et al. \ 1999; Faure et al. \ 2009), SDSS 0246 0805 (Inada et al. \ 2005; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 0435 1223 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2002; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 0924+0219 (Inada et al. \ 2003a; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 1004+4112 (Inada et al. \ 2003b), PG 1115+080 (Weymann et al. \ 1980; Kundi et al. \ 1997), Q 1355 2257 (Morgan et al. \ 2003; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 1104 1805 (Wisotzki 1993; Lidman et al. \ 2000), and Q 2237+0305 (Huchra et al. \ 1985), to measure the differential X-ray absorption between the lensed images, the metallicity, and the dust-to-gas ratio of lens galaxies.
Observations of QJ 0158 4325, HE 0435 1223, SDSS 0924+0219, SDSS 1004+4112, HE 1104 1805, and Q 2237+0305 were reported in Chen et al. \ (2012).

We detected no absorption in HE 1104 1805 and SDSS 0924 + 0219, and we had to drop QJ 0158 4325 and SDSS 0246 0825 since the differential extinction E(B-V) and absorption N_H are of opposite sign. This indicates that microlensing is more important than absorption. We know QJ 0158 4325 is strongly microlensed (Morgan et al. \ 2012) while SDSS 0246 0825 is less well studied.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q J0158-4325 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: Q J0158-4325 ( a.k.a. CTQ 0414, herein Q0158) is a doubly imaged z_ s =1.294 quasar lens with image separation 1.2 morgan99, faure09 . The lens redshift is z_ l =0.317 0.001 faure09 . The lens galaxy spectrum and light distribution are consistent with it being an elliptical galaxy. An over-density of galaxies is found at a photometric redshift z=0.5 0.1 by faure04 .

gmw_00005380.name: Q J1017-207
gmw_00005380.overview: Q J1017-207 is a doubly imaged quasar at a redshift of 2.545 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465, arXiv:1503.02074]. It is a galaxy-scale strong lens system with an image separation of approximately 0.85 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465, arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: The paper says Q1017 207 has an unidentified lens galaxy, but Mg II absorption at z=1.088 likely traces a massive galaxy capable of lensing.

quotes.quote: Q1017 207 : A doubly imaged quasar, with 0 85 image separation, discovered by Claeskens et al. (1996). The quasar is at a redshift of 2.545 and the fundamental-plane redshift of the lensing galaxy is z=0.78 0.07 (Kochanek et al. 2000). Due to limited S/N (only 1.6 hr, of a requested 4.0 hr exposure time, were actually obtained) we fail to identify the type or redshift of the lens galaxy. Claeskens et al. (1996) detected a Mg II 2800 absorber, with z=1.085 , in the combined spectra of the images, and suggested that it could be due to the lensing galaxy.

In our VLT spectra, the Mg II absorber is apparent in both images, and we refine its redshift to z=1.088 0.001 (Fig. Q1017_MgII_Abs ). The absorption equivalent widths are 9.4 and 5.5 (observed frame) for image A and B, respectively. After correcting for the expected contamination by image A, we estimate that the Mg II absorption equivalent width in image B is 4.2 (observed frame). The correction was made by calculating the ratio between the blended flux of image B (in the 5700-5800 range), to the expected flux of image A at the same cross-dispersion position, based on the point-spread-function wing of image A on the side opposite to image B. By inspecting the HST-FOS spectra (Fig. 2 in Surdej et al. 1997), we estimate that the S/N ratio of the VLT spectrum is 10-20 times higher than that of the HST/FOS spectra. This may explain the absence of the Mg II absorber in image B in the HST/FOS spectra. If, as we suspect, the Mg II absorber is indeed present in the image B spectrum, the physical separation at the redshift of the absorber, about 7 kpc, suggests that the absorber is a massive galaxy capable of being the lens.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q J1017-207 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: Q J1017-207 ( a.k.a. CTS J03.13, herein Q1017) was discovered by claeskens by careful analysis of the optical images for selected highly luminous quasars, which reveled that the J03.13 quasar consisted of at least two separate components. Further observations surdej suggested that the two sources were images of the same z_s=2.545 quasar separated by 0.849 0.001 . lehar2000 detect the galaxy in HST NICMOS imaging. kochanek2000 estimate the lens galaxy redshift to be z_l = 0.78^ +0.09 _ 0.05 based on Fundamental Plane fitting. A reliable spectroscopic redshift is not available, but ofek estimate z_ l =1.088 0.001 based on a Mg II absorption line in the quasar spectra.

gmw_00005381.name: MG1131+0456
gmw_00005381.overview: MG1131+0456 is a galaxy-scale strong gravitational lens system [arXiv:1503.02074] featuring a passively evolving giant elliptical main lens [arXiv:0802.4044]. The system exhibits a radio Einstein ring [arXiv:1503.02074] and contains two clusters or rich groups of galaxies in its field [arXiv:0802.4044].

sources.summary: The paper says MG1131+0456 has a passively evolving giant elliptical main lens and uses Einstein-ring Faraday rotation to infer a lens-galaxy magnetic field.

quotes.quote: The nature of the lensing object in the systems MG1131+0456 and 1938+666 has remained enigmatic for a long time, although the redshifts have now become available koch:00 , t45 . The main lens in both the systems appears to be a passively evolving giant elliptical, but probably there are two clusters or rich groups of galaxies in the field of MG1131+0456. Still, we believe that Rotation Measure estimates for these systems are important due to the presence of extended structures in the background source.

For the system MG1131+0456, the reported Faraday Rotation measurements chen:93 are primarily for bright regions of the Einstein Ring. It is conceivable that these features are, perhaps not identifiable with images of the same source-region and hence we cannot speculate on intrinsic magnetic field of any absorbers near the source. However, based on their detailed analysis (cf. Fig.6 chen:93 ), we speculate that the difference in Rotation Measure between spots R1 and R4, of 1200 rad m ^ 2 , is an indication of the presence of a magnetic field in the lens galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats MG 1131 + 0456 as a known lens galaxy system, assigning mass-distance and galaxy-mass estimates and finding its predicted Einstein ring radius close to the observed value.

quotes.quote: Finally, for case (2), we have considered two known lens systems, MG 1654 + 134 , and MG 1131 + 0456 . For these two systems, M/D_d is taken as 4.8 10^ 12 and 1.32 10^ 11 respectively. The mass estimates of the galaxies are known to be be 10^ 11 M_ and 10^ 12 M_ respectively.

In Figs. ( mag2 ) and ( mag3 ), we have shown the location of the ER for this data, where we have chosen = 10^ 3 , so that the lensing is effectively from the external Schwarzschild region. We find that for the system MG 1654 + 134, the predicted value of the ER is at 0.83'' , while it is at 0.75'' for MG 1131 + 0456. This is close to observed values of 1'' and 0.87'' for these galaxies, respectively , .

sources.summary: The paper describes MG1131+0456 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: MG1131+0456 (herein MG1131) was observed with the VLA as part of the MIT -- Green Bank (MG) survey. Even a short exposure revealed its unusual ring-like morphology. Follow-up observations hewit, chen identified it as a radio Einstein ring with two compact, embedded sources and revealed an even more complex morphology: a radio jet lensed into an elliptical ring, the radio core doubly-imaged, and the oposite radio jet unlensed. In the optical kochanek2000a , there is an incomplete ring image of the AGN host galaxy, while in H-band the ring image of the host galaxy is complete. Ground-based optical and IR imaging larkin showed that both the lens and source were extremely red.

gmw_00005382.name: HST J12531-2914
gmw_00005382.overview: HST J12531-2914 is an early-type galaxy that acts as a galaxy-scale strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1503.02074]. It produces four lensed images in an Einstein cross configuration [arXiv:1503.02074], requiring a shear of magnitude 0.2 that suggests a misalignment between the major axes of its light and mass potential [arXiv:astro-ph/9702021, arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: The paper finds HST 12531--2914 requires significant additional shear and a misalignment between the major axes of its lensing galaxy's light and potential.

quotes.quote: Application to these seven cases reveals two quadruple lenses CLASS 1608+656 and HST 12531--2914, requiring highly significant shear with magnitude 0.2 . For HST 12531--2914, there must be a misalignment between the major axis of light and the major axis of potential (mass).
For HST 12531-2914, this estimate is in rough agreement with the position angle of the light, _P=22^ .6 0.5 (R95).

First for HST 12531--2914, the positional angle of light (solid line) falls in the unphysical region where q^2 is negative. This means that there must be a misalignment between the major axis of the potential and that of the light. This conclusion is valid regardless of the radial profile of the potential.

sources.summary: The paper describes HST J12531-2914 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: HST J12531-2914 (herein HST12531) was discovered serendipitously in HST WFPC2 observations as part of the Medium Deep Survey MDS, ratnatunga . The four images in an Einstein cross configuration are separated by 1 and are much fainter ( I 25-27 ) than the early type lens galaxy I 19-22 . Based on fundamental plane fitting, kochanek2000 estimate z_l=0.63^ +0.20 _ 0.03 . Lens models of HST12531 require unusually large shear 0.2 witt , suggesting a misalignment between the galaxy and its DM halo, or perhaps a complex environment.

gmw_00005383.name: LBQS 1333+0113
gmw_00005383.overview: LBQS 1333+0113 is a doubly lensed quasar [arXiv:1503.02074] with a background source redshift of 1.57 [arXiv:1503.02074]. The system is gravitationally lensed by an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1503.02074] and exhibits microlensing in the red wings of its emission lines [arXiv:1206.0731].

sources.summary: SDSS J1335+0118 has low residual deconvolution, a B/A trend attributed to differential extinction rather than chromatic microlensing, and red-wing microlensing in emission lines.

quotes.quote: (j) SDSS J1335+0118: Deconvolution: During the deconvolution process, we forced the separation between the bright lens image A and the lens galaxy component G to be identical to the HST separation. Small residual flux left after deconvolution close to A and B amounts less than 0.03 of the QSO flux. Chromaticity: The change of B/A from the blue to the red is incompatible with CML, as the latter has to be stronger at bluer wavelengths. Instead, we hypothesise DE ( M( blue )= 0.21, M( red )= 0.23, = 1.396), image A being more reddened than image B . We discard the possibility that the observed chromatic trend is caused by residual contamination from the lensing galaxy as we estimate that more than 70 of the observed flux of the galaxy should contaminate image B to flatten the spectral ratio.
Broad Lines: We observe ML of the red wing of   and of . For and  , there is a second (very)-broad component which is microlensed.

sources.summary: LBQS1333+0113 was observed with X-shooter, has previous Mg II estimates, is analyzed among systems with multiple line-based masses, and shows agreement between Mg II and H.

quotes.quote: X-shooter LBQS1333+0113, QJ0158 4325 and Q1355 2257 were observed with X-shooter between August of 2019 and April of 2021 (ESO proposal ID 103.B-0566(A) ; PI: A. Melo). We used two Observing Blocks (OBs) for each system with a slit width of 1 0 11 for the UVB band (resolution of R = 5400) and 1 2 11 for VIS and NIR arm (R = 6500 and 4300 respectively).

The systems HE0047 1756, HE0435 1223, SDSS0924+0219, SDSS1226 0006, LBQS1333+0113, Q1355 2257 and WFI2033 4723 have previous estimates of M _ BH using the Mg II emission lines ( 2006Peng,2012Sluse, 2017DingI ) which we compare to our Balmer lines estimates in Figure fig:halphavsmgii . Lensed quasars that have one or both M _ BH estimates presented in this work are shown in color.

We separately examine the three systems observed with X-Shooter (QJ0158 4325, LBQS13333+0113, and Q1355 2257) because they have multiple M _ BH estimates using different emission lines. In the case of LBQS1333+0113, we only use H and Mg II because C IV line exhibits multiple absorption features and H has low S/N (Melo et al. in prep). Mg II and H are in good agreement with mean values of log _ 10 (M _ BH /M _ ) = 9.37 0.31, and 9.14 0.51, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes LBQS 1333+0113 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: LBQS 1333+0113 ( a.k.a. SDSS J1335 + 0118, herein LBQ1333) was identified as a quasar in the Large Bright Quasar Survey LBQS, hewett , but not as a lensed source: hewett1998 looked for lenses in LBQS, but did not discover this one because they were only sensitive to separations larger than 3 . oguri04 identified it as a doubly lensed z_s=1.57 0.05 quasar in the SDSS. The image separation is 1.56 . The lens redshift is z_l=0.440 0.001 eigenbrod . Based on its spectrum and colors, both eigenbrod and oguri04 conclude that the lens is an early type galaxy.

gmw_00005384.name: Q1355-2257
gmw_00005384.overview: Q1355-2257 is a doubly imaged quasar at a redshift of 1.373 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465, arXiv:astro-ph/0511026]. The system is strongly lensed by a foreground galaxy [arXiv:1503.02074] with a tentative redshift of 0.702 [arXiv:astro-ph/0612419, arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: Q1355-2257 needed a wavelength calibration shift; its continuum ratio varies nonmonotonically, suggesting complex microlensing of the continuum and broad-line region.

quotes.quote: (k) Q1355-2257 We found an error in the wavelength calibration of image B and therefore, we had to shift the original wavelength solution by 2  for that image. : Deconvolution: During the deconvolution process, we forced the separation between the bright lens image A and the lens galaxy component G to be identical to the HST separation. The deconvolution is good but low level residual flux, up to 0.2 of the faintest image, is visible aside the faintest lensed image and the lensing galaxy. A small excess of flux appears in the background and in the PSF component of the galaxy at the wavelength of the peak of the  emission.

Chromaticity: Contrary to what is observed for the other systems, the spectral ratio B/A in the continuum does not vary in a monotonic way (Fig.  fig:Ratiomain k). The factor A is roughly the same in the continuum for > 5450   but decreases significantly for bluer wavelengths. This trend cannot be explained by contamination from the lensing galaxy.

sources.summary: Q1355 2257 was observed with X-shooter and analyzed with multiple emission lines; its mass estimates vary by line, while Mg II agrees with a prior measurement.

quotes.quote: X-shooter LBQS1333+0113, QJ0158 4325 and Q1355 2257 were observed with X-shooter between August of 2019 and April of 2021 (ESO proposal ID 103.B-0566(A) ; PI: A. Melo). We used two Observing Blocks (OBs) for each system with a slit width of 1 0 11 for the UVB band (resolution of R = 5400) and 1 2 11 for VIS and NIR arm (R = 6500 and 4300 respectively).

We separately examine the three systems observed with X-Shooter (QJ0158 4325, LBQS13333+0113, and Q1355 2257) because they have multiple M _ BH estimates using different emission lines. In the case of LBQS1333+0113, we only use H and Mg II because C IV line exhibits multiple absorption features and H has low S/N (Melo et al. in prep). Mg II and H are in good agreement with mean values of log _ 10 (M _ BH /M _ ) = 9.37 0.31, and 9.14 0.51, respectively.

Q1355 2257 exhibits a wide range of mass estimates depending on the emission line with mean values of log _ 10 ( M _ BH /M _ ) = 8.25 0.17, 9.24 0.11 and 8.84 0.71 for C IV , Mg II and H , respectively (green color in figure fig:all ). The Mg II measurement from 2012Sluse (log _ 10 ( M _ BH /M _ ) = 9.04 0.34) agrees with our estimate using the same line.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Q 1355 2257, tying Mg II absorption to the lens spectrum and confirming a higher lens redshift near 0.702.

quotes.quote: Q 1355 2257 (CTQ 327) : This two-image quasar, discovered by Morgan et al. ( ), has a redshift of z =1.373 , and an image separation of 1.23 . The redshift of the lensing galaxies has been estimated by Morgan et al. ( ) to lie in the range 0.4 < < 0.6 . Ofek et al. ( ) note that their spectrum of the quasar component B shows an excess of emission longward of 5870 . They associate the location of this emission excess to the 4000- \ break of the lensing galaxy leading to a redshift of =0.48 . In a previous paper (Eigenbrod et al. ), we gave a significantly higher tentative redshift value of =0.701 .

A re-analysis of our data allows to extend slightly our spectra to the blue side and to unveil the Mg II absorption line in the spectrum of the quasar component B. The absorption is seen at the redshift we estimate for the spectrum of the lens, i.e. =0.702 0.001 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Q1355 2257 as a doubly imaged quasar whose lens galaxy is estimated around z=0.4-0.6, with spectral excess consistent with a z=0.48 break.

quotes.quote: Q1355 2257 : A doubly imaged quasar, with 1 23 image separation, discovered by Morgan et al. (2003). The quasar is at a redshift of 1.373 . Based on the flux of the galaxy and the Faber-Jackson (1976) relation, Morgan et al. (2003) estimated that the lens galaxy lies in the redshift range 0.4<z<0.6 , and suggested that the Mg II 2796,2803 absorption feature at z=0.48 identified in their HST/FOS spectra of the system, could be associated with the lens galaxy.

We note that our spectrum of quasar B, which would capture the most flux from the lens galaxy, shows an excess of emission relative to quasar A at wavelengths longward of 5870 (see Fig. QSOs_HE0047_Q1355 ). This roughly corresponds to the wavelength at which a z=0.48 galaxy would have its 4000 break.

sources.summary: The paper gives a tentative redshift of 0.701 for the lensing galaxy in Q 1355 2257, noting that extraction is difficult because it lies close to a much brighter quasar image.

quotes.quote: For the first time we measure the redshift of the lensing galaxy in three multiply-imaged quasars: SDSS J1138 + 0314 ( =0.445 ), SDSS J1226 0006 ( =0.517 ), SDSS J1335 + 0118 ( =0.440 ), and we give a tentative estimate of the redshift of the lensing galaxy in Q 1355 2257 ( =0.701 ).

Q 1355 2257 (CTQ 327) : This doubly imaged quasar has a redshift of z=1.373 and was discovered by Morgan et al. ( ). The quasar images are separated by 1.23 and the redshift of the lensing galaxies has been estimated by Morgan et al. ( ) to lie in the redshift range 0.4 < < 0.6 . The extraction of the spectrum of the lensing galaxy is particularly difficult because the galaxy lies at only 0.29 \ away from quasar image B and because it is 7 magnitudes fainter. We show the deconvolved spectrum of the lensing galaxy in Fig. Q1355_lens . Given the low signal-to-noise of this spectrum we cannot securely determine the lens redshift; we can only give a tentative estimate of =0.701 .

sources.summary: Q 1355 2257 is discussed in the detailed lens fits; its absorbed power-law model is acceptable, but the authors detect no iron-line emission in either image.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we analyze \ observations of 10 gravitational lenses, HE 0047 1756 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2004; Ofek et al. \ 2006), QJ 0158 4325 (Maza et al. \ 1995; Morgan et al. \ 1999; Faure et al. \ 2009), SDSS 0246 0805 (Inada et al. \ 2005; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 0435 1223 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2002; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 0924+0219 (Inada et al. \ 2003a; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 1004+4112 (Inada et al. \ 2003b), PG 1115+080 (Weymann et al. \ 1980; Kundi et al. \ 1997), Q 1355 2257 (Morgan et al. \ 2003; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 1104 1805 (Wisotzki 1993; Lidman et al. \ 2000), and Q 2237+0305 (Huchra et al. \ 1985), to measure the differential X-ray absorption between the lensed images, the metallicity, and the dust-to-gas ratio of lens galaxies.

In this paper, we only discuss the fits to Q 1355 2257, SDSS 0246 0805, HE 0047 1756, and PG 1115+080 in detail (Figure fig:spectra ). Power-law emission modified by foreground absorption gives acceptable fits for Q 1355 2257, HE 0047 1756, SDSS 0246 0825 (reduced ^2_ =1.09, 1.20, 1.15 for d.o.f.= 65 , 88, and 61, degrees of freedom, respectively), but not for PG 1115 + 080 (a reduced ^2_ =1.31 with d.o.f. = 363 ).
We detected no line emission in either image of Q 1355 2257.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q1355-2257 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: Q1355-2257 ( a.k.a. CTQ 0327, herein Q1355) was discovered by morgan during an HST STIS snapshot campaign to find small separation gravitational lenses. The two images of the z=1.37 quasar are separated by 1.22 , and the lens is an early type galaxy at z_l 0.4-0.6 based on the Faber-Jackson relationship morgan . eigenbrod07 give a tentative measurement of the lens redshift z_l=0.702 . The lens redshift is not in NED, but we have added it to our spectroscopic catalog.

gmw_00005385.name: HST J14113+5211
gmw_00005385.overview: HST J14113+5211 is an early-type galaxy located in the CL 140933+5226 cluster at a redshift of 0.465 [arXiv:1503.02074]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens, producing a quadruple image of a background source at a redshift of 2.811 [arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: The paper describes HST J14113+5211 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: HST J14113+5211 (herein HST14113) is a quadruple lens with maximum separation 2.28 discovered serendipitously by fischer in the z=0.46 cluster CL 140933+5226 ( a.k.a. 3C 295). The lens is identified as an early type galaxy most probably belonging to the cluster. lubin measure the lens and source redshifts: z_l=0.465 and z_s=2.811 . The cluster has velocity dispersion =1300 based on 21 members dressler .

gmw_00005386.name: SBS 1520+530
gmw_00005386.overview: SBS 1520+530 is a gravitationally lensed, doubly imaged quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0607133, arXiv:1811.07932] that exhibits intrinsic optical variations [arXiv:astro-ph/0505489] and microlensing [arXiv:astro-ph/0607133, arXiv:astro-ph/0505489]. Its host galaxy contains dust emission and shows radio properties consistent with active star formation [arXiv:1811.07932].

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 1520+530 as a doubly imaged quasar whose NOT light curves show residual trends and a broad bump interpreted as microlensing.

quotes.quote: SBS 1520+530 The doubly imaged quasar SBS 1520+530 (see Table table:1 ) was monitored between February 1999 and May 2001 at the NOT. In Fig. sbs we show the light curves of the two images, A and B, where B has been shifted in both time ( 130 days) and brightness ( 0.69 mag).

In both residual plots one notices a constant magnitude increase and an approximately 200-day-wide magnitude bump. In the following we characterize such residuals phenomenologically using linear or Gaussian f its. A linear f it shows that the slopes of the plots are 0.0073 0.0014 mag/100 days for m_ AB and 0.0070 0.0016 mag/100 days for m_ BA .

The agreement between these values indicates that the mean difference between the images increases by about 0.007 mag per 100 days. This is consistent with the observations of G05 who found an increase in the magnitude difference between the quasar images of 0.14 0.03 mag over 1500 days (0.009 0.002 mag/100 days). We interpret this signal as evidence for microlensing.

sources.summary: The paper compares two SBS 1520+530 data sets and, using NMF and MD analyses, revises the doubly lensed quasar delay to about 126 days.

quotes.quote: SBS 1520+530 Two data sets exist for this doubly lensed quasar: the set made available by 2002yCat..33910481B and the one published by 2005yCat..34400053G . 2002AA...391..481B were the first to publish a time delay for this system t_ AB = 128 3 days, where A is the leading image, or t_ AB = 130 3 days when using the iterative version of the method 2001AA...380..805B . 2005AA...440...53G used an independent data set and found four possible time delays, of which the one with the largest statistical weight, t_ AB = 130.5 2.9 , is perfectly consistent with the previously published time delays.

Applying different tests to 2002AA...391..481B 's light curves, shown in Fig. fig:SBS1520 , using the NMF method led to a time delay for which the error bars overlap with the published value: t_ AB = 126.9 2.3 . That the delay is slightly shorter than 2002AA...391..481B 's value can be explained by our use of the reduced _ red ^ 2 instead of the ^ 2 , the latter implying that longer delays are the more likely ones, as explained in Section sec:method .

The MD method yields a comparable time delay of t_ AB = 124.6 3.6 days and confirms the shape of the histogram: the highest peak value at t_ AB 125 days and a clear secondary peak at t_ AB 127.5 days. Combining the values from both methods implies that t_ AB = 125.8 2.1 days.

sources.summary: The paper detects lensed quasar SBS 1520+530 at 144 MHz, notes complex lensing and dust emission, and finds radio properties consistent with star formation.

quotes.quote: SBS 1520+530 is a gravitationally lensed quasar at z_s=1.86 that was discovered in the Second Byurakan Survey Chavushyan:1997 . The primary lensing galaxy is at z_l=0.72 Burud:2002 . There is also evidence for a secondary lensing galaxy with a photometric redshift of z_l 0.9 Faure:2002 . The two lensed images are separated by 1.59 .

Stacey:2018 detected dust emission, likely from the quasar host galaxy, at 250 m and 350 m, and blended emission at 500 m that included flux from nearby stars. The fitted dust temperature is 46.2^ +12.7 _ 7.2 K and the derived lensing-corrected SFR is 190^ +100 _ 60 M_ \ yr^ 1 assuming a typical magnification of 10. The peak of the dust emission is not well constrained, hence there are large uncertainties in the derived dust temperature and FIR luminosity for this object.

sources.summary: The paper studies the SBS1520 lensing galaxy, revising its likely redshift, morphology, environment, and lens model, and arguing interactions may steepen its mass profile.

quotes.quote: We report on an investigation of the SBS 1520+530 gravitational lens system. We have used archival HST imaging, Keck spectroscopic data, and Keck adaptive-optics imaging to study the lensing galaxy and its environment. The AO imaging has allowed us to fix the lens galaxy properties with a high degree of accuracy when performing the lens modeling, and the data indicate that the lens has an elliptical morphology and perhaps a disk.

The mass slope modeled in our analysis is steeper than expected for early-type galaxies e.g., koopmans , but the presence of a nearby galaxy, Galaxy M, suggests that SBS1520 may be undergoing interaction-induced steepening e.g., dobke . read have used pixelated mass models to model SBS1520 and find a nearly isothermal profile, = 1.95 . These models are able to account for `shape degeneracies' that may also effect interpretations of time delays saha , though the analysis of saha indicates that shape degeneracies are probably not important for SBS1520. Note that read do not explicitly model the lens environment and they are therefore measuring the joint projected mass slope of the lens and environment.

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 1520+530 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: SBS 1520+530 (herein SBS1520) is a doubly imaged z_ s =1.855 BAL quasar discovered in the Second Byurakan Survey SBS, sbs1520 . The images are separated by 1.005 . The redshift of the lens galaxy is ambiguous: burud02 measure z_l=0.71 0.005 , but auger08 claim z_ l =0.761 is more likely. Because of this ambiguity, neither redshift is added in our catalog. The time delay between the images is t=125.8 2.1 days eulaers11 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS 1520+530 as a doubly imaged quasar whose lensing potential requires the main lensing galaxy plus external shear from a larger group or cluster.

quotes.quote: is a doubly imaged quasar at z= 1.855 , with an angular separation of 1.568 ( _ 2000 =15 h 21 m 44.83 s , _ 2000 =+52 54 48.6 ). It was discovered by Chavushyan et al. (1997) in the course of the Second Byurakan Survey (Markarian Stepanian, 1983). Crampton et al. (1998) identified the lensing galaxy from near-IR adaptive optics images and proposed a simple lens model including exclusively the main lensing galaxy. They concluded however that an additional external shear was mandatory to model properly the system. Deep ground--based and HST data are used in the present paper to map the mass distribution along the line of sight to SBS 1520+530.

sources.summary: The paper monitors the double quasar SBS1520+530, finding small intrinsic optical variations, consistency with the known 130-day delay, and a changed image magnitude difference indicating microlensing.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a monitoring campaign of the double quasar SBS1520+530 at Maidanak observatory from April 2003 to August 2004. We obtained light curves in V and R filters that show small-amplitude m 0.1 mag intrinsic variations of the quasar on time scales of about 100 days. The data set is consistent with the previously determined time delay of t=(130 3) days by Burud02 . We find that the time delay corrected magnitude difference between the quasar images is now larger by (0.14 0.03) mag than during the observations by Burud02 . This confirms the presence of gravitational microlensing variations in this system.

gmw_00005387.name: MG J1549+3047
gmw_00005387.overview: MG J1549+3047 is a background radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.17 [arXiv:astro-ph/0609542, arXiv:1503.02074]. One of its radio lobes is gravitationally lensed into an Einstein ring by a foreground SB0 galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.11 [arXiv:astro-ph/0609542, arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: The paper models MG1549+3047 as a radio lobe lensed by an SB0 galaxy, using archival VLA data and revising the lens model, Einstein radius, enclosed mass, and core radius.

quotes.quote: MG1549+3047 MG1549+3047 (also known as 4C+30.29) is a z=1.17 radio lobe being lensed by a z=0.11 SB0 galaxy 1993AJ....105..847L,1996AJ....111.1812L,2003MNRAS.343L..29T . We use previously unpublished, archival VLA data of MG1549+3047 in this analysis. 1993AJ....105..847L used VLA data taken on 1991 August 2 in A configuration with a 20 min integration and achieved an RMS noise of 0.01 mJy beam ^ 1 . MG1549+3047 was re-observed on 1992 November 18 at 1.46 GHz (5 min), 4.89 GHz (30 min), 8.41 GHz (50 min) and 14.96 GHz (190 min) again with the VLA A configuration. We use this new 8.41 GHz data in this work because the 14.96 GHz data has very poor signal in the ring.

For MG1549+3047, we show that the previous lens model had an incorrect Einstein radius and therefore the mass enclosed in the ring was overestimated. In addition, we showed that the model requires a small non-singular core, consistent with expectations for a star-dominated mass distribution in the centre of the lens galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes MG J1549+3047 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: MG J1549+3047 (herein MG1549) was recognized as a gravitational lens by lehar1993a in a radio source mapped as part of the MIT-Greenbank-VLA survey lehar1991 . The z_ l =0.111 galaxy lenses one of the radio lobes of a z_ s =1.170 0.001 treu2003 background radio galaxy into an radio Einstein ring. The velocity dispersion of the SB0 lens galaxy is _ =227 18 lehar96 . A third object at z=0.604 0.001 lies projected between the lens and the radio galaxy producing the jet.

gmw_00005388.name: MG J1654+1346
gmw_00005388.overview: MG J1654+1346 is a system where a giant elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.254 acts as a gravitational lens [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594, arXiv:1503.02074]. This lens galaxy, situated in a complex environment with a group of neighboring galaxies, distorts a lobe of a background radio quasar into an Einstein ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594, arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: The paper treats MG 1654 + 134 as a known lens galaxy system, assigning mass-distance and galaxy-mass estimates and finding its predicted Einstein ring radius close to the observed value.

quotes.quote: Finally, for case (2), we have considered two known lens systems, MG 1654 + 134 , and MG 1131 + 0456 . For these two systems, M/D_d is taken as 4.8 10^ 12 and 1.32 10^ 11 respectively. The mass estimates of the galaxies are known to be be 10^ 11 M_ and 10^ 12 M_ respectively.

In Figs. ( mag2 ) and ( mag3 ), we have shown the location of the ER for this data, where we have chosen = 10^ 3 , so that the lensing is effectively from the external Schwarzschild region. We find that for the system MG 1654 + 134, the predicted value of the ER is at 0.83'' , while it is at 0.75'' for MG 1131 + 0456. This is close to observed values of 1'' and 0.87'' for these galaxies, respectively , .

sources.summary: MG 1654+1346 is described as a radio quasar lens whose southwest lobe forms a ring around a giant elliptical lens galaxy in a complex galaxy environment.

quotes.quote: MG 1654+1346 (hereafter MG1654) was originally detected in the MIT--Greenbank--VLA survey. langstonmg1654-88,langstonmg1654-89 recognized its unusual structure in a VLA snapshot and obtained radio and optical mapping. The source is a z_s=1.74 radio quasar with a compact core and two extended radio lobes. The southwest lobe is lensed into a ring by a z_l=0.254 giant elliptical galaxy langstonmg1654-88,kochanekfundplane-00 . langstonmg1654-89 noted an enhancement of the number density of galaxies near the lens; some of the nearby galaxies are comparable in brightness to the lens galaxy, suggesting a complex environment.

sources.summary: The paper describes MG J1654+1346 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: MG J1654+1346 (herein MG1654) was recognized as an unusual radio source in the MIT-Greenbank-VLA survey. A z_l=0.254 giant elliptical galaxy lenses one of the radio lobes of a z_ s =1.74 radio quasar into a radio Einstein ring langston88, langston89, kochanek2000 . langston89 notice an enhancement in the number density of galaxies around the lens, suggesting a complex environment. my identify a group of seven galaxies with =200^ +120 _ 80 at the lens redshift.

gmw_00005389.name: PMN J2004-1349
gmw_00005389.overview: PMN J2004-1349 is a two-image gravitational lens system discovered in a southern radio survey [arXiv:1503.02074]. The lensing galaxy is a spiral galaxy, while the background source is considered a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J2004-1349 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: PMN J2004-1349 (herein PMN2004), discovered by winn in a southern survey for radio lenses, is a two image lens. The radio spectral index of the source is typical for radio-loud quasars, so it is considered a quasar despite the lack of optical confirmation. The lens is a spiral galaxy. Based on photometry, winn suggest 0.5<z_l<1.0 , but extinction considerations used to explain the color differences between the images imply lower values of 0.03 z_l 0.36 winn03 .

gmw_00005390.name: CLASS B2114+022
gmw_00005390.overview: CLASS B2114+022 is a strong gravitational lens system featuring four radio components arranged in an atypical configuration [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594, arXiv:1503.02074]. The system contains two lens galaxies at different redshifts, including a foreground galaxy with an E+A spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594, arXiv:1503.02074].

sources.summary: B2114+022 is described as an unusual JVAS lens with four radio components, two lens galaxies at different redshifts, and a complex two-plane lensing geometry.

quotes.quote: B2114+022 (hereafter B2114) was discovered as part of the search for lenses in JVAS kingb2114-99 . Radio maps show four distinct components within 2.4 in a configuration that is atypical for lenses. Furthermore, the sources can be divided into two pairs with distinct radio surface brightnesses and radio spectra: sources A and D are similar to each other, and sources B and C are similar to one another, but the two pairs are clearly different.

Ground-based and HST optical imaging and spectroscopy do not detect the lensed images but reveal two lens galaxies at z_ l1 =0.3157 and z_ l2 =0.5883 augustob2114-01 , suggesting a complex lensing geometry. chaeb2114-01 could explain two of the radio components (A and D) as lensed images using a two-plane lens model. It is not known whether the other components (B and C) are images of the same source (unlikely), images of a different source, or structure related to the G1 lens galaxy. No lens models that explain these components have been published.

sources.summary: The paper describes CLASS B2114+022 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: CLASS B2114+022 (herein B2114) was discovered in the Jordell Bank - VLA Astrometric Survey king99 . The four radio sources are arranged in an atypical lens configuration. The four images can be divided into two distinct groups: A and D are similar to each other, as are B and C, but the pairs are different from one another. Optical observations fail to detect the images, but identify two lens galaxies with early type colors and morphologies at z_ l1 =0.3157 and z_ l2 =0.5883 augusto . The foreground galaxy G1 has a spectrum characteristic of E+A galaxies.

gmw_00005391.name: Mrk 1058
gmw_00005391.overview: Mrk 1058 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.07635]. Its central region features a strong-emission zone and gas blueshifts that suggest the presence of a nuclear outflow impacting the interstellar medium [arXiv:2107.07635].

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 1058's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: The largest extent of the SER of this Seyfert 2 galaxy is observed towards the SW, that can be identified with the orientation of the ionisation axis. This direction is almost perpendicular to the PMA. Excess blueshifts are observed in the gas relative to the symmetric velocity field everywhere, as previously shown by Freitas2018 . The blueshifts are more conspicuous in the velocity field along the ionisation axis and are associated with an increase in the , suggesting the presence of an outflow impacting the ISM there, even though 600 everywhere.

gmw_00005392.name: NGP-190387
gmw_00005392.overview: NGP-190387 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy at a redshift of z=4.4 [arXiv:2203.01980]. Discovered by the H-ATLAS survey, its star formation was likely boosted by a galaxy merger [arXiv:2203.01980].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGP-190387 as a lensed z=4.4 galaxy whose ALMA HF absorption and fluorine abundance require rapid enrichment from rotating massive Wolf-Rayet stars.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGP-190387 was discovered by the H-ATLAS survey with the Herschel satellite, with the redshift confirmed by the Northern Extended Millimetre Array (NOEMA). It is a gravitationally lensed galaxy with a magnification factor 5 . The lowest rotational transitions from the HF molecule appeared as an absorption line in the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) data.

This molecule is very stable and the dominant gas-phase form of fluorine in the ISM. Figure fig:franco shows the fluorine abundance of NGP-190387 (yellow point), comparing with galactic chemical evolution models with various star formation timescales. In the models, it is assumed that star formation took place soon after the reionization, which was boosted probably due to galaxy merger 100 Myr before the observed redshift z=4.4 .

gmw_00005393.name: MRC 2153--699
gmw_00005393.overview: MRC 2153--699 is a nearby radio galaxy located to the east of PKS 2153--69 [arXiv:astro-ph/0412547]. It has been identified with a 20th magnitude galaxy and exhibits X-ray emission from its nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0412547].

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra detection of MRC 2153--699, a nearby radio galaxy east of PKS 2153--69, modeling its X-ray spectrum and noting uncertain redshift and luminosity.

quotes.quote: The Nearby Galaxy MRC 2153--699 We also detect X-ray emission from the nucleus of the radio galaxy MRC 2153--699 that lies almost 4 to the east of PKS 2153--69 (Fig. fig:mrc2153-699 ). The spectrum of MRC 2153--699 is reasonably well described ( ^2/ d.o.f = 10 /8 ) by an absorbed ( N_ H = 1.1^ +1.0 _ 1.0 10^ 22 ) power law ( = 0.9^ +0.7 _ 0.4 ) with an observed 1 -- 5 keV X-ray flux of 1.9 10^ 13 . Its redshift and therefore luminosity are unknown, although it has been identified with a 20^ th magnitude galaxy 1992ApJS...80..137J , and imaged in the radio by 1998MNRAS.296..701F .

gmw_00005394.name: ID15
gmw_00005394.overview: ID15 is a star-forming galaxy that lies below the main sequence and exhibits a low star formation rate [arXiv:2109.07545]. It features a cuspy dark matter profile, although its halo concentration parameter is poorly constrained [arXiv:2109.07545].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID15 as a low-SFR, below-main-sequence SFG with a cuspy dark-matter profile, while noting that its halo concentration is poorly constrained and degenerate with virial velocity.

quotes.quote: These generally agree with the predicted mild anticorrelation between concentration and virial mass. fig:cMvir (left) shows the c_ vir M_ vir relation for the best 6 cases in our sample, that is excluding the two interacting galaxies (ID919, ID943) as well as ID15 because its concentration parameter remains unconstrained and degenerate with V_ vir (see fig:corner b).
SFGs below the MS with decaying SFR (like ID15) have low SFR densities owing to the low SFR, and show cuspy DM profiles, indicating that cusps reform when galaxies stop being active.

The halo mass for ID1002 and the concentration for ID15 are poorly constrained. fig:corner:cM shows the potential correlations between c_ vir and M_ vir from the \ fits. As in the left panel, the triangles represent the best-fit values. This shows figure shows that the halo concentration is well determined except for ID15 and ID943.

gmw_00005395.name: ID912
gmw_00005395.overview: ID912 is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2109.07545]. It has a low-inclination, face-on orientation and stands out as an exception to the expected dark matter scaling relations for its sample [arXiv:2109.07545].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID912 as a low-inclination MXDF star-forming galaxy, restricts its inclination fit, and identifies it as the exception to the sample's dark-matter scaling relation.

quotes.quote: We find good agreement except for ID912 and ID919, which have the most face-on inclination with i_ <30^ . For these two galaxies, we restrict the \ fits to i_ O2 <35^ .

We do not use priors on the inclination Except for the two low-inclination galaxies (ID912, 919). because the i-V degeneracy is broken from the simultaneous fit of the kinematics with the morphology, as discussed in section:kinematics .

For illustrative purposes, we show the recent z=0 sequence for low surface brightness (LSB) galaxies of DiPaoloC_19a . fig:cMvir shows that 5 of the 6 SFGs tend to follow the expected scaling relations for DM, the exception being ID912.

gmw_00005396.name: ID919
gmw_00005396.overview: ID919 is a star-forming galaxy that is actively interacting with a small satellite galaxy located at the same redshift [arXiv:2109.07545]. This system displays visible tidal tails and exhibits discrepancies in both its disk-mass and rotation-curve estimates [arXiv:2109.07545].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID919 as an interacting MXDF star-forming galaxy with a same-redshift satellite and tidal tails, and notes discrepancies in disk-mass and rotation-curve estimates.

quotes.quote: In particular, ID943 has a large companion 1" away (masked) and ID919 has a small satellite at the same redshift, as seen in fig:examples , and both of these galaxies show signs of tidal tails.
This figure shows that there is relatively good agreement between the disk mass estimates from our \ model fits (described in section:disk:halo ) and the SED-based ones, except for ID919 and ID943.

Comparing the solid with the dotted red lines in fig:diskhalo , one can see that there is generally good agreement between v_ () obtained from the HST photometry and from our disk-halo decomposition with \ of the MUSE data, except again for ID919 and ID943.

gmw_00005397.name: ID943
gmw_00005397.overview: ID943 is an interacting star-forming galaxy located at high redshift [arXiv:2109.07545]. The system features a large companion located approximately 1 arcsecond away, and it exhibits visible signs of tidal tails [arXiv:2109.07545].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID943 as an interacting MXDF star-forming galaxy with a large companion and tidal tails, and notes discrepancies in disk-mass and rotation-curve estimates.

quotes.quote: In particular, ID943 has a large companion 1" away (masked) and ID919 has a small satellite at the same redshift, as seen in fig:examples , and both of these galaxies show signs of tidal tails.
This figure shows that there is relatively good agreement between the disk mass estimates from our \ model fits (described in section:disk:halo ) and the SED-based ones, except for ID919 and ID943.

Comparing the solid with the dotted red lines in fig:diskhalo , one can see that there is generally good agreement between v_ () obtained from the HST photometry and from our disk-halo decomposition with \ of the MUSE data, except again for ID919 and ID943.

gmw_00005398.name: main source ID 2073
gmw_00005398.overview: The galaxy designated as main source ID 2073 is a high-redshift dropout system [arXiv:1409.0512]. It is strongly lensed, producing four distinct images located around the center of the cluster Abell 2744 [arXiv:1409.0512].

sources.summary: The paper identifies main source ID 2073 as a strongly lensed high-redshift dropout system with four images near A2744's center, revising one image position relative to earlier work.

quotes.quote: There is one system (main source ID 2073) with four images located around the center of the cluster (see Fig. fig:multi ). All images were selected as high- z dropouts (cf. Table tab:multiples ). This system was already identified in atek14b where only two images were included in the dropout selection. We corrected the position of one image (5.4) that was previously misidentified.

gmw_00005399.name: object 2994
gmw_00005399.overview: Object 2994 is a highly magnified, multiply imaged galaxy located in the Abell 2744 cluster field [arXiv:1409.0512, arXiv:2305.01793]. It has a red continuum [arXiv:1409.0512] and has been spectroscopically confirmed at a high redshift of z=9.76 [arXiv:2305.01793].

sources.summary: The paper treats object 2994 as image B of a reported z ~10 multiply imaged galaxy in A2744, but notes its red continuum and less convincing high-redshift solution.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, Zi14, hereafter zitrin14 recently reported the detection of a multiply-imaged system in A2744 with an estimated redshift of z 10 . We confirm one of the multiple images where object 2994 in our sample corresponds to image B of the z 10 galaxy. The primary image A was not selected because of its red color, even though it satisfies the continuum break condition. Our candidate has also a red continuum, falling at the edge of the selection window. The high-redshift solution derived from our photometric redshift estimate is less convincing than what has been reported in Zi14.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Candidate ID 10619 as a highly magnified multiple image of a z=9.76 galaxy, with its spectroscopic redshift matching the photometric estimate.

quotes.quote: Candidate ID 10619 is one of the three multiple images of a candidate galaxy previously identified in the HFF data by zitrin14 . It has the highest magnification ( 11.5 ). It has been spectroscopically confirmed at z=9.76 with NIRSpec prism spectroscopy by roberts-borsani22 . This value is in good agreement with our photometric redshift estimate of z=9.69_ 0.12 ^ +0.33 .

gmw_00005400.name: HATLASJ000330.6 321136
gmw_00005400.overview: HATLASJ000330.6 321136 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens galaxy at a photometric redshift of 0.38 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a spectroscopic redshift of 3.08, forms two regular arcs on the southeast and northwest sides of the lens [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ000330.6 321136 (HerBS155; S 1) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ phot =0.38 Shirley2021 with two Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source is located at z_ s ^ spec =3.08 , as measured by the BEARS survey Urquhart2022 . It forms two regular arcs on the SE and NW sides of the lens.

gmw_00005401.name: HELMSJ003619.8 + 002420
gmw_00005401.overview: HELMSJ003619.8 + 002420 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens galaxy at a redshift of 0.26 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a redshift of 1.62, forms an almost complete ring with a diffuse component and three bright knots [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HELMSJ003619.8 + 002420 (HELMS14; S 4) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ spec =0.26 Nayyeri2016 with three Sérsic components using GALFIT to account for the different location of their centres. The background lensed source is located at z_ s ^ spec =1.62 Cox2023 and forms an almost complete ring ( 2 arcsec) consisting of a diffuse component and three brighter knots located on the SE, SW, and NW sides of the lens. Two additional faint NE and SW structures are visible close to the ring, but they do not have a clear lensed morphology.

gmw_00005402.name: HERSJ011722.3 + 005624 _ 1,2
gmw_00005402.overview: HERSJ011722.3 + 005624 _ 1,2 is a type 3 foreground gravitational lensing system [arXiv:2311.01158]. The system is formed by a main galaxy at a photometric redshift of 0.87 and a secondary fainter galaxy, which lens a background source located at a spectroscopic redshift of 2.47 [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERSJ011722.3 + 005624 _ 1,2 (HERS10; S 6) : This type 3 foreground lensing system is formed by a main galaxy (labelled as `1' in Fig. fig:models_full ) at z_ l ^ phot =0.87 Nayyeri2016 and possibly a secondary fainter galaxy (labelled as `2'), for which the redshift is not yet available. This prevents us from being more conclusive about the lensing nature of the secondary galaxy. We model the two galaxies with GASP2D using one Sérsic and one Gaussian component for the brighter galaxy and a single Sérsic component for the fainter one. The background lensed source is located at z_ s ^ spec =2.47 Cox2023 and forms an arc on the E side of the lens and a diffuse secondary image on the NW side.

gmw_00005403.name: HERSJ012620.5 + 012950
gmw_00005403.overview: HERSJ012620.5 + 012950 is a type 1 foreground lens galaxy located at a photometric redshift of 0.43 [arXiv:2311.01158]. It gravitationally lenses a background source at a spectroscopic redshift of 1.45, which forms two arcs on the northeast and southwest sides of the lens [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERSJ012620.5 + 012950 (HERS5; S 7) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ phot =0.43 Nayyeri2016 with one Sérsic and one Gaussian component using GASP2D . The background lensed source is located at z_ s ^ spec =1.45 Cox2023 and forms two arcs on the NE and SW sides of the lens. The SW arc shows an additional diffuse radial component, which extends westward.

gmw_00005404.name: HERSJ020941.2 + 001558 _ 1,2
gmw_00005404.overview: HERSJ020941.2 + 001558 _ 1,2 is a dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.553 [arXiv:1511.06770, arXiv:2311.01158]. It is gravitationally lensed by a foreground system at z = 0.20, which distorts the background source into a bright extended arc and two knots [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper describes ACT-S J0210+0016 as a z=2.553 lensed DSFG, unusually bright for the ACT sample, with high apparent FIR luminosity and intrinsically luminous dust emission after lens correction.

quotes.quote: ACT-S J0210+0016 ( z=2.553 ) is the lensed DSFG published in Geach15 with a lens redshift from SDSS of z=0.202 . This source is discussed further in Section subsec:results_with_redshift and Appendix app:0210 .

In terms of these priors, one exception is the case of ACT-S J0210+0016; this source is so bright compared to the other ACT and W13 sources that the goodness-of-fit suffered with the prior on . We therefore use a uniform prior for when modelling ACT-S J0210+0016.

Geach15 have also studied ACT-S J0210+0016. Using a single-temperature, modified-blackbody SED model and the known redshift, they find that T = 38 4 K and = 1.8 0.4 , results that are in agreement with our estimate of T_ = 42.3 2.2 K and the assumption = 2.0 . However, Geach15 estimate the apparent FIR luminosity as = (1.3 0.1) 10^ 14 L _ , while our fitting gives = 2.2^ +0.2 _ 0.1 10^ 14 L _ .

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERSJ020941.2 + 001558 _ 1,2 (HERS1; S 8) : This type 3 foreground lensing system is formed by a main galaxy (labelled as `1' in Fig. fig:models ) and a second fainter galaxy (labelled as `2'), which are both located at z_ l ^ spec =0.20 Nayyeri2016, Liu2022 . We use GASP2D to model the brighter galaxy with three Sérsic components and the fainter one with a single Sérsic component. The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =2.55 Geach2015 forms a bright extended arc on the SW side of the lens and two knots on the E and SE sides. This system was studied in detail by Geach2015 and Liu2022 , who measured an Einstein radius of _ E =2.48^ +0.02 _ 0.01 arcsec.

gmw_00005405.name: HERMESJ032637 270044
gmw_00005405.overview: HERMESJ032637 270044 is a type 1 foreground gravitational lens [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source of this system forms two compact images on the east and west sides of the lens, which is circled by a fainter incomplete ring [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ032637 270044 (HECDFS05; S 9) : We model this type 1 foreground lens with a single Sérsic component using GASP2D . The background lensed source forms two compact images on the E and W sides of the lens, which is circled by a fainter incomplete ring. This system was studied in detail by Calanog2014 , who measured an Einstein radius of _ E =0.96^ +0.02 _ 0.03 arcsec.

gmw_00005406.name: HERMESJ033732 295353
gmw_00005406.overview: HERMESJ033732 295353 is a type 1 foreground lens galaxy located at a photometric redshift of 0.19 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source of this system forms a bright extended arc with two knots and an inner secondary image [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ033732 295353 (HECDFS02; S 10) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ phot =0.19 Shirley2021 with three Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source forms a bright extended arc (divided into two knots on the NE side of the lens) and an inner secondary image on the SW side. This system was studied in detail by Calanog2014 , who measured an Einstein radius of _ E =1.65^ +0.03 _ 0.05 arcsec.

gmw_00005407.name: HATLASJ083051 + 013225 _ 1,2
gmw_00005407.overview: HATLASJ083051 + 013225 _ 1,2 is a complex strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:1801.01831, arXiv:2311.01158]. Its foreground lens consists of two galaxies at different redshifts [arXiv:1801.01831, arXiv:2311.01158], while the background lensed source at redshift 3.11 exhibits two intersecting arcs [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper treats HATLASJ083051.0+013225 as a complex lensed system with two foreground lenses; possible background-galaxy emission and lens-parameter discrepancies make its modelling degenerate.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ083051.0+013225 : This is a relatively complex system (see Fig. 1 of N17), with two foreground objects at different redshifts (B17) revealed at 1.1 and 2.2 by observations with HST /WFC3 (N17; Negrello et al. in prep.) and Keck /AO 2014ApJ...797..138C , respectively. However the same data show some elongated structure north of the two lenses, which may be associated with the background galaxy, although this is still unclear due to the apparent lack of counter-images (a detailed lens modelling of this system performed on ALMA+ HST + Keck data is currently ongoing; Negrello et al. in prep.). In our modelling we have assumed that the two lenses are at the same redshift, consistently with the treatment by B13.

However, compared to B13, we derive an Einstein radius that is higher for one lens ( 0.57^ versus 0.43^ ) and lower for the other one ( 0.31^ versus 0.39^ ). The discrepancy is likely due to the complexity of the system, which may induce degeneracies among the model parameters; however it is worth mentioning that while we keep the position of both lenses as free parameters, B13 fixed the position of the second lens with respect to the first one, by setting the separation between the two foreground objects equal to that measured in the near-IR image.

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ083051 + 013225 _ 1,2 (HerBS4; S 11) : This type 2 foreground lens is formed by two galaxies, one (labelled as `1' in Fig. fig:models ) at z_ l ^ spec =0.63 and the other (labelled as `2') at z_ l ^ spec =1.00 Bussmann2013 . We deblend them with GALFIT by simultaneously modelling their surface brightness with a single Sérsic component each. The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =3.11 Yang2017 shows a very complex structure with two intersecting arcs on the NW side of the lens. This system was studied in detail by Enia2018 , Yang2019 , and Maresca2022 who modelled high-resolution SMA 340 GHz, Bussmann2013 , ALMA (Band 7, Amvrosiadis2018 ; and Band 4 Yang2019 ) observations.

gmw_00005408.name: HERMESJ100144 + 025709
gmw_00005408.overview: HERMESJ100144 + 025709 is a type 1 foreground lens galaxy located at a redshift of 0.61 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source forms three compact images surrounded by a fainter ring, with an Einstein radius measured at 0.91 arcseconds [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ100144 + 025709 (HCOSMOS01; S 12) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ spec =0.61 Calanog2014 with a single Sérsic component using GASP2D . The background lensed source forms three compact images on the NE, S, and NW sides of the lens, which is surrounded by a fainter ring. This system was studied in detail by Calanog2014 , who measured an Einstein radius of _ E =0.91^ +0.01 _ 0.01 arcsec. It was also observed with ALMA Band 7, Bussmann2015 in the sub-mm/mm wavelength range and with Keck/NIRC2 AO K _ s band, Calanog2014 in the near-IR.

gmw_00005409.name: HERMESJ103827 + 581544
gmw_00005409.overview: HERMESJ103827 + 581544 is a type 1 foreground lens galaxy located at a redshift of 0.59 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source forms two knots on the southeast and northwest sides of the lens, surrounded by fainter, extended arcs [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ103827 + 581544 (HLock04; S 13) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ spec =0.59 SDSS DR13, Albareti2017 with two Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source forms two knots on the SE and NW sides of the lens, which is also surrounded by other fainter and more extended arcs. This system was studied in detail by Calanog2014 , who measured an Einstein radius of _ E =2.40^ +0.01 _ 0.05 arcsec and it was also observed with SMA 340 GHz, Bussmann2013 .
