The sub-mm/mm follow-up observations show no significant difference in morphology with respect to the near-IR observations, with the exception of a positional offset.

gmw_00005410.name: HERMESJ110016 + 571736
gmw_00005410.overview: HERMESJ110016 + 571736 is a type 1 foreground lens galaxy located at a redshift of 0.78 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source of this system produces an extended arc on the northwest side and a faint, compact secondary image on the southeast side [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ110016 + 571736 (HLock12; S 14) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ spec =0.78 SDSS DR13, Albareti2017 with a single Sérsic component using GASP2D . The background lensed source gives rise to an extended arc on the NW side of the lens and a very faint compact secondary image on the SE side. This system was studied in detail by Calanog2014 , who measured an Einstein radius of _ E =1.14^ +0.04 _ 0.07 arcsec.

gmw_00005411.name: HATLASJ125126 + 254928
gmw_00005411.overview: HATLASJ125126 + 254928 is a background lensed source located at a spectroscopic redshift of 3.44 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The source forms four knots, three of which are connected by a faint northern arc and have detected sub-millimeter counterparts [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ125126 + 254928 (HerBS52; S 16) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ phot =0.62 Negrello2017 , with a single Sérsic component using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =3.44 Bakx2020 forms four knots located N, NE, S, and NW with respect to the lens, three of which are connected by a fainter arc extending on the N side. This object was also observed with ALMA (Band 6, Prop. ID 2018.1.00526.S, PI I. Oteo). The sub-mm/mm follow-up observations show the counterparts of the N, NE, and S images.

gmw_00005412.name: HATLASJ133008 + 245860
gmw_00005412.overview: HATLASJ133008 + 245860 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens galaxy at a redshift of 0.43 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a redshift of 3.11, forms three compact images and is surrounded by a faint, incomplete ring [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ133008 + 245860 (HerBS12; S 18) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ spec =0.43 Bussmann2013 with one Sérsic and one Gaussian component using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =3.11 Calanog2014 forms three compact images on the N, SE, and SW sides of the lens, which is also surrounded by a fainter incomplete ring. This system was studied in detail by Calanog2014 , who measured an Einstein radius of _ E =0.944^ +0.002 _ 0.001 arcsec. It was also observed with ALMA (Band 7, Prop. ID 2018.1.00966.S, N. Indriolo), SMA 340 GHz, Bussmann2013 , PdBI 240 GHz, Omont2013 , and NOEMA 283 GHz, Yang2016 in the sub-mm/mm wavelength range and with Keck/NIRC2 AO K _ s band, Calanog2014 in the near-IR.

gmw_00005413.name: HATLASJ133846 + 255057
gmw_00005413.overview: HATLASJ133846 + 255057 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a type 1 foreground lens at a photometric redshift of 0.42 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a photometric redshift of 2.34, forms two extended arcs with a sharp break on its north and south sides [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ133846 + 255057 (HerBS29; S 19) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ phot =0.42 Negrello2017 with a single Sérsic component using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ phot =2.34 Bakx2018 forms two extended arcs with a sharp break on the S and N sides of the lens. This system was observed with SMA 340 GHz, Bussmann2013 too. The sub-mm/mm follow-up observations show no significant difference in morphology with respect to the near-IR ones.

gmw_00005414.name: HERMESJ171451 + 592634
gmw_00005414.overview: HERMESJ171451 + 592634 is a type 1 foreground lens galaxy located at a redshift of 1.24 [arXiv:2311.01158]. It gravitationally lenses a background source at a redshift of 3.18, which forms two arcs on the northeast and southwest sides surrounded by a faint ring [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ171451 + 592634 (HFLS02; S 21) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ spec =1.24 Shirley2021 with a single de Vaucouleurs component using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =3.18 Hernan-Caballero2016 forms two arcs on the NE and SW sides of the lens, which is surrounded by a fainter ring. This system was studied in detail by Calanog2014 , who measured an Einstein radius of _ E =0.87^ +0.02 _ 0.05 arcsec.

gmw_00005415.name: HERMESJ171545 + 601238 _ 1,2
gmw_00005415.overview: HERMESJ171545 + 601238 _ 1,2 is a foreground gravitational lensing system consisting of a main galaxy at a photometric redshift of 0.40 and a secondary unresolved galaxy [arXiv:2311.01158]. This system lenses a background source at a spectroscopic redshift of 2.26, which appears as an irregular eastern arc and a compact western image [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ171545 + 601238 _ 1,2 (HFLS08; S 22) : This type 3 foreground lensing system is formed by a main galaxy (labelled as `1' in Fig. fig:models_full ) at z_ l ^ phot =0.40 Shirley2021 and possibly a secondary unresolved galaxy (labelled as `2'), for which the redshift is not yet available. This prevents us from being more conclusive about the lensing nature of the secondary galaxy. We used GASP2D to model the brighter galaxy with two Sérsic components and one PSF and the fainter one with a single Sérsic component. The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =2.26 Calanog2014 forms an extended and irregular arc E of the lens and a compact secondary image on its W side.

gmw_00005416.name: HATLASJ225844.8 295124
gmw_00005416.overview: HATLASJ225844.8 295124 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a background lensed source at a photometric redshift of 2.48 [arXiv:2311.01158]. This background source forms an extended arc on the southwest side of a foreground lens system composed of two blended galaxies [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ225844.8 295124 (HerBS26; S 23) : This type 2 configuration is consistent with two blended galaxies at z_ l ^ phot =0.69 Shirley2021 , which we deblend by simultaneously fitting them with GALFIT using one and two Sérsic components, respectively. The background lensed source is located at z_ s ^ phot =2.48 Bakx2018 . It gives rise to an extended arc encircling the lens on the SW side but without showing any clear secondary image. This is likely due to the shear effect caused by the multiple-lens system.

gmw_00005417.name: HELMSJ232210.3 033559 _ 1,2
gmw_00005417.overview: HELMSJ232210.3 033559 _ 1,2 is a foreground gravitational lensing system consisting of a main galaxy at a photometric redshift of 0.14 and a secondary fainter galaxy [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a spectroscopic redshift of 4.69, forms an extended irregular arc and multiple secondary images [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HELMSJ232210.3 033559 _ 1,2 (HELMS19; S 24) : This type 3 foreground lensing system is formed by a main galaxy (labelled as `1' in Fig. fig:models_full ) at z_ l ^ phot =0.14 Nayyeri2016 and possibly a secondary fainter galaxy (labelled as `2'), for which the redshift is not yet available. This prevents us from being more conclusive about the lensing nature of the secondary galaxy. We model the two galaxies with GASP2D using two and one Sérsic components, respectively. The background lensed source is located at z_ s ^ spec =4.69 Cox2023 and forms an extended irregular arc on the E side of the lens, a compact secondary image on the SW side, and possibly a third fainter image on the NW side.

gmw_00005418.name: HATLASJ233037.2 331217 _ 1,2
gmw_00005418.overview: HATLASJ233037.2 331217 _ 1,2 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens galaxy at a photometric redshift of 0.66 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a spectroscopic redshift of 2.17, displays a complex structure with multiple knots embedded in a main arc [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ233037.2 331217 _ 1,2 (HerBS123; S 25) : This type 3 foreground lensing system is formed by a main galaxy (labelled as `1' in Fig. fig:models_full ) at z_ l ^ phot =0.66 Shirley2021 and possibly a secondary unresolved galaxy (labelled as `2'), for which the redshift is not yet available. We deblend and model the two galaxies with GALFIT by using two Sérsic components and a single unresolved component, respectively. The background lensed source is located at z_ s ^ spec =2.17 , as measured by the BEARS survey Urquhart2022 . It shows a very complex structure with multiple knots embedded into a main arc and a second inner elongated image on the SE side.

gmw_00005419.name: HELMSJ001626.0 + 042613
gmw_00005419.overview: HELMSJ001626.0 + 042613 is a strongly lensed background source at a redshift of 2.51 [arXiv:1705.05413, arXiv:2311.01158]. The system forms a southern arc with two compact images [arXiv:2311.01158], exhibiting a high star formation rate of 980 solar masses per year [arXiv:1705.05413].

sources.summary: The paper finds HELMS J001626.0+042613 is an isolated double-image lens with a compact source, low magnification, bounded SED peak, high redshift, and typical SMG/ULIRG SFR-to-dust ratio.

quotes.quote: HELMS J001626.0+042613 This double image system is well described by an isolated power-law density profile and a relatively compact source. Both reconstruction methods suggest faint extended source structure but this only contributes a few per cent of the main source flux. The system has the lowest magnification factor in our sample of only 4.1 0.3 .

The peak of the source SED in this system is well bounded by the ALMA and SPIRE photometry giving robust temperature estimates. In the dual temperature SED, the warm component makes a larger contribution to the total dust mass than the other five sources but this is not well constrained owing to uncertainties in the shorter wavelength PACS photometry. The de-magnified source luminosity is (L_ FIR /L_ )=12.7 0.1 which agrees with the value quoted by N16. The z=2.51 source has a high SFR of 980 M_ /yr and its SFR to dust mass ratio is consistent with a typical SMG/ULIRG as indicated in Figure SFR_Md .

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HELMSJ001626.0 + 042613 (HELMS22; S 26) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ s ^ spec =0.22 Amvrosiadis2018 with two Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =2.51 Nayyeri2016 forms an arc on the S side of the lens. This system was observed with ALMA (Band 6, Amvrosiadis2018 ; Band 7, Prop. ID 2016.1.01188.S, S. Eales) in the sub-mm/mm wavelength range. The sub-mm morphology shows two compact images on top of the HST arc and a secondary image closer to the lens on its NE side.

gmw_00005420.name: HATLASJ002624.8 341737
gmw_00005420.overview: HATLASJ002624.8 341737 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens at a photometric redshift of 0.93 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a photometric redshift of 2.70, forms a faint secondary image and a southwestern arc split into two components [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ002624.8 341737 (HerBS22; S 27) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ phot =0.93 Ward2022 with two Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ phot =2.70 Zhang2018 forms an arc on the SW of the lens and a fainter secondary image on the opposite side. The SW arc is split into two components: a main one and a fainter one, which are W and S of the lens, respectively.

gmw_00005421.name: HELMSJ004723.6 + 015751 _ 1,2
gmw_00005421.overview: HELMSJ004723.6 + 015751 _ 1,2 is a type 2 foreground gravitational lens system formed by two galaxies [arXiv:2311.01158]. This system lenses a background source at redshift 1.44, which exhibits a complex morphology with a confirmed northern arc and potential multiple compact images [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HELMSJ004723.6 + 015751 _ 1,2 (HELMS9; S 28) : This type 2 foreground lens is formed by two galaxies at z_ s ^ spec =0.37 Amvrosiadis2018 , which we deblend by simultaneously modelling them with GALFIT using two Sérsic components for the main galaxy (labelled as '1` in Table tab:models ) and one de Vaucouleurs component for the second galaxy (labelled as '2`). The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =1.44 Nayyeri2016 shows a very complex structure with one confirmed arc on the N side of the lens and possibly multiple compact images. The background lensed source morphology is likely to be disturbed by additional features possibly related to the interaction with the two lenses. It was observed with ALMA Band 6, Amvrosiadis2018 in the sub-mm/mm wavelength range.

gmw_00005422.name: HATLASJ085112 + 004934
gmw_00005422.overview: HATLASJ085112 + 004934 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a type 1 foreground lens galaxy at a photometric redshift of 0.66 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a photometric redshift of 1.77, forms three arcs that are likely sheared by nearby galaxies [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ085112 + 004934 (S 30) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ phot =0.66 Shirley2021 with three Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ phot =1.77 Manjon-garcia2019 forms three arcs on the SW, SE, and NE sides of the lens. The SW arc is divided into two parts by a bright clump. This is likely due to the shear effect caused by nearby galaxies.

gmw_00005423.name: HATLASJ085359 + 015537
gmw_00005423.overview: HATLASJ085359 + 015537 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens modeled with two Sérsic components [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a redshift of 2.09, forms a ring that splits into two arcs on the east and west sides of the lens [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ085359 + 015537 (G09v1.40; S 31) : We model this type 1 foreground lens with two Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =2.09 Yang2016 forms a ring that splits into two arcs on the E and W sides of the lens. This system was studied in detail by Bussmann2013, Calanog2014 , and Butler2021 with ALMA (Band 6, Butler2021 and Prop. ID 2017.1.00027.S, S. Eales; Band 7, Amvrosiadis2018 and Butler2021 ), and SMA 340 GHz Bussmann2013 in the sub-mm/mm wavelength range and with Keck/NIRC2 AO K _ s band, Calanog2014 in the near-IR, respectively. These works measured an Einstein radius of _ E =0.56^ +0.01 _ 0.02 arcsec from the Keck observations and _ E =0.553 0.004 arcsec from SMA observations.

gmw_00005424.name: HERMESJ104549 + 574512
gmw_00005424.overview: HERMESJ104549 + 574512 is a type 1 foreground lens galaxy located at a photometric redshift of 0.20 [arXiv:2311.01158]. It gravitationally lenses a background source at a spectroscopic redshift of 2.91, which forms two distinct images on the northwest and southeast sides of the lens [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ104549 + 574512 (HLock06; S 32) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ phot =0.20 Wardlow2013 with two Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =2.91 Calanog2014 forms two images on the NW and SE sides of the lens. Bussmann2013 used the SMA dataset to measure an Einstein radius of _ E =0.10 0.03 arcsec.

gmw_00005425.name: HATLASJ132630 + 334410
gmw_00005425.overview: HATLASJ132630 + 334410 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.79 [arXiv:2311.01158]. The background lensed source, located at a spectroscopic redshift of 2.95, forms two distinct images on the northwest and southeast sides of the lens [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ132630 + 334410 (NAv1.195; S 35) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ l ^ spec =0.79 Bussmann2013 with one Sérsic component using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =2.95 Bussmann2013 forms two images on the NW and SE sides of the lens. This system was observed with ALMA (Band 3, Berman2022 and Band 6, Prop. ID 2017.1.01214.S, Y. Min), and SMA 340 GHz Bussmann2013 . The sub-mm/mm follow-up observations show no significant difference in morphology with respect to the near-IR ones.

gmw_00005426.name: HATLASJ133543 + 300404 _ 1,2,3,4
gmw_00005426.overview: HATLASJ133543 + 300404 _ 1,2,3,4 is a type 2 foreground gravitational lens formed by a galaxy cluster at a redshift of 0.98 [arXiv:2311.01158]. This cluster lenses a background source at a redshift of 2.69, which exhibits a complex morphology of arcs and secondary images [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ133543 + 300404 _ 1,2,3,4 (HerBS35; S 36): This type 2 foreground lens is formed by a cluster of galaxies (labelled as `1', `2', `3', and `4' in Fig. fig:models_full ) at z_ l ^ spec =0.98 Stanford2014, Nayyeri2017 , which we deblend by simultaneously modelling them with GALFIT using one Sérsic and one PSF component for galaxy 1 and one Sérsic component each for the remaining ones. The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =2.69 Nayyeri2017 shows a complex morphology with a compact bright arc and a faint secondary image NE and SW of the galaxy 1, one arc S of the galaxies 1 and 2, and another arc N of the galaxies 3 and 4. This system was observed with SMA 228 GHz, Nayyeri2017 , the JVLA 4 GHz, Nayyeri2017 , and Keck/NIRC2 AO K _ s and H band, Nayyeri2017 . The sub-mm/mm follow-up observations show a complex morphology with various arcs and images that are not detected in the near-IR observations.

gmw_00005427.name: 4C40.36
gmw_00005427.overview: 4C40.36 is a radio galaxy located at an estimated redshift of approximately 2.261 [arXiv:0811.3914]. The galaxy is characterized by a spatially extended, low surface brightness emission-line nebula [arXiv:0811.3914].

sources.summary: The paper treats 4C40.36 as a z~2.27 powerful radio galaxy with an extended emission-line nebula, estimating z=2.261 and registering its Ly emission to published U-band astrometry.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present our results based on PPAK observations of two powerful HzRGs, 4C40.36 ( z= 2.27) and 4C48.48 ( z= 2.34). Both were selected from the 4C ultrasteep spectrum compendium (e.g. Chambers et al. 1996a and reference therein), and are known to have spatialy extended, low surface brightness emission-line nebulae (Chambers et al. 1996b). Longslit spectroscopy for these two sources has been presented by Vernet et al. (2001) and Villar-Mart n et al. (2003).
Based on these wavelengths the estimated redshifts were z 2.261 for 4C40.36, and z 2.336 for 4C48.48, similar to the objects' nominal redshifts.

For 4C40.36 they published a registered U-band image, which samples Ly at the redshift of the object. The peak of the Ly emission is coincident with that of the UV continuum emission in this object, as shown in the available Keck spectra (e.g., Villar-Mart n et al. 2006, Humphrey 2005). We register the peak of the Ly emission image to the coordinates of the peak emission of the Chambers et al. (1996) U-band image.

gmw_00005428.name: 4C48.48
gmw_00005428.overview: 4C48.48 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.34 [arXiv:0811.3914]. The galaxy is surrounded by a spatially extended, low surface brightness emission-line nebula that features a well-defined Lyman-alpha emission peak [arXiv:0811.3914].

sources.summary: The paper treats 4C48.48 as a z~2.34 powerful radio galaxy with an extended emission-line nebula, estimating z=2.336 and registering its Ly narrow-band peak astrometrically.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present our results based on PPAK observations of two powerful HzRGs, 4C40.36 ( z= 2.27) and 4C48.48 ( z= 2.34). Both were selected from the 4C ultrasteep spectrum compendium (e.g. Chambers et al. 1996a and reference therein), and are known to have spatialy extended, low surface brightness emission-line nebulae (Chambers et al. 1996b). Longslit spectroscopy for these two sources has been presented by Vernet et al. (2001) and Villar-Mart n et al. (2003).
Based on these wavelengths the estimated redshifts were z 2.261 for 4C40.36, and z 2.336 for 4C48.48, similar to the objects' nominal redshifts.

For 4C48.48 Chambers et al. (1996) published a registered Ly narrow-band image of 60 \ bandpass. The Ly emission shows a well defined peak in this image. We used the coordinates of this peak to register our Ly image.

gmw_00005429.overview: The galaxy 12024790 exhibits [OII] emission that indicates ongoing or recent star formation [arXiv:1107.4640]. It has a star formation rate of 9.7 solar masses per year and a double-peaked [OII] profile that may trace the rotation of a gaseous disk [arXiv:1107.4640].

sources.summary: 12024790 shows [OII] emission consistent with recent star formation, a derived SFR of 9.7 solar masses per year, and a double-peaked [OII] profile that could trace disk rotation.

quotes.quote: The galaxies 12024790 and 12024321 have [ OII ] 3727 \ emission, implying ongoing or recent star formation. We combine the [ OII ] fluxes from our spectra with the rest-frame k corrected 24 m fluxes from RAINBOW to derive star formation rates (SFR) using the conversion from kennicutt09 based on Kroupa IMF. The total SFR _ [ OII ] +IR for the [ OII ] emission galaxies is 9.7 and 22.6 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

As noted in 2.2, the galaxy 12024790 shows a double peak in the [ OII ] emission. If such feature is due to rotation of a gaseous disk, it yields a projected velocity of V_r sin (i) 165 , which is comparable to its measured =156 . However, it is unlikely that this caused an important bias in the dynamical mass estimates.

gmw_00005430.overview: The galaxy 12024321 exhibits [OII] 3727 emission, which indicates that it has ongoing or recent star formation [arXiv:1107.4640]. It has a combined star formation rate of 22.6 solar masses per year derived from [OII] and infrared fluxes [arXiv:1107.4640].

sources.summary: 12024321 is one of the [OII]-emitting galaxies, with recent star formation and a 22.6 solar-mass-per-year combined SFR; unlike most of the sample, its masses agree within uncertainties.

quotes.quote: The galaxies 12024790 and 12024321 have [ OII ] 3727 \ emission, implying ongoing or recent star formation. We combine the [ OII ] fluxes from our spectra with the rest-frame k corrected 24 m fluxes from RAINBOW to derive star formation rates (SFR) using the conversion from kennicutt09 based on Kroupa IMF. The total SFR _ [ OII ] +IR for the [ OII ] emission galaxies is 9.7 and 22.6 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

Our galaxies have values that are systematically smaller than the originally published stellar masses by an average factor of 6. This is much larger than the combined error of photometric stellar mass and radius (typically a factor of 3). Note that for the galaxy 12024321 these masses are consistent within uncertainties.

gmw_00005431.overview: The galaxy 12019899 has been studied using spectroscopic observations to determine its velocity dispersion [arXiv:1107.4640]. These observations required specialized telluric corrections using an F-type star to account for absorption affecting the calcium H and K lines [arXiv:1107.4640].

sources.summary: For 12019899, the paper reports separate reduction for defocused exposures, telluric correction for affected Ca H+K lines, and a velocity-dispersion estimate combined from two instrumental resolutions.

quotes.quote: However, in one of the observing nights the spectrograph suffered a defocus that decreased the resolution, affecting half of the exposures of 12019899 and 12024453. For these frames we found _ inst =210 , and we reduced this data subset independently.

The telluric absorption band around 7600 \ was present in all of our spectra, affecting the Ca H+K lines in targets 12019899 and 12024321. To correct for telluric absorption in these two galaxies, we used a F-type star observed in the same slit as 12019899.

For the objects 12019899 and 12024453 we calculated as the weighted mean of the values derived from the two different instrumental resolutions, which favors the higher resolution value due to its smaller error. The fits to the spectra are displayed in Fig. fig1 .

gmw_00005432.overview: The target 12024453 is an astronomical object that has been analyzed through both photometric and spectroscopic studies [arXiv:1107.4640]. Its velocity dispersion has been measured using weighted means from different instrumental resolutions, showing consistency with earlier measurements [arXiv:1107.4640].

sources.summary: For 12024453, the paper reports independently reduced defocused exposures, a velocity-dispersion estimate weighted across instrumental resolutions, and agreement with an earlier photometric and spectroscopic measurement.

quotes.quote: However, in one of the observing nights the spectrograph suffered a defocus that decreased the resolution, affecting half of the exposures of 12019899 and 12024453. For these frames we found _ inst =210 , and we reduced this data subset independently.

For the objects 12019899 and 12024453 we calculated as the weighted mean of the values derived from the two different instrumental resolutions, which favors the higher resolution value due to its smaller error. The fits to the spectra are displayed in Fig. fig1 . Following jorgensen95 , we apply an aperture correction to a diameter of 1.19 h ^ 1 kpc, which increases our values around 6 . The final 's are given in Table table1 .
The target 12024453 has already been part of the photometric and spectroscopic study by , who find =173 16 from a spectrum of (S/N) =11 per . This is consistent with our measurement.

gmw_00005433.name: 4C09.72
gmw_00005433.overview: 4C09.72 is a massive type 1 quasar located at a redshift of z=0.433 [arXiv:1807.11481]. The active nucleus exhibits multi-band flux variability [arXiv:1604.04626], with its polarized flux spectrum showing a slope down-turn in the Balmer edge region and no emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0408105].

sources.summary: The paper measures optical continuum reverberation lags for the type 1 quasar PG 2308+098, deriving a 2500-angstrom disk radius slightly larger than the standard disk prediction.

quotes.quote: Two years' worth of u , g , , i , and z band optical light curves were obtained for the massive type 1 quasar PG 2308+098 at z=0.433 using the 1.05-m Kiso Schmidt telescope/Kiso Wide Field Camera, and inter-band time lags of the light curves were measured. Wavelength-dependent continuum reverberation lag signals of several tens of days relative to the u band were detected at g , , i , and z bands, where the longer wavelength bands showed larger lags. From the wavelength-dependent lags, and assuming the standard disk temperature radial profile T R_ disk ^ 3/4 and an X-ray/far-ultraviolet reprocessing picture, a constraint on the radius of the accretion disk responsible for the rest-frame 2500  \ disk continuum emission was derived as R_ disk = 9.46^ +0.29 _ 3.12  light-days. The derived disk size is slightly ( 1.2-1.8 times) larger than the theoretical disk size of R_ disk = 5.46  light-days predicted from the black hole mass ( M_ BH ) and Eddington ratio estimates of PG 2308+098. This result is roughly in accordance with previous studies of lower mass active galactic nuclei (AGNs), where measured disk sizes have been found to be larger than the standard disk model predictions by a factor of 3 ; however, the disk size discrepancy is more modest in PG 2308+098.

PG 2308+098, also known as 4C 09.72, is a bright ( V 16  mag) type 1 quasar at a redshift of z=0.433 . A BH mass estimate for PG 2308+098 is given by ves06 as (M_ BH /M_ )=9.6 0.1 , which is based on a broad H line width taken from bor92 and a monochromatic luminosity at 5100  \ of ( L_   ergs/s ) = 45.8 0.1 taken from sch83 . Using a bolometric correction factor of BC _ 5100 = 9.26 ( ), the bolometric luminosity and Eddington ratio of PG 2308+098 are estimated to be (L_ bol [ ergs/s ] ) = 46.8 0.1 and (L_ bol /L_ Edd ) = -0.9 0.1 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper monitors 4C09.72 as one of four polarization-decreasing quasars, finding five-band flux variability and variable spectra much bluer than the polarized flux spectra.

quotes.quote: To examine whether the disk continuum spectral shapes revealed as the polarized flux and as the variable component spectra are consistent with each other, we carry out multi-band photometric monitoring observations for a sample of four polarization-decreasing quasars of kis04 (4C09.72, 3C323.1, Ton 202, and B2 1208+32) to derive the variable component spectra and compare the spectral shape of them with that of the polarized flux spectra. Contrary to expectation, we confirm that the two spectral components of these quasars have totally different spectral shape in that the variable component spectra are significantly bluer compared to the polarized flux spectra.

We carried out u , g , , i , and z bands photometric monitoring observations for four of the five quasars with polarization dip in <4000 \ in kis04 's sample (4C09.72, B2 1208+32, Ton 202, and 3C323.1; see Table obstarget ) from April 2015 to February 2016, using the 1.05-meter Kiso Schmidt telescope being operated by the Kiso Observatory of the University of Tokyo.

We confirm that all of the four quasars show flux variability in all five bands during our observations, although the variability amplitude in B2 1208+32 is relatively small compared to the others. The observed variability amplitudes of g 0.1-0.4 mag within 200 days in the quasar rest-frame are consistent with the general property of the SDSS quasars (e.g., ).

sources.summary: The paper finds 4C09.72 has ISP-corrected polarized flux with no emission lines and Balmer-edge-region slope changes resembling Ton 202.

quotes.quote: Fig. res-4C09.72-cor100 shows the spectra for 4C09.72 which have been corrected for the contamination from the ISP in our Galaxy. Note that the S/N of the polarization spectra has been effectively degraded due to the reduction of the P magnitude from the subtraction of the ISP. The corrected polarized flux (unnormalized Stokes Q' , or q' ) shows spectral features that look similar to those seen in Ton 202 (Paper I). Firstly, there are essentially no emission lines, which are quantified in Table tab-hbeta and tab-mg . Secondly, in the Balmer edge region, there is a slope down-turn (or local maximum) at about 4000 , and there also seems to be a slope up-turn (or local minimum) at 3600 .

gmw_00005434.name: 3C323.1
gmw_00005434.overview: The active galactic nucleus 3C323.1 is a luminous, non-blazar quasar [arXiv:1701.03798] that exhibits multi-band flux and spectral variability [arXiv:1604.04626, arXiv:1701.03798]. Its host galaxy shows signs of structural disturbance and has a close companion that may represent a late-stage merger remnant [arXiv:0712.0595].

sources.summary: The paper argues 3C323.1 is affected by ISP, removes apparent emission-line polarization after correction, and retains weaker Balmer-edge features.

quotes.quote: The data for this object, shown in Fig. res-3C323.1 , are also likely to have been affected by ISP: the data corrected for the ISP are shown in Fig. res-3C323.1-cor090 . The original measurement in Fig. res-3C323.1 shows a PA rotation shortward of rest 4000 \ and also at emission line wavelengths, but on the q u plane, a linear alignment of the data points is seen (Fig. res-3C323.1-qu ). The direction of the shift of the data points is consistent with the observations of Galactic stars in the same field. Our observation of a nearby star shows P=0.7 at PA 84 \ (see Table tab-log-star ), and other observations of three Galactic stars within 3 \ He00 having estimated distance larger than 200pc also show a similar PA (85-95 ), though with a smaller P (0.2-0.3 ).

Corrected spectra The data which are best-corrected for the ISP are shown in Fig. res-3C323.1-cor090 . The emission lines look unpolarized (see also Table tab-hbeta and tab-mg ) after the ISP correction. At the Balmer edge region, there remains the now-familiar intrinsic features left: the slope down-turn at 4000 , and an up-turn at 3600 , though the feature looks weaker than for the previous objects.

sources.summary: The paper monitors 3C323.1 as one of four polarization-decreasing quasars, finding five-band flux variability and variable spectra much bluer than the polarized flux spectra.

quotes.quote: To examine whether the disk continuum spectral shapes revealed as the polarized flux and as the variable component spectra are consistent with each other, we carry out multi-band photometric monitoring observations for a sample of four polarization-decreasing quasars of kis04 (4C09.72, 3C323.1, Ton 202, and B2 1208+32) to derive the variable component spectra and compare the spectral shape of them with that of the polarized flux spectra. Contrary to expectation, we confirm that the two spectral components of these quasars have totally different spectral shape in that the variable component spectra are significantly bluer compared to the polarized flux spectra.

We carried out u , g , , i , and z bands photometric monitoring observations for four of the five quasars with polarization dip in <4000 \ in kis04 's sample (4C09.72, B2 1208+32, Ton 202, and 3C323.1; see Table obstarget ) from April 2015 to February 2016, using the 1.05-meter Kiso Schmidt telescope being operated by the Kiso Observatory of the University of Tokyo.

We confirm that all of the four quasars show flux variability in all five bands during our observations, although the variability amplitude in B2 1208+32 is relatively small compared to the others. The observed variability amplitudes of g 0.1-0.4 mag within 200 days in the quasar rest-frame are consistent with the general property of the SDSS quasars (e.g., ).

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 323.1 as a QSO with a bright infrared nucleus, a possible late-stage merger companion, disturbed host evidence, and possibly Doppler-boosted radio emission.

quotes.quote: 3C 323.1; z=0.264 This QSO has a bright nucleus that produces diffraction spikes in the near-infrared image. It has a close companion to the northwest that does not show clear signs of interaction, but is consistent with being the remnant nucleus of a galaxy in the final stages of merging miller+03,canalizostockton97 . The Keck spectroscopy presented by Miller et al. demonstrate that the host is not a normal elliptical galaxy, and from our H band image there is some evidence of disturbance, which may explain the small scale length fit to the data. It is also possible that the radio emission from this source is Doppler boosted, making it an artificially radio-loud object.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 323.1 as a luminous non-blazar quasar with continuum-confined polarization, variable polarized absorption features, and a proposed equatorial scattering/absorbing geometry.

quotes.quote: We examine the optical photometric and polarimetric variability of the luminous type 1 non-blazar quasar 3C 323.1 (PG 1545+210). Two optical spectro-polarimetric measurements taken during the periods 1996 98 and 2003 combined with a V band imaging polarimetric measurement taken in 2002 reveal that (1) as noted in the literature, the polarization of 3C 323.1 is confined only to the continuum emission, that is, the emission from the broad line region is unpolarized; (2) the polarized flux spectra show evidence of a time-variable broad absorption feature in the wavelength range of the Balmer continuum and other recombination lines; (3) weak variability in the polarization position angle ( PA ) of 4 deg over a time-scale of 4 6 years is observed; and (4) the V-band total flux and the polarized flux show highly correlated variability over a time-scale of one year. Taking the above-mentioned photometric and polarimetric variability properties and the results from previous studies into consideration, we propose a geometrical model for the polarization source in 3C 323.1, in which an equatorial absorbing region and an axi-asymmetric equatorial electron-scattering region are assumed to be located between the accretion disc and the broad line region. The scattering/absorbing regions can perhaps be attributed to the accretion disc wind or flared disc surface, but further polarimetric monitoring observations for 3C 323.1 and other quasars with continuum-confined polarization are needed to probe the true physical origins of these regions.

sources.summary: The paper finds that quasar 3C 323.1 is brighter than paired radio galaxy 3C 460 at shorter far-infrared wavelengths, but fainter at 160 microns.

quotes.quote: The pair consisting of quasar 3C 323.1 and radio galaxy 3C 460 displays a difference at the two short wavelengths. The radio galaxy is not detected at 60 m and has a flux density well below that of the quasar at 90 m. However, an interesting change occurs at long wavelengths; the 160 m flux density of 3C 460 exceeds the 3C 323.1 value.

gmw_00005435.name: Q1912-550
gmw_00005435.overview: Q1912-550 is a quasar that exhibits a decrease in polarization shortward of the rest-frame 4000 Å wavelength [arXiv:astro-ph/0408105]. Its broad-line components appear to be slightly polarized, and the object's observed data are likely affected by interstellar polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0408105].

sources.summary: The paper says Q1912-550 has a polarization drop, probable ISP contamination, slightly polarized broad-line components, and uncertain polarized-flux shape.

quotes.quote: Q1912-550 ISP correction ^1 As shown in Fig. res-Q1912-550 , the polarization clearly decreases shortward of rest 4000 , so the small blue bump is at least partially unpolarized. However, the H and [ OIII ] lines are polarized as seen in the q' spectrum. The data are probably affected by the ISP, and we show the ISP-corrected data in Fig. res-Q1912-550-cor010 . The data points in the q u plane shown in Fig. res-Q1912-550-qu align roughly linearly, suggesting the PA of the intrinsic polarization is roughly 0-10 .

Corrected spectra Fig. res-Q1912-550-cor010 shows the ISP-corrected spectra. In this object, the broad line components seem to be slightly polarized. One would then expect the small blue bump also to be somewhat polarized. Therefore, the decline of the intrinsic polarized flux continuum shortward of 4000 \ could be sharper than seen in the corrected polarized flux.

gmw_00005436.name: Q2349-014
gmw_00005436.overview: Q2349-014 is a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:0808.0918] with a highly disturbed host galaxy that shows clear morphological signs of gravitational interaction [arXiv:0808.0918]. These features include large tidal arms, diffuse off-center nebulosity, and emission-line regions containing both old and young stellar populations [arXiv:0808.0918].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2349-014 as a radio-loud quasar in a disturbed host showing tidal arms, diffuse off-center nebulosity, emission-line regions, mixed old and young populations, and star formation.

quotes.quote: PKS 2349-014 PKS 2349-014 (labeled 10 in our plots) is a radio-loud quasar at M _ V = 24.5 and L _ 5 GHz =2.9x10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . The QSO has a typical spectrum with strong broad permitted lines and and much narrower forbidden lines with a modest amount of Fe II emission. The host galaxy (M _ V = 23.2) appears very disturbed, although the radial profile is well described by an ^ 1/4 model bahcall97,hamilton02 . This galaxy shows obvious morphological signs of gravitational interaction, such as large tidal arms and extensive diffuse, off-center nebulosity. identify several distinct regions for which we have obtained spectra: the diffuse nebulosity 3 south of the nucleus and the ``wisps'' (possible tidal feature) 4 north of the nucleus. The velocity dispersions of these two components are the same within error bars, at < 250 and 302 km s ^ 1 , respectively, and are near the average of our QSO host galaxy sample.

sources.summary: The paper finds Q2349-014 formally has r_P below unity, but its polarization behaves differently and may include a small ISP effect.

quotes.quote: Q2349-014 Although the formal polarization ratio r_P is less than unity at 3 (Table tab-pol ), the polarization seems to behave differently from the other objects (Fig. res-Q2349-014 ) in that it looks constant over 4000 , but rather abruptly decreases shortward of 3500 . This interesting behavior should be confirmed and followed up with longer integrations. There could be a small effect from an ISP, since the polarization measurements of several Galactic stars show a rather clear PA alignment at 120-130 \ with P=0.2-0.4 .

gmw_00005437.name: QSO 2359--1241
gmw_00005437.overview: QSO 2359--1241 is an extensively studied broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:0807.0228]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus with an outflow that is modeled as a non-homogeneous, full-coverage absorber [arXiv:0807.0228].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes QSO 2359--1241 as an extensively studied BAL quasar whose outflow is best modeled as a non-homogeneous full-coverage absorber.

quotes.quote: We present high resolution spectroscopic VLT observations of the outflow seen in QSO 2359--1241. These data contain absorption troughs from five resonance \ lines with a resolution of 7 \ and signal-to-noise ratio per resolution element of order 100. We use this unprecedented high quality data set to investigate the physical distribution of the material in front of the source, and by that determine the column densities of the absorbed troughs.

The object we targeted is the extensively studied broad absorption line (BAL) quasar: QSO 2359--1241 (Arav et al. \ 2001a; Brotherton et al. \ 2001). Having detailed knowledge of the existing Keck/HIRES data, allowed us to tailor the new observations towards a definitive test for models of absorption material distribution. In future papers we will use these measured ionic column densities to determine the ionization equilibrium of the outflow, it's number density, distance and ultimately the mass flux and kinetic luminosity associated with the outflow.
Therefore, based on the better fit and plausibility of the physical model, we conclude that in QSO 2359--1241, the outflow covers the full extent of the emission source but in a non-homogeneous way.

gmw_00005438.name: S04590
gmw_00005438.overview: S04590 is a lensed galaxy at a redshift of z=8.50 [arXiv:2207.13693]. It resembles extremely low-metallicity systems in the local Universe and is unlikely to host an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2207.13693].

sources.summary: The paper finds S04590 is a z=8.50 lensed galaxy with extreme RO3, unlikely AGN evidence, and plausible HMXB or cosmic-ray ISM heating.

quotes.quote: In contrast, S04590 at z=8.50 appears to be significantly different from the other two galaxies, most resembling extremely low-metallicity systems in the local Universe. Perhaps the most striking spectral feature in S04590 is the curiously high [ O III ] 4363 / [ O III ] 5007 ratio (RO3) of 0.048 (or 0.055 when dust-corrected), implying either extremely high electron temperatures, 3 10^4 K, or gas densities >10^4 \ cm^ 3 . Observed line ratios indicate that this galaxy is unlikely to host an AGN.

We show that neither AGN nor shock models can simultaneously reproduce the flux ratios observed in S04590. However, if we assume that high-mass X-ray binaries or cosmic rays contribute significantly to the heating of the ISM, we can reasonably reproduce many of the properties of S04590.

gmw_00005439.name: S06355
gmw_00005439.overview: S06355 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a high redshift of z=7.67 [arXiv:2207.13693]. Its emission line ratios are qualitatively consistent with those of local Green Pea and Blueberry galaxies [arXiv:2207.13693].

sources.summary: The paper treats S06355 as a z=7.67 lensed galaxy whose line ratios resemble Green Peas, Blueberries, and the edge of SDSS galaxies.

quotes.quote: This study focuses on three objects confirmed to lie at z>7.5 : S04590 at z=8.50 (RA: 110.89533 , Dec: 73.44916 ), S06355 at z=7.67 (RA: 110.84452 , Dec: 73.43508 ), and S10612 at z=7.66 (RA: 110.89533 , Dec: 73.43454 ). These objects are all gravitationally lensed by the foreground cluster; however, crucially for this study, the achromatic nature of gravitational lensing means that the emission line ratios are magnification-independent, and therefore, the results presented here are not affected by magnification at all.
Within error bars, S06355 at z=7.67 (lime green star) is qualitatively consistent with Green Peas, Blueberries, and the edge of the distribution of SDSS galaxies.

Consistent with our qualitative analysis above, we see that S06355 at z=7.67 is marginally consistent with SDSS galaxies, Green Peas, and consistent with the spread in properties exhibited by Blueberries, while S10612 at z=7.66 is only consistent with Green Peas and Blueberries. In contrast, S04590 at z=8.50 is most similar to the extremely low metallicity galaxies of and sits at the very edge of the distribution of Blueberries. This quantitatively demonstrates that these three high-redshift galaxies, despite lying at similar redshifts, are diverse in nature, and multiple different low-redshift analogue samples are needed to realistically encapsulate their properties (cf. Schaerer2022,Rhoads2022 ). Since all three galaxies have high O32 and RO3 compared to SDSS galaxies, the ISM in these high-redshift systems likely have high ionization parameters, lower metallicities, and/or higher H II region electron temperatures and densities compared to that in the local Universe (see also Trump2022 ).

gmw_00005440.name: S10612
gmw_00005440.overview: S10612 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=7.66 [arXiv:2207.13693]. It exhibits exceptionally strong [Ne III] emission and high oxygen line ratios, which are likely driven by a high ionization parameter, low metallicity, or strong shocks rather than active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2207.13693].

sources.summary: The paper presents S10612 as a z=7.66 lensed galaxy with Blueberry-like ratios, strong [Ne III]/O32, and shocks explaining its non-AGN diagnostics.

quotes.quote: This study focuses on three objects confirmed to lie at z>7.5 : S04590 at z=8.50 (RA: 110.89533 , Dec: 73.44916 ), S06355 at z=7.67 (RA: 110.84452 , Dec: 73.43508 ), and S10612 at z=7.66 (RA: 110.89533 , Dec: 73.43454 ). These objects are all gravitationally lensed by the foreground cluster; however, crucially for this study, the achromatic nature of gravitational lensing means that the emission line ratios are magnification-independent, and therefore, the results presented here are not affected by magnification at all.

Based on oxygen emission lines and H , S10612 at z=7.66 (cyan star) is consistent with Blueberries, but seems to have higher O32 and RO3 compared to the Green Peas, and significantly higher [ ] 5007/H compared to the extremely low-metallicity galaxies from . This galaxy also shows exceptionally strong [ ] 3869, falling above the AGN demarcation line from when comparing [ ] 3869/H vs [ ] 3726, 3729/H . We show below that such emission line ratios can also be driven by strong shocks, so this galaxy is not conclusively an AGN, nor has a signature of higher excitation energy emission lines such as 4686.

However, with this initial analysis, using the dust-corrected values, two key features stand out: the very high RO3 in S04590 and the very high [ ] 3869/H and O32 in S10612. The latter can likely be explained by a high ionization parameter and low metallicity; however, the former requires more investigation, which we discuss further below.

gmw_00005441.name: NGC 3842
gmw_00005441.overview: NGC 3842 is a brightest cluster cD galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431, arXiv:1410.7515] hosting a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:0808.1521] and a central ultramassive black hole [arXiv:1211.3199, arXiv:1410.7515]. It features a complex radio morphology with long tails and a central H-shaped structure, suggesting possible restarted active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2202.08593].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3842 as the field's brightest cD galaxy, with a two-component X-ray spectrum whose soft component is interpreted as a thermal halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 3842 In optical this is the brightest galaxy in the field and also the cD galaxy of a galaxy concentration around it. In this observation, it is 13.9 ' off-axis in the S1 chip. The position of the X-ray source agrees with the optical nucleus. About 900 net counts were collected in this observation.

If a power law is adopted, the temperature of the soft component is 0.73 0.07 keV while the photon index of the hard component is 2.1 ^ +0.7 _ 0.4 . In each case, the total luminosity in the 0.5 - 10 keV band is 1.9 10 ^ 41 ergs s ^ 1 , while the luminosity of the soft component is about same - 9 10 ^ 40 ergs s ^ 1 (0.5 - 10 keV). The luminosity ratio of the hard X-ray component to the optical L _ hard (0.3 - 10 keV) / L _ B is 6.8 10 ^ 29 ergs s ^ 1 L ^ 1 _ B , which is quite similar to the values presented in S01 --- 8.1, 7.2 10 ^ 29 ergs s ^ 1 L ^ 1 _ B for NGC 4697 (E) and NGC 1553 (S0) respectively. This suggests the hard component may be dominated by X-ray binaries. The soft component is naturally explained as the thermal halo of NGC 3842.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3842 as a complex, possible restarted radio source with central edge-brightened/H-shaped structure, long tails, and a steep integrated spectrum.

quotes.quote: Several gETGs show a complex morphology, in at least two cases (namely, NGC 3842 and UGC 12482) presenting a central edge brightened structure and large diffuse tails, suggestive of restarted activity. From the point of view of the radio morphology, among the 46 extended sources, there are eight galaxies (NGC 2789, NGC 3665, NGC 3842, UGC 10097, IC 5180, NGC7436B, UGC 12482, and MCG+05-10-007; see brienza21 for a detailed study of this source) in which a bright, small-scale structure is accompanied by large-scale diffuse emission. These gETGs can be considered as possible restarted sources with the structures observed on different scales (and of different surface brightnesses) being produced by subsequent phases of activity.

NGC 3842 (complex). Two radio tails extend over 160 kpc. However, the central regions are dominated by a small-scale, H-shaped structure, 15 kpc wide, reminiscent of the morphology seen in 3C 171 neff95 . The steep spectral index ( _ 150 ^ 1400 = 1.85) might indicate that this is a restarted source and the tails are remnants of a previous phase of activity.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 3842 as part of a pair with reported ultramassive black holes that may be outliers and excludes it from final black-hole scaling fits.

quotes.quote: It is apparent that there is something unusual with the two ultramassive black holes reported for NGC 3842 and NGC 4889 by McConnell et al. \ (2011a). Figure Fig1 reveals that they may be are outliers biasing the M_ bh - relation defined by the remaining data. Performing a symmetrical linear regression on the core-S ersic galaxies, including NGC 3842 and NGC 4889, yields a slope of roughly 7.0 1 (see Table Tab3 ). Uncertain as to whether there is an error in the black hole masses for NGC 3842 and NGC 4889, or perhaps with their velocity dispersion measurements, or if instead these values are fine and these galaxies are representative (of a possibly new class) of galaxies which deviate from the M_ bh - relation as we know it, in the following section we have elected to repeat the M_ bh - regression analysis without these two galaxies. If these galaxies are outliers, then this additional approach provides a more robust statistical analysis of the M_ bh - relation.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC3842 in a Coma-supercluster cluster, cites its published surface-brightness and HST imaging, and reports a central kinematically decoupled core.

quotes.quote: NGC3842: This galaxy belongs to a cluster which forms part of the Coma supercluster. The surface brightness profile was published by Schombert (1986), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). The radial velocity profile of this galaxy reveals the presence of a kinematically decoupled core (KDC) in the centre.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 3842 light-profile derivation in the appendix, finds its mass deficit just below the black hole mass, and assembles its V-band profile from three pieces.

quotes.quote: The derivation of light profiles of NGC 3379, NGC 3842, NGC 6086 and NGC 7768 is described in the Appendix.
In three galaxies (IC 1459, NGC 3842 and NGC 5813), the mass deficit is just below the black hole mass.
The surface brightness profile for NGC 3842 is calibrated to the V band and is assembled from three pieces.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3842 as a bright cluster galaxy with a strong break-radius isophotal twist, an ellipticity minimum, and an ultramassive black hole.

quotes.quote: Also evident in Fig. FigV5 , is that despite the twists, the galaxies' orientations at their break radii are fairly aligned with the spheroid's outer orientation except for five galaxies (NGC 0741, NGC NGC 3842, NGC 4589, NGC 5061 and NGC 6849) with inner position angles which are twisted by 27^ from the outer position angle. NGC 0741, NGC 3842 and NGC 6849 show strong isophotal twists at their break radii coincident with local minima in their ellipticities, but for NGC 5061 the strong 30^ twist and the ellipticity minimum which occurs between 3 and 10 may be a result of tidal effects (Tal et al. \ 2009). While the correlation between high twist amplitudes and low isophote ellipticities has already been known for some time (e.g., Galleta 1980; Nieto et al. \ 1992; Rahman Shandarin 2004), these four galaxies have interesting features discussed in the literature. NGC 0741 is a nucleated galaxy (e.g., Dullo Graham 2012), NGC 3842 is a bright cluster galaxy that has an ultramassive black hole ( M_ BH = 9.7 10^ 9 M_ ) with a 1 .2 radius of influence (McConnell et al. \ 2011) whereas the S0 galaxy NGC 6849 has an inner bar which manifests as a peak in the ellipiticity profile (see Dullo Graham 2013).

gmw_00005442.overview: The radio galaxy 0326+39 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley Class I source [arXiv:astro-ph/0405020, arXiv:1107.2511]. It features twin radio jets that decelerate rapidly [arXiv:astro-ph/0405020] and are surrounded by well-defined emission lobes [arXiv:1107.2511], with the jets twisting and deflecting near the outer edges of these lobes [arXiv:1107.2511].

sources.summary: The paper includes 0326+39 among four modelled FR I radio sources, finding fitted radio/polarization structure and roughly constant post-deceleration jet speeds.

quotes.quote: Comparison of models and data The four sources we have modelled are 3C 31 LB02a , 0326+39, 1553+24 CL04 and NGC 315 (Cotton et al., in preparation). In Figs fig:compare1 - fig:compareh we compare model and observed images at the same resolution. Figs fig:compare1 and fig:compare2 show the full modelled areas at moderate resolution and display both total intensity and linear polarization. They demonstrate that we can successfully fit the observations in all cases, limited primarily by small-scale (and often non-axisymmetric) fluctuations.

At the closest distance from the nucleus where we can model the jets, all of their on-axis velocities are close to 0.8 . They all decelerate rapidly over a short distance in the flaring region, but at different distances from the nucleus. NGC 315, 0326+39 and 1553+24 then have roughly constant velocities in the range 0.1 -- 0.4 until the end of the modelled regions.

sources.summary: The paper uses lower-resolution observations of B2 0326+39 to show lobes surrounding the jets, traceable jet deflections near lobe edges, and steepening spectral-index gradients.

quotes.quote: We present deep, high-resolution imaging of the nearby Fanaroff-Riley Class I (FR I) radio galaxies NGC 193, B2 0206+35, B2 0755+37 and M 84 at frequencies of 4.9 and 1.4 GHz using new and archival multi-configuration observations from the Very Large Array. In addition, we describe lower-resolution observations of B2 0326+39 and a reanalysis of our published images of 3C 296. All of these radio galaxies show twin jets and well-defined lobes or bridges of emission, and we examine the common properties of this class of source. We show detailed images of total intensity, brightness gradient, spectral index, degree of polarization and projected magnetic-field direction.

0326+39 Fig. fig:0326_i_si18 shows the distributions of the 1.4-GHz total intensity and 1.4 to 4.9-GHz spectral index over 0326+39 at 18-arcsec FWHM resolution. As in the other sources studied here, the lobes of 0326+39 appear to surround the jets in projection. Even at this relatively low resolution, the jets are clearly traceable to the outer parts of the lobes in both total intensity, where they appear to twist and deflect close to the outer edges of the lobes, and spectral index, where they can be traced as regions of significantly lower index.

sources.summary: The paper uses 0326+39 in three-band colour-colour tests, finds no systematic jet spectral gradients, and cites earlier lower-resolution spectral-index profiles.

quotes.quote: For four sources (3C 31, 0326+39, 1553+24 and 3C 449), we have data in all of the 1.3 -- 1.7, 4.5 -- 5.0 and 8.3 -- 8.5 GHz bands and could potentially have addressed this issue.

Of the five sources where lobe emission is undetectable over the region of interest, NGC 315 and (at a much lower level) 3C 31 show the spectral flattening away from the axis mentioned earlier, 0326+39 and 3C 449 have no systematic gradients and 1553+24 is not well enough resolved.
0326+39 Bridle91 show spectral-index profiles derived from lower-resolution images at 1.5 and 4.9 GHz.

gmw_00005443.overview: 1553+24 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0405020]. It features a main jet with an estimated viewing angle that is very small [arXiv:astro-ph/0405020], alongside a weak counter-jet with a low signal-to-noise ratio [arXiv:1303.6664].

sources.summary: The paper discusses 1553+24 as an FR I radio source whose small inferred viewing angle implies very large, hard-to-detect side-on counterparts.

quotes.quote: Conversely, we find that 1553+24 has a very small value of 8^ . This means that its side-on counterparts must be very large. The length of the main jet is at least 60 arcsec corresponding to 388 arcsec (340 kpc) at = 60^ , the median angle to the line of sight. This is comparable in size with the longest jet in the B2 sample, in NGC 315 Willis81 , and far larger than the median ( 30 kpc; Parma87 ). There is cause for suspicion unless: (a) the side-on counterparts of B2 1553+24 are not members of the B2 sample or (b) sources in that sample have linear sizes far larger than previously realised.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 1553+24 has a weak counter-jet, low counter-jet signal-to-noise, a core-confused high-emissivity region, and limited measurable spectral-index range.

quotes.quote: 1553+24 (Fig. fig:binnedprofiles j) has a weak counter-jet with measurable spectral index only over a limited range of distance.

1553+24 Young showed images of this source (we include some of their visibility data) but did not discuss its spectrum quantitatively. The high-emissivity region is too close to the core for accurate spectral-index measurements to be made; this is the one source where it does not overlap with the deceleration region. The signal-to-noise ratio in the counter-jet is low.

gmw_00005444.name: Andromeda XVIII
gmw_00005444.overview: Andromeda XVIII is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located outside the virial radius of M31 [arXiv:1609.09706, arXiv:2101.08613]. This kinematically cold, dark-matter-dominated system has no bulk rotation [arXiv:2404.11804, arXiv:2101.03189] and has experienced no recent star formation for at least 1.5 billion years [arXiv:1609.09706].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST photometry of Andromeda XVIII, deriving its distance, location beyond M31's virial radius, old and intermediate-age star formation history, stellar mass, and lack of recent star formation.

quotes.quote: We present a photometric study of the Andromeda XVIII dwarf spheroidal galaxy associated with M31, and situated well outside of the virial radius of the M31 halo. The galaxy was resolved into stars with Hubble Space Telescope/Advanced Camera for Surveys revealing the old red giant branch and red clump. With the new observational data we determined the Andromeda XVIII distance to be D = 1.33_ 0.09 ^ +0.06 Mpc using the tip of red giant branch method. Thus, the dwarf is situated at the distance of 579 kpc from M31.

We model the star formation history of Andromeda XVIII from the stellar photometry and Padova theoretical stellar isochrones. An ancient burst of star formation occurred 12--14 Gyr ago. There is no sign of recent/ongoing star formation in the last 1.5 Gyr. The mass fractions of the ancient and intermediate age stars are 34 and 66 per cent, respectively, and the total stellar mass is 4.2 10 ^6 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper treats And XVIII as a dwarf spheroidal outside M31's virial radius but within the Local Group zero-velocity sphere in a homogeneous Local Group SFH comparison, finding old dominant populations, recent quenching, and low metallicity across the studied dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The first column of the table shows the name of the galaxy; (2) -- equatorial coordinates (J2000); (3) -- morphological type; (4) -- absolute magnitude in Johnson B filter; (5) - Holmberg diameter (at the level of _B = 26.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 isophote), in angular minutes; (6) -- the distance in Mpc. The distances to isolated galaxies were measured by us using tip of the red giant branch method (TRGB) in the works mentioned above. The three subsample of the list are indicated in the Table tab:param : highly isolated dwarfs KKR 25, KK 258 and KKs 03; the dSphs within the zero velocity radius, but outside the virial radius Tucana dwarf and And XVIII; the dSphs within the virial radius of M 31 And XXVIII, Cas dSph and And XXIX. Observations of all studied dwarf galaxies (with the exception of KKR 25) were made with HST/ACS.

We have estimated the star formation rate (SFR) and metallicity depending on the age of the stellar populations, as well as the stellar mass formed in each related period of star formation. A number of common trends follows from these results: the star formation has been quenched over the last 1--2 Gyrs which is quite consistent with the morphological type (dSph and dTr) of the studied dwarfs; there is an initial active burst of star formation from about 10--12 to 13.8 Gyrs ago. The fraction of stars formed during this period is different for our objects (we will discuss it below). In addition, there are stars formed about 4--8 Gyrs ago.

sources.summary: The paper presents Andromeda XVIII as an isolated dwarf spheroidal/backsplash candidate with no bulk rotation, quenching signatures, sharp metallicity cutoff, and possible gas loss from M31 passage or merger.

quotes.quote: Andromeda XVIII is an isolated dwarf galaxy 579 kpc away from the nearest large galaxy, M31. It is a candidate ``backsplash galaxy'' that might have been affected by a close passage to M31.

We estimated Andromeda XVIII's mean heliocentric velocity, rotation velocity, position angle of the rotation axis, and velocity dispersion using maximum likelihood coupled with a Monte Carlo Markov chain (MCMC). There is no evidence for bulk rotation, though sub-populations might be rotating.

The metallicity distribution is inconsistent with these models due to a sharp metal-rich cut-off. Together, the metallicity distribution and the mean velocity are consistent with a sudden interruption of star formation. One possible cause for this quenching may be rapid gas loss due to ram pressure stripping during a close passage by M31 in the past. We also consider a past major merger as another possible cause.

sources.summary: And XVIII is the most distant anchor galaxy; its HB/TRGB discrepancy is examined, but residuals and CMD shifts do not reveal a clear measurement problem.

quotes.quote: The sole exception is And XVIII, which is consistent at the 2- level. And XVIII is the most distant galaxy in the sample. Thus, for a fixed integration time the CMD is shallower, with the 50 completeness limits extending only 1 mag below the HB. We examined fit residuals to the HB and TRGB of And XVIII, but did not find any obvious issues (e.g., the HB fit quality did not appear to be lower by a completeness fraction at the HB).

For the given TRGB magnitude of And XVIII, the HB would have to be 0.16 mag fainter than is observed. Conversely, the TRGB would have to be 0.16 mag brighter for the given HB magnitude. Based on the CMD of And XVIII in Figure fig:m31_cmds , a 0.16 mag shift of either feature is not plausible.

sources.summary: The paper describes And XVIII as an M31-vicinity dwarf whose earlier chip-gap affected measurements are improved by new observations, showing a kinematically cold, dark-matter-dominated system.

quotes.quote: Andromeda XVIII And XVIII is in the vicinity of M31, and was discovered by as part of precursor observations for the Pan-Andromeda Archaeological Survey (PAndAS; McConnachie2009 ). Unfortunately, in these discovery observations, the majority of the galaxy fell in one of the large chip gaps on the detector, obscuring the central region. The dwarf's parameters were estimated under the assumption of circular symmetry to address the obscured portion. analysed the dwarf as part of a wider study of M31's dwarfs, however they also used the same data.

They estimated its structural parameters, and included a more robust surface brightness estimate, however its ellipticity was poorly constrained. Here, new observations, placing the dwarf well away from chip gaps, have been obtained which allow for a complete picture of its global structure. obtained kinematics of And XVIII stars as part of the SPLASH survey. They concluded that it is a kinematically cold, dark matter dominated dwarf.

sources.summary: The paper treats And XVIII as a passive field dwarf within two virial radii whose recent star-formation history is consistent with environmental backsplash quenching.

quotes.quote: Quenched Dwarfs in the Field As shown in sec:results , several of the passive dwarf systems in the local field population are potentially the product of environmental quenching mechanisms. Specifically, And XVIII, Phoenix, and Cetus currently reside within 2 of their nearest host, such that they can potentially be explained with this model. weisz14a measured the star formation histories (SFH) inferred from color-magnitude diagrams of resolved stars in Cetus and Phoenix see also hidalgo,monelli10a,monelli10b,monelli12 . Both objects are consistent with a quenching event occurring roughly 2 Gyr ago. Additionally, makarova17 inferred a relatively recent star formation event in And XVIII, approximately 1.5 Gyr ago. The quenched backsplash subhalos in our models that are currently located at similar distances from their most massive neighbor have a mean lookback time to infall of t_ infall = 5.5 1.5 Gyr . Assuming a quenching timescale ( _ quench ) of 2 Gyr , this suggests a quenching time of 2-5 Gyr ago, consistent with the quenching times inferred from the measured SFHs of Cetus, Phoenix, and And XVIII.

gmw_00005445.name: MG3 J225155+2217
gmw_00005445.overview: MG3 J225155+2217 is a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z=3.668 [arXiv:0802.0296, arXiv:0810.5243]. Its spectral energy distribution is dominated by inverse-Compton emission [arXiv:0810.5243], with a synchrotron peak potentially located in the X-ray or gamma-ray band [arXiv:0802.0296].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGR J22517+2218 with high-redshift QSO MG3 J225155+2217, reports its INTEGRAL position, and describes it as a distant flat-spectrum radio blazar with an unusual high-energy SED.

quotes.quote: IGR J22517+2218 was detected by IBIS with a significance of 7 at a position corresponding to R.A.(2000)=22h51m42.72s and Dec(2000)=+22 ^ 17'56.4" and with a positional uncertainty of 4.5' (90 confidence level). Within the INTEGRAL/IBIS error box we find the high redshift QSO MG3 J225155+2217 (z=3.668) but only follow up observations with the Swift satellite using the XRT telescope could confirm this association with certainty. Indeed only one bright and hard X-ray source was detected within the IBIS positional uncertainty and this source is the high z quasar (see left panel in Figure qso ).

Further analysis of the source characteristics indicates that IGR J2251+2218 is a blazar belonging to the class of Flat Spectrum Radio QSO. The source SED is also unusual among such type of blazars as it is compatible with having the synchrotron peak in the X/gamma-ray band ( i.e. much higher than generally observed) or alternatevely with the Compton peak in the MeV range (i.e. lower than typically measured).

sources.summary: The paper reports MG3 J225155+2217 as a high-redshift blazar serendipitously discovered by INTEGRAL near 3C 454.3, with SED modeling dominated by inverse-Compton emission typical of high-redshift FSRQs.

quotes.quote: MG3 J225155+2217 (z=3.668) Yet another gift from the large FOV of INTEGRAL . This source was first serendipitously detected in the FOV of 3C 454.3 observation in May 2005 (distance 6^ ; see Fig. fig:3CMG3 ) and reported as unidentified source in the Krivonos' Catalog . Later, it was associated with the high-redshift ( z=3.668 ) blazar MG3 J225155+2217 and it is the farthest detected by INTEGRAL to date. The modeling of the SED showed that the emission from this source is largely dominated by the inverse-Comtpon component , but still in the framework of a typical high-redshift FSRQ (like, e.g., Swift J 0746.3+2548 at z=2.979 ).

gmw_00005446.name: WHDF-EVLA-6
gmw_00005446.overview: WHDF-EVLA-6 is an obscured quasar located at a redshift of approximately z = 1.32 to 1.33 [arXiv:2010.07934, arXiv:1209.4660]. The source exhibits strong absorbed X-ray emission [arXiv:2010.07934], far-infrared emission [arXiv:2010.07934], and extended radio emission that is resolved into two components, suggesting the presence of a radio jet [arXiv:1209.4660].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LAB-11 as the known z=1.32 QSO WHDFCH007, with strong absorbed X-ray emission, FIR and radio detections, and AGN SED fits.

quotes.quote: LAB-11 This is a known QSO, WHDFCH007, with z=1.32 Bielby12 . In Fig. fig:hst_alma_spies and Table table:ALMA_mag we see that LAB-11 shows strong X-ray emission in the two harder bands, ( S_X(1.2-2keV)=2.02^ +2.0 _ 1.1 10^ 16 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and S_X(2-7keV)=4.81^ +1.6 _ 1.2 10^ 15 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ) that appears to be absorbed with a column estimated as N_H 5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 Bielby12 . Strong FIR emission is seen as well as 8.4 GHz radio emission.

The closest WHDF detection, whdf6423, is offset 0.''5 from the ALMA source. The HST detection is offset 0.''18 from ALMA and 0.''60 from whdf6423. The only discrepancy is that the VLA detection is 1.''23 offset from ALMA. It is classed as a galaxy in the [ 3.6 ] band with class-star-ch1=0.21 and a star in the [ 4.5 ] band with class-star-ch2=0.88 . In the SPIES data, [ 3.6 ] =15.99 (automag Vega) with [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] =1.08 for aperture 1 with a 1.''44 radius. The SED fit for the 1.4 m dip AGN model (see Fig. fig:lab101112 ) gives A_V=1.5 0.1 mag and z=1.6 0.1 , and T=32 5 K.

sources.summary: The paper identifies WHDF-EVLA-6 as a z=1.33 LABOCA-detected obscured quasar with extended two-component radio emission, a possible radio jet, UV excess, and red optical-near-infrared colors.

quotes.quote: WHDF-EVLA-6: This radio source corresponds to one of the LABOCA-detected obscured quasars at z = 1.33 and a faint infrared counterpart can be seen at the centre of the major radio peak. The radio emission is extended and resolved into two components. There is no clear optical or infrared counterpart for the lower radio peak suggesting that the quasar may host a radio jet. Paradoxically, the optical colours show an ultraviolet excess (UVX) while the R-H colours are quite red. At HST resolution the source may just be resolved.

gmw_00005447.name: WHDF-EVLA-8
gmw_00005447.overview: WHDF-EVLA-8 is an obscured, narrow-line quasar at a redshift of z = 2.12 [arXiv:1209.4660, arXiv:2010.07934]. Its host galaxy exhibits an edge-on spiral morphology [arXiv:1209.4660] and displays strong X-ray, far-infrared, and radio emission [arXiv:2010.07934].

sources.summary: WHDF-EVLA-8 is described as a z=2.12 LABOCA-detected obscured quasar whose images suggest an edge-on spiral host, anomalous colors, and increasing near-infrared host domination.

quotes.quote: WHDF-EVLA-8: The second LABOCA-detected obscured quasar at z = 2.12. In the HST I band image the object has no nucleus and looks like an edge-on spiral, but a nucleus is seen both in the blue and the near-infrared imaging. Again, the colours of the quasar are anomalous, having U-B = -1.25, i.e. very blue, whereas R-H = 3.44 i.e. very red. Again, the redder near-infrared colours may be explained by increasing host domination in these bands. On the same assumptions as for WHDF-EVLA-6 the host galaxy could have K 19.8 and galaxies are seen out to z 2 at this limit in the K20 survey.

sources.summary: The paper identifies LAB-05 as a known absorbed z=2.12 narrow-line QSO, with strong X-ray, FIR, radio, WHDF, and Spitzer detections.

quotes.quote: LAB-05 LAB-05 has been previously identified showing strong X-ray emission ( S_X(0.5-1.2keV)=1.11^ +1.5 _ 0.68 10^ 16 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , S_X(1.2-2keV)=7.18^ +3.4 _ 2.4 10^ 16 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and S_X(2-7keV)=2.09^ +1.1 _ 0.74 10^ 15 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ) that appears to be absorbed with a column estimated as N_H 1 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ( following ). This is confirmed with the X-ray source being only offset from the ALMA position by 0.''38 . Strong FIR emission is seen as well as 8.4 GHz radio emission, although the radio source EVLA-8 is offset by 1.''40 from the ALMA position. The closest WHDF counterpart is whdf6483, offset from ALMA by 0.''56 (see Fig. fig:hst_alma_spies and Table table:ALMA_mag ) and is classified as a galaxy in the WHDF band. This object is UVX and has spectroscopic confirmation as a z=2.12 narrow-lined QSO, WHDFCH008 from .

gmw_00005448.name: FCC062
gmw_00005448.overview: FCC062, also known as NGC 1341, is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2006.11828] that is considered an intermediate infaller in its group [arXiv:2311.15624]. The galaxy features a redder outer disc than its inner disc [arXiv:2311.15624, arXiv:2006.11828] and is likely in an early or ongoing stage of pre-processing [arXiv:2311.15624].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1341 has high EW(H) across radii, distributed very young stars, a secular-aging integrated value, and likely ongoing pre-processing.

quotes.quote: NGC 1341 This galaxy, an intermediate infaller in the group Raj2020 , has relatively high EW(H ) over all radii, indicating that the very young stars are spatially distributed throughout the galaxy. It has an integrated value that is consistent with the secular ageing sequence on the AD. The galaxy is outside the field of view of the MeerKAT commissioning observations, but has previously been detected by Courtois2015 . The outer disc of this galaxy is somewhat redder than its inner disc with (g - i)_ h_ out h_ in = 0.08 mag Raj2020 , and the gradient from the mass-weighted SSP-equivalent ages is relatively flat.
