Based on the AD alone, we can suggest that the galaxy is in the early or ongoing stage of pre-processing.
Further results based on the EW(H ) profiles suggest that it is in an ongoing stage.

sources.summary: FCC062/NGC 1341 is described as a barred spiral at the largest projected distance from NGC 1316, with a 5.28 kpc Type III break and redder outer disc.

quotes.quote: FCC062 Also known as NGC 1341, is a barred-spiral galaxy (SbcII) with morphological type T = 4 , located at furthest distance from NGC 1316, that is, D_ core =0.92 deg. We detect a Type III disc-break in its SB profile with disc break = 5.28 kpc. The outer disc of this galaxy is redder than its inner disc with spiral arms, Type III disc break with (g-i)_ h_ out h_ in = 0.08 mag.

gmw_00005449.name: NGC 5731
gmw_00005449.overview: NGC 5731 is a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2109.12136]. Analysis of its core spectrum indicates a host-galaxy metallicity range of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.65 to 9.04 dex and an estimated star formation rate of 0.29 solar masses per year [arXiv:2109.12136].

sources.summary: The paper derives NGC 5731’s host-galaxy metallicity from an SDSS core spectrum and estimates a star formation rate of 0.29 solar masses per year, describing it as star-forming.

quotes.quote: We determine an oxygen abundance 12 + log(O/H) in host galaxy NGC 5731 by using an SDSS spectroscopic observation taken on 14 April 2004. This spectrum was taken near the galactic core and therefore the metallicity at the explosion site could be slightly different. Using a combination of line flux ratios ( [ O iii ] / H and [ N ii ] /H ) into Equations 1 3 of , we determine a range of host metallicities of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.65 - 9.04 dex ( 0.99 - 1.04 Z _ ). Our derived metallicity range is higher than average SNe II host metallicities of 8.41 - 8.49 dex anderson16 .

We utilize the same pre-explosion SDSS spectrum nearby the host galaxy center to determine a star formation rate. We calculate a total H emission line luminosity of L_ H = 3.7 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . We then use Equation 2 from to estimate a star formation rate of SFR = 0.29 \ yr ^ 1 of the host galaxy. This star formation estimate is reflective of the star-forming characterization of host galaxy NGC 5731.

gmw_00005450.name: RX J1131-1231
gmw_00005450.overview: RX J1131-1231 is a quadruply imaged quasar located at a redshift of 0.657 [arXiv:1007.3142]. The system is strongly lensed by a foreground galaxy at a redshift of 0.295, which produces a bright Einstein ring [arXiv:1007.3142].

sources.summary: The paper describes RX J1131-1231 as a quadruply imaged quasar whose deconvolved astrometry agrees with earlier MCS work, with small offsets attributed to its bright Einstein ring.

quotes.quote: RX J1131-1231 (b) This quadruply imaged quasar (RA = 11^ h 31^ m 55^ s .39 and DEC = 12^ 31'54 99 ) was discovered in Sluse2003 by Sluse2003 They found a redshift of z_ l =0.295 0.002 for the lens while the source lies at z_ s =0.657 0.001 .

RX J1131-1231 (b) Sluse2006 reports astrometric measurements on the same frames with the MCS deconvolution algorithm but with no iterative strategy. Their results agree within the error bars with those presented here. An offset of up to 3 mas between both results is observed. This difference is probably due to the large brightness of the Einstein ring.

gmw_00005451.name: SDSS J1226-0006
gmw_00005451.overview: SDSS J1226-0006 is a doubly imaged quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.125 [arXiv:1007.3142]. The system is strongly lensed by an early-type galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.516, with a nearby galaxy providing the dominant source of shear [arXiv:1007.3142].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1226-0006 as a doubly imaged SDSS quasar with an early-type lens and finds that a nearby galaxy supplies the dominant model shear.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1226-0006 (e) This system (RA = 12^ h 26^ m 08^ s .02 and DEC = 00^ 06'02 19 ) is a doubly imaged quasar discovered in the framework of the SDSS by Inada2008 . The quasar is located at a redshift of z_ s =1.125 . According to Cosmograil3 , the lens is likely to be an early-type galaxy, with a spectroscopic redshift of z_ l =0.516 0.001 . This system has no measured time delay, no published relative astrometry and no detailed modeling study.
A large amount of shear is necessary to reproduce SDSS J1155+6346 and SDSS J1226-006. In the latter case, we identify a nearby galaxy as the dominant source of shear.

sources.summary: J1226-0006 required constrained deconvolution to reduce contamination; the spectra show strong blue-to-red flux-ratio changes and lens-flux deblending issues.

quotes.quote: (i) J1226-0006: Deconvolution: During the deconvolution process, we forced the separation between the 2 lensed images and the lensing galaxy to match the HST separation in order to reduce cross-contamination. Although a good deconvolution is obtained, we keep in mind that residual contamination shouldn't be excluded due to the small separation of the system ( AG = 0.437 , AB = 1.376 ) and of a pixel size twice larger than for the other lenses (i.e. 0.2 /pix.). Chromaticity: There is a strong change of the flux ratio from the blue to the red. We find evidence for unproper deblending of the QSO and of the lens flux, especially below 6500 corresponding to the 4000 break of the lens- where the continuum spectrum of the lens and of the QSO have similar shapes. The decomposition of the  line also shows imprint of the H and K absorption bands from the lens.

gmw_00005452.name: HS 2209+1914
gmw_00005452.overview: HS 2209+1914 is a bright, doubly imaged quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.07 [arXiv:1304.4474, arXiv:1007.3142]. The system is strongly lensed by a galaxy that exhibits a visible bulge and an extended structure, which may represent spiral arms or a distorted Einstein ring [arXiv:1007.3142].

sources.summary: The paper presents HS 2209+1914 as a bright lensed z=1.07 quasar monitored for 8.5 seasons, deriving the first roughly 20-day time delay for the system.

quotes.quote: During the Hamburg Quasar Survey Hagen1999 identified the system HS 2209+1914 as a bright z_ s =1.07 quasar having a galaxy neighbour detectable on their Schmidt plates. Nothing else was published on this object until Chantry2010 , who obtained precise astrometry for the two lensed quasar images and the lensing galaxy based on the deconvolution of Hubble Space Telescope images.

Deconvolution results and light curves The doubly lensed quasar HS 2209+1914 was observed by four telescopes involved in COSMOGRAIL: the Euler telescope started observations in May 2004 and was joined by the Mercator telescope, which continued the monitoring until December 2008. In May 2007, observations of this lens also started at the Maidanak Observatory during two seasons, and they were joined by the HCT the same year. This last telescope has continued observations until now. Even without the end of the 2012 season, we dispose of a total of 8.5 seasons of observations from 2004 to mid-2012. The lensed quasar can be observed from May to December suffering from a non-visibility window of four months.

Again, basically different methods converge towards the same value of t_ BA 20 days, so a final result of t_ BA = 20 5 is a reasonable and intermediate solution between possibly too optimistic and too pessimistic error bars, while not completely excluding higher values since these uncertainties are 1 error bars.

sources.summary: The paper describes HS 2209+1914 as a doubly imaged quasar with a detected lensing galaxy whose bulge and extended structure may indicate spiral arms or an Einstein ring.

quotes.quote: HS 2209+1914 (g) This system (RA = 22^ h 11^ m 30^ s .30 and DEC = +19^ 29'12 00 ) is a doubly imaged quasar, with z_ s =1.07 , discovered during the Hamburg-Cfa Bright Quasar Survey (HS) by Hagen1999 . They clearly detected the lensing galaxy. Nothing else is available for this system: no time delay, no lens redshift and no modeling.

HS 2209+1914 (g) A bulge is clearly observable but an additional extended structure is also visible. It could either be some spiral arms, in which case the lens would be a late-type galaxy, or even a distorted Einstein ring. A spectrum of the lens and higher resolution imaging would help to disentangle between these hypotheses.

gmw_00005453.name: Ruby's lens
gmw_00005453.overview: The Ruby is a highly obscured, gravitationally lensed starburst galaxy at redshift z=3.005 [arXiv:1703.02984, arXiv:1704.05853]. Its submillimeter emission forms a nearly complete 1.4-arcsecond Einstein ring around a faint, red, massive foreground lensing galaxy at redshift z=1.525 [arXiv:1703.02984, arXiv:1811.11215].

sources.summary: The paper presents Ruby's foreground lens as a faint red massive z=1.525 galaxy dominating optical/NIR morphology; lensing mass and Salpeter-IMF stellar mass agree, suggesting a bottom-heavy IMF.

quotes.quote: Ruby's lens. A massive, lensing galaxy at z=1.5 with a bottom-heavy IMF Ca nameras et al. Received / Accepted We study the properties of the foreground galaxy of the Ruby, the brightest gravitationally lensed high-redshift galaxy on the sub-millimeter sky as probed by the Planck satellite, and part of our sample of Planck's Dusty GEMS. The Ruby consists of an Einstein ring of 1.4 \ diameter at z=3.005 observed with ALMA at 0.1 \ resolution, centered on a faint, red, massive lensing galaxy seen with HST/WFC3, which itself has an exceptionally high redshift, z=1.525 0.001 , as confirmed with VLT/X-Shooter spectroscopy. Here we focus on the properties of the lens and the lensing model obtained with LENSTOOL .

The rest-frame optical morphology of this system is strongly dominated by the lens, while the Ruby itself is highly obscured, and contributes less than 10 to the photometry out to the K band. The foreground galaxy has a lensing mass of (3.70 0.35) 10^ 11 M _ . Magnification factors are between 7 and 38 for individual clumps forming two image families along the Einstein ring. We present a decomposition of the foreground and background sources in the WFC3 images, and stellar population synthesis modeling with a range of star-formation histories for Chabrier and Salpeter initial mass functions (IMFs). Only the stellar mass range obtained with the latter agrees well with the lensing mass.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Ruby as a bright gravitationally lensed z=3.005 submillimeter galaxy forming a 1.4 arcsec Einstein ring; it is highly obscured and resolved by ALMA into multiple clumps.

quotes.quote: We study the properties of the foreground galaxy of the Ruby, the brightest gravitationally lensed high-redshift galaxy on the sub-millimeter sky as probed by the Planck satellite, and part of our sample of Planck's Dusty GEMS. The Ruby consists of an Einstein ring of 1.4 \ diameter at z=3.005 observed with ALMA at 0.1 \ resolution, centered on a faint, red, massive lensing galaxy seen with HST/WFC3, which itself has an exceptionally high redshift, z=1.525 0.001 , as confirmed with VLT/X-Shooter spectroscopy.

The rest-frame optical morphology of this system is strongly dominated by the lens, while the Ruby itself is highly obscured, and contributes less than 10 to the photometry out to the K band. The foreground galaxy has a lensing mass of (3.70 0.35) 10^ 11 M _ . Magnification factors are between 7 and 38 for individual clumps forming two image families along the Einstein ring.

The morphology of the Ruby in the optical, near-infrared, and millimeter is shown in Fig. fig:stamps . The optical and near-infrared bands are dominated by a faint, red source in the center of a nearly complete Einstein ring with 1.4 \ diameter, which can only be seen at long wavelengths. With ALMA in CO( 4 3 ) we detect six clumps along this ring (Fig. fig:lensmodel ), which we associate with two systems of multiple images, as described below.

sources.summary: The paper highlights PLCK G244.8+54.9 as the brightest Ruby GEMS source, a lensed maximal starburst with co-spatial dense and diffuse gas and highly excited dense-gas emission.

quotes.quote: In PLCK G244.8+54.9, the brightest source at z=3.0 , the HNC(5--4) line emission at 0.1 \ resolution, together with other spatially-integrated line profiles, suggests comparable distributions of dense and more diffuse gas reservoirs, at least over the most strongly magnified regions. This rules out any major effect from differential lensing. This line is blended with CN(4--3) and, in this source, we measure a HNC(5--4)/CN(4--3) flux ratio of 1.76 0.86. Dense-gas line profiles generally match those of mid- J CO lines except in PLCK G145.2+50.8, which also has relatively lower dense-gas line fluxes, perhaps due to fewer dense cores and more segregated dense and diffuse gas phases in this source. The HCO^+/HCN 1 and HNC/HCN 1 line ratios in our sample are similar to those of nearby ultraluminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs) and consistent with photon-dominated regions without any indication of important mechanical heating or AGN feedback. We characterize the dense-gas excitation in PLCK G244.8+54.9 using radiative transfer models assuming pure collisional excitation and find that mid- J HCN, HCO ^+ , and HNC lines arise from a high-density phase with H _ 2 density n 10^5 - 10^6 cm ^ 3 , although important degeneracies prevent determining the exact conditions.

In PLCK G244.8+54.9, blue and red spectral components exhibit HCN/CO(5--4) ratios of 0.074 0.020 and 0.062 0.019, respectively, using the CO line fitting from canameras18b , which is consistent with the ratio of 0.071 0.009 obtained from integrated line fluxes. These minor variations suggest similar distributions of gas phases traced by CO and HCN across the gaseous disk of this maximal starburst. The velocity-integrated HCN(5--4) map of Fig. fig:almamaps broadly confirms that nearly 100 of the dense-gas emission arises from the southern arc, which also emits 65 of the CO(4--3) emission. In addition, the HNC(5--4) (Fig. fig:almamaps ) and CO(4--3) canameras17b maps at a common resolution of 0.1 \ are well suited to compare both phases. The emission-line regions have very similar distributions, with 0.1--0.2 \ offsets between local peaks. Assuming a constant CN(4--3) line contamination, the local variations of HNC/CO along the Einstein ring remain within a factor of two for all clumps identified in CO. Ratios are therefore comparable over star-forming regions that are strongly magnified in PLCK G244.8+54.9, despite nearly four orders of magnitude difference in critical density between the two transitions. Finally, we also find higher HCN/CO and HCO ^+ /CO integrated J=4 -3 ratios in PLCK G244.8+54.9 compared to the low-redshift galaxies from zhang14 , which could either indicate higher dense-gas fractions or different excitations of the gas reservoirs probed by each molecule.

sources.summary: The Ruby is a nearly complete lensed Einstein-ring starburst whose well-sampled CO SLED has double peaks, multiple gas phases, and comparable blue/red component gas masses.

quotes.quote: Firstly, in canameras17b , the analysis of ALMA 0.1 resolution observations of PLCK G244.8+54.9 (hereafter the ``Ruby'') showed that the submm emission forms a nearly complete Einstein ring of 1.4 \ diameter around a red massive foreground galaxy detected with HST /WFC3. VLT/X-Shooter spectroscopy revealed that the main deflector is one of the most distant strong lensing galaxies known to date, at z=1.525 .

The SLEDs of PLCK G092.5+42.9 and PLCK G244.8+54.9, the two brightest GEMS with well-sampled SLEDs up to J_ up =10 -11 exhibit double peaks that strongly suggest the presence of two distinct gas excitation components. We will provide further evidence of these multiple gas phases using detailed excitation models in Sect. sec:coexc .

In PLCK G244.8+54.9, the two components have comparable amplitudes over the entire SLED, and the CO(1--0) fluxes extrapolated from the two-component gas excitation models suggest that they contain similar gas masses. The properties of the red component are consistent with those listed in Table tab:radout . However, the SLED of the blue component rises from J_ up =8 to J_ up =10 , which given the large uncertainties, provides marginal evidence that the warm gas phase in the blue component is more excited, with molecular gas densities about one order of magnitude higher than those determined from the integrated SLED.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the Ruby as a bright lensed z=3 dusty starburst, resolving CO and dust into clumps in an Einstein ring with extreme star-formation intensities.

quotes.quote: Received / Accepted We present an analysis of high-resolution ALMA interferometry of CO(4--3) line emission and dust continuum in the ``Ruby'' (PLCK G244.8 + 54.9), a bright, gravitationally lensed galaxy at z =3.0 discovered with the Planck all-sky survey. The Ruby is the brightest of Planck 's Dusty GEMS, a sample of 11 of the brightest gravitationally lensed high-redshift galaxies on the extragalactic sub-mm sky. We resolve the high-surface-brightness continuum and CO line emission of the Ruby in several extended clumps along a partial, nearly circular Einstein ring with 1.4 \ diameter around a massive galaxy at z=1.5 . The Ruby lies at, and in part even above, the starburst sequence in the Schmidt-Kennicutt diagram, and at the limit expected for star formation that is self-regulated through the kinetic energy injection from radiation pressure, stellar winds, and supernovae.

We presented an analysis of ALMA extended-baseline observations of CO(4--3) and the 3-mm continuum in the z=3.0 dusty starburst galaxy PLCK G244.8 + 54.9, the ``Ruby'' at 0.1 \ spatial resolution, probing spatial scales down to approximately 60 pc in the source plane. This galaxy is the brightest of Planck 's Dusty GEMS, a set of exceptionally bright high-redshift galaxies discovered with the Planck and Herschel satellites.

gmw_00005454.name: VV 655
gmw_00005454.overview: VV 655 is a gas-rich dwarf irregular companion galaxy located approximately 32 kpc from the lenticular luminous infrared galaxy NGC 4418 [arXiv:1812.08953, arXiv:2004.09534]. The two galaxies are connected by an H I bridge from a past tidal interaction, and VV 655 exhibits clumpy star formation in multiple discrete regions [arXiv:1812.08953, arXiv:2004.09534].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VV 655 as NGC 4418's 32 kpc companion, with an H I bridge and likely past interaction; spectra indicate low-metallicity dwarf-galaxy gas, not the source of NGC 4418's nebular material.

quotes.quote: It has a neighbor galaxy about 32 kpc ( 3 2 ) away (VV 655=MCG+00-32-013=SDSS J122704.47-005420.6; cz_ SDSS; helio. =2204 km s ^ 1 ; evans03,varenius17 ). found a bridge of H i 21 cm emission connecting these two galaxies and argued their strong tidal interaction about 190 Myr ago.

The star-formation activities in NGC 4418 might have been triggered by an interaction with its neighbor galaxy VV 655 located at 32 kpc ( 3 2 ) away on the sky evans03,varenius17 . discovered an H i 21 cm bridge connecting the two and argued that they first passed each other 190 Myr ago.

Spectra of the companion galaxy VV 665 were taken with the Robert Stobie Spectrograph on the Southern African Large Telescope. These show emission line ratios, such as a small [ N ii ] /H ratio, that are typical of ionized gas in a low metallicity dwarf galaxy (Boettcher et al. in preparation).

sources.summary: The paper describes VV 655 as a gas-rich dwarf irregular companion to NGC 4418, with lower metallicity, ionized outflows, clumpy star formation, and ongoing interaction-driven gas loss.

quotes.quote: Draft: VV 655, a dwarf irregular galaxy with HI tidal debris, is a companion to the lenticular luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) NGC 4418. NGC 4418 stands out among nearby LIRGs due to its dense central concentration of molecular gas and the dusty, bi-polar structures along its minor axis suggestive of a wind driven by a central starburst and possible nuclear activity. We seek to understand the consequences of the ongoing minor interaction between VV 655 and NGC 4418 for the evolution of the LIRG. Specifically, we consider the origin of the gas supply responsible for the unusual nuclear properties of NGC 4418. We investigate the structural, kinematic, and chemical properties of VV 655 and NGC 4418 by analyzing archival imaging data and optical spectroscopic observations from the SDSS-III and new spectra from SALT-RSS.

As shown in Fig. Fig:vv655_uv_36mu , the ultraviolet (UV) structure of VV 655 shows multiple UV-bright starforming regions, consistent with the spatially extended emission lines observed in our SALT spectrum (see sec_ionized .) Thus, while the outer isophotes of VV 655 are fairly regular, the star formation in this galaxy is found in multiple, discrete clumps, suggestive of the irregular elliptical (iE) class of blue compact dwarf galaxies e.g., Loose86 . For VV 655, we measured integrated magnitudes from GALEX far-UV (FUV) and Spitzer 3.6 m band-1 images (see Fig. Fig:vv655_uv_36mu ).

gmw_00005455.name: H i PASS1321-31
gmw_00005455.overview: H i PASS1321-31 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9905114]. It is a member of the Centaurus A group and has a diameter of approximately 0.5 kiloparsecs [arXiv:astro-ph/9905114].

sources.summary: The paper presents H i PASS1321-31 as a representative newly discovered Cen A dwarf: low-surface-brightness, about 0.5 kpc across, morphologically akin to the gas-rich dwarf irregular members found by H i PASS.

quotes.quote: Figure hp1321b shows an example of the final B image of one of the newly identified galaxies, H i PASS1321-31. Optical counterparts were identified for all of the new Cen A group members detected by H i PASS. The galaxy presented in Figure hp1321b , H i PASS1321-31, is typical of the dwarfs which were discovered through the H i PASS. In general these dwarfs are very low surface brightness ( _0=24.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 in B for H i PASS1321-31), with ``by-eye'' diameters of order 30 ^ , which corresponds to 0.5 kpc at the distance on the Cen A group.

The newly detected dwarfs are also represented by H i PASS1321-31 morphologically. Like H i PASS1321-31, most of the new members appear to be dwarf irregulars. This is not unexpected, as the H i PASS is going to select out galaxies which possess atomic gas, introducing a bias toward late-type systems.

gmw_00005456.name: J0038 + 4128
gmw_00005456.overview: J0038 + 4128 is an ongoing galaxy merger system that is classified as a kiloparsec-scale dual active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1510.02303]. The system features co-rotating gaseous and stellar disks, a 150 km/s nuclear velocity offset, and several surrounding knots of strong star formation [arXiv:1510.02303].

sources.summary: The paper studies J0038 + 4128 as a kiloparsec-scale dual AGN candidate, finding a 150 km/s nuclear velocity offset, co-rotating gas and stellar disks, and star-forming knots.

quotes.quote: We present long-slit spectroscopy of the J0038 + 4128, a kiloparsec-scale dual AGN candidate discovered by Huang et al. recently, using the Yunnan Faint Object Spectrograph and Camera (YFOSC) mounted on Li-Jiang 2.4-m telescope at Yunnan observatories. From the long-slit spectra, we find that the average relative line-of-sight (LOS) velocity between the two nuclei (J0038 + 4128N and J0038 + 4128S) is about 150 km s ^ 1 . The LOS velocities of the emission lines from the gas ionized by the nuclei activities and of the absorption lines from stars governed by the host galaxies for different regions of the J0038 + 4128 exhibit the same trend. The same velocities trend indicates that the gaseous disks are co-rotating with the stellar disks in this ongoing merge system. We also find several knots/giant regions scattered around the two nuclei with strong star formation revealed by the observed line ratios from the spectra.

gmw_00005457.name: SDSS J115537.98-004614.2
gmw_00005457.overview: SDSS J115537.98-004614.2 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2404.17720]. Its optical spectrum exhibits both absorption and emission lines [arXiv:2404.17720].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J115537.98-004614.2 as a barred spiral galaxy example, showing its SDSS image sonification and comparing its downloaded spectrum with sonoUno output.

quotes.quote: The Object SDSS J115537.98-004614.2 is a barred spiral galaxy (More information: https://skyserver.sdss.org/dr17/VisualTools/quickobj?objId=1237674649391333465 here ). Figure fig:barred_spiral shows an image of this object captured from the SDSS database that could be appreciated from the Visual Tool Navigate. Using an image sonification script developed as part of the sonoUno project IIIWAIcasado2023 , a video with the image sonification was generated and could be found https://youtu.be/3CC2Jl0Vw9U here . The object page on the database contains a link to download the spectrum as CSV, that file could be opened with sonoUno to display the plot and sonification.

To be able to compare the sonoUno display, a combination of the spectrum obtained from the database and that plotted by sonoUno is shown in Figure fig:barred_spiral_spectrum1 . It could be appreciated that the plots exhibit the same absorption and emission lines.

gmw_00005458.name: SDSS J170437.70+603506.4
gmw_00005458.overview: SDSS J170437.70+603506.4 is a double-nucleus galaxy [arXiv:2404.17720]. Its optical spectrum exhibits both absorption and emission lines [arXiv:2404.17720].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J170437.70+603506.4 as a double-nucleus galaxy example, sonifying its SDSS image and spectrum and comparing the spectrum with sonoUno output.

quotes.quote: The Object SDSS J170437.70+603506.4 is a double nucleus galaxy (More information: https://skyserver.sdss.org/dr17/VisualTools/quickobj?objId=1237651225171197972 here ). Figure fig:double_nucleus shows an image of this object captured from the SDSS database. Using the same image sonification script mentioned above, a video with the image sonification was generated and could be found https://youtu.be/uDPWlBCzAXk here . Moreover, the CSV downloaded from the database was opened in sonoUno to produce the plot and sonification.

Repeatedly, to be able to compare, Figure fig:double_nucleus_spectrum1 shows the spectrum obtained from the database first, and then the spectrum saved from sonoUno software. Equal to the previous galaxy, the plots exhibit the same absorption and emission lines.

gmw_00005459.name: SDSS100401
gmw_00005459.overview: SDSS100401 is a luminous quasar characterized by high ultraviolet luminosity and strong near-infrared emission [arXiv:1101.2855]. The galaxy exhibits a blue 3 to 8 micron continuum and a spectral feature that peaks around 9.7 microns [arXiv:1101.2855].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS100401 as a luminous quasar example with strong near-infrared and high UV emission, a blue 3-8 micron continuum, and a 9.7 micron feature.

quotes.quote: SDSS100401 shows strong near-IR emission, and has high UV luminosity (Figure fig:seds ).

The observed spectra should then have blue 3--8 continua, which is indeed the case for luminous objects like SDSS100401 and SDSSJ151307 (both from program 50328), as can be seen in Figure fig:seds , bottom row of panels.
In most objects, the feature either peaks around 9.7 (SDSS100401) or is mostly flat (SDSS160950).

gmw_00005460.name: SDSSJ142730.19+324106.4
gmw_00005460.overview: SDSSJ142730.19+324106.4 is an obscured, infrared-luminous quasar [arXiv:1101.2855]. The galaxy exhibits a deep 10-micron absorption feature, and its absorbed emission lines suggest it may be a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1101.2855].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSSJ142730.19+324106.4 as an obscured, IR-luminous quasar with deep 10 micron absorption, absorbed emission lines possibly indicating a BALQSO, and constrained torus-model q.

quotes.quote: Obscured sources (for SDSSJ142730, program 50087) are significantly more IR luminous than sources with similar observed UV luminosities (for SDSSJ172522, program 50087) that are probably not as strongly obscured based on their optical SDSS spectra.

SDSSJ160950 is weak in the UV and also has weak 3 and 10 features; SDSS142730 has a deep 10 absorption feature. This source shows a power-law optical/UV continuum in its SDSS spectrum, but its emission lines are absorbed (and may be a BALQSO), which is consistent with its mid-IR nature.
In the case of model, q appears to be constrained only for source SDSSJ142730, which has a deep 10 absorption feature.

gmw_00005461.name: SDSS151307
gmw_00005461.overview: SDSS151307 is a luminous quasar [arXiv:1101.2855]. The galaxy exhibits a blue 3–8 micron continuum and a distinct silicate plateau spanning from 9.7 to 11.4 microns [arXiv:1101.2855].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS151307 as a luminous quasar with a blue 3-8 micron continuum, a 9.7-11.4 micron silicate plateau, and high signal-to-noise torus-fit degeneracy.

quotes.quote: The observed spectra should then have blue 3--8 continua, which is indeed the case for luminous objects like SDSS100401 and SDSSJ151307 (both from program 50328), as can be seen in Figure fig:seds , bottom row of panels.
In some cases, there is a well-defined plateau from 9.7 to 11.4 (SDSS151307, last right-most panel in Figure fig:clumpy_fits ).
This is observed in the number of accepted models in Table tab:torus_par_stats ; even for objects with S/N 25 (SDSS100401, SDSS151307), the number of accepted models is 1000 .

gmw_00005462.name: R5519
gmw_00005462.overview: R5519 is a massive empty ring galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.19 [arXiv:2201.01430]. The galaxy has a symmetric, round morphology without a central nucleus and is situated near a companion galaxy [arXiv:2201.01430].

sources.summary: The paper uses simulations to explain R5519 as a massive z=2.19 empty ring galaxy with a companion, symmetric morphology, no central nucleus, and a possible head-on collision origin.

quotes.quote: Our results provide a promising explanation of the empty ring galaxy recently observed in R5519 at redshift z=2.19 .

A recent observation has discovered a massive empty ring galaxy, named as R5519, at z=2.19 Yuan+2020 , nearby which there is a companion galaxy. Yuan+2020 believed that this is a CRG in the very young universe. Confusingly, the morphology of this CRG is an symmetric empty ring galaxy without a central nucleus.

The ring structure observed in the early universe is quite round and thus is more likely to be produced by a head-on collision. However, a head-on collision usually leaves a compact nucleus e.g., Gerber+1996 , which was not observed in R5519. Since galaxies are observed to be clumpy at the redshift of the R5519, can a clumpy progenitor undergoing a collision reproduce R5519-like empty ring galaxy?

gmw_00005463.name: SDSS J101714.23+390121.1
gmw_00005463.overview: SDSS J101714.23+390121.1 is an early-type foreground galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.211 [arXiv:2311.15009]. It belongs to the infall region of the foreground cluster Abell 963 and is unrelated to the radio source 4C +39.29 [arXiv:2311.15009].

sources.summary: The paper rejects SDSS J101714.23+390121.1 as the 4C +39.29 host, interpreting it as an early-type foreground galaxy whose SDSS spectrum is blended with higher-redshift emission.

quotes.quote: Liao_2020 and also Nascimento_2022 associated the CSS source 4C +39.29 with the optical source SDSS J101714.23+390121.1 at z=0.211 . The SDSS spectrum shows the typical stellar continuum and absorption lines of an early-type galaxy at z=0.211 , but it also contains a system of strong emission lines at higher redshift. This means that the observed spectrum is the blend of two sources at different redshifts. We fitted the SDSS early-type galaxy template spectrum, redshifted to z=0.211 , to the extinction-corrected galaxy spectrum.

Figure fig:HST_SDSSJ1017 shows the co-added two 700 sec exposures, where we marked the two galaxies SDSS J101714.23+390121.1 (galaxy 1) and SDSS J101714.10+390123.8 (galaxy 2). The large galaxy SDSS J101713.86+390131.3 indicated at the upper edge of Fig. fig:Subaru_SDSSJ1017 has no SDSS spectrum. We found a spectrum in the CfA Optical/Infrared Science Archive https://oirsa.cfa.harvard.edu/ taken with the Hectospec Multi-Fiber Spectrograph Fabricant_2005 (proposal ID 2008a-SAO-3, PI: K. Rines) for the Hectospec Cluster Survey Rines_2013 , which shows absorption lines at z = 0.211 , i.e. at the same redshift as SDSS J101714.23+390121.1 (galaxy 1). Both galaxies belong to the infall region of the foreground cluster A 963 and are unrelated to 4C +39.29 .

gmw_00005464.name: SDSS J101714.10+390123.8
gmw_00005464.overview: SDSS J101714.10+390123.8 is a highly luminous type 2 quasar at a redshift of z = 0.536 [arXiv:2311.15009]. It is characterized by strong oxygen emission [arXiv:2311.15009], high core obscuration [arXiv:2311.15009, arXiv:astro-ph/0604184], and a radio jet that interacts with the interstellar medium of a nearby satellite galaxy [arXiv:2311.15009].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J101714.10+390123.8 as the likely 4C +39.29 counterpart: a type 2 quasar/AGN with strong oxygen emission, mid-infrared emission, and jet-cloud interaction.

quotes.quote: Based on a detailed analysis of observations at X-ray, optical, and radio wavelengths, the authors concluded that SDSS J101714.10+390123.8 (`source 1' in their notation, galaxy 2 in Fig. fig:HST_SDSSJ1017 ) is the most likely counterpart of 4C +39.29 . They presented an optical spectrum of this galaxy, which shows strong oxygen emission lines at z = 0.536 . A long-slit spectrum presented by these authors reveals an extended emission line region (EELR) at the same redshift whose position is consistent with our source 3. Gandhi_2006 classified SDSS J101714.10+390123.8 (galaxy 2) as a powerful type 2 quasar with a weak radio core and high core obscuration compared to other narrow-line radio quasars.

Here we note in addition that it is positionally coincident with the Herschel detected source J101714.14+390124.4 which has been classified as a highly luminous type 2 AGN Xu_2015 . We inspected the images from the IRAC camera at 3.6 m and 4.5 m and the MIPS camera at 24 m aboard the Spitzer Space Telescope and confirmed that the bright mid-IR source is at the position of galaxy 2. Following Gandhi_2006 , we conclude that the radio jet of 4C +39.29 most likely interacts with the interstellar medium of a small, gas-rich satellite of galaxy 2, possibly part of an extended tidal structure.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C +39.29 with a background obscured Type 2 quasar, dominated by complex radio lobes and linked to extended emission from an ongoing jet-cloud interaction.

quotes.quote: This cloud lies in close proximity to the peak of the compact steep spectrum radio source 4C +39.29, which has previously been identified with a foreground galaxy in the cluster Abell 963. We present evidence to show that 4C +39.29 is associated with the background X-ray Type 2 quasar. The radio luminosity is dominated by lobes with complex structure and the radio core is weak in comparison to narrow-line radio galaxies at the same X-ray luminosity. The morphology and emission line properties of the extended region are consistent with an on-going jet--cloud interaction.
We find that the likely counterpart of 4C +39.29 is source 1, which is a powerful Type 2 quasar obscured by column densities of cold gas approaching N_ H 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 .

Compared to other narrow-line radio quasars, 4C +39.29 has a weak radio core, and suggests that the relation between compact steep spectrum radio sources and X-ray Type 2 quasars should be further explored. 4C +39.29 lies in the regime of the brightest and most obscured AGN when compared to other Type 2 quasars.

gmw_00005465.name: SDSS J075756.71+395936.1
gmw_00005465.overview: SDSS J075756.71+395936.1 is a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2405.17552] hosting a Type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2405.17552] and a high-excitation radio source [arXiv:1411.0361]. It exhibits a highly disturbed morphology [arXiv:1905.01315] with ring or shell-like structures [arXiv:2311.15009] and an external feature that may be a merger relic [arXiv:2405.17552].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J075756.71+395936.0 with bulge and disk components, finding a late-type spiral host with a pseudo-bulge and possible merger relic.

quotes.quote: In the case of J075756.71+395936.0 <B3 0754+401>, ( z =0.065, Type 2 AGN), the PSF was not needed to properly model the source. The best fit was achieved with a Sérsic function for the bulge, plus an exponential disc (parameters listed in Tab. tab:tab1 ). The Sérsic index of the bulge, being around 2, confirms the presence of a pseudo-bulge.

We found consistent results for the bulge and the exponential disc with the one obtained for the y-band, confirming that this AGN is hosted in a late-type galaxy with a pseudo-bulge. The galaxy has been visually classified as a spiral by Nascimento22 . The i-band image of this source is shown in Fig. fig:image2 where the galaxy can be clearly identified as a disc galaxy, showing a prominent disc and outer halo, with another external feature that may be the relic of a past merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes B3 0754+401 as a compact low-luminosity CSS/HEG with unresolved MERLIN morphology, systemic H I absorption, and line parameters indicating unsettled gas.

quotes.quote: B3 0754+401 This source is part of the low luminosity CSS sample presented by . The largest linear size of the source is 0.25 kpc, and the morphology remains unresolved in the multi-element radio linked interferometer network (MERLIN) observations. classified this object as a High Excitation Galaxy (HEG). We detect \ in this source at the systemic velocity. The width and asymmetry parameters of the \ suggest that the gas in this galaxy is not entirely settled.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0757 as one of eight highly disturbed HERG host galaxies, associating the group with major interactions, high radio powers, and mixed [OIII] luminosities.

quotes.quote: Following cursory visual inspection, however, eight of the galaxies in the sample show clear, highly disturbed morphologies or are involved in interactions with large-scale tidal tails, suggestive of major interactions: J0757, J0836, J0902, J1243, J1257, J1351, J1358 and J1412 (large red stars in Figure  fig:RPvsOIII ). All of these galaxies lie in the half of the sample with the highest radio powers, and hence none lie in the half with the lowest radio powers. Using a two-proportion Z-test, it is found that the null hypothesis that the proportion of highly disturbed galaxies in these two halves of the sample are the same can be rejected at a confidence level of 99.9 per cent (3.3 ). We note that given that there were only eight of such galaxies in the entire sample, a more significant result could not have been observed. Only 5 out of these 8 galaxies lie in the half with the highest [ OIII ] 5007 luminosities, however, with the remaining 3 out of 8 seen to be in the half with the lowest [ OIII ] 5007 luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper uses galaxy E as an individual MergerMonger diagnostic example, concluding from marginalized probabilities that it is more likely a pre-coalescence minor merger.

quotes.quote: Using galaxy E from Figure fig:distabc and Table tab:marginalized_p , we show a diagnostic diagram in Figure fig:diagnostic created using compare classifications.py as an informative example for how to decide between merger type and stage for an individual galaxy.

For galaxy E using the major and minor merger p_ merg,50 values, we are able to conclude that it is more likely a minor merger. We can then further distinguish between the minor merger stages, finding that it is more likely a pre-coalescence minor merger.

sources.summary: The paper reports ring or shell-like structures and a fan-like low-surface-brightness extension around SDSS J075756.71+395936.1, interpreting them as gravitational distortion or merger signatures.

quotes.quote: SDSS J075756.71+395936.1 shows a system of ring or shell-like structures around the main body in the RGB image similar to that in SDSS J105731.17+405646.1 (below). In addition, an extended fan-like structure of low surface brightness stretches from the south of the main body to the west. Such structures are most likely the result of gravitational distortion or a merger with a smaller galaxy.

gmw_00005466.name: SDSS J093430.68+030545.3
gmw_00005466.overview: SDSS J093430.68+030545.3 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.225 [arXiv:2311.15009]. Its morphology indicates it is in an intermediate or late stage of a merger, which has produced a very long, 70 kpc tidal tail [arXiv:2311.15009].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J093430.68+030545.3 as the highest-redshift merger-image example, whose morphology is best explained by an intermediate or late merger with a long tidal tail.

quotes.quote: SDSS J093430.68+030545.3 is the galaxy with the highest redshift ( z = 0.225 ) in Figure fig:PSS_merger . The morphology is best explained as an intermediate or late stage of a merger, which produced a very long ( 70 kpc) tidal tail. Such extended tidal structures are typically produced when at least one disk galaxy is involved Toomre_1972, Springel_1999 .

gmw_00005467.name: SDSS J104029.94+295757.7
gmw_00005467.overview: SDSS J104029.94+295757.7 is an intermediate or late-stage galaxy merger [arXiv:2311.15009] that exhibits a post-starburst spectrum falling in the LINER region [arXiv:1508.03287]. The system features a tilted core, a curved eastern tail, and an extended low-surface brightness tidal tail indicating a disrupted cold component [arXiv:2311.15009].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J104029.94+295757.7 as an intermediate or late-stage merger with tidal structures, a disrupted cold component, a curved tail, and a tilted core.

quotes.quote: SDSS J104029.94+295757.7 is an intermediate or late stage of a galaxy merger. The brightest tidal structure is clearly visible in the RGB image east of the core. The contrast-enhanced greyscale image also shows an extended low-surface brightness tidal tail in the opposite direction.

Such extended tidal structures indicate that at least one dynamically cold system is involved, which has been tidally disrupted (see also objects 4 above and 8 below). This is in agreement with the best-matching template of the MBSEB (Table tab:low_z_PSS ). The curved tail to the east of the core and the tilted structure of the core are other remnants of this process.

sources.summary: The paper says B2 1037+30 needs a young component for blue and UV data and shows a post-starburst spectrum in the LINER region.

quotes.quote: B2 1037+30. The blue and UV data can only be fitted (and well) by a young galaxy component. This object has a typical (post-)starburst spectrum, with the higher Balmer lines present in absorption. In the diagnostic diagrams it falls in the liner region.

gmw_00005468.name: SDSS J105731.17+405646.1
gmw_00005468.overview: SDSS J105731.17+405646.1 is a nearby early-type galaxy identified as NGC 3468 [arXiv:2311.15009]. It features a system of shells, ripples, and two faint streamers that suggest a past merger or infall event [arXiv:2311.15009].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J105731.17+405646.1 with NGC 3468 and describes shells, ripples, and faint streamers that support a merger or infall scenario.

quotes.quote: SDSS J105731.17+405646.1 is the nearby E/SO galaxy NGC 3468. It clearly shows a system of shells and ripples, the most pronounced of which lie south of the main body. The most popular scenarios for the formation of such shell-like structures are based on gravitational distortions: fly-by interactions with neighbouring galaxies Thomson_1991 or infall of one or possibly several dynamically cold galaxies into the potential of a giant E-type system Schweizer_1980 . Such shell galaxies often show also other signs of previous mergers, such as prominent central dust structures Richtler_2020 and unusually low central metallicity Carlsten_2017 . The contrast-enhanced greyscale image in Fig. fig:PSS_merger reveals in addition two faint streamers, extending from the main body towards the north-east, which support the infall scenario.

gmw_00005469.name: NGC3226
gmw_00005469.overview: NGC 3226 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0402110, arXiv:1112.1408] hosting a low-luminosity LINER active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0105083, arXiv:astro-ph/0209607] that exhibits X-ray [arXiv:1305.2225, arXiv:1407.0663] and ultraviolet variability [arXiv:1407.0663]. It is dynamically interacting with its neighbor NGC 3227 [arXiv:1902.08485, arXiv:2405.20162], with which it shares tidal debris [arXiv:2405.20162] and a common stellar cocoon [arXiv:1902.08485].

sources.summary: For NGC 3226, the paper reports large hardness-ratio changes and absorber/normalization variability, while warning that NGC 3227 contamination prevents assessing short-timescale variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 3226 We used two XMM Newton observations in November 2000 and December 2006 and one Chandra observation from December 1999. Variations in the HR of 100 were obtained both in Chandra and in XMM Newton data (see Column 8 in Table obs ). However, ObsID 0400270101 from XMM Newton was not used for further discussion (see below). The observation from 2000 gave as best fit the 2PL model, while that from 2006 was best fitted with a PL. The simultaneous spectral fitting was better represented by a 2PL model varying N_ H2 (i.e. SMF1, Figure bestfig ), with a 74 amplitude variation.

Data from 2006 showed ^2_r = 4.7 and a normalized excess variance _ NXS = 1.8 0.1 10^ 2 indicating variability (see Figure lightcurves3226 , upper-right). However, NGC 3227 is a strong variable Seyfert 1 located 2 from NGC 3226. Thus, we analyzed the possibility that NGC 3226 was contaminated by its emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra X-ray imaging of NGC 3226, identifying a nuclear X-ray source, variability, low-luminosity AGN evidence, and nearby candidate black-hole binaries.

quotes.quote: We present the first high resolution X-ray image of the dwarf elliptical galaxy NGC 3226. The data were obtained during an observation of the nearby Seyfert Galaxy NGC 3227 using the Chandra X-ray Observatory . We detect a point X-ray source spatially consistent with the optical nucleus of NGC 3226 and a recently-detected, compact, flat-spectrum, radio source. The X-ray spectrum can be measured up to 10 keV and is consistent with a power law with a photon index 1.7 2.2 , or thermal bremmstrahlung emission with 4 kT 10  keV. In both cases the luminosity in the 2--10 keV band 10^ 40 h_ 75 ^ 1 \ erg \ s^ 1 . We find marginal evidence that the nucleus varies within the observation.

These characteristics support evidence from other wavebands that NGC 3226 harbors a low-luminosity, active nucleus. We also comment on two previously-unknown, fainter X-ray sources 15  arcsec from the nucleus of NGC 3226. Their proximity to the nucleus (with projected distances 1.3 h_ 75 ^ 1  kpc) suggests both are within NGC 3226, and thus have luminosities ( few 10^ 38 -few 10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 ) consistent with black-hole binary systems.

sources.summary: The paper reviews multiple NGC 3226 X-ray fits and finds its spectra are described by an absorbed power law with variable intrinsic column density and mean photon index near 1.9.

quotes.quote: georgeapj01:ngc3226 fit the 0.5-10 keV spectrum extracted from the long \ observation with an absorbed ( N_ H 5 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 ) power-law ( 1.9 ). terashima03apj:rloud fit the spectrum of the 2.5 ks snapshot \ observation with a moderately absorbed power-law with N_ H 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and 2.2 . gondoin0411:ngc3226 fit the data of the 35 ks \ observation with a partial covering absorber to a bremsstrahlung and found that the X-ray emitting region, with a temperature kT 0.9 keV is 90 covered by an absorber with N_ H 5 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . binder09apj:ngc3226 studied the 100 ks \ observation and fit the spectrum with a partially covered power-law with 1.9 , a covering fraction of 90 , and an intrinsic hydrogen column density of 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . We fit the spectra of both \ and the long \ observations simultaneously with an absorbed power-law and found an intrinsic column density varying between the observations from 3 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 to 9 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 and a mean photon index =1.9 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3226 as the dwarf elliptical companion interacting with NGC 3227, embedded in a shared cocoon and possibly affecting NGC 3227's bar-spur appearance.

quotes.quote: First, three galaxies of intermediate morphological types with trapezoidal inner isophotes (corresponding to the main buckling region) are suspected of having the ongoing buckling Erwin_Debattista2017,Li_etal2017 . It is worth noting, however, that one of the galaxies NGC 3227 is interacting with the dwarf elliptical galaxy NGC 3226. Moreover, both galaxies are embedded in a common cocoon of stars and warm gas, which is supposed to be the result of merging of the third galaxy Appleton_etal2014 . Thus, the shape of the isophotes of NGC 3227 can be greatly distorted due to this interaction. And it is not surprising that one of the two outer-bar spurs of NGC 3227 at the long side of the trapezoid is pointed out exactly to the satellite NGC 3226, which may create a false impression of the position of the spurs.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3226 as NGC 3227’s interacting companion, linked by gas arms and tidal debris, possibly itself a recent merger contributing material to Arp 94.

quotes.quote: NGC 3227 has a complex dynamical history through its interactions with its companion NGC 3226. documented the galaxy pair in depth, using long-slit spectroscopy to identify nuclear gas outflows and an arm extending from NGC 3227 to NGC 3226 at a mean velocity of 550 km s ^ 1 with respect to the NGC 3227/3226 system. followed up with neutral H I imaging to detect two tidal tails on NGC 3227, one of which extends 70 kpc northward towards and beyond NGC 3226, and the other extending 30 kpc southward.

Seminal works from and have studied the tidal connections between NGC 3226 and NGC 3227 that have led to a richly intertwined dynamical history for the two galaxies. note the complex structure of H I and optical gas in this system comprising NGC 3226 and NGC 3227, also known as the Arp 94 system, and suggest that NGC 3226 may itself be the result of a recent merger, and may be sharing tidal debris from that event with NGC 3227.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3226 as a substantially absorbed LINER 1.9 and reports that its HETG luminosity agrees with the authors' absorption-corrected Chandra value.

quotes.quote: One galaxy (NGC 3169, a LINER 2) has a large absorption column ( = 1.1 10^ 23 ), while two galaxies (NGC 4548, a LINER 2, and NGC 3226, a LINER 1.9) show substantial absorption ( 1.6 10^ 22 , and 0.9 10^ 22 , respectively). Obscured LLAGNs In our sample, we found at least three highly absorbed LLAGNs (NGC 3169, NGC 3226, and NGC 4548).

NGC 3226 (L1.9) . --- This galaxy was observed with the HETG in 1999 December (George et al. 2001). They obtained an intrinsic luminosity of 3.2 10^ 40 ( (2.7-4.6) 10^ 40 , 68 confidence limit) in the 2--10 keV band after conversion to a distance of 23.4 Mpc. This luminosity is consistent with our value of 5.5 10^ 40 ( (4.6-7.0) 10^ 40 , 90 confidence range) after correction for absorption.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3226 as a nearby AGN-hosting LINER elliptical, arguing that it is too massive and large to be a true dwarf elliptical.

quotes.quote: One object that has been referred to as a dwarf elliptical with an AGN is the nearby galaxy NGC 3226 geo01 . Its nucleus is a LINER with a broad component to the \ emission line hfs97 , and it is also an X-ray source geo01 . While there is no question that NGC 3226 hosts a genuine AGN, it is too massive and too large to be classified as a true dwarf elliptical galaxy. It has an absolute magnitude of M_B = -19.4 and 180 \ sp98 , which suggests a likely black hole mass of order 10^8 \ from the \ relation. Thus, NGC 3226 appears to be a small but ordinary elliptical, probably with a fairly massive black hole that is accreting at an extremely sub-Eddington rate, and it is very different from the ``dwarf AGNs'' in POX 52 and NGC 4395 with < 10^6 .

sources.summary: NGC 3226 is described as a dwarf elliptical LINER interacting with NGC 3227, with a semicircular dust lane, additional dusty strands, outer filaments, and nuclear ionized gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 3226 --- Fig. 1(f) : The dwarf elliptical LINER NGC 3226 and its spiral neighbor NGC 3227, a popular Seyfert galaxy, form a well-known interacting pair of galaxies. The dust morphology of NGC 3226 is dominated by a semi-circular lane wrapping around the eastern side of the nucleus. Its outer radius is 160 pc ( 1 7 ) and it lies at PAs of 20 200 . The northern tip of the lane is connected to a chaotic group of dusty strands. Like the central lane, these are oriented roughly along a north-south axis, close to the major axis of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3226 as a Leo Cloud elliptical with optical dust lanes, interaction with NGC3227, and compact 250 micron emission relative to its optical extent.

quotes.quote: A semicircular lane of dust in NGC3226 is seen as absorption in the optical (Martel et al. \ 2004) with dusty strands extending north--south. The source is known to be interacting with the nearby spiral NGC3227 (the bright source to the south of the elliptical---Figure fig:spireegals ), which has clear tidal gas trails (Mundell et al. \ 2004). The elliptical appears slightly extended in the 250 m image but more compact than in the optical image (Figure fig:spireegals ). NGC3226 is part of the Leo Cloud.

sources.summary: The paper reports CO(1-0) and CO(2-1) detections in UGC 5617, a single-peaked CO profile, and CO fluxes broadly consistent with earlier IRAM measurements.

quotes.quote: We report the new detection of CO(1-0) emission in four galaxies (UGC 0968, UGC 5617, UGC 6946, and UGC 8355) and CO(2-1) emission in two of them (UGC 0968 and UGC 5617).

Among the 4 sources showing CO(1-0) detection, UGC 0968 and UGC 6946 exhibit double-horn profiles, while UGC 5617 and UGC 8355 have single-peak profiles. Galaxies detected in the CO(2-1) line (UGC 0968 and UGC 5617) have profiles similar to those found in the CO(1-0) line.

For UGC 5617, lisenfeld08 measured both the CO lines with IRAM telescope: I _ CO(1-0) = 1.3 K km s ^ 1 , consistent with our IRAM CO flux and I _ CO(2-1) = 0.7 K km s ^ 1 , slightly smaller than our measurement.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3226 is well fitted by a power law plus neutral absorber, while previous studies differ over bremsstrahlung versus power-law interpretations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3226 Only a single summed spectrum of NGC 3226 could be produced from the Swift data. We find that the spectrum is very well-fitted by a power law and a neutral absorber ( ^2_R = 0.90 ). Past studies disagree somewhat as to whether the best-fitting model for the spectrum of NGC 3226 is bremsstrahlung or a simple power law e.g. Gonzalez-Martin2009,Gondoin2004, Younes2011 , but previous work agrees on the presence of a neutral absorber. We find a high value for the photon index, but with large uncertainties ( = 2.57 0.42 ); Gondoin2004 and Younes2011 find value which is lower, but which agree with ours within our errors.

sources.summary: NGC 3226 is discussed as a LINER with compact milliarcsecond radio emission, a flat 15-5 GHz spectrum, and no apparent 5 GHz variability between VLBA and MERLIN measurements.

quotes.quote: In the radio band, NGC 3226 is detected with the VLBI techniques at milliarcsecond resolution, most likely to be a compact point-like source falcke00ApJ:VLBAliners . nagar00apj:radioliner calculated a VLA flux density of 5.4 mJy at 2 cm, which results in a flat radio spectrum between 15 and 5 GHz. filho06AA:radio detected NGC 3226 with MERLIN at subarcsec scale and found a peak flux density at 5 GHz of about 3.5 mJy, comparable to the value reported for the VLBA data (see Table ngcall-sed-tab ), therefore, filho06AA:radio concluded that NGC 3226 does not appear variable at 5 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 3226 as an elliptical dominated by stellar emission, with a faint 8 micron tidal feature, weak MIR emission, IRAS upper limits, and no significant AGN infrared contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 3226: As an elliptical galaxy, NGC 3226's emission is dominated by its stars, although there appears to be a faint tidal feature in the 8 emission directed roughly to the north. NGC 3226 is particularly faint in the MIR relative to its NIR emission and only has upper limits in the MIR-FIR from IRAS . It has a LINER nucleus, but the decomposition of its IR spectrum indicates no significant contribution form an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 3226 from long-term X-ray variability because of possible contamination, but reports low-confidence Chandra light-curve variations and UV variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 3226 : Long-term X-ray variations from this source are not taken into account due to possible contamination from NGC 3227. We refer the reader to HG13 for details. The analysis from the Chandra light curve shows variations in the soft and total bands below 2 confidence level.

gmw_00005470.name: NGC1349
gmw_00005470.overview: NGC 1349 is a lenticular galaxy composed of a central bulge and a surrounding disc [arXiv:1901.08075]. While its central region exhibits ionization consistent with evolved post-AGB stars [arXiv:1511.01300], the outer disc hosts active star formation that suggests outside-in growth [arXiv:1901.08075].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1349 to show a two-zone early-type galaxy whose bright peripheral star-forming zone can be missed, causing aperture-biased LINER or retired classifications.

quotes.quote: This two-zone morphology can be best illustrated in the case of NGC 1349 : Its central part ( 8 , equivalent to 3.4 kpc) shows a low, nearly constant \ of 1  , which is consistent with gas photoionization by the evolved ( 10^8 yr) post-AGB stellar component (cf, e.g., G15a), whereas the high ( 10  ) \ and BPT ratios in its periphery ( 8 ) imply gas photoionization by massive OB stars. Using the isophotal annuli technique P13 P02 we extracted the spectrum within the inner and outer zone, which are shown overlaid with the integrated spectrum in Fig.  fig:spec a. The middle panel shows the pure nebular component in the region around the \ Balmer line, as obtained after subtraction of the best-fitting stellar model (see G15a for details). From this diagram it can be appreciated how significantly the 2ha \ flux ratio decreases from the inner to the outer zone.

In the case of NGC 1167 , inclusion of its very faint SF rim has a marginal impact on its AGN/LINER classification, whereas in NGC 1349 the much brighter peripheral SF zone results in a down-left shift beneath the LINER demarcation curve, moving the ETG into the locus of `composite SF/LINER' galaxies. Had this galaxy been observed within a FoV of 20 , then only its central, almost emission-line free zone would have been considered in the analysis, prompting its spectroscopic classification as a retired ETG/LINER, in the definition by sta08 . Obviously, the same conclusion would had been drawn from SDSS or GAMA spectra yor00,Baldry2010 . Only beyond z 0.45 would the aperture of those surveys encompass the whole ETG, revealing its true nature.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC1349 as lenticular, with a bulge and disc, metallicity gradients, central ionisation unlike NGC1167, residual disc star formation, and outside-in disc mass growth.

quotes.quote: The morphological analysis performed in mendezabreu18 revealed that NGC1349 and NGC3106 can be photometrically classified as lenticulars, therefore hosting a bulge and a disc. However, NGC1167 was classified as unknown. In this paper, we used the combination of c2d + Pipe3D , applied to the CALIFA IFS data, to provide further constraints on their formation and evolution, and possibly to dissentangle the actual number of structural components in each galaxy.

On the contrary, the bulges of both NGC1349 and NGC3106 are more metal-rich at their centers showing a steep decline at larger radii while their discs show an increase in the metallicity at large radii (beyond the effective radius of disc), but they are constant or decreasing for the bulge and disc in the more central regions.

However, the ionised-gas of NGC1349 and NGC3106 tells us again a different story. The current SF in those discs is located mainly in the outer part of the galaxies with some residual SF close to their centers. This is suggestive of a recent outside-in disc growth possibly driven by a central stabilisation of the disc due to the presence of the bulge martig09 .

gmw_00005471.name: NGC3106
gmw_00005471.overview: NGC 3106 is a nearly face-on lenticular galaxy containing both a bulge and a disc [arXiv:1901.08075, arXiv:1907.07261]. It hosts a series of star-forming rings [arXiv:1907.07261], with active star formation occurring primarily in its outer disc and near its center [arXiv:1901.08075].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC3106 as lenticular, with a bulge and disc, metallicity gradients, central and outer disc star formation, and evidence for outside-in disc growth.

quotes.quote: The morphological analysis performed in mendezabreu18 revealed that NGC1349 and NGC3106 can be photometrically classified as lenticulars, therefore hosting a bulge and a disc. However, NGC1167 was classified as unknown. In this paper, we used the combination of c2d + Pipe3D , applied to the CALIFA IFS data, to provide further constraints on their formation and evolution, and possibly to dissentangle the actual number of structural components in each galaxy.

On the contrary, the bulges of both NGC1349 and NGC3106 are more metal-rich at their centers showing a steep decline at larger radii while their discs show an increase in the metallicity at large radii (beyond the effective radius of disc), but they are constant or decreasing for the bulge and disc in the more central regions.

The analysis of the ionised gas in the BPT diagram (Fig. fig:BPT ) shows that NGC1167 has no recent star formation in the disc, reinforcing the idea that it was formed from the remnant gas just after the merger leading to the bulge formation. However, the ionised-gas of NGC1349 and NGC3106 tells us again a different story. The current SF in those discs is located mainly in the outer part of the galaxies with some residual SF close to their centers. This is suggestive of a recent outside-in disc growth possibly driven by a central stabilisation of the disc due to the presence of the bulge martig09 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3106 was studied by CALIFA, but its wider-field data reveal multiple star-forming rings and a measurable rotating gas velocity field in the nearly face-on galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 3106. This galaxy, including its gas velocity field, was studied in the frame of the CALIFA survey califa . They have found an `outer' starforming ring in this lenticular galaxy with a radius of some 20^ (8 ) gomes16 . However, our data with a much larger field of view reveals a series of starforming rings in NGC 3106 (Fig. fig_fpi ), among which the ring reported by the CALIFA is the innermost one. The galaxy is close to face-on, nevertheless the rotation is visible in Fig. fig_fpi , and the orientation of the kinematical major axis is measured quite certainly with our data.

gmw_00005472.name: SDSS J155059.30+395029.5
gmw_00005472.overview: SDSS J155059.30+395029.5 is a type-II quasar that emits a radio flux density likely originating from large-scale radio jets [arXiv:1006.3751]. The galaxy is surrounded by an extended ionized nebula showing evidence of both active galactic nucleus photoionization and shock ionization [arXiv:1006.3751].

sources.summary: The paper describes this type-II QSO as radio-emitting and surrounded by an extended ionized nebula with perturbed kinematics, a large outflow, and AGN/shock ionization evidence.

quotes.quote: SDSS J155059.30+395029.5 Although this source meets our criteria for being radio-quiet, it does emit a significant radio flux density. The flux density measurement from the FIRST survey (Becker, White Helfand 1995), which has a spatial resolution of 5 (24 kpc), is 10.6 0.5 mJy. From the NVSS survey (Condon et al. 1998), with a resolution of 45 (220 kpc), the flux density is measured to be 13.1 0.6 mJy. The fact that the NVSS measurement is significantly higher suggests that there is emission on large spatial scales, and this is almost certainly emission from radio jets. This object is associated with a spatially extended emission line nebula (Fig 1), with an integrated [ OIII ] 5007 luminosity of 1.5 0.2 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

We interpret the kinematic pattern in SDSS J155059.30+395029.5 as an outflow of 285 km s ^ 1 velocity which extends across an area of 20 kpc 20 kpc, with a mass ejection rate of 2 M_ yr ^ 1 . Plausible mechanisms to power this apparent outflow include a starburst, the radio jets, or the AGN radiation field. Furthermore, the nebula associated with SDSS J155059.30+395029.5 shows evidence for AGN photoionization, based on the detection of extended [ NeV ] emission, and evidence for ionization by shocks, based on a relatively high [ NeIII ] / [ OIII ] ratio (see e.g. Dopita Sutherland 1996).

gmw_00005473.name: SDSS J084041.08+383819.8
gmw_00005473.overview: SDSS J084041.08+383819.8 is a type-II quasar associated with an extended ionized nebula that spans at least 40 kpc [arXiv:1006.3751]. The nebula exhibits highly perturbed kinematics in some regions, and its line emission is spatially elongated toward the west [arXiv:1006.3751].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this type-II QSO as one of two systems with an extended ionized nebula, measuring line extents, [O III] luminosity, elongated emission, and perturbed gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: SDSS J084041.08+383819.8 We measure spatial extents of 5 (23 kpc) and 6 (27 kpc) for the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 and [ OII ] 3727 emission lines, respectively. The total luminosity of the [ OIII ] 5007 line is 9.8 1.5 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 . While the [ OII ] flux peaks at the position of the optical continuum peak, the [ OIII ] emission flux reaches its maximum 1 (5 kpc) W,1 (5 kpc) S of this position. The spatial distribution of the line emission is elongated towards the West.

Two of these sources, SDSS J155059.30+395029.5 and SDSS J084041.08+383819.8, were found to be associated with extended ionized nebulae, which have minimum spatial extents of 27 kpc and 40 kpc, respectively. Both nebulae exhibit varied kinematic properties: some regions show relatively quiescent kinematics, consistent with gravitational motion, while in other regions of the nebulae the kinematics are highly perturbed, with FWHM exceeding 1000 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00005474.name: W0134--2922
gmw_00005474.overview: W0134--2922 is a high-redshift hot dust-obscured galaxy [arXiv:1712.00031, arXiv:2104.09495] that is detected in the soft X-ray band [arXiv:1712.00031]. Observations of its ionized carbon emission line reveal a very small velocity gradient [arXiv:2104.09495].

sources.summary: The paper treats W0134--2922 as a serendipitously observed Hot DOG, reports soft-band detection, non-detection in the hard band, and estimates its obscuring column from hardness ratio.

quotes.quote: W0134--2922 We considered only the (serendipitous) observation (see   sample_indiv ), in which the source is detected at an off-axis angle of 3.6 arcmin. W0134--2922 is detected in the soft band with a significance of (1-P_B)=99.985 (corresponding to a 3.8 detection in the Gaussian approximation) and undetected in the hard band ( 1-P_B=99.654 , corresponding to 2.9 ). The small number of total detected counts in the source region (3 in both the soft and hard bands) prevented us from performing a spectral fitting analysis.

We used the BEHR http://hea-www.harvard.edu/astrostat/behr/ code Park06 , which accounts for the Poisson nature of both source and background counts, and derived HR=-0.03 0.37 . Assuming the MYTorus model with photon index fixed to =2 and accounting for the appropriate response matrices, the nominal value of the hardness ratio corresponds to an obscuring column density of N_H=2.6 10^ 23 . Although considering the uncertainties on HR we can only set an upper limit on the column density ( N_H<6.7 10^ 23 ), we estimated the flux and intrinsic luminosity fixing it to the nominal value.

sources.summary: The paper robustly detects the line in W0134--2922, measures a small redshift offset from rest-frame UV, and finds only a very small velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: The line is robustly detected (SNR 20) in four out of the seven Hot DOGs in the sample: W0134--2922, W0220+0137, W0831+0140, and W2246--0526, the latter of which was already reported in DS2016 .

The redshifts derived from the line, z _ CII , for those galaxies in which the line is identified and the profile fully sampled have velocity offsets with respect to z _ UV of v -37 75, 989 74, 49 62 and --64 55 for W0134--2922, W0220+0137, W0831+0140, and W2246--0526, respectively (see Table  t:linefluxes ).

As can be seen, the kinematics are very diverse: W0134--2922 shows a very small velocity gradient; W0220+0137 displays a smoothly varying structure more consistent with a rotating disk; W0831+0140 shows a complex velocity distribution; and W2246-0526 shows relatively slow rotation, with v 150 .f., DS2016 .

gmw_00005475.name: W0220+0137
gmw_00005475.overview: W0220+0137 is a hyper-luminous, heavily dust-obscured quasar [arXiv:1711.10615] that is classified as a hot dust-obscured galaxy [arXiv:1712.00031]. The galaxy displays a smoothly varying velocity structure consistent with a rotating disk [arXiv:2104.09495], alongside asymmetric velocity features that suggest a possible molecular outflow or a gas-rich merger [arXiv:1711.10615].

sources.summary: The paper identifies W0220+0137 as a hyper-luminous dust-obscured QSO whose ALMA CO emission has two components and an asymmetric velocity structure suggesting possible molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: We select three hyper-luminous, heavily dust-obscured QSOs (W0149+2350, W0220+0137 and W0410 0913) from a new population recently discovered in Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer ( WISE , wright2010 ) all-sky survey, by using a so-called `` W1W2 dropout" method wu2012,eisenhardt2012 . The criterion is to select objects which are prominent in the WISE 12 m ( W3 ) or 22 m ( W4 ) bands, and faint or undetected in the 3.4 m ( W1 ) and 4.6 m ( W2 ) bands. This selection is very effective for finding high luminosity dust-obscured galaxies with dominant hot dust emission mostly at redshift z 1-4 assef2015a . Using X-ray observations stern2014,piconcelli2015,ricci2017 and the detailed spectral energy distribution (SED) analysis fan2016b , clear evidence has been found that the selected mid-IR luminous objects are actually highly dust-obscured, possibly Compton-thick AGN, with a high accretion rate close to the Eddington limit wu2018 .

We fit the CO (4-3) line with a double Gaussian, which gives a better fit with the reduced ^2=0.74 than a single Gaussian does with reduced ^2=0.86 . The two emission line components have a 320 km s ^ 1 velocity separation and an angular separation of 0.25 arcsec, which corresponds to 1.9 kpc at z 3.1 . We can not determine which emission line component is associated with the continuum emission.
W0220+0137 and W0410 0913 have asymmetric velocity structures, suggesting possible molecular outflows, though a gas-rich merger scenario could also explain this.

sources.summary: The paper reports W0220+0137 as a long-exposure Hot DOG target detected in both soft and hard X-ray bands and analyzed with the same model used for W0116--0505.

quotes.quote: W0220+0137 was observed in three separate pointings. We used the merge obs tool to merge the individual observations and produce exposure maps in the same three energy bands.

W0220+0137 W0220+0137 is detected with significance P_B 5 10^ 4 in both the soft and hard bands. For this source, we repeated the same analysis performed for W0116--0505, but, due to the smaller number of detected counts, we fixed the normalization of the scattered component with respect to the intrinsic power-law to unity during the fit. Best-fitting parameters are reported in Tab.  phot_tab .

sources.summary: The paper robustly detects the line in W0220+0137, measures a large positive velocity offset, and describes a smoothly varying velocity field consistent with a rotating disk.

quotes.quote: The line is robustly detected (SNR 20) in four out of the seven Hot DOGs in the sample: W0134--2922, W0220+0137, W0831+0140, and W2246--0526, the latter of which was already reported in DS2016 .

The redshifts derived from the line, z _ CII , for those galaxies in which the line is identified and the profile fully sampled have velocity offsets with respect to z _ UV of v -37 75, 989 74, 49 62 and --64 55 for W0134--2922, W0220+0137, W0831+0140, and W2246--0526, respectively (see Table  t:linefluxes ).

As can be seen, the kinematics are very diverse: W0134--2922 shows a very small velocity gradient; W0220+0137 displays a smoothly varying structure more consistent with a rotating disk; W0831+0140 shows a complex velocity distribution; and W2246-0526 shows relatively slow rotation, with v 150 .f., DS2016 .

gmw_00005476.name: W2026+0716
gmw_00005476.overview: W2026+0716 is a hot dust-obscured galaxy at redshift z=2.542 that is optically classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1712.00031]. It exhibits a possible companion galaxy about 10 arcseconds away [arXiv:1406.2506] and shows signs of highly disturbed gas or outflows [arXiv:1911.09828].

sources.summary: The paper highlights W2026 + 0716 as the only target whose 850 m flux rises in a larger aperture, suggesting multiple components or a Herschel-detected companion; it adopts the 15-arcsec beam flux due to uncertainty.

quotes.quote: W2026 + 0716 is the only target whose 850 m flux increases when measured in a larger aperture (see Figure w2026scuba2wise ). It has a 850 m flux density of 2.1 mJy with a 15-arcsec beam-sized aperture. However, when increasing the diameter to 29 arcsec, the flux density increases to 7.3 mJy. The higher flux is likely because the target has multiple components on scales bigger than the SCUBA-2 beam.

The WISE extended source flag is 0 for the W1 through W4 bands, which means that the detected source is not extended at WISE wavelengths (see Figure w2026scuba2wise ). There are no obvious signs of a cluster of sources nearby in Spitzer images (Wu et al. priv. comm.). However, Herschel 160 m imaging shows a possible companion about 10 arcsec away that could contribute enhanced flux in larger apertures in this source (Bridge et al. priv. comm.). Due to this uncertainty, the quoted 850 m flux density in Table 1 is the 15-arcsec beam-sized aperture flux density (2.1 mJy).

sources.summary: The appendix describes W2026+0716 as a z=2.542 Hot DOG/AGN, excluded from the main high-luminosity sample, with X-ray non-detections and upper limits consistent with heavier-obscured Hot DOGs.

quotes.quote: W2026+0716 (RA=20:26:15.18, DEC=+07:16:24.0) is a Hot DOG at z=2.542 (optically classified as AGN; Eisenhardt et al., in prep.) and was observed for 25.5 ks (PN exposure time, filtered for background flares) as part of the same program targeting W1838+3429 and W2246--0526. However, its bolometric luminosity ( L_ bol =5 10^ 13 L_ , computed similarly to the L_ bol values in Tsai15 ; see Tsai et al., in prep.) is lower than for the other objects we considered in this work ( L_ bol 10^ 14 L_ ). Therefore, we did not include it in our main analysis, but we considered it when discussing other L_ bol <10^ 14 L_ Hot DOGs (e.g., Fig.  L_NH - LX_Lbol ). Its monochromatic luminosity at 6 m is L_ 6 m =1.7 10^ 13 L_ (Tsai et al., in prep.).

W2026+0716 is undetected in both the soft and hard bands, although somewhat enhanced emission can be visually identified in the hard band, suggesting that this source is heavily obscured. Following the procedure applied for W1838+3429 and W2246--0526 in   results_indiv , we computed upper limits (at 90 confidence level) on its photometry, fluxes, and luminosity, assuming the MYTorus model and an obscuring column density of log N_H [ ] =24 .

Taking into account the proper response matrix and ancillary file, these values correspond to observed fluxes F_ 0.5-2 keV <8.69 10^ 16 and F_ 2-10 keV <1.20 10^ 14 , and an intrinsic luminosity L_ 2-10 keV <1.62 10^ 45 . In particular, the upper limit on luminosity is in agreement with the results we found for our sample of more luminous Hot DOGs.

sources.summary: W2026+0716 shows a rest-UV continuum downturn, an [O III] narrow-component velocity-offset exception, and significantly broad [O II] that the paper links to outflowing or disturbed gas.

quotes.quote: Next, we fit the rest-frame UV wavelengths, separately in the 1150--2000 and 2000--3500 \ windows in order to better fit the continuum, which often shows a downturn at wavelengths shorter than 2000--3000 \ (e.g., W2026+0716, W2042--3245, also, e.g., Jun13 for quasars in general).

This assumption appears valid for our sample as most of the narrow Gaussian components have offsets within 100 \ to the systemic redshift (with exceptions for W1103--1826 and W2026+0716), for non- H 2 BPT sources with line S/N 5 (Table 3 and Figure 5).

In addition to the likelihood of outflows in the narrow Balmer line region for some Hot DOGs, we plot in the remaining panels of Figure 8 the 3/6 BPT non- H 2 sources with a significantly broad [ O 2 ] ( _ O II , broad/narrow >400 , W1719+0446, W2026+0716, W2216+0723) with S/N > 5. Additionally, 1/3 of the remaining (3/6) sources (W0114--0812) shows _ O II , broad/narrow >300 . This can either be explained by strong AGN outflows broadening the [ O 2 ] line profile, or the kinematics of the ISM in the star-forming region being highly disturbed by AGN outflows and/or galaxy merging (e.g., Dia16,Dia18 ). As [ O 2 ] is a forbidden line, a fraction of them being broad adds support for the presence of outflows, and hints for kpc scale, narrow line region origin for at least some the broadened Balmer lines.

gmw_00005477.name: IIZw71
gmw_00005477.overview: IIZw71 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy classified as a kinematically confirmed polar-ring galaxy [arXiv:0901.2274, arXiv:astro-ph/0011155]. It features several luminous star-forming knots along its polar ring [arXiv:0901.2274] and exhibits a gaseous bridge of neutral hydrogen indicating an ongoing interaction with a companion blue dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0011155].

sources.summary: The paper studies IIZw71 as a blue compact dwarf polar-ring galaxy, measuring its ring kinematics, knot abundances, stellar populations, and interaction-linked chemical evolution.

quotes.quote: The blue compact dwarf galaxy IIZw71 is catalogued as a probable polar-ring galaxy, and along its long axis it has several very luminous knots showing recent episodes of star formation.
