Our main aim is to study the physical properties, the stellar content, and the kinematics in the brightest knots of the polar ring We carried out long-slit spectroscopic observations of the polar ring in the spectral range 3500 - 10000 taken with the William Herschel Telescope (WHT). The spectroscopic observations complemented by the available photometry of the galaxy in narrow H filters. We measured the rotation curve of the ring, from which we infer a ratio M/L_B 3.9 inside the star-forming ring.

Spectrophotometric observations of the galaxy IIZw71 were carried out to study the physical properties of the bursts of star formation along its polar ring. We extracted information on the four brightest knots in H that we labelled from A to D. The electron temperature of [ O iii ] was measured in knots B and C, allowing the direct derivation of ionic abundances of oxygen, sulphur, nitrogen, neon and argon. The total abundances of these species are in the same range of metallicities measured in HII galaxies, but they are slightly lower than the abundances previously reported from measurements of the integrated galaxy acording to strong-line calibrations.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 9562 as a kinematically confirmed polar-ring galaxy with HI in the ring and bridge-like gas indicating interaction with a blue dwarf companion.

quotes.quote: \ (UGC 9562) was classified as a ``probable'' polar-ring galaxy in the Polar-Ring Catalog ( ), based upon its optical appearance. We present 21 cm and optical observations of this galaxy and its companion, the blue star-forming dwarf . Our 21 cm observations show that 5 10^8 solar masses of \ is present in a polar ring orbiting , and show a spatially and kinematically contiguous streamer with 2.5 10^8 solar masses of \ gas between the two galaxies. This gaseous bridge, plus our observations of \ line emission in the polar ring and in , are strong evidence for an ongoing interaction between the two galaxies.

\ (UGC 9562) is classified as ``B-17'', a ``probable'' polar ring system, in the PRC. An optical image of this galaxy and its blue dwarf companion, is shown in Figure rcont ; the polar ring runs NE to SW. Optical spectroscopy in the \ emission line of \ by Reshetnikov \ Combes (1994) revealed two gaseous components with orbits nearly perpendicular to each other --- one with a velocity gradient along the apparent major axis of the polar ring, and another with a velocity gradient along the apparent major axis of the host galaxy. \ is thus a kinematically-confirmed polar-ring galaxy.

gmw_00005478.overview: The object 235144-260358 is a disturbed late-type galaxy falling into the center of the massive cluster Abell 2667 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703012]. It is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy experiencing a nuclear starburst and exhibits stripping signatures within its optical disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0703012].

sources.summary: The paper presents 235144-260358 as a disturbed late-type galaxy in Abell 2667, with stripping signatures, a nuclear starburst, high infrared luminosity, and luminous-infrared-galaxy status.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present a multiwavelength analysis of two peculiar galaxies (hereafter referred as 235144-260358 and 131124-012040) falling into the centers of the massive clusters Abell 2667 ( z 0.23) and Abell 1689 ( z 0.18). Both these systems are associated with extended trails of bright blue knots and diffuse wisps and filaments of young stars, features observed so far only in one other galaxy at similar redshift owen06 .

From the HST images, 235144-260358 appears to be a late-type galaxy (see Fig. colimage ), as confirmed by its structural parameters (see Table tabgal ). However this object is definitely not normal: it shows a disturbed morphology, with clear indications of stripping within its optical disk and a prominent one-armed spiral component as is typically observed in gravitationally perturbed systems vollmer03 . Moreover, there is a significant nuclear enhancement in the optical surface brightness profiles ( 2 mag within the central kpc: see Fig. colprofiles ), suggesting that it is experiencing a nuclear burst of star formation.

The resulting total infrared luminosity of 235144-260358 L(IR) 3( 0.25) 10^ 11 L_ , implies a current star formation rate SFR 53( 4.3) M_ yr^ 1 (using the relation of kenn98 ), consistent with a SFR 57 M_ yr^ 1 obtained from VLA continuum observations and the relation of . This galaxy is a rare example of a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) in a dense cluster. All the properties of 235144-260358 point out the peculiarity of its recent evolutionary history.

gmw_00005479.overview: 131124-012040 is a disrupted galaxy falling into the center of the massive cluster Abell 1689 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703012]. It features an extended trail of bright blue knots and has recently quenched its central star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0703012].

sources.summary: The paper describes 131124-012040 as a disrupted Abell 1689 galaxy with a long blue-knot trail, anomalous color gradients, little residual star formation, and recently quenched central activity.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present a multiwavelength analysis of two peculiar galaxies (hereafter referred as 235144-260358 and 131124-012040) falling into the centers of the massive clusters Abell 2667 ( z 0.23) and Abell 1689 ( z 0.18). Both these systems are associated with extended trails of bright blue knots and diffuse wisps and filaments of young stars, features observed so far only in one other galaxy at similar redshift owen06 .

Abell 1689 The disrupted galaxy in Abell 1689 is illustrated in Fig. colimage (lower panel) and its main properties are listed in Table 1. This galaxy lies at a projected distance 0.24 h_ 70 ^ 1 Mpc from the cluster center (see Fig. whole1689 ) and is 2.5 mag fainter than the perturbed galaxy in Abell 2667 (i.e. with a luminosity of 0.1 L ^ * , wilson97 ). Its redshift is z 0.1866 confirming that it belongs to A1689.

131124-012040 is neither detected at 24 m by Spitzer nor in VLA continuum images (see Table tabgal ). This is consistent with the optical spectrum of this galaxy (see Fig. spectrum ), which shows strong Balmer lines in absorption ( EW(H ) 6 , D(4000) 1.21) and very little residual star formation ( EW( [ OII ] ) 1.8 ). This overall behavior suggests that the galaxy center has recently ( t 100 Myr, i.e. poggia97,shioya02, kauff03 ) stopped forming stars.

gmw_00005480.name: Mkn 421
gmw_00005480.overview: Mkn 421 is a nearby TeV blazar and one of the brightest BL Lacertae objects in the ultraviolet and X-ray bands [arXiv:1506.03629, arXiv:1603.06690]. The galaxy is highly variable across all wavelengths [arXiv:1603.06690], exhibiting rapid flux variability in the X-ray band [arXiv:1506.03629].

sources.summary: The paper studies Mkn 421 as a nearby TeV BL Lac blazar with a major April 2013 X-ray flare, rapid X-ray variability, curved spectra, and weak correlated UV/gamma flaring.

quotes.quote: Mkn 421 is the closest ( z=0.031 ) and the most well studied TeV blazar. It was also the first detected extragalactic TeV source punch , and one of the brightest BL Lac objects seen in the UV and X-ray bands. It is a HBL, with the synchrotron spectrum peaking in the X-ray regime. Moreover, the X-ray emission is known to be highly correlated with the TeV emission multi421,Katarzynski_hbl,Qian , but like other HBLs, shows moderate correlation with the GeV emission Xray-GeV_corr .

There was rapid flux variability in the X-ray band, with a minimum doubling timescale of 1.69 0.13 hrs. There were no corresponding flares in UV and gamma bands. The variability in UV and gamma rays are relatively modest with 8 and 16 respectively, and no significant correlation was found with the X-ray light curve. The observed X-ray spectrum shows clear curvature which can be fit by a log parabolic spectral form.

sources.summary: The paper presents long-term multiwavelength monitoring of Mkn 421, identifying it as a nearby TeV blazar with strong variability and modeling its quiescent-state emission.

quotes.quote: 6 Now at Dept. of Physics, University of the Free State, PO Box 339, 9300 Bloemfontein, South Africa. atreyee@tifr.res The HAGAR Telescope Array at Hanle, Ladakh has been regularly monitoring the nearby blazar Mkn 421 for the past 7yrs. Blazars show flux variability in all timescales across the electromagnetic spectrum. While there is abundant literature characterizing the short term flares from different blazars, comparatively little work has been done to study the long term variability. We aim to study the long term temporal and spectral variability in the radiation from Mkn 421 during 2009-2015.

Mkn 421 is the closest ( z=0.031 ) and the best-studied TeV blazar. It is also the first detected extragalactic TeV source punch , and one of the brightest BL Lac objects seen in the UV and X-ray bands. It is an HBL, with the synchrotron spectrum peaking in the X-ray regime. It is highly variable on all timescales and across all wavelengths from radio to TeVs, and has been well studied during its various flaring episodes by several authors MAGIC421_2008,MAGIC421_2006,MAGIC421_outburst,Amit421,MAXI421,Mkn421_2000,Suzaku421_2006,Swift421_2006,multi421_2005-2006,Integral421,421_march2001,MAGIC421,XMM421_2003, my421, vaidehi421,donna . fermi421 studied the quiescent state emission from this source with the best-sampled SED to date.

gmw_00005481.name: J0249 0815
gmw_00005481.overview: J0249 0815 is a green valley dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 130 Mpc [arXiv:2405.11750]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with a candidate intermediate-mass black hole [arXiv:2405.11750] and exhibits weak soft X-ray excess emission [arXiv:0801.3663].

sources.summary: The paper targets J0249 0815, a dwarf-galaxy IMBH candidate, with spectroscopic and photometric reverberation mapping, finding weak AGN contrast and no significant continuum variability.

quotes.quote: In this work, we report our time series results from the Las Cumbres Observatory Global Telescope LCOGT, Brown2013 and Devasthal Fast Optical Telescope (DFOT) https://www.aries.res/facilities/astronomical-telescopes/-telescope light curves for SDSS J024912.86 081525.6 (hereafter J0249 0815), which serves as the first experimental test beyond NGC 4395. We describe the technical details of source selection, observations, and data analysis in sec:obs . The continuum lags are estimated in sec:lag .

Target Selection J0249 0815 is a candidate IMBH residing within a dwarf galaxy at z = 0.0297 or D_ L 130 Mpc shown in Figure fig:image . This IMBH candidate was initially identified by Greene2004 from the First Data Release of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS DR1). The stellar mass is 9.0 10 ^9 M_ obtained from the NASA-Sloan Atlas http://www.nsatlas.org . The dust corrected colour index of u- is 1.9, making it a green valley galaxy Schawinski14 .
The galaxy light is dominated by the exponential disk, a bulge ( n = 0.89 ) and a bar ( n=0.26 ), consisting of 96.4 of the total flux.

sources.summary: The paper reports weak soft X-ray excess residuals for J0249-0815; adding a multicolor disk blackbody improves the power-law fit only marginally, with listed best-fit parameters.

quotes.quote: The residuals of the observed data from the power-law model showed evidence for weak soft excess emission in the case of J0249-0815, J1140+0307 and J1357+6525. Addition of a multicolor disk blackbody component to the PL model improved the fit marginally for J0249-0815 only at a level of 97.4 ( ^2 = -8.5 for two additional parameters) based on the f-test. The best-fit parameters are kT_ in = 169_ 50 ^ +43 and = 1.72 0.30 .

gmw_00005482.name: R1
gmw_00005482.overview: R1 is an old continuum-break red galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.317 [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443]. It is devoid of strong emission lines and exhibits weak mid-ultraviolet breaks, with a stellar content that is best-matched by early-type F dwarf stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443].

sources.summary: The paper identifies R1 as an old continuum-break red galaxy at z=1.317 with weak mid-UV breaks and stellar-content differences from R8 and R10.

quotes.quote: R1, R8, and R10 --- Old Continuum-Break Galaxies Three of the red galaxies on our slitmask --- R1, R8, and R10 --- are devoid of strong emission lines (Figures errspec ), but they do show continuum breaks identifiable as the rest-frame mid-UV (2000 -- 3300 ) breaks of B2640 and B2900 at z= 1.317, 1.298, and 1.290, respectively. The breaks arise from metal-line blanketing on either side of the tophat-shaped feature from 2640--2720 . These features, which were first suggested by 1977ApJ...212..438M as possibly being useful for redshift determinations at z>0.75 , have been used recently to identify old galaxies out to z 1.55 1996Natur.381..581D,dey00 .

The spectra of R10 rises most rapidly to the red and has the strongest 2640 \ and 2900 \ breaks, while R1's continuum is noticeably flatter and its breaks are the weakest, especially the 2900 \ break. R8's spectrum is intermediate between R10 and R1. Results from both cross correlation analysis ( contbreak-spectra ) and examination by eye find R1 is best-matched by early-type F dwarfs, R8 by mid-type F dwarfs, and R10 by late-type F and early-type G dwarfs (Figure absrestframe ) in this wavelength region.

gmw_00005483.name: R6
gmw_00005483.overview: R6 is an emission-line red galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.203 [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443]. Its spectrum indicates a small amount of active star formation or weak AGN activity, while its optical and near-infrared morphology suggests central dust reddening [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443].

sources.summary: The paper describes R6 as an emission-line red galaxy at z=1.203 with inferred star formation, possible weak AGN activity, and optical/near-IR morphology suggesting central dust reddening.

quotes.quote: R6 and R7 --- Emission-Line Galaxies The spectrum of R6 shows a single emission line with an observed equivalent width (EW) of 60 \ (Figure spec-r6+r7 ). There is ample continuum blueward of the line so we identify it as , placing R6 at z=1.203 , slightly in the foreground of the other redshifts in this field. Assuming this emission originates from \ regions of massive young stars, we infer a star formation rate of 10 h_ 50 ^2 yr \ using the \ calibration of 1992ApJ...388..310K , comparable to the most active spirals in the local neighborhood 1983ApJ...272...54K .

For the red galaxies in the SW field, their JHK colors are also consistent with unreddened z=1-2 ellipticals (Figures ero-colors and ero-colors-jhk-sw ); the one SW galaxy with a Keck spectrum, R6, supports this idea as it lies at z=1.203 , though its redshift is based on weak \ emission. (The spectrum has insufficent S/N to detect any continuum breaks, if present.) So even if R6 is composed of an old, red stellar population (its band flux is only marginally brightened by the \ line), the emission line points to a small amount of simultaneous star-formation or weak AGN activity. In fact, R6 has quite a different appearance in optical as compared to the near-IR (Figure zoom-contours ) which may suggest significant dust reddening in its central regions, unlike most of the other red galaxies (see below).

gmw_00005484.name: R7
gmw_00005484.overview: R7 is a galaxy at a redshift of 1.319 that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443]. It exhibits a K-band color that is significantly redder than typical Seyfert galaxies, suggesting it may be dust-reddened [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443].

sources.summary: The paper finds R7 hosts an AGN at z=1.319, is probably too far from the quasar to cause the Mg II absorber, and may be dust reddened.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of R7 reveals the presence of an active galactic nucleus at z=1.319 (Figure spec-r6+r7 ). The data show strong lines of \ and C 2 ] 2326 with observed equivalent widths of 50 . The C 3 ] 1909 line is possibly detected, though this line falls near the blue end of the spectrum where the signal is quite low.

The spectrum of QSO 1213--0017 also shows an Mg 2 absorption system at z=1.3196 1987ApJ...322..739L,1992ApJS...80....1S , similar to the redshift of 1.319 0.002 for galaxy R7. Could this galaxy be responsible for the absorption? It is located 15 \ from the QSO, which is 130 kpc.
The other galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift, R7, has an optically revealed AGN. Its K color is far too red compared to typical Seyferts e.g., 1994MNRAS.266..953K .

gmw_00005485.name: R8
gmw_00005485.overview: R8 is an old continuum-break red galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=1.298 [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443]. Its mid-ultraviolet spectral features and colors suggest a metal-poor stellar content similar to local populations [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443].

sources.summary: The paper classifies R8 as an old continuum-break red galaxy near z=1.298, with mid-UV features, an intermediate spectrum, and UV indices suggestive of metal-poor stellar content.

quotes.quote: R1, R8, and R10 --- Old Continuum-Break Galaxies Three of the red galaxies on our slitmask --- R1, R8, and R10 --- are devoid of strong emission lines (Figures errspec ), but they do show continuum breaks identifiable as the rest-frame mid-UV (2000 -- 3300 ) breaks of B2640 and B2900 at z= 1.317, 1.298, and 1.290, respectively. The breaks arise from metal-line blanketing on either side of the tophat-shaped feature from 2640--2720 . These features, which were first suggested by 1977ApJ...212..438M as possibly being useful for redshift determinations at z>0.75 , have been used recently to identify old galaxies out to z 1.55 1996Natur.381..581D,dey00 . At least two of the red galaxies may also have \ in absorption. These spectral features are characteristic of old stellar populations, such as Galactic and M31 globular clusters, M32, and the cores of local elliptical galaxies e.g., and references therein 1999AJ....117.2213R,1998AJ....116.2297P . Galaxies R8 and R10 also may show the 4000 \ break at 9200 . For these three galaxies, we determined the redshifts using the 4300--8000 \ region of the spectra in the cross-correlation analysis.

Figure customindex plots the results juxtaposed against individual main-sequence stars 1992ApJS...82..197F , local old spheroids 1998AJ....116.2297P , and the z=1.552 galaxy LBDS 53W091. The UV indices of R8 and R10 are consistent with local populations, though the W2640 strengths of R8 and R10 differ, with the former having a weaker 2640 \ break than even the local elliptical sample. Also, the two galaxies have different 2600-3000 colors, with R8 lying closer to the metal-poor local populations, which have bluer mid-UV colors.

gmw_00005486.name: R10
gmw_00005486.overview: R10 is an old continuum-break red galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.290 [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443]. It exhibits strong mid-ultraviolet breaks and has experienced little to no recent star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0002443].

sources.summary: The paper describes R10 as an old continuum-break red galaxy at z=1.290, with strong mid-UV breaks, little recent star formation, and a comparably high formation redshift.

quotes.quote: R1, R8, and R10 --- Old Continuum-Break Galaxies Three of the red galaxies on our slitmask --- R1, R8, and R10 --- are devoid of strong emission lines (Figures errspec ), but they do show continuum breaks identifiable as the rest-frame mid-UV (2000 -- 3300 ) breaks of B2640 and B2900 at z= 1.317, 1.298, and 1.290, respectively. The breaks arise from metal-line blanketing on either side of the tophat-shaped feature from 2640--2720 . These features, which were first suggested by 1977ApJ...212..438M as possibly being useful for redshift determinations at z>0.75 , have been used recently to identify old galaxies out to z 1.55 1996Natur.381..581D,dey00 . At least two of the red galaxies may also have \ in absorption. These spectral features are characteristic of old stellar populations, such as Galactic and M31 globular clusters, M32, and the cores of local elliptical galaxies e.g., and references therein 1999AJ....117.2213R,1998AJ....116.2297P . Galaxies R8 and R10 also may show the 4000 \ break at 9200 . For these three galaxies, we determined the redshifts using the 4300--8000 \ region of the spectra in the cross-correlation analysis.

The similar break amplitudes of LBDS 53W091 and R10 suggest these two galaxies are comparably old. If the analysis of 53W091's spectrum is correct, its inferred age is barely compatible with present cosmological parameters, even with the assumption of solar metallicity to reduce the inferred age 1997ApJ...484..581S . Therefore, R10 must have had a comparably high formation redshift, since the difference in look-back time is only 5 between z=1.55 and z=1.31 , and also must have experienced little or no recent star-formation.

gmw_00005487.name: SBS 1159+545
gmw_00005487.overview: SBS 1159+545 is an astronomical object characterized by its chemical abundance properties [arXiv:1012.2385]. Analysis of the galaxy shows a high temperature solution of 17,100 K when incorporating an O III prior, which subsequently increases its calculated helium abundance [arXiv:1012.2385].

sources.summary: The paper presents SBS 1159+545 as a better-behaved abundance object where the O III prior raises the fitted temperature and slightly increases the helium abundance.

quotes.quote: Figure 1159-y exhibits the solution for one of these, SBS 1159+545. For these objects the shapes of the likelihood plots are similar to those found in the synthetic results of figures Syn-y , Syn-He_4panel , and Syn-H_3panel , prior to the inclusion of the [ O III ] prior. For SBS 1159+545, the T(O III) prior noticeably increases the helium abundance found in the solution. This occurs primarily due to the higher temperatures found when the prior is included (using the T _ OIII value of 18,600 K for SBS 1159+545 raises the solution temperature, T, from 15,900 K to 17,100 K). Reassuringly, this large temperature difference only raises the abundance by 1 (0.08326 to 0.08416), compared with an uncertainty of 5 .

gmw_00005488.overview: The active galaxy 0748+126 features a straight radio jet extending to the southeast that terminates at a hotspot [arXiv:1802.04714]. This source exhibits aligned parsec- and kiloparsec-scale jets [arXiv:1802.04714], along with a transverse rotation measure gradient across its core region with a significance of nearly 6 sigma [arXiv:1503.03411].

sources.summary: The note describes 0748 + 126 as a source with aligned pc- and kpc-scale radio jets, a detected jet and hotspot, and no HST jet features.

quotes.quote: 0748 + 126 (PKS B0748 + 126) The source has a straight radio jet to the southeast ending at a hotspot 15 \ from the core. The PA of the VLBI jet is only 15 \ from that of the kpc scale jet and superluminal motion at just under 20 was found. The jet and hotspot are both clearly detected in the 5.6 ks observation. No features are apparent in the HST image at any of the radio jet knots.

sources.summary: The paper selects 0748+126 for RM-gradient reanalysis and reports a nearly 6 sigma transverse RM gradient across its core region.

quotes.quote: A visual inspection of the published RM images indicated the possible presence of transverse RM gradients across the jets of 0355+508 and 0748+126, as well as 1749+096, whose transverse RM gradient was previously reported by Gabuzda et al. (2008).
0748+126. Our map in Fig. fig:rmmaps8 b shows a transverse RM gradient across the core region, with a significance of nearly 6 .
Monotonic transverse RM gradients with significances of at least 3 are observed across the core regions of 0355+508, 0748+126, 1156+295, 1749+096, 2007+777 and 2155 152.

gmw_00005489.overview: The active galactic nucleus 0256+075 is classified as a BL Lac object [arXiv:1212.3423] with faint, small radio lobes to the northeast and west [arXiv:1802.04714]. Its radio jet exhibits a transverse rotation measure gradient [arXiv:1503.03411] and a core electric field oriented parallel to the jet direction [arXiv:1212.3423].

sources.summary: The paper treats 0256+075 as a BL Lac object whose core-region RM gradient is monotonic but too small to be reliable, with a jet-parallel core electric field.

quotes.quote: Polarization observations of 6 radio--quasars (0133+476, 0420-014, 0745+241, 0906+430, 1633+382 and 1954+513) and 2 BL Lac objects (0256+075 and 1823+568) were carried out in a 24 hour session on November 2, 2008 using the VLBA.

0256+075 shows a gradient in the core region spanning a bit more than a single beam size and forming an angle with the direction of the jet. The values for the RM found in this source vary monotonically from 2000 rad/m ^2 in the upper region of the core to about 1000 rad/m ^2 as we approach the upstream region of the jet. However, this gradient is too small both in size and slope to be considered reliable.
In several sources, the electric field in the core seems to be either perpendicular (0745+241, 1954+513) or parallel (0133+476, 0256+075, 0420--014, 0906+430) to the direction of the jet.

sources.summary: The paper reexamines 0256+075 and finds an inner-jet RM gradient whose significance reaches 3.2 sigma with a circular beam, with sign changes supporting helical fields.

quotes.quote: 0256+075. An RM map made from the same visibility data was published by Mahmud Gabuzda (2008), but without any error analysis. Our map in Fig. fig:rmmaps5-1 a shows a monotonic transverse RM gradient across the inner jet, whose significance is 2.9 . We also made this RM map using a circular beam; the RM gradient remains visible, and its significance increases to 3.2 .
Sign changes are observed in the transverse RM gradients detected in 0256+075, 0735+178, 0745+241 and 1652+398.

sources.summary: The note describes 0256 + 075 as having faint lobes, no detected X-ray emission from the radio jet, and a possible westward HST knot.

quotes.quote: 0256 + 075 (PKS B0256 + 075) The VLA image shows faint, small lobes to the northeast and due west. The latter is closer to the core and defines the region of interest for X-ray analysis. No X-ray emission was detected from the radio jet. The HST image shows what may be a knot about 1 \ due west of the core but the radio map shows no clear extension in this direction.

gmw_00005490.overview: The active galactic nucleus 2007+777 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:0805.1021, arXiv:1101.5822] with a hybrid FR I/II radio morphology [arXiv:0805.1021, arXiv:1101.5822]. It features a long, deprojected radio jet [arXiv:2308.11370] that is also detected in X-rays [arXiv:0805.1021, arXiv:1101.5822] and exhibits subluminal apparent speeds [arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper treats 2007+777 as one of five AGN targets observed by KVN at 22-130 GHz for multi-frequency VLBI astrometry, map registration, position shifts, and jet-related alignment tests.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:source_pos shows the distribution in the sky of the five selected sources (1803+784, 1807+698, 1842+681, 1928+738 and 2007+777) along with their angular separations, which range between 3.6^o and 11^o . None of the sources had been observed previously with VLBI at 130 GHz. The observations consisted of 3 minutes long scans alternating between the sources in each of the two triangles shown in Figure fig:source_pos , and between the triangles ca. every hour and a half, at the four bands simultaneously. Alternating between multiple sources allowed us to develop a strategy to decompose the relative astrometric measurements into single source position shifts that allow, for example, the registration of the images at different frequencies.

Figure fig:sc shows self-calibration images for the five AGN sources in the multi-frequency KVN observations, including the first images of these sources at 130 GHz. The visibility datasets were modelfitted and imaged and show little divergence from point-source core dominated images; in some cases there appears to be some elongation aligned with the jet direction as seen in higher resolution maps from MOJAVE mojave . Table tab:flux lists the total flux values as measured from the maps, at all frequency bands.

sources.summary: The paper describes S5 2007+777 as a BL Lac HYMOR source with a Chandra-detected kpc-scale X-ray jet whose radio/X-ray morphology and hard continuum resemble powerful FRII jets.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the evidence for the existence of a fourth extended X-ray jet in the classical radio-selected source 5, which for its hybrid FRI/II radio morphology has been classified as a HYMOR (HYbrid MOrphology Radio source). Our \ ACIS-S observations of this source revealed an X-ray counterpart to the 19 long radio jet. Interestingly, the X-ray properties of the kpc-scale jet in 5 \ are very similar to those observed in FRII jets.

The radio morphology of 5 \ with an edge-brightened lobe on the Eastern side and a core-jet morphology on the Western side - led to its classification as a HYMOR (HYbrid MOrphology Radio source; Gopal-Krishna Wiita 2000), a relatively rare class of sources. Their existence has been taken to argue in favor of the role of the ambient gas in determining the FRI-II division, as opposed to purely the properties of the central engine. Indeed, the core and jet 4.9 GHz luminosities in 5 \ are 5 10^ 33 and 10^ 32 ergs/s/Hz, respectively. The magnitude of the host galaxy of 5 \ is M _R=-23.89 (Urry et al. 2000).

sources.summary: The paper finds 2007+777 has a flat-to-inverted radio core, a steep downstream jet, core-shifts aligned with the parsec-scale jet, and compact-region equipartition.

quotes.quote: In Fig. 2007spix we show the spectral index distribution of 2007+777 from 4.6--43 GHz. For this source we find a flat to inverted spectrum in the core region, falling off to a steep optically thin spectrum further downstream in the jet. The slices for the 4.6--7.9 GHz and 8.9--12.9 GHz maps (Fig. 2007spix a, b) show optically thick regions at the edge of the jet 8 mas from the core; however, these are possibly spurious features due to edge effects when combining the two maps. We only show the spectral index map from 15.4--43 GHz due to loss of flux in the 22-GHz map.
All measured core-shifts for 2007+777 are listed in Table 5. The average direction of the measured shift is 89 13^ , which is consistent with the parsec scale jet direction of 90^ .

sources.summary: The paper describes S5 2007+777 as a radio source with mixed FR I/FR II jet properties, a long deprojected jet, central black hole, redshift, and five selected knots.

quotes.quote: S5 2007+777: The jet of S5 2007+777 exhibits properties of both FR I and FR II radio galaxies Sambruna2008a . The jet has an angle of <32 to the line of sight, and the deprojected jet length significantly exceeds 150 kpc Sambruna2008b . The source harbors an SMBH of mass 2.5 10^ 7 Wu2002 . The measured redshift of its host galaxy is z = 0.342 , corresponding to a conversion scale of 1 = 4.80 kpc Sambruna2008b . We select five knots, including K3.6, K5.2, K8.5, K11.1, and K15.9.

sources.summary: The paper classifies the source as a BL Lac active galaxy with hybrid FR I/II morphology, subluminal VLBI structure, a long VLA jet, and Chandra detection along almost the entire jet.

quotes.quote: 2007 + 777 (S5 2007 + 77) This active galaxy is considered to be a BL Lac object with hybrid FR I/II morphology 2000A&A...363..507G . VLBI images show structure along a PA of -95 \ with a maximum apparent speed of 0.82 , the only jet in our sample where the fastest components are clearly subluminal lister01,vlba,2004A&A...428..847P . The VLA image shows a feature about 10 \ east of the core and a 20 \ long jet oriented at an average PA of -105 \ with some significant deviations from the average direction. The Chandra image shows that the jet is detected along almost its entire length and that the knot at the half-way point is particularly strong 2008ApJ...684..862S .

sources.summary: The paper presents a new RM map for 2007+777, showing a 5.5 sigma transverse gradient and classifying it among robust core-region gradients.

quotes.quote: 2007+777. This RM map, shown in Fig. fig:rmmaps5-1 d, has not been published previously. It shows a monotonic transverse RM gradient with a significance of 5.5 .
Monotonic transverse RM gradients with significances of at least 3 are observed across the core regions of 0355+508, 0748+126, 1156+295, 1749+096, 2007+777 and 2155 152.

gmw_00005491.name: NGC 4676B
gmw_00005491.overview: NGC 4676B is an interacting barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1405.7814, arXiv:2001.02955] featuring an amorphous disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377] with twisted stellar and gas kinematics [arXiv:1405.7814]. The galaxy hosts a possible low-luminosity active nucleus [arXiv:1405.7814] classified as a LINER [arXiv:2001.02955], which exhibits low central star formation [arXiv:1405.7814].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4676B as a strongly barred inclined disk with unusual twisted stellar and gas kinematics, low central star formation, and possible low-luminosity AGN activity.

quotes.quote: The CALIFA data reveals that the dominant rotation of both the ionised gas and stars in NGC 4676B is close to being around the major axis of the galaxy (offset between morphological and kinematic PAs of 50-60 degrees). While these Z-shaped (or S-shaped) isovelocity contours are a common feature in the gas velocity fields of strongly barred galaxies e.g. Contopoulos:1980p8403 , and weak warps can be seen in the outer regions of stellar disks Saha:2009p9231 , such strong disturbance of the inner stellar velocity field is unusual for one other example see NGC 4064, Cortes:2006p9398 . BarreraBallesteros:2014p9452 have confirmed that bars have no significant effect on stellar velocity fields in the full CALIFA sample. Studies of larger samples are needed to see whether such strong twists are common in merging galaxies (see Barrera-Ballesteros et al, in prep., for such a study within CALIFA).

Our stellar continuum decomposition of NGC 4676B shows a complete absence of young stars within 1 kpc of the nucleus (Figure fig:2dsfh ). The upper limit on the SFR surface density from the dust attenuation corrected \ luminosity is _ SFR <0.2 \ within the central 5 . It is clear that NGC 4676B is not currently undergoing or has not recently undergone a nuclear starburst. The strong bar has not yet driven significant gas into the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4676b in the southeast, models its disk orientation and bar, and finds shock-induced simulations reproduce ongoing star formation near its curved tail.

quotes.quote: The individual galaxies are NGC 4676a, to the north, and NGC 4676b, to the southeast. The length and straightness of the bright northern tail imply that we are viewing the aftermath of a nearly-direct passage roughly edge-on to the orbit and disk planes, while the curve of the fainter southern tail is consistent with a more face-on view of an inclined disk.

Comparing the model with the observations, the main discrepancy is the position angle of NGC 4676b's bar; optical images show the bar runs roughly NNE--SSW, while the bar in the model is roughly N--S or even NNW--SSE. The pattern speed of a bar depends on the details of the rotation curve, so this discrepancy might be resolved by adjusting the mass model used for NGC 4676b. It's also possible that NGC 4676b's bar predates its encounter with NGC 4676a, in which case the bar's current position angle would depend on its phase at pericenter GCA90 .

H images also show ongoing star formation at the base of the curved tail to northeast of NGC 4676b. This region has a relatively high surface brightness and is conspicuously blue in the ACS images (e.g. dG+03 ). It is nicely reproduced in the shock-induced simulations; gas from the curved tail falls back into its original disk, and the resulting shock sustains a small, coherent patch of extra-nuclear star formation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4676B as a low-S/N merging LINER in the Mice complex, finding a possible broad component but excluding it as a true BLR detection.

quotes.quote: The remaining galaxy, NGC 4676B , belongs to the Mice galaxies complex, two interacting spiral galaxies. Both spirals have been told to present features indicative of the presence of outflows Masegosa2011, Wild2014 . Its HST /STIS nuclear spectrum has not been studied before as the S/N is rather low, which makes it difficult to analyze (see Fig. Panel_NGC4676B ). Despite this, we attempted to model the emission line, being the unique measurement of this kind for this object with HST /STIS data.

Our fit has revealed the presence of a broad component for the H N [ II ] complex, while a narrow component is sufficient to fit the [ S II ] lines. Even though the ^ 2 improved with the addition of this component, the residuals are inside the 3 _ line limits also with a single narrow-modeling for all the lines. The presence of this component could be affected by the noise and the FWHM of this component is not as broad (748 km s ^ 1 ; Table T_kin ) as the other detections in our sample.

sources.summary: NGC 4676B has an easily identified nucleus in an amorphous disk, weak patchy H emission, surrounding dust, and morphology suggesting an earlier Hubble type than NGC 4676A.

quotes.quote: The nucleus is easily identified in NGC 4676B or Nuc 2. It is surrounded by a rather amorphous disk, and a dust lane that seems to wrap around the whole disk, ending near the location of the nucleus (Fig. f:n4676n2 ). There is a peak of H emission offset by about 0 2 from the nucleus of NGC 4676B, but in general the H emission is weak and patchy. The nucleus has V-I colors close to 1.3. A dust patch near the nucleus has a V-I color as red as 1.7.

The majority of the disk outside the dust lanes and dust patches has a V-I color of 1.2--1.3. The overall appearance of NGC 4676B, particularly the well-defined bulge/nucleus, is consistent with it being of an earlier Hubble-type than its interacting partner, NGC 4676A yun01 .

gmw_00005492.name: SL2SJ021801-080247
gmw_00005492.overview: SL2SJ021801-080247 is an early-type galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:2307.15044]. Located at a redshift of z = 0.884, the galaxy has an estimated stellar mass of approximately 10^11.46 solar masses [arXiv:2307.15044].

sources.summary: The paper chooses SL2SJ021801-080247 as a z=0.884 target lensing galaxy, matches simulated analogs to its stellar mass and size, and studies descendant mass-density slopes.

quotes.quote: We selected the lensing galaxy in SL2SJ021801-080247 Sonnenfeld_SL2S_2015_0884 as our target galaxy based on its highest lens redshift among these five samples, which is at z = 0.884 . The target galaxy has an estimated stellar mass of log _ 10 (M_*/M_ )=11.46 0.17 (after conversion) and a size of log _ 10 (R_ e / kpc )=0.91 0.13 . To identify analogs of the target galaxy in the simulations, we applied the following criteria: The simulated galaxies have the same stellar mass as the observed target galaxy within 1 uncertainties, that is logarithmic stellar masses in the range of 11.46 0.17 ;

We chose the strong lensing galaxy at z=0.884 in SL2SJ021801 080247 as a starting point and identify 158 simulated galaxies at the same redshift from the IllustrisTNG300 simulation as analogs of the target galaxy according to the stellar mass and size. We then followed the merger trees of the 158 simulated galaxies and successfully found, with the evolution of stellar mass and size taken into account, 11 observed strong lensing galaxies at redshifts of 0.7--0.2 that are analogs of the simulated galaxies and therefore considered descendants of the target galaxy. The total mass-density slopes of the target galaxy and its 11 descendants were estimated from strong lensing and stellar kinematics data, and the slopes of the 158 simulated galaxies at eight redshift slices from 0.1 to 0.884 were computed directly from the total mass distributions.

gmw_00005493.name: NGC 1052-DF2
gmw_00005493.overview: NGC 1052-DF2 is an ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy located in the NGC 1052 group [arXiv:2312.11350, arXiv:1804.06421]. It exhibits an extremely low velocity dispersion [arXiv:2405.09104, arXiv:2405.10462], an exceptionally luminous globular cluster population [arXiv:2312.11350, arXiv:2405.09104], and a deficiency in dark matter [arXiv:2312.11350, arXiv:1804.06421].

sources.summary: The paper models DF2 as a dark-matter-deficient UDG near NGC1052, linking its motion, globular clusters, and low dark matter to an 8 Gyr mini-bullet collision.

quotes.quote: Recent observations of an aligned trail of 7 - 11 UDGs near NGC1052, including DMDGs DF2 and DF4, suggesting a common formation site and time, 8.9 1.5 Gyr ago, provide a test.

It was notable, therefore, when vD18a, vD19 reported the existence of two ultra-diffuse galaxies (UDGs), NGC1052-DF2 and NGC1052-DF4 (hereafter DF2 and DF4, respectively), that are located in close proximity to the massive elliptical galaxy NGC1052 and exhibit a deficiency in dark matter, rather than the dark matter-dominance described above UDGs are defined to be dwarf galaxies with effective half-light radius R_ eff 1.5 kpc and surface brightness (g,0) > 24 mag arcsec ^ 2 vD15 . Subsequently, dark matter-deficient galaxies (DMDGs) have been identified across various environments and mass scales. These include the local group and isolated low-mass galaxies Guo20 , a distant low-mass galaxy Mancera-Pina22 , and even a massive early-type galaxy in a cluster environment Comeron23 . The formation model that explains DF2 and DF4's dark matter deficiency should also address their exceptionally luminous globular cluster (GC) population vD18b, vD19 .

Recently, vD22 presented a new clue that supports the ``Mini-bullet" scenario in the case of DF2 and DF4 by measuring their line-of-sight velocities. The authors conclude that both UDGs were formed from a single event that occurred about 8 Gyr ago, likely a high-velocity galaxy collision, which has been shown to be capable of producing the observed lack of dark matter and bright GC populations Shin20, Lee21 . Using the HST observation of DF2 and DF4 and a catalog of low-surface brightness galaxies in the NGC1052 group studied by Roman21 , the authors identified an alignment of 7-11 UDGs as a possible ``trail of DMDGs" see Figure 1 in vD22 . This argument is further substantiated by follow-up observation performed by the same group, which revealed that the GCs of DF2 and DF4 have the same color vD22b .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC1052-DF2 as a dark-matter-deficient ultra-diffuse galaxy whose mass profile and effective radius can be reproduced by tidal stripping in the NGC1052 group.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage Recent observations revealed a mysterious ultra diffuse galaxy, , in the group of a large elliptical galaxy, NGC1052. Compared to expectations from abundance matching models, the dark matter mass contained in is smaller by a factor of 400 . We utilize controlled N body simulations of the tidal interaction between NGC1052 and a smaller satellite galaxy, that we suppose as the progenitor of , to test if tidal stripping can explain dark-matter deficiency at such levels. We find that when assuming a tightly bound and quite radial orbit and cored density structure for the dark halo of the satellite, our simulations reproduce well both the mass profile and the effective radius inferred from the observations of . Orbital parameters are in the tail, but still consistent with measurements of their distributions from cosmological simulations.

Such strongly dark-matter deficient galaxies, in our scenario, are thus expected to be relatively rare in groups and clusters, and not present in the field. galaxies: individual: NGC1052-DF2 -- galaxies: formation -- dark matter -- methods: numerical.

sources.summary: NGC 1052-DF2 is discussed as an irregular NGC 1052 group UDG with very low velocity dispersion, bright globular clusters, possible tides, and resolved distance controversy.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052-DF2 We classify NGC 1052-DF2 as being in the NGC 1052 group. However, there is the possibility that it is no longer bound to the NGC 1052 group as a result of its formation mechanism (e.g., Shen2021, vanDokkum2022 ). NGC 1052-DF2 is irregular for a galaxy in having both an extremely low measured velocity dispersion vanDokkum2018, Danieli2019 and an excess of bright GCs beyond what is expected given the established GC luminosity function for normal galaxies vanDokkum2018b, Shen2021 . The addition of a weak rotational component, as allowed by the data, may help alleviate the paucity of dark matter suggested by its velocity dispersion Emsellem2019, Lewis2020, Montes2021 . Furthermore, it may currently be undergoing a tidal interaction ( Keim2021 , although see Montes2021, Golini2024 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1052-DF2 as the comparison UDG motivating the DF4 analysis, noting its low velocity dispersion, dark-matter controversy, and possible shared origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052-DF2 is one such claimed matter deficient UDG in the NGC 1052 group. 2018ApJ...864L..18V first reported that NGC1052-DF2 has a comparatively small velocity dispersion of about 3.2 , which is the basis for the galaxy's low mass estimate, and hence implied deficiency of dark matter. This finding prompted controversy since dark matter is thought to be essential to galaxy formation and evolution and presumed to be significant in almost all galaxies. Through a Bayesian-based analysis, 2018ApJ...859L...5M concluded that NGC 1052-DF2's inherent velocity dispersion is about 9.5 , which somewhat mitigates the shortage of dark matter.

Moreover, it is suggested by 2022Natur.605..435V that NGC 1052-DF2 and NGC 1052-DF4, the two dark matter deficient galaxies which are the focus of this study, originated in an individual event, a ``bullet dwarf'' collision, roughly eight billion years ago.

gmw_00005494.name: northern TDG-candidate of Arp 105
gmw_00005494.overview: The northern TDG-candidate of Arp 105 is a tidal dwarf candidate associated with the interacting system Arp 105 [arXiv:astro-ph/0402250, arXiv:astro-ph/0605350]. Large-scale kinematics suggest its high estimated mass may be inflated by projected foreground or background material along the line of sight [arXiv:astro-ph/0406169, arXiv:astro-ph/0605350].

sources.summary: The paper separates Arp 105 northern-tail projection effects from the southern tail, where HI, CO, stars, and HII kinematics indicate a genuine tidal-dwarf candidate.

quotes.quote: Arp 105: Two tidal tails and TDG candidates were observed in that system. Studying the orientation and internal structure of the northern tail, Duc et al. (1997) concluded that a projection effect may explain part of its properties. Indeed, the PV diagram of the HI component, integrated along the full width of the tail (see Fig. Arp105N ), shows the kinematical signature expected from simulations: a loop feature similar to case (b) in Fig. schema2 with characteristic amplitudes consistent with the values indicated in Sect. 3.1.

The PV diagram of the southern tail of Arp 105 is shown in Fig. PV105 . This tail emanates from the spiral galaxy, passes in front of the elliptical galaxy and ends in a compact blue object. The velocity gradient along the tail, traced by several HII regions, is positive from the spiral galaxy outwards and never changes its sign.

Thus, the massive condensation of HI, CO and stars, observed at the extremity of this tidal tail (Duc Mirabel 1994; Duc et al. 1997; Braine et al. 2001) cannot be caused by a projection effect, but is on the contrary a genuine object.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 105 as a spiral-elliptical interaction whose southern tidal-tail condensation appears real rather than projected, with molecular gas and local velocity gradients.

quotes.quote: We present here our results for three systems (more in Bournaud et al., 2004) where the presence of massive TDGs had previously been claimed: IC 1182, a merger in the Hercules cluster (see Fig. 2 and van Driel et al., in these proceedings), Arp 105 (NGC 3561, ``The guitar"), an interacting system between a spiral and an elliptical (see Fig. 3) and Arp 242 (NGC 4676, ``The Mice"), an interacting system between two spirals with prominent tidal tails (see Fig. 4, and Hibbard et al., in this volume).

The shapes of the position-velocity diagrams of the two other systems, Arp 105 and Arp 242 rule out projection effects: the massive condensations observed at the extremity of their tidal tails seem real. This is consistent with the detection in these TDG candidates of molecular gas probably formed in the dense HI tidal clouds (Braine et al., 2001).
Velocity gradients of typically 50--100 km/s were observed in the tails of Arp 105, IC 1182, and NGC 5291. We best explained them by the local effect of gravity.

sources.summary: The paper argues the northern Arp 105 tidal-dwarf candidate is probably too massive to be genuine, perhaps because projected foreground or background material inflated its mass estimate.

quotes.quote: For instance, the northern TDG-candidate of Arp 105 duc97 has a mass of nearly 10 percent of the parent galaxy mass ( 7 10^9 solar masses in HI, the dominant component). An object with such a mass is then unlikely to be a real tidal dwarf, unless its mass had been over-estimated due, for instance, to the contribution of projected foreground or background material along the line-of-sight. The presence of such a projection effect in the northern tail of Arp 105 is indeed suggested by the large-sale kinematics of the tail bournaud04 .

gmw_00005495.name: PG1307+085
gmw_00005495.overview: PG1307+085 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:1510.03864] hosted by a compact elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1510.03864]. It features an extended narrow-line region reaching 30 kpc [arXiv:1510.03864] and displays a south-west elongation in both optical and radio wavelengths [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540].

sources.summary: MUSE resolves PG 1307+085 as a compact elliptical QSO host, measures its stellar velocity dispersion, finds an extended narrow-line region, and links its gas to group-environment interaction.

quotes.quote: We report deep optical integral-field spectroscopy with the Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) at the Very Large Telescope of the luminous radio-quiet quasi-stellar object (QSO) PG 1307+085 ( z=0.154 ) obtained during the commissioning of the instrument. Given the high sensitivity and spatial resolution delivered by MUSE, we are able to resolve the compact ( r_ e 1.3 arcsec) elliptical host galaxy. After careful spectroscopic deblending of the QSO and host galaxy emission, we infer a stellar velocity dispersion of _*=155 19 . This places PG 1307+085 local M_ BH _* relation within the intrinsic scatter but offset towards a higher black hole mass with respect to the mean relation. The observations with MUSE also reveal a large extended narrow-line region (ENLR) around PG 1307+085 reaching out to 30 kpc.

We conclude that PG 1307+085 is a normal elliptical host in terms of the scaling relations, but that the gas is most likely affected by the environment through gravity or ambient pressure. It is possible that the ongoing interaction with the environment, mainly seen in the ionized gas, is also be responsible for driving sufficient gas to feed the black hole at the centre of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper finds compact optical and radio components plus south-west elongation in PG1307+085, possibly a radio-jet termination point associated with diffuse emission.

quotes.quote: PG1307+085 . At both (radio and optical) wavelengths, a compact component can be seen as well as an elongation to the south--west (Fig. 7). While the radio map displays a secondary component in the south--west that possibly is part (or the termination point) of a radio jet, the HST image shows diffuse emission in the same region. While these results are consistent with a radio--NLR interaction, they are by no means conclusive.

gmw_00005496.name: PG1119+120
gmw_00005496.overview: PG1119+120 is a local, high-luminosity quasar [arXiv:2302.09370] hosting an old, red stellar population and faint spiral arms [arXiv:2302.09370]. Radio observations reveal a triple structure consisting of a lobe, core, and hotspot [arXiv:2301.07929], with a bent, one-sided jet that interacts with the narrow-line region gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540].

sources.summary: The paper studies PG 1119+120 as a local high-luminosity quasar with reverberation mapping, super-Eddington accretion evidence, and a red host dominated by an old stellar population.

quotes.quote: In this work, we perform continuum and emission-line reverberation mapping of the local z=0.0502 deVaucouleurs1991 , high-luminosity quasar , first identified through the Palomar-Green survey Green1986 . Monitoring of this object was chosen as it is a potential super-Eddington accreting massive black hole Davis2011 , with an Eddington ratio 1 .
The corresponding luminosity distance at the redshift z=0.0502 of \ is 231 Mpc.

In Fig. fig:Image we show a colour composite image of PG 1119+120 using the F438W, F814W, F160W filters from Hubble Space Telescope (HST) data Shangguan2020 and g , and z band images from The Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey (DECaLS) DECALS2019 . From this image, the aperture excludes much of the star forming regions in the faint spiral arms of . This confirms the detection of an old and red stellar population from our best fitting template spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper reports that PG 1119+120 is compact in uGMRT but shows a VLA triple radio structure and spectral-index gradient consistent with jet emission.

quotes.quote: PG 1119+120 appears compact in the uGMRT image. Its VLA image detects a triple radio structure comprising of a lobe-core-hotspot in the NE-SW direction. This is consistent with the spectral index gradient detected at 6 level (based on the spectral index slices) in the 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image of its uGMRT core. The in-band VLA spectral index image of this source reveals a flat spectrum in the core. PG 1211+143 appears compact in both uGMRT and VLA images. We had concluded from our uGMRT study that the AGN rather than the stellar activity was the primary contributor of radio emission in this source Silpa20 . The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image reveals a marginally steep spectrum ( _ 2-freq = -0.59 0.07 ) uGMRT core, which could suggest either jet or lobe emission on sub-arcsec scales or optically thin synchrotron emission from an AGN-driven wind. Interestingly, the in-band VLA spectral index for the core is 0.5 0.1 which is at the cusp of flat-steep division. This could suggest that the steeper spectrum component associated with the radio outflow may have been missed out by the higher-resolution VLA observations and what is detected here is the synchrotron self-absorbed base of a low-power or `frustrated' jet. The uGMRT images of PG 1229+204 and PG 1244+026 reveal kpc-scale curved jets or lobe-like emission to the south. The VLA image of PG 1229+204 also detects extended emission in the same direction, resembling a curved jet. This extension is weak however, and should be treated with caution. The VLA image of PG 1244+026 reveals only a compact core. The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image of PG 1229+204 reveals a flat spectrum in the uGMRT core whereas its in-band VLA spectral index image reveals a steep spectrum VLA core. The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image detects a steep spectrum uGMRT core in PG 1244+026. PG 1426+015 appears compact in both the uGMRT and VLA images, and exhibits a flat spectrum uGMRT core in its 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image. The in-band VLA spectral index images of PG 1244+026 and PG 1426+015 reveal flat spectrum in their cores, which could suggest synchrotron self-absorbed bases of small-scale jets (such as on sub-arcsec scales). Within errors, the possibility of flat spectrum ( _ in-band = -0.11 0.08 ) arising from the thermal free-free emission processes from AGN or HII regions cannot be ruled out in PG 1244+026. Radio Polarization Properties of Individual Sources Out of the 11 sources, we detect marginal linear polarization in four sources, viz., PG 0050+124, PG 0923+129, PG 0934+013, and PG 1229+204; this needs confirmation with deeper observations. The fractional polarization varies between 2 and 25 in them. The polarised emission in PG 0050+124 is found to be offset from the core; this could suggest jet-medium interaction. The in-band VLA spectral index image suggests this region to be optically thin. Therefore, the inferred B-fields are perpendicular to the vectors in this region. PG 0923+129 shows two distinctly polarized regions, one in the core and the other slightly offset from the core (annotated as C1 and C2 in Figure fig:2 , top right panel, respectively). The in-band VLA spectral index image reveals the C1 component to be optically thin and the C2 component to be optically thick. Since it is difficult to establish the origin of radio emission (among jet/AGN wind/starburst wind; see Section morphology ) in this source with the current data, we cannot as yet ascertain the relationship between the B-field structures and the direction of the radio outflow. Nevertheless, the polarization structures suggest the presence of a marginally resolved bent jet on the basis of (i) the drastically varying orientation of the EVPA vectors across the polarized knots, and (ii) the suggestion of jets in quasars being threaded by poloidal magnetic fields in the literature Miller12, MehdipourCostantini19 as well as from our previous polarization studies Silpa21a, Silpa21b, Silpa22 . Interestingly, the recent VLBA study by Chen23 has revealed three discrete components in PG 0923+129 at 1.4 and 4.9 GHz. The central component exhibits a flat spectrum, suggesting core emission while the extended components reveal a steep spectrum, most likely arising from a radio outflow. The polarized knots in PG 0934+013 and PG 1229+204 are slightly offset from their radio cores. From their in-band spectral index images, the polarized region is marginally steep in PG 0934+013 and very steep in PG 1229+204, suggesting these regions to be optically thin. The inferred B-fields in PG 0934+013 are transverse to the tentative jet feature to the south. Recent studies of RQ sources with the uGMRT and VLA have reported toroidal B-fields in the kpc-scale radio cores Silpa21a, Silpa21b, Silpa22 . In PG 1229+204, the inferred B fields are parallel to the direction of the southern jet. It is worth noting that poloidal B-fields in the jet have been observed in III Zw 2 Silpa21a and Mrk 231 Silpa21b as well as in the RL PG quasars Baghel2023 . Origin of Radio Emission and Jet - Medium Interaction Based on the multi-frequency, multi-resolution radio data from the uGMRT, VLA and VLBA, we deduce that the radio emission in PG 0003+199 and PG 0050+124 is most likely associated with sub-arcsec scale jets. The radio emission in PG 0049+171, PG 1211+143, PG 1244+026 and PG 1426+015 is likely to be arising from the synchrotron self-absorbed base of a low-power or ‘frustrated' jet. Within errors, the possibility of stellar or AGN-related thermal free-free emission processes cannot be ruled out in PG1244+026. Signatures of jet emission are revealed by the uGMRT-VLA study in PG 0923+129, PG 1119+120 and PG 1229+204.

sources.summary: The paper reports three radio components in PG1119+120, including a bent one-sided jet embedded in diffuse emission, with line ratios suggesting jet interaction with NLR gas.

quotes.quote: PG1119+120 . Three different components are visible in this source: A compact component in the south--west and at the centre as well as a one--sided jet to the north--east (Fig. 9). This jet is nearly two arcseconds long before it sharply bends to the north--west. The whole structure is embedded in a more diffuse emission, especially to the north of the south--western blob (Fig. 9, right). Leipski Bennert ( ) have shown that extended [ O iii ] emission exists in this source and that the emission--line ratios suggest an interaction of the radio jet with the NLR gas.

sources.summary: The paper flags Mrk 734 as a narrow-line outlier with one of the smallest [OIII] widths and a comparatively large black-hole mass; excluding it strengthens the fitted MBH relation.

quotes.quote: As seen in Figures  fig:msigma_mf and fig:msigma_geb , three objects, Mrk 382, Mrk 734 and PG 1448+273, which have the smallest [ OIII ] widths, deviate from the other NL objects. The reason that they have somewhat larger MBHs than others is not known. However, if excluding them, the correlation becomes even stronger with R_ s =0.762 ( P_s=2.0 10^ 15 ), which can be fitted (using OLS bisector) by M_ BH = 10^ (7.81 0.006) M_ FWHM _ OIII /2.35 200 km s ^ 1 ^ 3.70 0.51 , as shown by the dotted-long dashed line in the right panel of Figures  fig:msigma_mf and fig:msigma_geb .

gmw_00005497.name: PG1149 110
gmw_00005497.overview: PG1149 110 is a quasar that exhibits a triple radio structure extending 1.5 arcseconds in an east-west direction [arXiv:2308.16780, arXiv:2210.16764]. High-resolution observations reveal a compact radio core near the optical nucleus [arXiv:2308.16780, arXiv:2210.16764] and a steep-spectrum, jet-like extension pointing to the northwest [arXiv:2308.16780].

sources.summary: PG 1149-110 shows a compact VLBI radio core plus a northwest jet-like extension, steep-spectrum compact emission, and significant core variability.

quotes.quote: PG 1149 110 High-resolution ( 0.44 0.33 ) 5 GHz VLA images show a triple structure extending 1 5 along the east-west direction 2006A&A...455..161L . In other higher-frequency VLA images, only the central component is detected 1998MNRAS.297..366K,2022MNRAS.510.1043B , and the spectral index of the central VLA core is = -0.65 . In previously published VLBI images 2022ApJ...936...73A,2023MNRAS.518...39W , PG 1149-110 shows an unresolved component with a flat spectrum of ^ 4.8 _ 1.4 = -0.30 0.11 close to the optical nucleus and can be identified as the radio core (jet base).

Our new 4.7-GHz VLBA image (Figure fig:1149 b ) clearly shows a core and a jet-like extension to the northwest. The jet-like feature is not detected in the 6.2 and 7.6 GHz images, indicating that it has a steep spectrum ^ 7.6 _ 4.7 < -1.83 . The compact unresolved component has a steep spectral index of ^ 7.6 _ 1.6 = -0.74 0.03 (Fig. fig:1149 e ) and a brightness temperature of up to 10^8 K.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1149 110 as a source with double-to-triple VLA structure, a higher-frequency unresolved nuclear component, and a VLBA core near the optical nucleus.

quotes.quote: PG 1149 110 (LEDA 37161) was imaged by the VLA in A-array (resolution of 0.5 ) and exhibited double components along the east-west direction 1993MNRAS.263..425M,1994AJ....108.1163K . At higher resolution (0.44 0.33 ) and sensitivity at 5 GHz, the source showed a triple structure extending 1.5 \ in the east-west direction 2006A&A...455..161L . However, at higher frequencies of 85 and 45 GHz, the VLA A-array only detected an unresolved component close to the optical nucleus, with a spectral index of 0.65 1998MNRAS.297..366K,2022MNRAS.510.1043B . In our VLBA image and the image obtained by 2022arXiv220801488A , PG 1149 110 shows an unresolved component close to the optical nucleus with a flux density of 0.32 mJy.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG1149 110 as an east-west triple radio source and notes that earlier studies detected only an unresolved core, missing the additional components.

quotes.quote: PG1149 110 . A triple radio source extending 1.^ 5 in roughly east--west direction (Fig. 10, left). Although the western component seems to become steadily weaker it has a peak that can be seen with different contour spacings. Neither Kellermann et al. ( ) nor Kukula et al. ( ) were able to detect any components in addition to an unresolved core.

gmw_00005498.name: PG1612+261
gmw_00005498.overview: PG 1612+261 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540, arXiv:2208.01488] hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.11631] with a luminous extended narrow-line region [arXiv:1210.0566]. The galaxy exhibits large-scale gas rotation [arXiv:1210.0566] and features a radio jet structure [arXiv:2308.16780, arXiv:2210.16764] that displays parsec-scale emission variability [arXiv:2308.16780].

sources.summary: PG 1612+261 has a triple VLA radio structure and faint parsec-scale VLBI components; recent variability supports jet emission rather than a wind.

quotes.quote: PG 1612+261 (Ton 256, z=0.131 ) shows a triple structure in the VLA images at 5 and 8.4 GHz aligned along the northeast-southwest direction 1993MNRAS.263..425M,1998MNRAS.297..366K,2000ApJ...530..704K,2006A&A...455..161L , with the eastern jet being brighter but shorter than the western jet. The total flux density of PG 1612+261 has a steep spectrum between 5 and 8.5 GHz with ^ 8.5 _ 5.0 = -1.57 , indicating that the VLA core could be dominated by optically thin emission 2019MNRAS.482.5513L . In the 5-GHz VLBA image of 2023MNRAS.518...39W , PG 1612+261 shows a faint compact source with a peak flux density of 0.17 mJy.

Due to the weakness of PG 1612+261 at 5 GHz, in the new VLBA observations, we only detected it at 1.6 GHz. Figure fig:1612 a displays our 1.6 GHz VLBA image, showing two components. From the comparison of the 1.6 and 5 GHz images, we can infer that the west component corresponds to the radio core, with an estimated spectral index of ^ 4.9 _ 1.6 =-0.28 0.03 (Fig. fig:1612 b ). Recent VLBI observations ( 2022ApJ...936...73A,2023MNRAS.518...39W and the present paper) show the presence of variability in the parsec-scale emission structure within a few years, supporting that the radio emission comes from the jet rather than a wind.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1612 + 261 as a QSO with a luminous ENLR, disc-like large-scale gas rotation, and non-gravitational [O III] motions near a radio hot spot.

quotes.quote: The ENLR apparently reaches beyond the PMAS FoV in three cases, HE 2158 0107, Mrk 1014, and PG 1612 + 261, for which we set lower limits to their ENLR sizes.

EELRs dominated by rotational motion We find signatures of large scale rotational motion around I Zw 1, PG 0052 + 251, PKS 1545 + 21, PG 1612 + 261, HE 2158 0107 and HE 2353 0420. The corresponding gas velocity fields are shown in Fig. fig_pmas:EELR_kin_rot . Because the turn-over point in the velocity curve is not reached in most cases, we modelled the synthetic longslit curves with a straight line ( v_r = v_r +v_0 ) to search for the major kinematic axis with the steepest velocity gradient v_r among all 90 < PA <90 in steps of 3 . The velocity curves along this kinematic major axis are also shown in Fig. fig_pmas:EELR_kin_rot and the corresponding parameters are given in Table tab_pmas:kin_axis_rot . The majority of these systems (4/6) are morphological disc-dominated galaxies for which the kinematic major axis is well aligned with the photometric major axis within 14 (rms).

We detect significantly asymmetric [ O iii ] \ line profiles close to the nucleus of Mrk 1014 and PG 1612 + 261 right at the location of their known radio hot spots (Fig. fig_pmas:EELR_outflows ). The line width and radial velocity offset are 508 84 km s ^ 1 and 1012 48 km s ^ 1 for Mrk 1014 and 424 24 km s ^ 1 (FWHM) and 220 20 km s ^ 1 for PG 1612 + 261 at region A, respectively, indicating clearly non-gravitational motions.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG1612+261 as having a one-sided south-west radio jet with three peaks, a high R value for a radio-quiet quasar, and no added B-array structure.

quotes.quote: PG1612+261 . An one--sided jet heads 1.^ 5 towards the south--west (Fig. 11, left). As in the case of , three peaks can be identified in this source with adequate contour spacings. While one peak lies very close in the north--east of the central peak, another peak appears at the tip of the jet. Nevertheless, this source holds its one--sidedness.

The R value of PG1612+261 is remarkably high (2.81, Kellermann et al. 1989) for a radio--quiet quasar and comparison with flux values given by Kellermann et al. ( ) indicates variability. On an additional B--Array map there is no further structure detectable and only 14 of the B--Array flux is missed by the A--Array observations.

sources.summary: The paper reports [NeV] 3347 and 3427 emission in J 1614, with extended CL emission out to 5.9 and 8.3 kpc; its [NeV] status places it among MaNGA AGNs.

quotes.quote: We measure [ NeV ] 3427 emission in six galaxies from MPL-8: J 1614 , J 1714 , J 0736 , J 0752 , J 2051 , and J 2116 . In two of these galaxies, J 1614 and J 1714 , we also measure [ NeV ] 3347 emission. We present [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 flux maps for J 1614 ( z = 0.13; no GZ2 classification) and J 1714 ( z = 0.09; elliptical) in Figure nevmaps . For J 1614 , we identify extended [ NeV ] 3347 emission out to 5.9 2.9 kpc from the galactic center; 8.3 3.9 kpc for [ NeV ] 3427 .

We cross-match these galaxies with the largest existing MaNGA AGN catalog (``MaNGA AGN catalog''; 2020arXiv200811210C ; AGN classifications determined using WISE mid-infrared color cuts, Swift /BAT hard X-ray observations, NVSS/ FIRST 1.4 GHz radio observations, and SDSS broad emission lines; Section tab:mangaagn ), and determine that all [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 galaxies are classified as AGNs.

sources.summary: PG 1612+261 is detected only at 1.4 GHz in VLBA data; its high-Eddington radio emission is interpreted as diffuse, steep-spectrum emission produced mainly by an outflow.

quotes.quote: Since PG 1612 + 261 and PG 0050 + 124 were only detected at 1.4 GHz according these criteria, we examined their 4.8 GHz images for emission spatially coincident with that observed at the lower frequency. No such emission was detected in the 4.8 GHz image of PG 1612+261 (Fig. \ figure:1 ), but a 4-5 excess was detected in the 4.8 GHz image of PG 0050+124 (Fig. \ multi_comp ). While this does not the satisfy the detection criteria described above, the statistical significance of this spatial coincidence suggests it is 4.8 GHz emission from this RQQ, and is treated as such below.

However, all of the high Eddington ratio PQ RQQs undetected in our 4.8 GHz VLBA observations (PG 1440+356, PG 1612+261, and PG 2130+099) were detected with a nearly constant flux densities in multiple 4.8 GHz VLA observations (Table Extended Table 1 ), suggesting their radio emission primarily originates from a large region and therefore produced by an outflow.

Finally, the diffuse nature (low 1.4 GHz compactness) and steep radio spectra ( 1 ) of the nuclear emission of high Eddington ratio RQQ PG 1612+261 and the first component in high Eddington RQQ PG 0050+124 (PG 0050+124-C1; Fig. \ multi_comp ) suggesting they are both produced by outflows.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1612+261/Ton 256 as an extended radio quasar with one-sided VLA jet structure, steep spectrum, 1.4-GHz VLBA elongation, and a weak 5-GHz nuclear component.

quotes.quote: PG 1612+261 (Ton 256) was identified as an 'extended' source with a one-sided jet toward the east-west in VLA images 1993MNRAS.263..425M,1989AJ.....98.1195K,2006A&A...455..161L . The brighter component (the VLA core) was resolved into double sources within 0.5 \ along with some more diffuse emission toward the west 1998MNRAS.297..366K . Overall, PG 1612+261 has a steep spectrum of =-1.57 between 5 and 8.5 GHz for the unresolved VLA core. PG 1612+261 is clearly detected in the 1.4-GHz VLBA image of 2022arXiv220801488A , showing an elongated structure in the east-west direction, but is not detected in their 5-GHz VLBA image. In our 5-GHz VLBA image, a 6 component is detected at the location of the optical nucleus of PG 1612+261.

gmw_00005499.name: Mrk 612
gmw_00005499.overview: The active galaxy Mrk 612 contains four distinct radio components [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540]. Three of these components are aligned in a north-south direction, while the northernmost structure is interpreted as a transverse shock [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540].

sources.summary: The paper identifies four radio components in Mrk 612, three aligned north-south and one west of the brightest source, with the northern structure interpreted as a transverse shock.

quotes.quote: Mrk 612 . Four different components can be identified in this source (Fig. mrk612 , left). Three of them are aligned in north--south direction over 2 ^ , whereas the fourth one lies closely to the west of the brightest source. Nagar et al. ( ) identified the most southern component to be the central one. Since the northern structure is elongated perpendicular to the source axis they interpreted this as a transverse shock.

gmw_00005500.name: CASSOWARY 2
gmw_00005500.overview: CASSOWARY 2 is a group-scale strong-lensing system featuring two massive, bright luminous red galaxies acting as lenses [arXiv:0804.3743, arXiv:1207.4489]. The lensed background source, also known as SDSS J1038+4849, is a z=2.20 galaxy merger with complex kinematics and an inverted metallicity gradient [arXiv:1207.4489].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1038+4849 as a lensed z=2.20 merger with northern and southern components, complex kinematics, and an inverted metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1038+4849 (hereafter J1038) is a group-scale lensing system discovered in the CASSOWARY survey for wide-separation gravitational lenses in SDSS imaging Belokurov09 . We obtained a near-infrared spectrum of the z=2.20 arc using NIRSPEC on Keck which showed bright nebular emission lines suitable for resolved spectroscopy with OSIRIS. We show in sec:kinematics that the source is a merger of at least two systems with stellar mass ratio of (6 3):1. Therefore in much of the analysis we consider the two components separately.

The fourth, J1038, is comprised of multiple spatially and kinematically distinct regions indicating that this source is undergoing a merger. The merging system J1038 has significantly lower nuclear metallicity than the rotating galaxies, and exhibits a more complex metallicity distribution. The merging galaxy J1038 has an ``inverted'' gradient ( >0 dex/kpc) - a phenomenon which has also been observed in local mergers Rich12 and high-redshift sources Cresci10, Queyrel12 .

sources.summary: The paper describes CASSOWARY 2 as a strong-lensing system with two massive LRG lenses in a loose group and notes its relevance to binary-galaxy lens models with external shear.

quotes.quote: For example, the lens CASSOWARY 2 has two very bright and massive LRGs at redshifts of z = 0.426 and 0.432 respectively acting as lenses for a blue star-forming galaxy at z=0.970 . Although the LRGs lie in a loose group, a natural starting point is that the lens is a close pair of galaxies.
First, Binary galaxies may be embedded in a loose group or cluster (as in fact is the case for CASSOWARY 2), in which case tidal shear will distort the caustics and critical curves.
Note that the elevenfold imaging configuration bears a remarkable similarity to the recently discovered lens CASSOWARY 2, although of course this is not a fit.

gmw_00005501.name: CASSOWARY 5
gmw_00005501.overview: CASSOWARY 5 is a gravitational lensing system featuring two early-type lensing galaxies [arXiv:0804.3743]. These two galaxies are separated by approximately 5 arcseconds on the sky and have three visible images of a high-redshift source straddling them [arXiv:0804.3743].

sources.summary: The paper describes CASSOWARY 5 as a two-galaxy strong lens separated by about 5 arcsec, matching the binary-isothermal image configuration 3B-1 and potentially needing deeper imaging.

quotes.quote: CASSOWARY 5 has two early-type galaxies separated by 5^ on the sky acting as lenses. The three images of the high redshift source visible in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey data straddle the two lenses.

A good example is provided by CASSOWARY 5, which has two lensing galaxies separated by 5^   Be08 . The three images straddle the two lensing galaxies, much as the illustration in the lower panel of Fig.  fig:BE-BCP-FS_3ims . CASSOWARY 5 looks like an example of our image configuration 3B-1, though it important to bear in mind that the existing, primarily Sloan Digital Sky Survey, data is comparatively shallow ( 23 ). Deeper follow-up imaging may revel the presence of more images in CASSOWARY 5.

gmw_00005502.name: NGC 6621
gmw_00005502.overview: NGC 6621 is the northern component of an interacting galaxy merger [arXiv:1110.3046, arXiv:astro-ph/0309377] that features a starburst nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377]. Its dusty central region contains patchy star clusters, ionized gas, and a large offset reservoir of cold molecular gas [arXiv:1110.3046, arXiv:astro-ph/0309377].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6621 as an early merger whose unequal components show sharply different mid-infrared emission, with overlap-region emission and offset cold molecular gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: NGC 6621 The two merging components of NGC 6621 have a projected separation of 41 ( 17 kpc). Unlike the two previous discussed major mergers, NGC 4676 and NGC 7592, which have a NIR light ratio of 1:1, the two components of this system have a ratio of 1:2. However, this ratio changes dramatically in the light of different wavelengths. The mid-IR light of the smaller component, as given by our IRS continuum measurement, is at least two orders smaller than the larger component (nearly all of the mid-IR emission in NGC 6621 is coming from only one of the two nuclei).

multi_NGC6621 , we find no significant FUV, H _2 , CO, and HI emission at the position of the smaller galaxy, and only very faint emission from PAH features. Instead, we find significant emission originating from a peak in the overlap region between the two merging galaxies.

The distribution of the cold molecular gas, as observed in the CO emission line Ion05 , is not co-spatial to the stellar (NIR), warm gas (H _2 ) and PAH distribution: besides the CO emission peak of the main galaxy, we find an additional CO concentration ( 30 of the total CO emission) between the main galaxy and the overlap region as seen in the FUV and NIR light, and mid-IR features. This significant offset suggests a large reservoir of cold molecular gas with no signs of star formation in the mid-IR features.

sources.summary: NGC 6621 is the northern interacting component with dusty central structure, star clusters, ionized gas, H emission along ridges and patches, and a starburst nuclear spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 6621 and NGC 6622 The northern component of this interacting galaxy pair, NGC 6621, has a dusty central region punctuated by patchy star clusters and ionized gas emission (Fig. f:n6621 ). Two major dust lanes lead into the central region. One of them comes in from the northwest, following the long tidal tail at larger radii. The other dust lane intersects the nuclear region from the north, cutting a bright ridge of emission regions into two parts.

H emission is associated with the bright ridge of emission regions seen in the V and I images, and also with another emission patch across the dust lane coming in from the northwest. The emission ridge has V-I colors around 1.0, but in the surrounding dusty area the V-I colors are as red as 2.
