The southwestern emission region across the dust lane from the emission ridge has a blue V-I color of 0.5. The nuclear spectrum of NGC 6621 has been classified to be of starburst type vlx95 .

gmw_00005503.name: J0746+4526
gmw_00005503.overview: J0746+4526 is a red double-double radio quasar [arXiv:1405.1869]. It exhibits an obscured optical spectrum with weaker hydrogen Balmer lines and a lower broad-line velocity dispersion than the quasar J0935+0204 [arXiv:1405.1869].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0746+4526 as a red double-double radio quasar, with a red obscured spectrum, weaker Balmer lines, and lower broad-line velocity dispersion than J0935+0204.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report the discovery of a red DDRQ J0746+4526, discuss some of its properties and also compare these with J0935 + 0204.

The Galactic extinction and redshift corrected optical spectrum of J0746+4526 is presented in Figure spec_com , along with the spectrum of J0935+0204. The spectrum of J0935+0204 is similar to the typical spectra of normal quasars, while the spectrum of J0746+4526 is typical of red quasars. For both quasars, the \ lines at 4960 \ and 5008 \ associated with the narrow-line-region (NLR) clouds, are quite prominent, while there is a striking difference in the strength of the hydrogen Balmer lines which are weaker in J0746+4526.

For J0746+4526, FWHM of 2800 \ is 35.22 , whereas for J0935+0204 it is about 61.33 \ 2012MNRAS.422.1546K . These demonstrate that the velocity dispersions of several broad-line-region (BLR) clouds are much higher for J0935 + 0204 in comparison to that for J0746+4526 and further indicate that the former galaxy hosts a higher mass central black hole than the latter one.

gmw_00005504.name: SDSS J1553+0056
gmw_00005504.overview: SDSS J1553+0056 is a low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0502207]. It features weak emission lines and narrow absorption troughs, giving its low-resolution spectrum a resemblance to high-redshift starburst galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0502207].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1553+0056 as a LoBAL quasar rather than an LBG, with broad absorption troughs and weak emission lines resembling starburst galaxies.

quotes.quote: As shown by Fig. completespectrum the spectrum of SDSS J1553+0056 is dominated by broad absorption troughs of resonance lines and blends. The shapes (see Fig. completespectrum ) and widths (see Table 1) of these lines are clearly reminiscent of the spectra of the Broad Absorption Line (BAL) quasars see e.g. 1991ApJ...373...23W,2002ApJS..141..267H,2003AJ....126...53B,2003AJ....125.1711R . But apart from Ly the spectrum of SDSS J1553+0056 shows no strong emission lines. Absorptions troughs corresponding to low-ionization resonance lines are prominent. Thus, SDSS J1553+0056 obviously belongs to the LoBAL sub-class of the BALQSOs as defined by 1993ApJ...413...95V .

Our high-resolution spectroscopic observations of SDSS J1553+0056 have shown that this object is not an LBG but instead a LoBALQSO that probably belongs to the FeLoBALQSO class. Compared to other BALQSOs it has weak emission lines, relatively narrow absorption troughs; and it shows (relative to other FeLoBALQSOs) little evidence of dust reddening. Because of these properties the low-resolution spectrum of SDSS J1553+0056 resembles those of high-redshift starburst galaxies.

gmw_00005505.name: JO49
gmw_00005505.overview: JO49 is a galaxy undergoing advanced ram pressure stripping, which has produced unwinding tails [arXiv:2208.11955, arXiv:2111.02538]. The galaxy hosts a central LINER-like nucleus [arXiv:2208.11955, arXiv:2111.02538] and exhibits a deficiency in its overall star formation rate [arXiv:2208.11955].

sources.summary: The paper presents JO49 as a GASP jellyfish galaxy with advanced stripping, a LINER-like nucleus, similarly truncated radio and H emission, and leading-edge radio-devoid regions.

quotes.quote: For two galaxies of our sample, JW100 and JO49, the LOFAR images have been already presented in Ignesti_2020b,Ignesti_2021 and Roberts_2021a respectively.

According to the SFR-stellar mass relation Vulcani_2018c , half of the sample (JW100, JW39 and JO49) shows a deficiency in SFR that indicates an advanced stage of stripping. Finally, the sample also presents a high incidence of AGN with 5 out of 6 galaxies showing nuclear activity according to the Baldwin, Phillips Terlevich diagnostic diagrams BPT, Baldwin1981 : JW39, JO85, JO49 Peluso_2021 host LINER-like nuclei, while JW100 and JO206 have a Seyfert nucleus Poggianti2017b, Radovich2019 .

Figure radio-halpha shows galaxies in which radio and H emission are similarly truncated with respect to the stellar disk (JW39 and JO49), or where radio emission appears to be more truncated than H (JW100 and JO206), or where only radio is truncated whilst H closely follows the stellar disk (JO60). JW39, JO49 and JO206 show radio-devoid regions located on their leading edge, that become more evident in the smoothed images (Figure smooth ).

sources.summary: JO49's tail is classified as star-formation dominated in the main diagnostics, has a strong OI-diagram SF component, but only about 1-2% of total SFR lies in the tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

Considering instead only the pure SF component (no Composite) in the NII DD, this dominates ( 80 ) the emission in the tails of 9 of our galaxies (JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO201, JO206, JO49, JO95, JW39). Four of our galaxies have a particularly strong SF component according to the OI DD ( 40-60 in JO206, JO49, JW39 and JO194). In another 5 galaxies the tail SFR is about 4-5 of the total (JO60, JO147, JO175, JO95, JO194), while for the remaining 5 galaxies it is at the 1-2 level (JO113, JO141, JO135, JO49, JO160).

sources.summary: The paper describes JO49 as an unwinding ram-pressure-stripped galaxy with central LINER-like/composite ionization and an XMM-Newton X-ray source.

quotes.quote: JO49 has unwinding tails due to RPS bellhouse+2021 . It presents a central LINER-like region surrounded by a thin Composite-like ring, which in the BPT diagram correspond to a long finger of points encompassing the Composite region extending well beyond the K01's line. We note that JO49 hosts also an X-ray source of luminosity 1.2 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 , detected by XMM-Newton ( webb+2020 The 4XMM-DR10 catalog contains source detections covering an energy interval from 0.2 keV to 12 keV. On the other hand, the Chandra energy range goes from 0.5 to 7 keV. ).

gmw_00005506.name: JO147
gmw_00005506.overview: JO147 is an inclined, highly extincted disk galaxy undergoing ram pressure stripping in the north-west direction [arXiv:2111.02538]. The galaxy features opposite LINER-like cones [arXiv:2111.02538], and its stripped tail is dominated by composite and LINER-like emission where approximately 4% to 5% of its total star formation occurs [arXiv:1811.00823].

sources.summary: JO147's tail is described as warm and dominated by LINER/composite-like emission in OI/NII estimates, with a 10% LINER component and about 4-5% of total SFR occurring in the tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147). The [ OI ] LINER-like emission dominates the tails of JO147 and JW100. It is interesting that the two galaxies with the ``warmest'' tails, JO147 and JW100 (and to a less extent JO201), are those for which the OI DD finds a strong LINER component.

SF+Composite emission accounts for at least 98 of the tail emission in all galaxies, except in JO135 and JO204 that, as discussed above, have AGN ionization cones extending in the tail, and in JO147 where there is a 10 LINER component. Finally, as previously mentioned, JO147 and JW100 have tails dominated by a Composite emission, also in the diffuse component. In addition to JW100 and JO147, whose NII and OI estimates agree remarkably well indicating that the tail emission is dominated by Composite/''LINER-like'' processes, in all the other galaxies the OI fraction of tail diffuse emission powered by SF ranges from 2 to 63 , with a median of 18 .
In another 5 galaxies the tail SFR is about 4-5 of the total (JO60, JO147, JO175, JO95, JO194), while for the remaining 5 galaxies it is at the 1-2 level (JO113, JO141, JO135, JO49, JO160).

sources.summary: The paper describes JO147 as an inclined, highly extincted ram-pressure-stripped disk with LINER-like cones and an XMM-Newton X-ray luminosity measurement.

quotes.quote: JO147 (first described by merluzzi+2013 , see also poggianti+2019 ) is an inclined highly-extincted disk, and is stripped in the north-west direction. We find that it has LINER-like opposite cones embedded in wider Composite cones. Its luminosity in the X-ray band observed by XMM-Newton is 2.4 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 webb+2020 .

gmw_00005507.name: FRB 190523 host
gmw_00005507.overview: The FRB 190523 host is a massive galaxy [arXiv:2005.02371]. The fast radio burst FRB 190523 is located at an estimated offset of 3.7 arcseconds, or approximately 26.5 kiloparsecs, from the host galaxy's center [arXiv:2005.02371].

sources.summary: The paper places the FRB 190523 host among massive FRB hosts, estimates a large host offset, and finds it more consistent with SN Ibc, SN II, or SN Ia hosts.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: host coordinates from the PanSTARRS, stack RA 13:48:15.426 (207.06427 degree), DEC + 72:28:14.6 (72.47072 degree)

quotes.quote: For example, FRB 180924, FRB 190523 and FRB 180916.J0158+65 are located in massive galaxies.

We estimate the offset of FRB 190523 with the coordinates (J2000) of the FRB, right ascension (RA) 13:48:15.6(2), declination (DEC) + 72:28:11(2), and the host coordinates from the PanSTARRS, stack RA 13:48:15.426 (207.06427 degree), DEC + 72:28:14.6 (72.47072 degree). The offset is estimated to be 3.7^ +2.2 _ 2.1 arcsec, corresponding to 26.5^ +15.5 _ 14.9 kpc for its redshift.
FRB 190523 also disfavors a LGRB/SLSN origin but is consistent with the SN Ibc, SN II or SN Ia origins.

gmw_00005508.name: FRB 180916.J0158+65 host
gmw_00005508.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 180916.J0158+65 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2001.02222] with a gas-rich, near-quiescent star-forming disk [arXiv:2201.07271]. It exhibits disturbed neutral hydrogen tails and a distorted velocity field, suggesting a recent minor merger with a companion galaxy or gas cloud [arXiv:2201.07271].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J015800.28+654253.0 as the significant host galaxy of the repeating FRB source, describing it as a nearby, face-on star-forming spiral with solar-neighborhood metallicity.

quotes.quote: The precise EVN position shows that is spatially coincident with a galaxy catalogued in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey as SDSS J015800.28+654253.0. Given the maximum expected redshift of approximately 0.11, as inferred by the measured DM (Methods), we find that the probability for chance coincidence is less than 1 for any type of galaxy with mass greater than 40 that of the FRB 121102 host (Methods). Moreover, we thus conclude that the association of to this particular galaxy is significant.

Deep optical imaging reveals that the host is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy (Figure fig:galaxy ) with a total stellar mass of approximately 10^ 10 times that of the Sun (Methods), which is comparable to the total stellar mass of the Milky Way. No other comparably large and bright galaxy is visible in the broader field covered by Gemini-North (Extended Data Figure fig:galaxy-wide-FoV ). The milli-arcsecond precision of the EVN localisation shows that the FRB source is close to a bright feature in ^ band approximately 7 arcsec (projected separation of roughly 4.7 kpc) from the core of the host galaxy.

From the , N_ II , and S_ II line fluxes, we estimate the metallicity of the galaxy as 12 + ( O / H ) = 8.82 , close to solar neighborhood metallicity, and five times higher when compared to the host of FRB 121102 ( <8.1 on the same metallicity scale ). Chance alignment probability with host galaxy. The projected position of is coincident with a star-forming clump in the spiral galaxy SDSS J015800.28+654253.0.

sources.summary: SDSS J0158+6542 is the FRB 20180916B host, a gas-rich near-quiescent spiral with high H I mass, disturbed H I tails, companions, and evidence for a recent minor merger.

quotes.quote: Radio interferometric observations of FRB 20180916B have shown that the source is located in the outskirts of a spiral galaxy, SDSS J015800.28+654253.0 (hereafter, SDSS J0158+6542), at z = 0.03399 Marcote2020 . While no optical continuum or H emission is detected at the FRB location, the FRB lies 250 pc from the brightest pixel of a region of active star-formation Marcote2020,Tendulkar2021 . The H emission from the FRB host is well fit by a thin rotating disk model (with an inclination of 33^ ), as expected for a spiral galaxy Tendulkar2021 . We have used the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) to carry out a deep \ study of SDSS J0158+6542, aiming to map its \ emission and determine its \ properties.

This implies an \ mass of M_ H i = (2.74 0.33) 10^9 M_ . The star-formation rate (SFR) and stellar mass of SDSS J0158+6542 are (0.06 0.02) yr ^ 1 and M_ = (2.15 0.33) 10^9 M_ , respectively Heintz2020 ; this gives a specific star-formation rate (sSFR) of (0.028 0.010) 10^ 9 per year. Combining the stellar mass and the H i mass, we obtain an H i to-stellar mass ratio of f_ H i M_ H i /M_ 1.3 .

Taken together, our results suggest that SDSS J0158+6542 has recently undergone a minor merger with a gas cloud or a galaxy, that resulted in an increase in its gas content, disturbed the H i in the galaxy disk, compressed the H i near the FRB location, and yielded a distorted velocity field.

sources.summary: The paper describes the FRB 180916.J0158+65 host as massive, estimates its SFR, metallicity and half-light radius, and links it to older transient host classes.

quotes.quote: For example, FRB 180924, FRB 190523 and FRB 180916.J0158+65 are located in massive galaxies. For FRB 180916.J0158+65, the global SFR is scaled from that in the FRB position as SFR =0.016*6.57/1.002=0.1 M_ \ yr ^ 1 . The metallicity of FRB 180916.J0158+65 is estimated based on .

The half light radius R_ 50 of FRB 180916.J0158+65 is not available from the paper. We estimate R_ 50 with the Petrosian half-light radius R_ 50, petro =4.66" and the 90 radius as R_ 90, petro =8.53" from SDSS catalog, using the formula R_ 50 =R_ 50, petro /(1-8 10^ 6 (R_ 90, petro /R_ 50, petro )^ 8.47 )=4.7" 2005AJ....130.1535G . This gives a physical distance of 3.3 kpc.
FRB 180916.J0158+65 has a very low probability to belong to the LGRB or SLSN samples, but is consistent with being belong to either of the SN Ibc, SN II, SN Ia or SGRB samples.

sources.summary: The paper observes the FRB 180916 host galaxy during a burst window, adopts its redshift-derived distance, and discusses host extinction, optical enhancement, and local star-forming-region environment.

quotes.quote: At the time of the burst and for 30 minutes thereafter, we observed the FRB 180916 host galaxy with the APO telescope, but we did not detect any transient optical emission at the FRB site. Based on the non-detections, we place constraints on the allowed burst properties and circumburst density in the synchrotron maser model.

We assume the redshift z=0.0337 0.0002 derived for the host galaxy of FRB 180916 in Marcote20 along with Planck15 cosmology, from which we derive a luminosity distance of D_ L =153 1 Mpc or a distance modulus of =35.92 0.02 mag.

The interstellar burst host extinction could be dominated by dust with no indication in the radio signal, and indeed, FRB 180916 appears coincident with a small enhancement in the optical emission from its host galaxy Marcote20,Tendulkar20 . This finding suggests that FRB 180916 is located within or near a star-forming region it is 250 pc from a young stellar clump based on a H detection in Hubble Space Telescope imaging; Tendulkar20 where there could be excess gas and dust obscuring the optical counterpart similar to, e.g., stripped-envelope SNe, which evolve from very massive, young stars and thus are observed with high average interstellar host extinction near their birth environments; Stritzinger17 . A constraint on the total extinction in the FRB 180916 host galaxy comes from the DM observed toward this source, which is known to be on average 350 pc cm ^ 3 and 352.6 3.2 pc cm ^ 3 for the 3 September 2020 burst.

gmw_00005509.name: FCC 153
gmw_00005509.overview: FCC 153 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy located in the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1904.01260, arXiv:2104.06412]. It features a fast-rotating stellar disk [arXiv:1105.4809], a thick disk with flaring outer regions [arXiv:1904.01260], and a metal-rich nuclear star cluster [arXiv:2104.06412].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes FCC 153 as an edge-on Fornax S0 with old thick disks, younger thin-disk and nuclear populations, flaring, and distinct stellar populations.

quotes.quote: We used deep MUSE observations to perform a stellar-kinematic and population analysis of FCC 153 and FCC 177, two edge-on S0 galaxies in the Fornax cluster. The geometrical definition of the different structural components of these two galaxies allows us to describe the nature of their thick disks. These are both old, relatively metal poor and [ Mg/Fe ] enhanced, and their star formation history (SFH) reveals a minor younger component whose chemical properties suggest its later accretion. Moreover, the outer regions of these geometrically defined thick disks show higher values of metallicity and lower values of [ Mg/Fe ] . These stars probably formed in the thin-disk region and they were dynamically heated to form the flares present in these two galaxies.

We have measured ages down to 4 Gyr in FCC 153, and down to 2 Gyr in FCC 177 in their nuclear regions (hereafter "nuclei"), which include not only the pure stellar populations of the NSC but all components observed in the line of sight. On the other hand, their thin disks appear approximately coeval with one another ( 4-5 Gyr old in their youngest regions).

sources.summary: The paper identifies FCC 153 as a lower-mass edge-on S0 whose NSC is older than the immediate surroundings, highly metal-rich, and likely built substantially by central star formation.

quotes.quote: Lower-mass edge-on S0 galaxies FCC 153 and FCC 177 are edge-on S0 galaxies like FCC 170, but at significantly lower galaxy mass (see e.g. Pinna2019b, Poci2021 for a detailed analysis using the F3D MUSE data). and the Voronoi binned radial profiles presented in this work generally find young ages toward the galaxy centres. However, our background-subtracted NSC spectra find the NSCs to be older than their immediate surrounding although at a similar high metallicity, but with a minor young stellar population.

The NSCs are more metal-rich than any GCs found in the galaxies. The high NSC metallicity and the presence of young stars indicate a large contribution from in-situ star formation to their build-up. This star formation might have happened directly in the NSCs or in clustered star formation in the central regions of these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses FCC 153 as a unimodal globular-cluster-system example, finding one GC color peak and a shallower but significant negative color gradient.

quotes.quote: The right panels of Figure fig:cp show the color distributions for GCs in FCC 47 (NGC 1336, panel ) and FCC 153 (IC 335) panel d ). The GC color distribution of FCC 47 displays two peaks, while the GCs in FCC 153 has just one peak in color. In panel d , the black curve shows the best fitting of color distribution of whole GC systems using a single Gaussian function.

The left panels of Figure fig:cp show color profiles of GC systems in FCC 47 (panel a ) and FCC 153 (panel b ). The GC system in the unimodal galaxy FCC 153 also shows a shallower but significant negative color gradient.

sources.summary: IC 335 is presented as an edge-on disc system with distinct bulge and disc components, fast rotation, and a possible central profile artifact.

quotes.quote: IC 335 = FCC 153 This galaxy has a prominent edge-on disc, and the isophotes cannot be fitted with ellipses (thus, the definition and value of can be debated). The photometric profile reveals the distinct bulge and disc components. The maximum rotation, V_ max , reaches 130 , and V_ max / > 1 . In the central region, out to 1-2 arcsec, the GMOS profile shows an important drop, which is less marked on the FORS2 profile. Due to a lower S/N the FORS2 data were binned in the central region, but to check the hypothesis of a drop we analysed the non-binned data: The 2 arcsec inner profile is consistent with a flat .

gmw_00005510.name: UGC 4599
gmw_00005510.overview: UGC 4599 is a nearby, low-luminosity lenticular galaxy [arXiv:2212.08951, arXiv:1102.3184] that superficially resembles Hoag's Object [arXiv:1102.3184]. It features an old central body surrounded by a luminous, star-forming outer ring [arXiv:2212.08951, arXiv:1102.3184] and a massive, extended gaseous disk [arXiv:2212.08951, arXiv:1102.3184].

sources.summary: The paper studies UGC 4599 as a nearby S0 galaxy with a star-forming outer ring, subsolar ring metallicity, old central stars, and gas likely accreted from a filament.

quotes.quote: The moderate-luminosity nearby S0 galaxy, UGC 4599, is studied here. By applying long-slit spectroscopy at the Russian 6m telescope, we have measured stellar kinematics for the main body of the galaxy and strong emission-line flux ratios in the ring. After inspecting the gas excitation in the ring using line ratio diagrams and having shown that it is ionised by young stars, we have determined the gas oxygen abundance by using conventional strong-line calibration methods. We have inspected the gas kinematics in the ring with Fabry-Perot interferometre data obtained at the William Herschel Telescope.

The pattern and properties of the brightest star formation regions are studied with the tunable filter MaNGaL at the 2.5m telescope of the Caucasian Mountain Observatory of the Sternberg Astronomical Institute (CMO SAI MSU). The gas metallicity in the ring is certainly subsolar, O/H =-0.4 0.1 dex , that is different from the majority of the outer starforming rings in S0s studied by us which have typically nearly solar metallicity. The total stellar component of the galaxy which is old in the center is less massive than its extended gaseous disk. We conclude that probably the ring and the outer disk of UGC 4599 are a result of gas accretion from a cosmological filament.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 4599 as a low-luminosity Hoag-like galaxy with a twisted spheroidal core, luminous star-forming ring, extended HI disk, and likely origin in a major gas-rich interaction.

quotes.quote: We present a photometric study of UGC 4599, a low-luminosity galaxy superficially resembling Hoag's Object in that on sky survey images it appears to be a complete ring surrounding a roundish core. The nature of the outer ring of Hoag-type galaxies is still debated and may be related either to slow secular evolution or to environmental processes, such as galaxy-galaxy interactions. As the nearest of its kind, UGC 4599 is a perfect target for a detailed study of the peculiar structure of this type of objects. Surface photometry of the central body of the ring and the ring itself was performed using GALEX , Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and Two Micron All Sky Survey (2MASS) photometric data available on-line.

Although classified in HyperLEDA as S0 with an external ring, we show that in UGC 4599 (a) the nearly round central body follows well an ^ 1 4 light profile almost all the way to the centre, (b) the isophotes are strongly twisted with a sharp 45^ transition at a radius of 6 arcsec, () the blue ring seems to have reached near-equilibrium configuration with the central body, (d) the ring is actually composed of a one-and-a-half turn spiral feature, and (e) one side of the spiral shows conspicuous star formation in the form of at least nine HII regions, revealed by their H emission. Based on the photometric data, together with HI information from the literature, we characterize UGC 4599 as an elliptical-like object surrounded by a luminous ring and a massive, extremely extended HI disc. Given its observed properties, we rule out UGC 4599 as representing a late phase in barred early-type galaxies evolution. We discuss the origin of UGC 4599 and conclude that this galaxy could be the result of a major interaction between two gas-rich spiral galaxies that took place at least 5 Gyr ago.

gmw_00005511.name: CGCG 061-011
gmw_00005511.overview: CGCG 061-011 is a small dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass of 3 × 108 solar masses [arXiv:2212.08951]. It contains ionized gas with a mean oxygen abundance of 8.30 dex and has been evaluated as a potential gas donor to the nearby galaxy UGC 4599 [arXiv:2212.08951].

sources.summary: The paper evaluates CGCG 061-011 as a nearby dwarf candidate gas donor, but finds its gas mass and metallicity make it unsuitable for supplying UGC 4599.

quotes.quote: In the latter paper, the authors proposed a hypothesis of gas flow to UGC 4599 from the nearby dwarf PGC 24666 (CGCG 061-011) because several HI clumps were detected between UGC 4599 and CGCG 061-011. But CGCG 061-011 is a very small galaxy, with a stellar mass of 3 10^8 \ spogs and HI mass of 4 10^8 \ alfalfa ; it seems improbable that CGCG 061-011 may provide a two order larger mass of neutral hydrogen for UGC 4599. Moreover, the metallicity of the ionised gas may be higher in the dwarf CGCG 061-011 than in UGC 4599.

For CGCG 061-011 we have obtained a long-slit spectrum with the SCORPIO-2 on October 31, 2022. After we have excluded the more metal-rich galaxy core, the remaining [ NII ] 6583 / H profile up to =13 has appeared to be flat, and it reveals the mean nitrogen-to-hydrogen line ratio of 0.809 0.005 corresponding to 12+ (O/H) =8.30 0.09 dex marino13 . So the ionised gas in the starforming ring of UGC 4599 is poorer by oxygen than the gas in the dwarf galaxy CGCG 061-011: it cannot be a donor.

gmw_00005512.name: GB 1508 + 5714
gmw_00005512.overview: GB 1508 + 5714 is a radio-loud blazar and quasar located at a redshift of approximately 4.3 [arXiv:1202.2193, arXiv:1702.04006]. It features an extremely compact core [arXiv:1406.4797] and a kiloparsec-scale, southwesterly X-ray and radio jet [arXiv:1202.2193, arXiv:1406.4797] that exhibits relativistic beaming [arXiv:astro-ph/0001299].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1508+572 as the most distant sample object at z=4.30, with first inner pc-scale jet direction measurements, a known southwest X-ray/radio jet, and little core Faraday rotation.

quotes.quote: 1508+572 This is the most distant object in our sample at a redshift of 4.30 hook1995 . Hence, the frequencies of the emitted synchrotron radiation are the highest in our sample at 24.6 and 44.6 GHz. The images presented here are the first observations to show the direction of the inner pc-scale jet. This quasar also has an X-ray jet extending in a south-westerley direction on kpc (arcsecond) scales detected with Chandra siemiginowska2003, yuan2003 .

Although an optically thin jet component is detected at both frequencies roughly 2 mas to the south of the core, this component proved too weak to be used to align the two images. Both the core and this jet component were detected in polarization at 5 GHz, while only the core was detected at 8.4 GHz. The smallest angle between the 5 and 8.4 GHz core EVPAs is only 1^ , which we take to indicate an absence of appreciable Faraday rotation.

sources.summary: The paper describes GB 1508 + 5714 as a high-redshift radio-loud quasar with extended jet-linked emission, strong X-ray enhancement, and an apparently high Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: The extended radio emission of GB 1508 + 5714 coincides with the extended \ emission, showing the signature of a relativistic jet (Cheung 2004).

The SEDs of RX J1028 0844, GB 1428 + 4217, and GB 1508 + 5714 all reach maximal L values (over the 10^9 - 10^ 19 Hz range) in the \ band. This type of SED has been modeled as the emission generated by Compton scattering of synchrotron emission from the jet itself and/or of a powerful external photon field such as the thermal emission of the accretion process (SSC/EC; see Fabian et al. 1998, 1999; Ghisellini et al. 1998).

GB 1508 + 5714 has a smaller M_ BH but an Eddington ratio L/L_ Edd 3 . This object is similar to the Fermi detected FSRQs in Shaw et al. (2012) which have smaller M_ BH and higher Eddington ratios than those of optically selected quasars. The objects in Shaw et al. (2012) show significant non-thermal (synchrotron) emission in the optical band associated with the jet. However, GB 1508 + 5714 has a smaller NTD value (consistent with unity; see discuss:lines ) than the majority of the objects in Shaw et al. (2012; see their Fig. 3), which suggests that this object does not have substantial jet-linked optical/UV continuum emission.

sources.summary: GB 1508 + 5714 is described as a z=4.3 quasar with a kpc-scale X-ray jet, a high X-ray-to-radio luminosity ratio, and complex residual X-ray structure.

quotes.quote: At the time our program began, there were two high- z quasars with kpc-scale X-ray jets: GB 1508 + 5714 at z=4.3 sie03,yua03,che04 and 1745 + 624 at z=3.9 che06 . They were observed to have large values consistent with the IC/CMB model sch02,che04 , although the small number of high- z detections precluded any definitive statements kat05,che06 . We have therefore obtained \ X-ray observations of an additional four high-redshift ( z>3.6 ; GB 1508 + 5714 was previously analyzed) and six intermediate-redshift ( 2 z 3 ) quasars with known radio jets.

Note that the in the case of 1508 + 5714, a significant secondary structure in the complementary region `C' is seen, implying that not all the X-ray features were captured by the radio-based regions. This object has a deep \ observation of about 90 ksec, the longest one in our sample, and it is not unusual to find such structure in long observations.

An early hint at the advantage of studying high-redshift jets came from the z =4.3 quasar 1508 + 5714 (Siemiginowska et al. \ 2003; Yuan et al. \ 2003). This quasar has higher X-ray to radio luminosity ratio ( > 100) than any of its lower- z counterparts (Cheung 2004).

sources.summary: The paper reports a Chandra-detected X-ray blob near blazar GB 1508+5714, interpreting it as inverse-Compton emission from jet-fed relativistic electrons at substantial distance from the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We have found a blob of extended X-ray emission about 3 arcsec away from the powerful blazar GB 1508+5714. This corresponds to a projected distance of about 21 kpc, but could be 100 kpc or more if it is the outer part of the blazar jet. Interpretation of this X-ray emission is not straightforward given the paucity of information from other wavebands. The most obvious emission mechanism is inverse Compton scattering of Cosmic Microwave Background photons. At the quasar redshift of 4.3 the energy density in the CMB is 790 times that now.

The relativistic electron population scattering the CMB photons is plausibly due to the blazar jet. It can either be a quasi-static diffuse cloud fed by the jet, or an outer extension of the jet with a bulk Lorentz factor 10 .

sources.summary: The note identifies this compact double as the second-highest-redshift quasar then known with kiloparsec-scale X-ray emission and a compact core.

quotes.quote: J1510+5702: The extended structure for this compact double is not visible at higher frequencies. This is currently the second highest redshift ( z = 4.309 ) quasar to exhibit kiloparsec-scale X-ray emission (Siemiginowska et al. 2003, Cheung 2004). The core component is extremely compact. Helmboldt et al. (2007), Pushkarev Kovalev (2012) and O'Sullivan et al. (2011) all see a short southern extension at frequencies between 2 and 8 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper describes GB 1508+5714 as one of the few known radio-loud z>4 quasars, with previously published X-ray data and evidence for relativistic beaming.

quotes.quote: We have not detected the other three quasars in Table 1 since the data for RX J1028.6 0844 are not public (it was detected in the ROSAT All-Sky Survey), GB 1508+5714 was not observed by ROSAT , and RX J105225.9+571905 could be detected only in the ``ultradeep'' HRI survey (see Schneider et al. 1998 and references therein). At present only a few radio-loud z>4 quasars are known. These include GB 1428+4217, GB 1508+5714, and RX J1028.6 0844 which have had their X-ray data published prior to this work (Table 1), PKS 1251 407, for which we present an X-ray upper limit, and GB 1713+2148, whose ROSAT observation is not yet public. Out of these objects, GB 1428+4217 and GB 1508+5714 show evidence for relativistic beaming (e.g., Moran Helfand 1997; Fabian et al. 1999).

sources.summary: NVSS J151002+570243 is identified as the farthest known gamma-ray blazar at z=4.31, with intense hard X-rays and a higher modeled black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Among these five objects, NVSS J151002+570243 is now the most distant known ray emitting blazar at z=4.31 .
NVSS J151002+570243 is one of the best studied among all of the objects and exhibits an intense and hard X-ray spectrum e.g., 1995AJ....110.1551M,1997ApJ...484L..95M,2013ApJ...763..109W .

Both methods predict the existence of massive black holes ( 10^ 8-10 M _ ) and match reasonably well within a factor of three One should keep in mind the large uncertainties ( 0.4 dex) associated with the virial BH mass estimation methods see, e.g., 2006ApJ...641..689V,2011ApJS..194...45S . , except for NVSS J151002+570243, for which the modeling approach predicts a higher black hole mass.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1508+5714 as a z = 4.3 high-redshift quasar with near-infrared emission far above the low-redshift median, a likely upper-limit 40 K dust component possibly contaminated by non-thermal radio emission, and a possible blue upturn.

quotes.quote: The highest redshift sources: 1202 0727 ( z = 4.69 ); 1508+5714 ( z = 4.3 ); and 1413+1143 ( z = 2.551 ), are factors of 10--100 brighter in the near-IR than the low- z median SED (Figure fg:hizseds ).

The 40 K component in 1508 + 5714 is based on only the observed 850 m flux; it is likely to be an upper limit, because the FIR slope is flattening at those wavelengths, indicating possible contamination by a high frequency extension of the non-thermal radio emission.
There is an indication of a blue upturn in 1508 + 5714, but the requisite luminosity remains unobserved.

gmw_00005513.name: 040m-1
gmw_00005513.overview: The galaxy 040m-1 is a spectroscopically confirmed source at a redshift of z=0.407 [arXiv:1702.05107]. It features a large, extended H-alpha blob spanning roughly 40 kiloparsecs across and is likely powered by an active galactic nucleus associated with a radio source [arXiv:1702.05107].

sources.summary: The paper describes 040m-1 as the only z=0.40 large H-alpha blob, spectroscopically confirmed, radio-associated, likely AGN-powered, and morphologically suggestive of an outflow.

quotes.quote: At z=0.40 , there are in total 6 s selected with isophotal area above 900 kpc ^2 , but only one object, which we call 040m-1, shows the \ emission extended over 1500 kpc ^2 . Specifically, the isophotal area of 040m-1 is 1549 kpc ^2 , roughly corresponding to 40 kpc across (Figure fig_hab040_final ). This object is spectroscopically confirmed to be at z=0.407 simpson12 . The extended \ flux of this blob is 1.18 10^ 15 . The \ luminosity is 6.94 10^ 41 .

040m-1 is also identified as a radio source with 1.4-GHz flux density of 120 Jy (Simpson C. in private communication). Hence 040m-1 is likely to be powered by an AGN. Unfortunately, we do not have the X-ray data covering this object.

gmw_00005514.name: 063s-1
gmw_00005514.overview: The galaxy 063s-1 is spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of z=0.621 [arXiv:1702.05107]. It features extended emission lines that are significantly more widespread than its stellar continuum, and it is likely a merging system with two distinct emission line components [arXiv:1702.05107].

sources.summary: The paper confirms 063s-1 as a z=0.621 blob with multiple emission lines, extended emission beyond its stellar continuum, and two components consistent with a merger.

quotes.quote: For the largest blob at z=0.63 ( 063s-1), we detect another emission line at 8045 , which perfectly corresponds to 4959 emission. Furthermore, we also detect the \ emission line at 6040 . Therefore, 063s-1 is spectroscopically confirmed to be at z=0.621 .

The \ emission lines of the s are significantly more extended than the stellar continuum, especially 063s-1. Two components of emission line are obviously seen. Both components are roughly at the same redshift of z=0.61 , suggesting that 063s-1 is probably a merging system, even though only one stellar component has been detected in the broadband continuum image.

gmw_00005515.name: Q0130-4021
gmw_00005515.overview: Q0130-4021 is a quasar [arXiv:0801.1692] that hosts an associated absorber system located approximately 4000 km/s blueward of its emission redshift [arXiv:1008.2953]. X-ray observations of the active nucleus show that both Compton-reflection and absorber power-law models fit its spectrum comparably well [arXiv:0801.1692].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Q0130-4021 X-ray data and finds that Compton-reflection and absorber power-law models both fit the spectrum comparably well.

quotes.quote: Q0130-4021. --- Page et al. (2003) searched for AGNs in the \ field of this quasar, and detected 9 objects including Q0130 4021 itself. We reduced the same \ data taken on 2001 June 04 for 23.0 ks (pn) and 25.2 ks (MOS) (Obs-ID: 0112630201, PI: Turner). The pn and MOS spectra were binned with a minimum of 50 and 30 counts per bin, respectively. Model mod:compt (power law plus Compton reflection) provides a marginally better fit than model mod:pow (simple power-law model) to the \ spectrum of this object, P_F(1)=0.0011 (see Table tab:fits ). However, the fit with model mod:compt appears to be as good as the fit with model mod:abspow (absorber power-law).

sources.summary: The paper models the intrinsic absorber toward HE0130 4021 as a complex, partially covered associated system with weak Ly absorption and O VI components affected by blends.

quotes.quote: HE0130 4021 ( z_ abs =2.973915 ; Fig. fig-q0130-sys ): The system is found 4000 \ blueward of the quasar emission redshift, just within the associated region. This system is kinematically more complex than the other two, with 9 components in each N v line, spreading over 500 . All but one of these components have small coverage fractions ( C_ f < 0.5 ; see Table tab-nvfit ). The strong features in the Ly panel of Figure fig-q0130-sys at 280 , 50 , 110 , and 210 \ cannot be Ly components because the corresponding Ly components are not detected. Thus only portions of the Ly profile can be used as constraints, but it is clear that the absorption in Ly is quite weak.

gmw_00005516.name: Q1107+4847
gmw_00005516.overview: Q1107+4847 is an active galactic nucleus classified as an X-ray quasar [arXiv:0801.1692]. Its X-ray spectrum can be modeled using a simple power-law, an absorbed power-law, or a power-law with Compton reflection and intrinsic absorption [arXiv:0801.1692].

sources.summary: The paper reexamines Q1107+4847 X-ray observations, reduces the usable data set, and finds fits consistent with prior power-law and absorption limits.

quotes.quote: Q1107+4847. --- \ observed this target twice on 2002 April 25 and June 1. The analysis of the second data set was presented by Brocksopp et al. (2004). They fitted the spectra with a power law model with photon index of = 1.96 and obtained an upper limit on the intrinsic absorption of < 1.5 10^ 22 \ with 99 \ confidence. We examined both \ data sets (Obs-ID: 0059750401 and 0104861001, PI: Mann and Mason), but reduced only the second one because the first observation was performed during a period of high background (background-to-signal ratio is > 20).

The pn and MOS spectra were binned with a minimum of 50 and 15 counts per bin, respectively. The results of our fits are consistent with the results of Brocksopp et al. (2004). Model mod:abscompt (power law plus Compton reflection and intrinsic absorption) provides an equally good fit to the \ spectrum of this object as model mod:pow (simple power-law model) and model mod:abspow (absorber power-law).

gmw_00005517.name: Q1700+6416
gmw_00005517.overview: Q1700+6416 is an extremely radio-quiet, high-redshift narrow absorption line quasar [arXiv:1205.6587] located near two galaxy clusters [arXiv:0801.1692]. It exhibits long-term variability in its X-ray flux and absorption [arXiv:1205.6587], as well as in its ultraviolet and optical emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0504014].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HS 1700+6416 as a high-redshift luminous NAL quasar with variable X-ray absorption, broad iron-band features, and high-velocity ionized outflows that can provide mechanical feedback.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of the X-ray emission of HS 1700+6416, a high redshift ( z=2.7348 ), luminous quasar, classified as a Narrow Absorption Line (NAL) quasar on the basis of its SDSS spectrum. The source has been observed 9 times by \ and once by \ from 2000 to 2007. Long term variability is clearly detected, between the observations, in the 2-10 keV flux varying by a factor of three ( 3-9 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ) and in the amount of neutral absorption (N _ H < 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 in 2000 and 2002 and N _ H =4.4 1.2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 in 2007). Most interestingly, one broad absorption feature is clearly detected at 10.3 0.7 keV (rest frame) in the 2000 \ observation, while two similar features, at 8.9 0.4 and at 12.5 0.7 keV, are visible when the 8 contiguous \ observations of 2007 are stacked together.

Here we present a detailed analysis of the X-ray emission of the quasar HS 1700+6416, a high redshift (z=2.7348), high luminosity NAL QSO. HS 1700+6416 is one of the most luminous, non-lensed (Reimers et al. 1997), QSOs in the SDSS ( M_i=-30.24 ). It is not detected in the NVSS radio survey, that has a 5 sensitivity limit of 2.5 mJy at 1.4 GHz. The source should be therefore considered extremely radio quiet (log(F _ 1.4GHz /F _R < -5 ) F _ 1.4GHz is the 1.4 GHz radio flux density )).

sources.summary: The paper presents Q1700+6416 X-ray spectra for the first time, noting nearby clusters, acceptable extracted spectra, and different binning for Chandra and XMM data.

quotes.quote: Q1700+6416. --- There are two clusters of galaxies discovered near the quasar, RX J1701.3 + 6416 at z = 0.45 and Abell 2246 at z = 0.22. Both the \ and the \ observed this quasar field for a purpose of studying these clusters (Lumb et al. 2004; Georgantopoulos Georgakakis 2005) and serendipitously detected a number of low-redshift galaxies in the field (Hornschemeier et al. 2005). We reduced the \ data (Obs-ID: 547, PI: Vanspeybroeck) and the \ data (Obs-ID: 0107860301, PI: Jansen) and we present the spectrum of Q1700+6416 for the first time.

Although the \ exposure was terminated prematurely and most of it was dominated by high background from the particle belt (Lumb et al. 2004), we were still able to extract spectra of acceptable quality. The \ spectrum was binned with a minimum of 20 counts per bin. The \ pn and MOS spectra were binned to a minimum of 50 and 15 counts per bin, respectively, at E < 2 keV and to a minimum of 150 and 45, respectively at E > 2 keV.

sources.summary: The paper treats HS 1700 + 6416 as a He II quasar sightline, excluding geocoronal contamination and recalibrating, restricting, and FUSE-aligning its extreme-UV COS spectrum.

quotes.quote: Over the short range with useful G140L segment B data for HE 2347 4342 and HS 1700 + 6416 ( 2250 pixels, 1000--1180 ) spectral variations of the dark current are insignificant.
For HE 2347 4342 and HS 1700 + 6416 we also excluded geocoronal H 1 Ly emission contaminating the He 2 Ly forest at z 2.37 .

The extreme UV ( <1150 ) spectra of HS 1700 + 6416 and HE 2347 4342 recorded on COS detector segment B in the G140L 1230 \ and 1280 \ setups required wavelength recalibration e.g. shull10,syphers13 . Due to the combination of flux calibration uncertainties, low signal, and He 2 Ly absorption we restricted our analysis to >1000 . We adopted the COS G140L dispersion given by shull10 and aligned the COS spectra with their archival FUSE spectra after convolving them with the COS G140L line spread function. Considering the background subtraction problems of FUSE zheng04,fechner06 and possible source variability, the spectra were aligned by eye using several sharp features in the emerging He 2 Ly forest.

sources.summary: The paper describes the HS1700 + 6416 intrinsic absorber as a three-component, partially covered system with detected C and N doublets, Lyman lines, and evidence for variability.

quotes.quote: HS1700 + 6416 ( z_ abs =2.7125 ; Fig. fig-q1700-sys ): This system is 750 km s ^ 1 blueward of the quasar emission redshift. The \ and \ doublets are detected in this system, as well as Ly and Ly (Fig. fig-q1700-sys ). The same system was presented in bhs97 , who found it to vary significantly on a time scale of 6.5 months in its rest-frame. The central absorption is detected at z 2.7125 ( _ rel 0 km s ^ 1 ) and a weaker component is found at 325 km s ^ 1 . The central component shows an asymmetry, indicating the need for two Voigt components for an adequate fit.

sources.summary: The paper studies HS 1700+6416 as a bright z=2.73 QSO with a rich metal-line spectrum, UV variability, seven optically thin Lyman limit systems, observable He II, and predicted far-UV metal-line content.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present a study of the rich metal line spectrum of the QSO HS 1700+6416 ( z = 2.73 , (2000.0) = 17^ h 01^ m 00 6 , (2000.0) = +61 12 09 ). This object is exceptionally bright in the optical ( V=16.1 ). Although it provides 7 optical thin Lyman limit systems (LLS) along the line of sight and is variable in the UV reimersetal2005b , HS 1700+6416 is one of the few quasars, where He ii is observable davidsenetal1996, reimersetal_fuse . Due to its brightness, HS 1700+6416 has been addressed in several analyses dealing with metal line systems e.g. reimersetal1992, vogelreimers1993, vogelreimers1995, koehleretal1996, petitjeanetal1996, trippetal1997, simcoeetal2002, simcoeetal2005 , and due to its rich metal line spectrum, it has been target of deep direct observations in several spectral ranges aiming to identify objects which give rise to the Lyman limit absorption reimersetal1995, reimersetal1997b, teplitzetal1998, erbetal2003, shapleyetal2005 . Our main objective is to predict the metal line content of the FUSE spectral range in the spectrum of this quasar, which will be considered in the analysis of the He ii Ly forest towards this quasar.

sources.summary: The paper treats HS 1700+6416 as a quasar sight line, using FUSE and Keck spectra to analyze its He II and H I Ly forest and assess proximity-effect evidence.

quotes.quote: We present a reanalysis of the He ii Ly absorption towards the quasars HS 1700+6416 and HE 2347-4342 using new high S/N , optical observations.

The UV spectrum of HS 1700+6416 taken with FUSE was first presented in reimersetal_fuse . We use the reduced and normalized data set from fechneretal2006b , which has a resolution of R 20 000 and a signal-to-noise ratio of 5 . The corresponding H i Ly forest was observed with Keck/HIRES.

On the other hand, no proximity effect can be seen towards HS 1700+6416, even though there are metal absorption systems showing material like N v and O vi supposed to be exposed to the quasar's radiation field.

sources.summary: The paper reports decade-scale UV/EUV and optical variability of the luminous z=2.72 QSO HS 1700+6416, including a factor-three FUSE brightening.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report on UV (intrinsic EUV) and optical variability of HS 1700+6416 (z = 2.72), one of the most luminous QSOs known and also one of the few known objects where such an empirical study is possible. Discovered by the Hamburg Quasar Survey it was observed in the UV by IUE (Reimers, ), HST (Reimers et al., ), HUT (Davidsen et al. ), and FUSE. It was also monitored in the optical in the years 1988 - 1995 and 1998 by the Hamburg Quasar Monitoring Program (Borgeest Schramm, ) and from 1995 on at the Wise observatory (Kaspi et al. ). While the variability of HS 1700+6416 was therefore known before, also in the UV (cf. discussion by K ohler et al. ), motivation for the present study came from FUSE observations taken in Feb/March 2003 when it was recognized that HS 1700+6416 had brightened by a factor of 3 since Oct. 2002 at FUSE wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper presents FUSE far-UV observations of quasar HS 1700+6416, using its z_em=2.72 sightline to resolve the He II Ly-alpha forest and study post-reionization He II opacity.

quotes.quote: We present the far-UV spectrum of the quasar HS 1700+6416 taken with FUSE. This QSO provides the second line of sight with the He ii absorption resolved into a Ly forest structure. Since HS 1700+6416 is slightly less redshifted ( z_ em = 2.72 ) than HE 2347-4342 , we only probe the post-reionization phase of He ii , seen in the evolution of the He ii opacity, which is consistent with a simple power law.

gmw_00005518.name: ALESS65.1
gmw_00005518.overview: ALESS65.1 is an isolated submillimeter starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z = 4.44 [arXiv:1302.3297, arXiv:2308.15955]. It has a cold-gas mass upper limit of 1.7 billion solar masses [arXiv:1302.3297] and shows no detected extended emission [arXiv:2308.15955].

sources.summary: The paper observes ALESS65.1 in CO(2-1), reports no CO detection, places a cold-gas mass upper limit, and interprets its line ratios as extended CII emission or subsolar metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present ^ 12 CO(2--1) observations of the submillimetre galaxy ALESS65.1 performed with the Australia Telescope Compact Array at 42.3 GHz. A previous ALMA study of submillimetre galaxies in the Extended Chandra Deep Field South detected [ CII ] 157.74 m emission from this galaxy at a redshift of z = 4.44 .

No ^ 12 CO(2--1) emission was detected but we derive a firm upper limit to the cold gas mass in ALESS65.1 of M_ gas < 1.7 10^ 10 M _ . The estimated gas depletion timescale is <50 Myr, which is similar to other high redshift SMGs, and consistent with z > 4 SMGs being the likely progenitors of massive red-and-dead galaxies at z > 2 . The ratio of the [ CII ] , ^ 12 CO and far-infrared luminosities implies a strong far-ultraviolet field of G_0 10^3 , as seen in Galactic star forming regions or local ULIRGs. The observed L_ CII /L_ FIR = 2.3 10^ 3 is high compared to local ULIRGs and, combined with L_ CII /L_ CO 2700 , it is consistent with ALESS65.1 either having an extended (several kpc) [ CII ] emitting region or lower than solar metallicity.

The ^ 12 CO(2--1) line ( _ rest = 230.538 GHz) in ALESS65.1 (RA(J2000) = 03 32 52.26, Dec(J2000) = 27 35 26.3) (S12) was observed over a period of four consecutive nights, 9 -- 12 August 2012, with the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA), using the Compact Array Broadband Backend (CABB).

sources.summary: The paper treats ALESS65.1 as an isolated high-redshift SMG with submillimeter SED data, estimated stellar and halo masses, and no detected extended emission.

quotes.quote: They are about one order of magnitude fainter than the quasar in BR1202-0725; Two isolated SMGs (with no AGN) with spectroscopic redshift from [ CII ] and CO, ALESS61.1 and ALESS65.1 Swinbank2012,Birkin2021 . ^ a Redshifts from Carilli2013 , Fu2017 and Birkin2021 . We construct the 8-1000 m spectral energy distribution using the datapoints from Omont1996a (at 1250 m with IRAM 30 m) and Iono2006 (at 900 m with the Submillimeter Array) for BR1202-0725, from Fu2017 (at 250, 350 and 500 m with Herschel/SPIRE and 870 m with ALMA) for G09-0902+0101 and G15-1444-0044, and from Swinbank2012 (at 250, 350 and 500 m with Herschel/SPIRE and 870 m with ALMA) for ALESS61.1 and ALESS65.1. Specifically, the dust masses of ALESS61.1 and ALESS65.1 are in agreement with the values estimated by Birkin2021 .

For the two SMGs, ALESS61.1 and ALESS65.1, we can rely on their stellar masses obtained through SED fitting by Birkin2021 , log( M_ * / [ M_ ] )=10.33^ +0.18 _ 0.01 and log( M_ * / [ M_ ] )=10.48^ +0.19 _ 0.13 , respectively. Therefore, the expected DM halo masses using the halo mass-stellar mass relation by Moster2018 for the targeted sources are: log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 12.3 for BR1202-0725, log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 12.5 and log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 11.9 for G09-0902+0101 and G15-1444-0044, and log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 11.9 and log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 12.0 for ALESS61.1, and ALESS65.1, respectively.

Specifically, our observations comprise systems with different degrees of quasar illumination: a QSO+SMG system (BR1202-0725), two SMGs hosting a QSO (G09-0902+0101 and G15-1444-0044), and two SMGs (ALESS61.1 and ALESS65.1). We do not detect any extended emission around the two SMGs.

gmw_00005519.name: HE 1254 0934
gmw_00005519.overview: HE 1254 0934 is an ongoing major merger system with a dynamical mass of 1.2 billion solar masses [arXiv:1705.03076]. The system features two nuclei separated by 8 kpc, which isolate its quasar and starburst regions and exhibit an enhanced molecular gas fraction [arXiv:1705.03076].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HE 1254 0934 as an ongoing major merger with enhanced molecular gas, a large dynamical mass, and two nuclei separating the QSO and starburst regions.

quotes.quote: The two ongoing major mergers, HE 1254 0934 and HE 1405 1545, show clearly enhanced molecular gas fractions. This corresponds to dynamical masses of M_ dyn =1.2 10^ 11 M_ for HE 1254 0934 and M_ dyn =1.0 10^ 11 M_ for HE 1405 1545, respectively, adopting an inclination of i=40^ in both cases.

It is interesting to note in this respect that the major merger system HE 1254 0934 shows two nuclei in continuum light separated by 8 kpc containing the QSO and the starburst region, respectively. This naturally limits the AGN to influence the gas content just by geometrical considerations.

gmw_00005520.name: HE 1405 1545
gmw_00005520.overview: HE 1405 1545 is a quasar host galaxy currently undergoing a major merger [arXiv:1705.03076]. This system exhibits an enhanced molecular gas fraction and has an estimated dynamical mass of 1.0e11 solar masses [arXiv:1705.03076].

sources.summary: The paper discusses HE 1405 1545 as an ongoing major-merger QSO host with enhanced molecular gas, measured radial velocity, dynamical mass, and H-alpha disk size.

quotes.quote: The two ongoing major mergers, HE 1254 0934 and HE 1405 1545, show clearly enhanced molecular gas fractions. For HE 1245 0934 we measure a radial velocity of V_r= 120 km/s at R_0=12 kpc and for HE 1405 1545 we obtain V_r=125 km/s at R_0=14 kpc. This corresponds to dynamical masses of M_ dyn =1.2 10^ 11 M_ for HE 1254 0934 and M_ dyn =1.0 10^ 11 M_ for HE 1405 1545, respectively, adopting an inclination of i=40^ in both cases. HE 1254 0934 and HE 1405 1545, we measure the size of the H disc along the major-axis with respect to the kinematic centre assuming a circular disc.

gmw_00005521.overview: 23 32 27.8, +10 08 19 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.063 [arXiv:2208.12177]. It hosts a central supermassive black hole and exhibits steep X-ray emission, a strong soft excess, and large-amplitude X-ray variability [arXiv:2208.12177].

sources.summary: The paper models a coordinate-identified NLS1 active galaxy, discussing its high black-hole mass, steep and variable X-ray emission, soft excess, and competing reflection or absorption scenarios.

quotes.quote: In this work, we investigate how the models introduced above may explain the \ observations of the active galaxy \ at z=0.063 23 32 27.8, +10 08 19, clements81, huchra99 . \ was classified as a NLS1 galaxy by boroson92,constantin03 . The H emission of this NLS1 has a width of 2250--2800 km s ^ 1 boroson92,grupe04,mullaney08 , which is higher than the values of typical NLS1s. The mass of the central BH in \ is estimated to be around 4.57 10^ 8 M_ peterson04 using broad emission-line reverberation-mapping data.

In the X-ray band, cardaci09 reported a steep X-ray emission from \ and strong soft excess emission as well as the EXOSAT data of the same source singh91 . cardaci09 also found Fe `Unresolved Transition Array' (UTA) absorption in the soft X-ray band of , which indicates the existence of photoionised absorbers, e.g. warm absorption commonly seen in AGNs e.g. reynolds97,george98 . Large-amplitude X-ray variability of \ has been realised for decades and was first discovered by singh91,grupe01 .

gmw_00005522.name: GD153 236
gmw_00005522.overview: GD153 236 is an obscured quasar candidate located at a redshift of z=0.909 [arXiv:astro-ph/0612023]. The active galactic nucleus exhibits moderate X-ray absorption and an intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity of 1.59e44 erg s^-1 [arXiv:astro-ph/0612023].

sources.summary: The paper describes GD153 236 as an obscured QSO candidate with a narrow H line, moderate X-ray absorption, and intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity of 1.59e44 erg s^-1.

quotes.quote: The H_ FWHM of GD153 236 (H2XMMJ125654.1+215318; z=0.909, AGN2) is <1000 km s ^ 1 . Faint [ OII ] and [ OIII ] emission is present in the spectrum of A1835 262 (H2XMMJ140130.8+024532; z=0.746, ELG). The latter source is obscured also in the X-ray band, having a 0.5-2 keV flux less than 20 times the observed 2-10 keV flux; to be more quantitative, the X--ray spectral fit yields a rest frame N _H (z) 7.9^ +3.3 _ 2.2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , fixing the spectral energy index _E (F(E) E ^ _E ) to 0.9. GD153 236 is less extreme in this regard, the X--ray spectral fit yields a rest frame N _H (z) = 0.3^ +0.4 _ 0.3 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , fixing the spectral energy index _E to 0.9. The 2--10 keV intrinsic luminosity is 1.10 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 for A1835 262 and 1.59 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 for GD153 236, making them obscured QSO candidates.

gmw_00005523.name: 3C 196
gmw_00005523.overview: 3C 196 is a non-variable, radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.871 [arXiv:astro-ph/0105228, arXiv:1211.1300]. The source features a pair of compact lobes and bright hotspots symmetrically placed around a weak core [arXiv:astro-ph/0105228, arXiv:1211.1300], surrounded by an extended emission-line region aligned with its radio structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603591].

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C196 as its phase-center QSO calibrator, describing its known 74 MHz flux, four-Gaussian radio source model, high-resolution structure, and agreement with lower-frequency flux observations.

quotes.quote: The QSO 3C196 is a very bright radio source with known flux (130 Jy at 74 MHz; kassim2007 ) and is located at the phase center of the field. It can be used as a flux calibrator to determine the station band-pass gains. We use a model of 3C196 V. N. Pandey via private communication which has 4 Gaussian components to describe the source, and the source spectrum is described by a second-order log-polynomial.

This model was iteratively derived using LOFAR-HBA (full Dutch array with baseline range of 100 m to 120 km) observation data of 3C196 over the frequency range of 115-185 MHz. The parameters of the model components including the spectral indices were obtained by fitting in the visibility domain. The source model includes the flux at large angular scales and also represents the high resolution structures (with arcsec accuracy). We compared the 3C196 model flux extrapolated at lower frequencies with other 3C196 observations at 74 MHz using Very Large Array (VLA) kassim2007 and at 60 MHz using Serpukov radio-telescope aslanian1968 . The model flux matches the VLA observation within 4 error and the Serpukov radio-telescope observation within 2 error.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 196 as a z=0.871 radio-loud quasar with bright compact hotspots and reports a weak optical component consistent with SSC emission near equipartition.

quotes.quote: In this note I report on a possible detection of optical SSC emission from the distant quasar 3C 196.

3C 196 is a z=0.871 quasar with a compact, FRII-like radio structure (Laing 1982; Lonsdale Morison 1983, hereafter LM; Brown 1990). It came to our attention as a possible target for Chandra observations because of its very bright, compact hotspots, with flux densities around 2 Jy at 5 GHz (LM).

A weak optical component coincident with the northern radio hotspot is detected in 3C 196. The radio spectrum makes this component very unlikely to be due to synchrotron emission. If it is used as an upper limit on any optical SSC emission, it requires the magnetic field strength in the hotspot to be greater than or equal to the equipartition value. If it is taken to be a detection of SSC emission, its flux level is in good agreement with a model similar to the one found to work in X-ray detected hotspots; the low-energy cutoff is around = 500 and the magnetic field strength is close to the equipartition value.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C196 as a quasar with two compact lobes around a weak core, non-variable enough to be an excellent low-resolution flux-density calibrator below 10 GHz.

quotes.quote: 3C196 is a quasar at redshift z = 0.871. The source comprises a pair of compact lobes symmetrically placed about a weak core, with maximum angular extent of about 7 arcseconds, as shown in the 22 GHz, 1 arcsecond resolution image in the left panel of Fig. fig:3C196Maps , made from the data taken for this program. In the right panel is shown the secular variation.

This source is one of the non-variable objects we have identified. Because of this, its small angular size, and remarkably straight spectrum ( Sca12 ), 3C196 is an excellent flux density calibration source for low-resolution observations for frequencies up to 10 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 196 as a simulated LOFAR QSO dataset, building a high-resolution spectral-index model with hotspots and lobes to test forced-spectrum deconvolution.

quotes.quote: To create complex and realistic simulated data, we take a high-resolution, bandwidth-integrated LOFAR model of the 3C 196 QSO RA 08^ h 13^ m 36 . ^ s 07 , Dec +48^ 13'02 . ''58 in J2000; 3C:spinrad_etal:1985,paris_etal:2014 to provide the spatial information, and apply different spectral indices to different parts of the image. The spectral information is taken from a simple, older 4-component model of the source, consisting of Gaussians whose position and shape are reported in Table tab:3C196spix together with the associated spectral index at a reference frequency of 150 MHz. Despite the morphological simplicity of this model, its spectral indices are consistent with the expected synchrotron emission lonsdale_morison:1980 . The source has been divided into two hotspots, northern (N) and southern (S), and two lobes, eastern (E) and western (W), as shown with the dashed contours in Fig. fig:3C196_sim . The high-resolution model was obtained from a joined deconvolution of LOFAR-VLBI HBA-mid (116--171 MHz) and HBA-high (230--244 MHz) observations taken in 2016, using MS-MF deconvolution.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C 196 as an early quasar/radio source, comparing its colors and absorption spectra to white dwarfs and interpreting its optical emission as synchrotron-dominated.

quotes.quote: Prior to this, these objects were found to be radio sources whose optical appearance was star-like instead of galaxy-like - the first such source to be identified was 3C 48 1961S&T...21..148M followed by 3C 196 and 3C 286 1962PASP...74R.406M,1963ApJ...138...30M .

We recall that 1963ApJ...138...30M had noted a similarity between the colours of old novae/white dwarfs and quasars (3C 48, 3C 196, 3C 286). This was supported by 1967ApJ...147.1219B who commented on the similarities in the absorption spectra of the white dwarf Hz 29 1957ApJ...126...14G and quasars.

For example, 1963ApJ...138...30M found that while they could extrapolate the optical flux for 3C 48 and 3C 196 from the radio, it was not possible to do so for 3C 286 - one way to understand this would be that the optical emission in 3C 48 and 3C 196 is dominated by synchrotron emission whereas in 3C 286, its a combination of synchrotron and thermal emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 196 as a LOFAR calibrator, processing LBA and HBA observations to correct systematic effects and modeling it with four Gaussian components.

quotes.quote: We processed two LOFAR observations of the calibrator 3C 196: the first using the Low Band Antenna (LBA) system at 42--66 MHz and the second using the High Band Antenna (HBA) system at 115--189 MHz. We were able to isolate and correct for the effects of clock drift, polarisation misalignment, ionospheric delay, Faraday rotation, ionospheric scintillation, beam shape, and bandpass. The designed calibration strategy produced the deepest image to date at 54 MHz.
We use a model of 3C 196 described by four Gaussian components, in which each component has a spectrum described by a second order log-polynomial (V.N. Pandey priv. comm.)

sources.summary: The paper finds an extended emission-line region around 3C 196 aligned with radio hot spots, with high-velocity components supporting a jet-cloud interaction.

quotes.quote: Here we investigate the [ O ii ] EELR around the quasar 3C 196 at z=0.871 using integral field spectroscopy. We also detect extended [ Ne ii ] emission at a distance of about 30 kpc from the core. The emission is aligned with the radio hot spots and shows a redshifted and a blueshifted component with a velocity difference of 800 km s ^ 1 . The alignment effect and large velocities support the hypothesis that the EELR is caused by a jet-cloud interaction, which is furthermore indicated by the presence of a pronounced bend in the radio emission at the location of the radio hot spots.

gmw_00005524.name: NGC 5743
gmw_00005524.overview: NGC 5743 is an interacting luminous infrared galaxy paired with NGC 5734, exhibiting a diffuse hydrogen-alpha extension toward its companion [arXiv:1109.1372]. Although optically classified as an H II galaxy, it contains an active galactic nucleus and is likely composite in nature [arXiv:1109.1372].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5743 as an interacting LIRG with IRS spectroscopy, pairing with NGC 5734, diffuse H extension, optical HII classification, [Ne V] detection, and likely composite AGN/star-forming nature.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, NGC 5743 and NGC 7769 (see Table 1), have IRS spectroscopy (see Section 2.2), but were not originally included in the IRAS RBGS. However, both galaxies are in interaction with RBGS IRAS sources see Surace2004 . NGC 5743 is paired with NGC 5734 and shows a diffuse H extension toward NGC 5734 Dopita2002 . NGC 7769 is part of the NGC 7771/NGC 7770 group, and there is evidence that NGC 7769 is undergoing a direct encounter with NGC 7771 Nordgren97 . Additionally, these two galaxies not included in the RBGS have IR luminosities similar to the rest of the galaxies (see Table 1) and thus we included them in our sample.

Finally, NGC 5743 and MCG+04-48-002 were optically classified as HII, but both have a [ Ne v ] detection. Both have also relatively low / ratios, which are typical of star-forming galaxies (see Table 4 and Section 5.4). Therefore, although these two galaxies do contain an AGN, they are likely to be composite in nature.

gmw_00005525.name: NGC 4324
gmw_00005525.overview: NGC 4324 is a gas-rich lenticular or early-type spiral galaxy located in the outskirts of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2210.07786, arXiv:1907.07261]. The galaxy features an inner ring containing young, regularly spaced star-forming complexes, which may have been fueled by the accretion of gas-rich satellites [arXiv:2210.07786, arXiv:2307.07803].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4324 as a lenticular galaxy with young star-forming clumps in an inner ring, likely fed by minor-merger gas accretion.

quotes.quote: We present the results of our study of starforming regions in the lenticular galaxy NGC 4324. During a complex analysis of multiwavelength observational data -- the narrow-band emission-line images obtained with the 2.5-m telescope at the Caucasus Mountain Observatory of the Sternberg Astronomical Institute of the Moscow State University and the archival images in the broad bands of the SDSS, GALEX and WISE surveys -- we have detected young starforming complexes (clumps) located in the inner ring of the lenticular galaxy NGC 4324, and we have established a regular pattern of their distribution along the ring, which, nevertheless, changes with time (with age of starforming regions). We suggest several possible evolutionary paths of the lenticular galaxy NGC 4324, of which the accretion of gas-rich satellites or giant clouds (the so-called minor merging) is the most probable one.

The nearby early-type galaxy NGC 4324 being investigated here is remarkable for its bright blue ring (Fig. 1) embedded in a large-scale stellar disk typical for lenticular galaxies, having a reddish color and without distinct structural features, except the ring. The blue color of the ring points to current or recent star formation within it.

The regularity in the distribution of starforming complexes in the ring of NGC 4324 suggests that the physical star formation mechanisms over local scales are the same in spiral and lenticular galaxies, while the differences arise when we start the analysis of large-scale structures: in spiral galaxies the major star formation occurs in spiral density waves, while in gas-rich lenticular galaxies it is organized into ring structures (Pogge and Eskridge 1993; Salim et al. 2012).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4324 as a lenticular ring galaxy where young complexes and HII regions show regular spacing, with younger regions lying between older complexes.

quotes.quote: In the second paper of 2022, discovered a regularity in the distribution of young stellar complexes and HII regions in the ring of the lenticular () SA0+ galaxy NGC 4324 (Fig. figure:fig1 ). By studying this galaxy in the FUV, NUV, u bands and H line, the authors found that the distances between neighboring HII regions within the ring were 650 pc, while the stellar complexes visible in u had distances of 1.0 kpc. At the same time, relatively younger star formation regions (HII regions with an age of up to 10 Myr) often occupied positions in the middle between relatively old ( t 100 Myr) stellar complexes. The numerical results obtained in closely agree with the data from concerning the ring of NGC 6217. It should be noted that the galaxy NGC 4324 is even further away from us than NGC 6217 ( d=26.2 Mpc tully2013 ), and investigating potetial regularities on smaller scales (300-500 pc) does not appear possible.

Stellar complexes of are only a specific case of such regions with a higher concentration of the young stellar population. discovered a drift of star formation along the ring of NGC 4324, where relatively younger star formation regions often lie between relatively older ones.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4324 as a gas-rich Virgo-outskirts lenticular with a molecular-gas starforming ring and clumpy outer starforming regions mostly in regular gaseous-disk rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4324. This lenticular galaxy inhabits outskirts of the Virgo cluster, and it is very gas rich (see the Table 1). It is also a part of the ATLAS-3D survey atlas3d_1 and demonstrates a blue starforming ring with a radius of some 2 \ which also contains all the molecular gas detected so far atlas3d_18 . However, dup_sch detected \ up to 2 optical radii in this galaxy that gave a hope that ionized gas also may be extended beyond the inner starforming ring. Indeed, in Fig. fig_fpi we see clumpy outer starforming regions up to R 70^ 90^ (7.5 ) from the center; most of them take part in the general regular rotation of the gaseous disk. However, a few -- for example that to the north-east from the galaxy -- do not participate in the rotation of NGC 4324 and may be gas-rich satellites.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4324 as a Virgo S-cloud Sa galaxy with star formation in a distinct ring and atomic hydrogen extending well beyond that ring.

quotes.quote: NGC4324 is a Sa galaxy belonging to the Virgo S cloud goldmine . Star formation is segregated in a ring, also visible in SDSS images, distinct from the central bulge component. Although maps are not available, detect up to 2 optical diameters suggesting that the atomic hydrogen is not only concentrated in the star-forming ring.

gmw_00005526.name: CXOCY-J125315.2-091424
gmw_00005526.overview: CXOCY-J125315.2-091424 is a highly obscured type-2 quasar located at a redshift of z=1.154 [arXiv:astro-ph/0411325]. It is characterized by narrow optical emission lines and a high neutral hydrogen column density along the line of sight [arXiv:astro-ph/0411325].

sources.summary: The CYDER survey identifies CXOCY-J125315.2-091424 as its only QSO-2, a hard-band-only, very obscured AGN at z=1.154 and the brightest obscured AGN in the sample.

quotes.quote: In our sample there is only one source classified as QSO-2 based on its observed X-ray luminosity and optical spectrum: CXOCY-J125315.2-091424 at z=1.154 , located in the C2 field. 52 counts were detected in the hard X-ray band, while no emission was detected in the soft X-ray band and therefore the hardness ratio is -1. The optical spectrum of this source is presented in Figure qso2_spec . Narrow emission lines like CIII, MgII and OII are clearly visible in this spectrum and were used to calculate the redshift of the source. The total (0.5-8 keV) observed X-ray luminosity of this source is L_X 2 10^ 44 ergs s ^ 1 making this the brightest obscured AGN in our sample.

Given the observed hardness ratio and redshift of this source, the expected neutral hydrogen column density in the line of sight is 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 assuming either an intrinsic power law with exponent 1.9 or 1.7, consistent with the optical classification of very obscured AGN.

gmw_00005527.name: KISSR872
gmw_00005527.overview: KISSR872 is a type 2 LINER galaxy undergoing a merger with a second galaxy [arXiv:2106.09304, arXiv:2312.11402]. The system features double optical nuclei, a northern tidal tail [arXiv:2106.09304], and a radio-quiet active nucleus hosting a relativistic, superluminal jet [arXiv:2312.11402].

sources.summary: The paper describes KISSR 872 as a merging LINER with two optical nuclei, a tidal tail, a 200 pc parsec-scale jet, and line ratios best fit by shock-precursor models.

quotes.quote: The Double Optical Nuclei Galaxy KISSR 872 The double nuclei associated with the LINER galaxy KISSR 872 (N1 and N2 in Figure fig1 ) turn out to be two individual galaxies at around the same redshift, undergoing a merger. The projected separation of optical centres N1 and N2 is 7.25 kpc; a tidal tail is observed to the north. While KISSR 872 is a 10^ 11 solar mass galaxy, the second galaxy is a 10^ 10.5 solar mass galaxy that falls in the composite region of the BPT diagram Baldwin81 stellar masses are from the SDSS database which are calculated using the method of Chen12 .

In the 1.5 GHz VLBA image, the core-jet (C to J3, Figure fig3 ) extent is nearly 200 parsec (135 mas) at a PA of 145 . The jet appears to be remarkably straight along its length. The distance between components C to J2 (see Figure fig2 ) is 60 parsec. At 4.9 GHz, only the inner 10 parsec jet is detected (C and J0 in the left panel of Figure fig3 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports superluminal parsec-scale jet motion in the radio-quiet LINER galaxy KISSR872, arguing that even low-accretion radio-quiet AGN can host relativistic jets.

quotes.quote: We report superluminal jet motion with an apparent speed of _ app =1.65 0.57 in the radio-quiet (RQ) low ionisation nuclear emission line region (LINER) galaxy, KISSR872. This result comes from two epoch phase-referenced very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) observations at 5 GHz. The detection of bulk relativistic motion in the jet of this extremely radio faint AGN, with a total 1.4 GHz flux density of 5 mJy in the 5.4 resolution Very Large Array (VLA) FIRST survey image and 1.5 mJy in the 5  milli-arcsec resolution Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) image, is the first of its kind in a RQ LINER galaxy. The presence of relativistic jets in lower accretion rate objects like KISSR872, with an Eddington ratio of 0.04, reveals that even RQ AGN can harbor relativistic jets, and evidentiates their universality over a wide range of accretion powers.

KISSR872 is a type 2 LINER galaxy at the redshift of 0.08255 from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 13 as obtained in January 31, 2017 and listed in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) . It has a peak intensity at 1.4 GHz of 4.80 0.15  mJy beam ^ 1 and total flux density of 5.25 0.27  mJy in the VLA FIRST survey ( =5.4 ).

gmw_00005528.name: SPT2319 55
gmw_00005528.overview: SPT2319 55 is a gravitationally lensed, gas-rich dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.293 [arXiv:1809.01676]. It is undergoing rapid star formation and hosts a massive, blueshifted molecular outflow detected via OH absorption [arXiv:1809.01676].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SPT2319 55 as a lensed, gas-rich, rapidly star-forming high-redshift DSFG and detects a massive blueshifted molecular outflow in ALMA OH observations.

quotes.quote: We present observational evidence of a massive molecular wind being launched from SPT S J231921 5557.9 (SPT2319 55 hereafter), a DSFG observed when the Universe was only one billion years old. SPT2319 55 was discovered in the 2500deg ^2 South Pole Telescope survey on the basis of its thermal dust emission. Earlier observations of this source from the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) determined its redshift to be z_ source = 5.293 and showed that it is gravitationally lensed by an intervening foreground galaxy . As is typical for these objects, SPT2319 55 is both gas-rich, containing 1.2 10^ 10 of molecular gas, 1.2 10^8 of dust, and is forming stars very rapidly, SFR 790 yr ^ 1 (these values account for the lensing magnification).

We detect a molecular outflow from SPT2319 55, seen in blueshifted absorption against the bright dust continuum emission, a signature of outflowing molecular material (Figure 1).
