We fit the spectrum in Figure 1 with the sum of two velocity components, each consisting of two equal-amplitude Gaussian profiles separated by 520 , the separation of the two components of the OH doublet . As is common practice for low-redshift observations of OH, we assign the higher-frequency component of the doublet to the systemic velocity of the galaxy. Because the redshift of this source is known to better than 50 from other observations , we fix the velocity offset of one pair of Gaussian profiles to the systemic velocity. This component represents absorption due to gas from within the galaxy, with a fitted full-width-at-half-maximum (FWHM) linewidth of 330 80 .

gmw_00005529.name: Corkscrew Galaxy
gmw_00005529.overview: The Corkscrew Galaxy is a bent-tailed radio galaxy located in the Abell 3627 cluster [arXiv:2405.04374, arXiv:1412.8596]. It features an exceptionally long, helical radio tail [arXiv:2405.04374, arXiv:1412.8596] that exhibits intricate filamentary structures and oscillations [arXiv:2405.04374].

sources.summary: The paper reports ASKAP imaging of the Corkscrew Galaxy, describing a 570 kpc helical radio tail with collimation, oscillations, filaments, and a possible proto-lobe.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of intricate filamentary structure in and beyond the 28 \ (570 kpc) long, helical radio tail of the Corkscrew Galaxy (1610--60.5, ESO 137-G007), which resides in the X-ray bright cluster Abell 3627 ( D = 70 Mpc). Deep radio continuum data were obtained with wide-field Phased Array Feeds on the Australian Square Kilometer Array Pathfinder (ASKAP) at 944 MHz and 1.4 GHz. The Corkscrew Galaxy is located 15 \ north of the prominent wide-angle tail (WAT) radio galaxy 1610--60.8 (ESO 137-G006) near the cluster centre. While the bright (young) part of its radio tail is highly collimated, the faint (old) part shows increasing oscillation amplitudes, break-ups, and filaments. We find a stunning set of arc-shaped radio filaments beyond and mostly orthogonal to the collimated Corkscrew tail end, forming a partial bubble.

This may be the first detection of a "proto-lobe" seen in 3D MHD simulations by Nolting et al. (2019), formed by the face-on impact of the Corkscrew Galaxy with a shock front in the cluster outskirts. Interactions of the radio galaxy tail with the ICM are likely responsible for the tail collimation and shear forces within the ICM for its increasingly filamentary structure.

sources.summary: ESO 137-G 007 is presented as a corkscrew bent-tailed radio galaxy in the Norma cluster whose helical tails can probe cluster magnetic-field coherence through Faraday-rotation path differences.

quotes.quote: One promising new way to measure the coherence of the cluster magnetic field unambiguously on such scales is through the use of so-called `cork screw' BTs such as ESO 137-G 007 in the Norma cluster (Figure fig:helix ; left panel). `Corkscrew' BTs result when the radio galaxy spirals as it falls into the cluster potential giving rise to long helical tails with narrow pitch angles. In such tails the front and back parts of the helix are probing two different path-lengths through the ICM which only differ by 20 kpc in cases such as shown here ( d in the right panel of Figure fig:helix ). This path difference between the near and far side of the jets should result in a two distinct Faraday rotation measure (RM) values, due to the extra RM component ( RM) over the additional distance, d .

sources.summary: The paper describes B1610-60.5 as an exceptionally long head-tail radio galaxy whose radio emission aligns with A3627 X-ray structure and remains largely undistorted.

quotes.quote: The emission from the radio galaxy B1610-60.5 (Jones Mc Adam 1992) has been drawn into the right panel of Fig. 3 as well. It is one of the longest known head-tail galaxies (26 armin, 710 kpc). The radio emission aligns over nearly its full extent with the 3rd contour of the main X-ray component. The lack of distortion of the radiolobes of both radio galaxies and the compactness of the subclump imply that the suspected merger must be in an early stage.

gmw_00005530.overview: The radio galaxy 1610--60.8 is a wide-angle tail source located near the center of the Norma cluster [arXiv:2004.03203, arXiv:2405.04374]. It features bent radio lobes [arXiv:2004.03203, arXiv:astro-ph/9611099] that are linked by narrow, collimated synchrotron threads [arXiv:2004.03203, arXiv:2405.04374].

sources.summary: MeerKAT observations describe ESO 137-006 as a bright Norma-cluster radio galaxy whose bent lobes are linked by newly discovered collimated synchrotron threads, the main one extending about 80 kpc.

quotes.quote: We present MeerKAT 1000 MHz and 1400 MHz observations of a bright radio galaxy in the southern hemisphere, ESO 137-006. The galaxy lies at the centre of the massive and merging Norma galaxy cluster. The MeerKAT continuum images (rms 0.02 mJy/beam at 10 resolution) reveal new features that have never been seen in a radio galaxy before: collimated synchrotron threads of yet unknown origin, which link the extended and bent radio lobes of ESO 137-006. The most prominent of these threads stretches in projection for about 80 kpc and is about 1 kpc in width. The radio spectrum of the threads is steep, with a spectral index of up to 2 between 1000 and 1400 MHz.

ESO 137-006 (RA _ J2000 = 16:15:03.8, Dec _ J2000 = 60:54:26) is one of the most luminous galaxies in the Norma cluster and lies near the peak of the X-ray emission. It is one of the brightest radio galaxies in the southern sky (L _ 1.4 GHz = 2.5 10^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 ; Sun2009 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 1610--60.8 as the prominent WAT radio galaxy near the A3627 center, with MeerKAT-imaged lobes and synchrotron threads confirmed by ASKAP.

quotes.quote: The cluster centre, as determined from the peak of the X-ray distribution Boehringer1996,Tamura1998 , approximately coincides with the wide-angle tail (WAT) radio galaxy 1610--60.8 Ekers1970 , with host elliptical galaxy ESO 137-G006 ( \ = 5448 ). The X-ray derived gas temperature of 6.7 0.3 keV for the central region of A3627 Tamura1998 is consistent with its high velocity dispersion and among the highest known e.g., similar to A2256, Rottgering1994 .

Both cluster radio galaxies, 1610--60.8 and 1610--60.5, were studied in detail using the Fleurs Radio Telescope at 1415 MHz Christiansen1977 , the Molonglo Observatory Synthesis Telescope (MOST) at 843 MHz JonesMcAdam1992, JonesMcAdam1994 and the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) at 1360 and 2370 MHz JonesMcAdam1996 . Recent MeerKAT observations, focused only on the WAT radio galaxy 1610--60.8, delivered stunning new 1.0 GHz and 1.4 GHz radio continuum and spectral index images with an rms of 20 Jy beam ^ 1 at 10 \ resolution Ramatsoku2020 . Narrow synchrotron threads of unknown origin are found stretching between the two bent radio lobes, also visible in Fig. fig:a3627-field-labels .
We confirm the numerous synchrotron threads within and between the lobes of the neighbouring WAT radio galaxy 1610--60.8, recently revealed by MeerKAT Ramatsoku2020 .

sources.summary: The paper presents first high-resolution observations of 1610 608, showing characteristic WAT inner jets, no compact terminal structure, and polarization-angle evidence for a Faraday screen.

quotes.quote: The radio source was shown to have WAT-like structure by the low-resolution ATCA observations of Jones McAdam (1996); its proximity gives it a claim to be called the closest WAT source. Ours are the first high-resolution observations. Because we do not have short-baseline data, the large-scale structure of the source is not shown.

However, our observations (Fig. 1610 ) clearly show the characteristic inner jets of the WAT class. Neither jet appears to terminate in any particularly compact structure. The inner regions of the source are quite strongly polarized, and there is little evidence for depolarization between the two frequencies, but the position angles of the polarization vectors differ markedly between 5 and 8 GHz, particularly in the E plume, suggesting that a resolved Faraday screen is present.

sources.summary: The paper applies new radio-imaging algorithms to ESO 137-006 MeerKAT data, improving resolution and dynamic range around the radio galaxy and its complex emission.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We introduce the first AI-based framework for deep, super-resolution, wide-field radio-interferometric imaging, and demonstrate it on observations of the ESO 137-006 radio galaxy.
The resulting algorithms were deployed to form images of a wide field of view containing ESO 137-006, from 19 gigabytes of MeerKAT data at 1053 and 1399 MHz.

In this work, we propose a new parallelised and automated framework for wide-field high-resolution high-dynamic range monochromatic intensity imaging, which we use to revisit observations of the radio galaxy ESO 137-006, the loudest radio galaxy in the Norma cluster.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS1610-60.8 as a cD radio galaxy in A3627, coincident with a secondary X-ray peak and associated with bent 210 kpc radio lobes.

quotes.quote: The X-ray center is offset from the center of the cluster, the strong radio source PKS1610-60.8, one of the two cD galaxies. The latter coincides with the second central X-ray peak. The X-ray emission is not symmetric but extended towards the SE (left-bottom) corner. Subtracting a spherical symmetric model leaves a residual component ( right panel). This subcluster contains the wide-angle-tail radio galaxy PKS1610-60.8 whose large radio lobes of 8 arcmin ( 210 kpc) have a bending angle of 45 .

gmw_00005531.name: NGC 5987
gmw_00005531.overview: NGC 5987 is a luminous spiral galaxy of Sb morphology [arXiv:1008.2797]. It is one of the largest spiral galaxies in the nearby universe, with an estimated isophotal major diameter of 51.7 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1008.2797].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5987 as the closest luminous foreground galaxy to the absorber, describing its size, luminosity, spiral morphology, velocity separation, and possible circumgalactic connection.

quotes.quote: From all these known extended foreground sources, the luminous galaxy NGC 5987 is closest in impact parameter to the absorber. NGC 5987 [ ( J 2000) = 15^h39^m57.4^s, ( J 2000) = +58^ 04^ 46^ ] is at a projected distance of = 424 h^ 1 kpc from the sight line. The galaxy has a redshift of z = 0.0100 v _ HELIO = 3010 devaucouleurs91 which gives it a | v| = 71 systemic velocity with respect to the BLA - absorber. The galaxy has an estimated isophotal major diameter of D_ 25 = 51.7 kpc, and is one of the largest spiral galaxies in the nearby universe romanishin83 . Although the impact parameter is large, the small velocity separation and the large size of the galaxy makes it possible for the absorber to be in some way related to NGC 5987 's circumgalactic environment.

NGC 5987 is a spiral galaxy of Sb morphology with a B band magnitude of B = 12.72 or M_B - 5 log h = -20.4 devaucouleurs91 . This corresponds to a luminosity of L 2 L^* assuming M^*_B = -19.6 . No published spectrum is available for this galaxy.

gmw_00005532.name: A2204
gmw_00005532.overview: The central galaxy of the Abell 2204 cluster is a dusty brightest cluster galaxy that may be interacting with a nearby companion [arXiv:0902.4720, arXiv:astro-ph/0606305]. It hosts a modest, compact central radio source with a flat-spectrum nucleus [arXiv:0811.0743] and features irregular gas filaments [arXiv:0902.4720, arXiv:astro-ph/0606305].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1632+0534 as a modest compact central radio source in Abell 2204, with a flat-spectrum nucleus, north-south compact components, and limited angular extent.

quotes.quote: Although noted in a few surveys, the radio source at the centre of the Abell 2204 cluster, TXS1630+056 = 1632+0534, has received little attention. This is understandable given its relatively modest flux and small angular size compared to most radio galaxies in cooling core clusters. At 8.4 GHz J1632+0534 consists of a compact nucleus with flux density 16.7 mJy with weaker compact components to the north and south. The total flux density is 22 mJy. The nucleus has a flat spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper describes the central galaxy in A2204 as having filamentary gas, possible interaction with a secondary galaxy, and cool gas pulled outward by old radio bubbles.

quotes.quote: In A2204 the gas shows 3 or 4 filaments reaching radii of 10 , three of which lie towards `ghost bubbles' seen in X-ray imaging by Sanders et al. Regions with much lower ratios highlight active star formation and are often kinematically quiescent (FWHM <200 ). Our findings suggest that the three clusters may be captured in different stages of the `cold feedback' cycle of Pizzolato Soker, with A1664 in a short-lived phase of extreme cooling and star formation prior to an AGN heating event; PKS 0745-191 in an outburst state with the AGN accreting from a cool gas disk, and A2204 in a later phase in which cool gas is dragged out of the galaxy by the buoyant rise of old radio bubbles.

In Wilman et al. (2006), A2204 was found to have an irregular morphology consisting of a several weakly-defined filaments extending from the nuclear regions, possibly coincident with dust features seen in an Hubble Space Telescope optical image. It is thus plausible that the two galaxies are interacting.

sources.summary: The paper describes the A2204 CCG as dusty and possibly interacting, with irregular filamentary H emission, disturbed radio structure, and high nuclear line widths.

quotes.quote: Abell 2204 lies at z=0.1514 and has an observed H slit luminosity of 10^ 42 (uncorrected for reddening), although the inferred visible star formation rate is modest ( 1 Crawford et al. 1999). The system is also a strong emitter of near-infrared H _ 2 ro-vibrational emission (Edge et al. 2002), and the CO(1-0) emission from IRAM 30-m observations implies a molecular gas mass of 2.3 0.6 10^ 10 (Edge 2001). Chandra X-ray observations reveal that the cluster has a disturbed core morphology, suggestive of a recent merger, with cold fronts at radii of 28 kpc and 54.5 , and the central radio source is also disturbed with three components within 10 arcsec, aligned roughly N-S (Sanders, Fabian Taylor 2005). The latter shows that the CCG is dusty and possibly interacting with galaxy 1, which is 15 away in projection at a relative velocity of 250 (see Jenner 1974 and section 5.1). The H emission also has an irregular morphology with a number of filaments emanating from the nuclear regions, possibly coincident with the dust obscuration.

gmw_00005533.name: Zw8193
gmw_00005533.overview: The core of Zw8193 is a complex system containing a brightest cluster galaxy with multiple nearby companion galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0606305]. One companion, B3 1715+425, is a compact radio galaxy hosting an active supermassive black hole that has been tidally stripped during its passage through the cluster core [arXiv:1606.04067].

sources.summary: The paper identifies B3 1715+425 as a compact radio galaxy offset from the ZwCl 8193 BCG, arguing it is a tidally stripped remnant hosting a nearly naked SMBH.

quotes.quote: During a systematic search for supermassive black holes (SMBHs) not in galactic nuclei, we identified the compact symmetric radio source B3 1715+425 with an emission-line galaxy offset 8.5 kpc from the nucleus of the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in the redshift z = 0.1754 cluster ZwCl 8193. B3 1715+425 is too bright (brightness temperature T_ b 3 10^ 10 K at observing frequency = 7.6 GHz ) and too luminous (1.4 GHz luminosity L_ 1.4 GHz 10^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 ) to be powered by anything but a SMBH, but its host galaxy is much smaller ( 0.9 kpc 0.6 kpc full width between half-maximum points) and optically fainter (R-band absolute magnitude M_ 18.2 ) than any other radio galaxy. Its high radial velocity v_ 1860 km s ^ 1 relative to the BCG, continuous ionized wake extending back to the BCG nucleus, and surrounding debris indicate that the radio galaxy was tidally shredded passing through the BCG core, leaving a nearly naked supermassive black hole fleeing from the BCG with space velocity v 2000 km s ^ 1 .

We conclude that the correct optical identification of the radio source B3 1715+425 is only the circled object, and not the BCG nucleus 2 85 8.5 kpc to the south or other tidal debris. The 2MASX infrared source is a marginally significant 0 43 north of the optical BCG nucleus, which is consistent with the = 2.16 m source being a blend of emission from the BCG and material to the north. The compact galaxy that we call the unique optical identification of B3 1715+425 is listed in the HLA catalog with F606W AB magnitude m = 21.417 0.048 measured through a 0 3 radius circular aperture.

sources.summary: The paper presents Zw8193 as a complex CCG core with multiple nearby galaxies, three H blobs, varying velocity, and likely disturbance by a small cluster galaxy.

quotes.quote: The core of Zw 8193 is complex in both space and velocity and initial optical spectroscopy of this cluster yielded discrepant redshifts for the emission ( z=0.1829 0.0001 ) and absorption line ( z=0.1725 0.0001 ) components (Allen et al. 1992). Fig. fig:Zw8193 shows that there are three galaxies within 2--3 arcsec of the CCG; UKIRT long-slit spectroscopy revealed Pa emission in and between the CCG and its neighbour to the north, with an apparent velocity difference of 400-500 (Edge et al. 2002). The benefit of higher spatial and spectral resolution OASIS IFU data now reveals the full complexity of the system. The H emission consists of three distinct `blobs', one centred on the CCG, one on a galaxy to the north and one slightly offset from the latter to the west. The velocity ranges over 250 (relative to z=0.1825 ) and reconstructing the placement of the 1.2 wide N-S slit used for the UKIRT observations shows the consistency of the Pa and H velocity fields.

gmw_00005534.name: 3C35
gmw_00005534.overview: 3C 35 is a giant Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.067 [arXiv:2101.07021, arXiv:1302.1585]. It features a symmetric double-lobe structure with detected core emission [arXiv:2101.07021, arXiv:1003.0623], which appears to drive and eject surrounding fossil-group X-ray gas [arXiv:1302.1585].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT and VLA observations of 3C 35, describing it as a giant FR II radio galaxy with symmetric lobes, spectral ageing analysis, and no evidence for episodic jet activity.

quotes.quote: The galaxy 3C 35 is a giant FR II class radio galaxy and has a redshift of z=0.0670 Sp85 . The projected linear size of 3C 35 is 869.2 kpc which makes 3C 35 a giant radio galaxy Is99 . The radio power of 3C 35 at 178 MHz is P_ 178 MHz = 10^ 24.99 W Hz^ 1 sr^ 1 Ow89 .

Radio morphology of the source 3C 35 is a classic example of a large-sized FR II type radio galaxy extended along the north-south direction. From Figure 3C35 , it is noticed that 3C 35 has a similar type of lobe structure along both sides of the galaxy core. The distance of the northern and southern lobes from the core of the galaxy is nearly the same. The radio emission of the southern lobe is brighter in comparison to the northern lobe.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C35 as a classical FRII giant radio source with clear double lobes, detected core emission, and an old synchrotron age.

quotes.quote: The source 3C35 is a classical double radio source with a regular FRII structure ; its principal characteristics are listed in Table source . It has been previously studied at frequencies of 608 MHz, 1.4 and 5 GHz with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) ( vanBreugel1982 ; Jaegers1987 ; Schoenmakers2000 ). The VLA radio images at 74 and 327 MHz of the radio galaxy 3C35 are shown respectively in the left and in the right panel of Fig. 3c35cntrnat ; sensitivities and resolutions are listed in Table obs . In the contours map the regular double-lobe structure of the source is distinctly clear, as well as the presence of the two hot-spots. In the image the core and surrounding low brightness emission have both been detected.
For 3C35 the estimated age is about 143 20 Myr while for 3C223 is about 72 4 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 35 as a nearby giant FR II radio galaxy whose lobes appear to drive a fossil-group X-ray gas belt, linking its radio activity to AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: We report results from XMM-Newton and Chandra observations of the nearby ( z=0.067 ) giant radio galaxy 3C 35. We find evidence for an X-ray emitting gas belt, orthogonal to and lying between the lobes of 3C 35, which we interpret as fossil-group gas driven outwards by the expanding radio lobes. We also detect weak emission from a second, more extended group-type environment, as well as inverse-Compton X-ray emission from the radio lobes.

Furthermore, the radio source is powerful enough to eject galaxy-scale gas out to distances of 100 kpc, and the ages of the two features are comparable ( t_ synch 140 Myr, t_ belt 80 Myr). 3C 35 seems to be the first case where we see the X-ray halo of a fossil group being ejected following an interaction with the radio structure.

gmw_00005535.name: H-ATLAS J131611.5+281219
gmw_00005535.overview: H-ATLAS J131611.5+281219 is a gravitationally lensed starburst galaxy at redshift z = 2.95 [arXiv:2012.01448]. Reconstructions of the source plane suggest it contains two compact, rotating components that are likely merging [arXiv:2012.01448].

sources.summary: The paper studies the lensed z=2.95 starburst H-ATLAS J131611.5+281219, reconstructing its dust and molecular gas, compact merging components, dense core, and possible gas inflow.

quotes.quote: Exploiting the sensitivity of the IRAM NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) and its ability to process large instantaneous bandwidths, we have studied the morphology and other properties of the molecular gas and dust in the starburst galaxy, H-ATLAS J131611.5+281219 (HerBS-89a), at z =2.95. High angular resolution ( 0 3 ) images reveal a partial 1 0 diameter Einstein ring in the dust continuum emission and the molecular emission lines of ^ 12 CO (9-8) and H_2O (2_ 02 1_ 11 ) . Together with lower angular resolution ( 0 6 ) images, we report the detection of a series of molecular lines including the three fundamental transitions of the molecular ion OH^+ , namely (1_1-0_1) , (1_2-0_1) and (1_0-0_1) , seen in absorption; the molecular ion CH^+(1-0) seen in absorption (and tentatively in emission); two transitions of amidogen ( NH_2 ), namely (2_ 02 1_ 11 ) and (2_ 20 2_ 11 ) seen in emission; and HCN(11-10) and/or NH(1_2-0_1) seen in absorption.

Modelling the lensing of HerBS-89a, we reconstruct the dust continuum (magnified by a factor 5.0 ) and molecular emission lines (magnified by 4 - 5 ) in the source plane, which probe scales of 0 1 (or 800 pc). The ^ 12 CO(9-8) and H_2O (2_ 02 1_ 11 ) emission lines have comparable spatial and kinematic distributions; the source-plane reconstructions do not clearly distinguish between a one-component and a two-component scenario, but the latter, which reveals two compact rotating components with sizes of 1 kpc , that are likely merging, more naturally accounts for the broad line widths observed in HerBS-89a. In the core of HerBS-89a, very dense gas with n_ H_2 10^ 7-9 cm^ 3 is revealed by the NH_2 emission lines and the possible HCN(11-10) absorption line. HerBS-89a is a powerful star forming galaxy with a molecular gas mass of M_ mol = (2.1 0.4) 10^ 11 M_ , an infrared luminosity of L_ IR = (4.6 0.4) 10^ 12 L_ , and a dust mass of M_ dust = (2.6 0.2) 10^ 9 M_ , yielding a dust-to-gas ratio _ GDR 80 .

gmw_00005536.name: AGN.1317
gmw_00005536.overview: AGN.1317 is an active galactic nucleus located near the edge of a high-redshift galaxy cluster [arXiv:1206.1513]. Its spectrum displays broad-line and narrow-line regions, as well as an AGN-driven outflow reaching velocities in excess of 1000 km/s [arXiv:1206.1513].

sources.summary: The paper presents J- and H-band spectroscopy of AGN.1317, finding broad-line and narrow-line features plus blue-shifted [O III] wings interpreted as a powerful AGN-driven outflow near the cluster edge.

quotes.quote: Three of them are found within 1.5 ^ of the radio galaxy, including AGN.1317. We present the spectroscopic observations of AGN.1317 both in J and H bands. As mentioned in Section sec_obs , it was not possible to perform a proper flux calibration of the J-band data. To obtain a rough calibration, we re-scaled the J-band data to match the H-band continuum by a simple visual comparison of the two spectral bands. The spectrum of AGN.1317 (Fig. agn ) shows clear broad features, as (FWHM = 4800 500 km/s) and (FWHM = 5520 250 km/s), associated with the broad line region (BLR), and forbidden lines primarily associated with the AGN narrow line region (NLR): the [ OIII ] , [ NII ] and [ SII ] doublets.

The [ OIII ] doublet primarily traces the gas in the NLR ionized by the AGN, thus the most probable explanation is that the strong outflow might be driven by the AGN radiation pressure itself. The origin of the wind from the AGN is also supported by the huge velocities (the out-flowing gas reaches an excess of 1000 km/s) which cannot be explained by supernovae-driven outflows nath09 . This is the first time that such powerful AGN-driven winds are detected in source located at the edges of a high redshift cluster (the projected distance between the central radio galaxy and AGN. 1317 being 780 kpc).

gmw_00005537.name: SDSS1115+0544
gmw_00005537.overview: SDSS1115+0544 is a bulge-dominated, early-type galaxy whose pre-flare spectral energy distribution fits an elliptical template [arXiv:1902.04163]. Weak emission-line ratios classify the host as a LINER [arXiv:1902.04163], and its broad emission lines have nearly disappeared in recent observations [arXiv:2111.12729].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes SDSS1115+0544 as a quiescent, bulge-dominated early-type galaxy whose pre-flare SED fits an elliptical template and whose weak emission-line ratios place it among LINERs.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: SDSS115+0544 has the sky position of RA = 11:15:36.57 and DEC = 05:44:49.73 Equinox J2000.0 and at redshift of 0.08995.

quotes.quote: Before SDSS1115+0544 displayed optical and infrared flare in 2015, its quiescent state has multi-band photometry in ugriz (SDSS), YJHK (UKIDSS), and 3.4 ( W1 ), 4.6 ( W2 ), 12 ( W3 , not detected) and 12 m ( W4 , not detected) (ALLWISE) and 24 m ( Spitzer ). SDSS1115+0544 is a fairly bright, bulge dominated galaxy with g \ magnitude of 17.96 and 17.06 mag (AB) respectively. Its pre-flare, panchromatic photometry is shown in the bottom panel of Figure fig:host , and at the optical/near-IR wavelength, is well fit by the spectral energy distribution (SED) template of an elliptical galaxy Polletta2007 . In its quiescent state, the host galaxy was not detected in the near-UV (2267 ) by GALEX ( >23.5 mag), in the WISE W3 and W4 bands, and in the FIRST survey ( 1 = 0.15 mJy).

SDSS1115+0544 falls within the lower-right region of the O III /H_ N II/H_ diagram, i.e. Baldwin, Phillips \ Telervich (BPT) diagram. The very weak H and H lines and the corresponding line ratios indicate that SDSS1115+0544 is aligned with the definition of a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission Line Region (LINER), based on the diagnostics by Kewley2006,Cid2010 . The SDSS DR14 archive also classified this object as a LINER.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1115+0544 as a MIRONG source previously considered a turn-on AGN, but whose broad emission nearly disappeared by the 2021 follow-up.

quotes.quote: Besides, there are also two spectra acquired for J1115+0544 from Keck and LAMOST ( Yan2019 ). In fact, J1115+0544, another object in our sample, has been thought of as a reliable bona fide turn-on AGN because its \ has steadily been there for at least two years (2016-2018; Yan2019 ). However, the latest observation performed at UT 2021-01-07 shows that its \ has almost disappeared, to our surprise.

In particular, the \ of J0205+0004, J1549+3327, and J1632+2345 has vanished rapidly within one year and J1115+0544 has also returned back to the flux level of SDSS though the strong broad \ has remained for at least 2 yr.

gmw_00005538.name: J0500--4242
gmw_00005538.overview: J0500--4242 is a giant radio source hosted by a quasar candidate at a photometric redshift of approximately 1.1 [arXiv:2111.08807]. It is classified as an FR II-naked radio galaxy because its outer lobes are barely resolved and show little to no indication of a radio tail or bridge [arXiv:2111.08807].

sources.summary: The paper presents J0500--4242 as the second-largest new giant radio source, hosted by a quasar candidate, probably the largest known giant radio quasar above redshift one.

quotes.quote: The second-largest is J0500--4242, hosted by the =18.9 mag QSO candidate DES J050007.44--424238.7 at z_ phot =1.1 (based on values of 0.85, 1.1, and 1.5 from ), giving an LLS of 2.9 Mpc. In the six literature compilations (Sect. sect:compar ) there is only one GRS with both z>1 and LLS > 2 Mpc, the QSO FBQS J0204--0944 at z_ spec =1.004 with LLS=2.1 Mpc, making our new find of J0500--4242 the largest GRQ known at z>1 . We classify the latter as ``FR IInaked" since the outer lobes are barely resolved with little or no indication of a radio tail or bridge pointing towards the host. Future higher-resolution and sensitivity radio observations should show some extended structure around these hotspots.

gmw_00005539.name: J0133--4655
gmw_00005539.overview: J0133--4655 is a remnant-type giant radio galaxy hosted by the galaxy DES J013320.80--465501.4 [arXiv:2111.08807]. The system features diffuse radio lobes, with the southern lobe being closer to the host and more luminous than the northern lobe [arXiv:2111.08807].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0133--4655 as a remnant-type giant radio source with a DES galaxy host, diffuse lobes, and an asymmetric southern lobe consistent with statistical expectations.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:rrgs shows two examples: at left is J0133--4655, hosted by the =20.3 mag galaxy DES J013320.80--465501.4 at z_ phot =0.65 with LLS=1.04 Mpc. The galaxy near the centre of the South (S) lobe is barely detected in WISE and is considered a chance superposition. The fact that the S lobe is closer to the host and 1.4 times more luminous than the North lobe, is consistent with statistical expectation .

gmw_00005540.name: J0925+1403
gmw_00005540.overview: The starburst galaxy J0925+1403 is characterized by a low metallicity of approximately one-fifth solar [arXiv:1606.00053]. Its spectral energy distribution is dominated by strong emission lines and is well fitted using a Small Magellanic Cloud attenuation law [arXiv:1606.00053].

sources.summary: The paper models J0925+1403 as the first LyC leaker example, fitting SDSS and GALEX photometry with nebular emission, SMC attenuation, low metallicity, and strong emission lines.

quotes.quote: Figure 2 shows for illustration the SED fit of the first of our LyC leakers, J0925+1403 from I16a, to the broad-band photometry from the SDSS and the two GALEX bands. The fits are compatible with the more detailed SED fits to the observed COS and SDSS spectra discussed in I16ab. The fit has been obtained with a version of the Hyperz code including nebular emission, described in and which has extensively been used to fit large samples of high- z LBGs cf. dBSS14 . Since the attenuation law and the metallicity are constrained/measured (I16a), we have used the SMC law and a metallicity=1/5 solar, the closest value available for the models. Clearly the SDSS photometry is dominated by strong emission lines in bands at 5500 , and the SED is well fitted with the average emission line ratios taken from (here for 1/5 solar metallicity) and adopted in our models.

gmw_00005541.name: Ion2
gmw_00005541.overview: Ion2 is a compact, high-redshift star-forming galaxy at a redshift of approximately 3.2 [arXiv:1502.04708, arXiv:1606.00053]. It is a confirmed Lyman continuum emitter with negligible dust extinction [arXiv:1606.00443, arXiv:1904.07941], characterized by low metallicity [arXiv:1606.00053], strong optical emission lines [arXiv:1606.00053, arXiv:1904.07941], and a structure resolved into two distinct ultraviolet components [arXiv:1502.04708, arXiv:1507.06648].

sources.summary: The paper presents Ion2 as a z=3.218 LyC candidate with ultraviolet excess, P(fesc=0)=0, tentative blueward flux, and two close components.

quotes.quote: Another source at z=3.218 (CANDELS ID 18320, guo13 ) with a significant excess in the ultraviolet spectrum has been identified in the same field with a U band magnitude U=26.85 0.06 and color ( U-B )= 1.66 0.07 . The B band data used here come from the ultradeep VIMOS observation of GOODS-S, Nonino et al. in preparation. At this redshift the U band probes the wavelength range <948 rest-frame, and any significant signal suggests a LyC leakage. From the observed ( U-B ) color and following the methodology described above, we derive P(fesc=0) =0.0 (Fig. CS2 ), which confirms that this source is a LyC candidate (named here Ion2 ). This result also supports the non-zero emitting flux blueward of the Lyman limit that was tentatively detected in the VIMOS low-resolution spectrum, R=180 (see Fig. CS1 ), but with a low S/N 2 balestra10 .

By examining the morphology of the system in more detail, two distinct objects are visible at a separation of 0.2^ in the HST/ACS imaging (corresponding to a physical separation of 1.5kpc if they are at the same redshift, Fig. CS2 ), both of them showing a drop blueward of the V_ 606 band. In the following, we refer with a) to the brighter and with b) to the fainter component. They have been detected as a single object both in the optical ACS catalog v2.0 ( giava04 and see vanz09 ) and in the near-infrared CANDELS catalog guo13 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Ion2 as a z=3.218 LyC-leaking galaxy similar to the z~0.3 leakers in UV luminosity, mass, metallicity, compactness, ionization, and strong emission lines.

quotes.quote: In many respects, the properties of the compact z 0.3 leaking sources are very comparable to those of the z=3.218 Lyman continuum leaker Ion2 found by and . First Ion2 is also bright in the UV, M_ UV =-21 which is M^ _ UV (z=3) . Its low stellar mass, 1.6 10^9 , is comparable to = (0.2 - 4) 10^9 \ (a median of 1 10^9 ) of our five sources (I16ab). The metallicity of Ion2 , determined from rest-frame UV and optical emission lines is 1/6 solar, compared to (0.1-0.2) solar. The non-detection of \ translates to a 2- lower limit of a high ratio O _ 32 >10 for Ion2 , even higher than for our objects.

Furthermore, Ion2 is also a compact source with a size 300 70 pc cf. de-Barros2016An-extreme-O-II . Interestingly the two latter properties are found a posteriori , since the source was selected from a peculiar color selection. Different selection criteria finding LyC leakers may thus pick up sources with similar properties. Finally, Ion 2 shows strong rest-frame optical emission lines, e.g. \ EW(5007) =1103 60 \ or even larger by a factor 2 if corrected for a large escape fraction of ionizing photons, compared to EW(5007) =900-1260 \ of the z 0.3 leakers (I16ab).

sources.summary: The paper compares Ion2 with Q1549-C25, finding Ion2 more extreme in LyC escape, similarly dust-free, and stronger in Ly and [OIII] emission.

quotes.quote: The only other z 3 galaxy with a direct spectroscopic detection of uncontaminated LyC emission is Ion2 at z=3.21 debarros2016,vanzella2016 . It is therefore useful to compare the properties of Ion2 and Q1549-C25. In terms of the escape of LyC emission, Ion2 appears more extreme than Q1549-C25.

Ion2 , like Q1549-C25, is characterized by negligible dust extinction, and therefore f_ esc,rel ( LyC )= f_ esc ( LyC ) . debarros2016 report a significantly lower escape fraction for Ion2 ( f_ esc ( LyC )=0.64 0.1 ), but assume a more transparent model for IGM opacity inoue2014 , and L_ 900 /L_ 1500 =1/3 . Regardless of the particular IGM model assumed, both sources are characterized by significantly higher f_ 900 /f_ 1500 values and f_ esc ( LyC ) distributions than average at z 3 (Steidel et al., in prep), suggesting large variations in these quantities among high-redshift star-forming galaxies. Along with a higher f_ 900 /f_ 1500 , Ion2 also has significantly larger rest-frame Ly and [ OIII ] 5007 emission equivalent widths than Q1549-C25, with W_ Ly ,0 =94 \ and W_ [ OIII ] ,0 =1500 . In addition, no low-ionization metal absorption lines are detected in the rest-UV spectrum of Ion2 , suggesting a lower covering fraction of neutral gas than the spectrum of Q1549-C25, in which several low-ionization lines are detected.

sources.summary: Ion2 is a z=3.212 LyC-emitter candidate in GOODS-S/CANDELS, resolved into two UV blobs, with direct ionizing-photon detection, low metallicity, high ionization parameter, and no clear AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: We investigate the physical properties of one of the best Lyman continuum emitter candidate at z=3.212 found in the GOODS-S/CANDELS field with photometric coverage from U to MIPS 24 \ band and VIMOS/VLT and MOSFIRE/Keck spectroscopy. These observations allow us to derive physical properties such as stellar mass, star-formation rate, age of the stellar population, dust attenuation, metallicity, and ionization parameter, and to determine how these parameters are related to the Lyman continuum emission. Investigation of the UV spectrum confirms a direct spectroscopic detection of the Lyman continuum emission with S/N>5 .

Ion2 is composed by two blobs separated by 0.2'' (see Figure fig:fig1 Created with the Rainbow Navigator tool, https://rainbow.fis.ucm.es/Rainbow_navigator_public/ ) each one with a half-light radius Re 0.3 kpc, or less than 0.1'' at z=3.212 .

\ is an object at z=3.212 composed of two distinct blobs, nucleated and resolved in the rest-frame UV, indicating emission from star-forming regions. \ is also a candidate Lyman continuum emitter with U band flux consistent with a leakage of ionizing photon ( P( =0)<10^ 4 ), as reported in V15. Contamination from foreground galaxies can be ruled out.

sources.summary: Ion2 is a z=3.2121 CDFS LyC emitter with high escape fraction; X-Shooter finds a triple-peaked profile, narrow high-ionization lines, broad emission suggesting young Wolf-Rayet stars, and high O32.

quotes.quote: Ion2 is a well known LyC emitter at z=3.2121 lying in the CDFS showing an escape fraction higher than 50 \ vanz15,vanz16b . Here we present new VLT/X-Shooter observations that improve (in terms of depth, spectral resolution and wavelength coverage) our previous analysis debarros16 . X-Shooter observations Ion2 was observed during November 2018 for a total integration time of 5 hours under optimal seeing conditions, typically 0.5 - 0.7 arcsec.

In the case of Ion2 the relative contribution of nebular and stellar components is not measurable. However, the presence of a broad \ emission profile suggests a spectrum dominated by a young stellar population containing hot Wolf-Rayet stars with main-sequence lifetimes less than 5 Myr JC19 . Differently from the previous analysis based on a much shallower Keck/MOSFIRE spectrum in which the optical \ and \ lines were not detected debarros16 , here we measure a rest-frame equivalent width (EW) of EW( ) 100 , O32 = 9.18_ 1.32 ^ +1.82 and / = 8.55_ 1.41 ^ +1.96 (see Table tab ). While the \ profile further confirms Ion2 to be a genuine LyC emitter, the detailed geometry of the LyC emission and the origin of the ionising radiation is still unknown vanz16b .

gmw_00005542.name: SDSS J105530.40+132117.7
gmw_00005542.overview: SDSS J105530.40+132117.7 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a robust extreme Population A source [arXiv:2001.08765]. This object exhibits a high accretion ratio and displays clear blueshifted emission [arXiv:2001.08765].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J105530.40+132117.7 as the sole robust xA source in the host-contaminated sample, with high accretion-ratio selection and clear blueshifted emission.

quotes.quote: As mentioned above, only one source (SDSS J105530.40+132117.7) is confirmed as xA in the full HG sample after SSP analysis, applying the selection criterion \ 1.2 . This source will be individually discussed in disc . The restriction to 1.2 is operational, to avoid contamination from a fraction of borderline objects that may not be really xA: since typical uncertainties are 0.1 at 1 \ confidence level, the presence of ``imitators'' should be reduced by 95 \ the number expected with the limit at =1. Therefore, source SDSS J105530.40+132117.7 should be considered true xA and analysed as such at a confidence level 4 .
We see one clear example of blue shifted \ in the source SDSS J105530.40+132117.7, which has the highest \ in our sample. Only this object appears to be a bona fide xA source.

gmw_00005543.name: Haro 14
gmw_00005543.overview: Haro 14 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy that exhibits a pronounced stellar asymmetry and a lopsided continuum distribution [arXiv:1703.04672, arXiv:2108.09107]. Its extended star formation includes a current starburst, intermediate-age clusters, and a several-gigayear-old underlying host galaxy [arXiv:1703.04672, arXiv:2108.09107].

sources.summary: The paper studies Haro 14 with VIMOS integral-field spectroscopy, mapping its extended star formation, multiple stellar populations, inhomogeneous extinction, shocks, and irregular velocity field.

quotes.quote: We aim to probe the morphology, stellar content, and kinematics, along with the nebular excitation and ionization mechanism, in the BCG Haro 14 by means of integral field observations. We observed Haro 14 at the Very Large Telescope, working with the Visible Multi-Object Spectrograph. From these data we build maps in continuum and in the brighter emission lines, produce line-ratio maps (interstellar extinction, density, and diagnostic-line ratios), and obtain the velocity and velocity dispersion fields. We also generate the integrated spectrum of the major H ii regions and young stellar clusters identified in the maps to determine reliable physical parameters and oxygen abundances. We find as follows: i) the current star formation in Haro 14 is spatially extended with the major H ii regions placed along a linear (chain-like) structure, elongated in the north-south direction, and in a horseshoe-like curvilinear feature that extends about 760 pc eastward; the continuum emission is more concentrated and peaks close to the galaxy center; ii) two different episodes of star formation are present in the central galaxy regions: the recent starburst, with ages 6 Myrs and the intermediate-age clusters, with ages between 10 and 30 Myrs; these stellar components rest on a several Gyr old underlying host galaxy; iii) the H /H pattern is inhomogeneous, with excess color values varying from E(B-V) =0.04 up to E(B-V) =1.09; iv) shocks play a significant role in the galaxy; and v) the velocity field displays a complicated pattern with regions of material moving toward us in the east and north galaxy areas.

sources.summary: The paper maps Haro 14 with MUSE, finding an asymmetric stellar distribution, a bright cluster candidate, multiple stellar populations, and lopsidedness consistent with mergers or interactions.

quotes.quote: The stellar distribution shows a pronounced asymmetry; the intensity peak in continuum is not centered with respect to the underlying stellar host but is displaced by about 500 pc southwest. At the position of the continuum peak we find a bright stellar cluster that with M _ v =-12.18 appears as a strong super stellar cluster candidate. We also find a highly asymmetric, blue, but nonionizing stellar component that occupies almost the whole eastern part of the galaxy. We conclude that there are at least three different stellar populations in Haro 14: the current starburst of about 6 Myr; an intermediate-age component of between ten and several hundred million years; and a red and regular host of several gigayears. The pronounced lopsidedness in the continuum and also in the color maps, and the presence of numerous stellar clusters, are consistent with a scenario of mergers or interactions acting in Haro 14.

gmw_00005544.name: 1ES 0120+340
gmw_00005544.overview: 1ES 0120+340 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=0.272 [arXiv:2311.12695] that is classified as a potential extreme blazar candidate [arXiv:2311.12695]. The source exhibits month-scale variability, showing changes in both its flux and its spectral index [arXiv:astro-ph/0103343].

sources.summary: The paper studies 1ES 0120+340 as an extreme blazar candidate with hard X-ray and GeV spectra, available AstroSat observations, SED fitting, and unsuccessful single-shock modeling.

quotes.quote: We present a spectral study of four eHBLs, namely 1ES 0120+340, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 1741+196, and one HBL 1ES 2322-409 using new X-ray data from AstroSat, together with quasi-simultaneous Fermi-LAT and other archival multi-frequency data.

This paper reports on recent observations carried out using \ and \ of five sources: 1ES 0120+340 (redshift z=0.272 ), RGB J0710+591 ( z=0.125 ), 1ES 1101-232 ( z=0.186 ), 1ES 1741+196 ( z=0.084 ), and 1ES 2322-409 ( z=0.1736 ), each displaying a unique range of spectral characteristics. Among these, RGB J0710+591 and 1ES 1101-232 are well-known for being extreme TeV sources with hard intrinsic TeV spectra. Although TeV data is unavailable for 1ES 0120+340, it presents itself as a potential \ candidate with hard X-ray and GeV spectra.

1ES 0120+340 The fits to 1ES 0120+340 are shown in Fig. fig:models0120 , while the model parameters are given in Tab. tab:models0120 . The fits to the data are generally good. Differences occur in the VHE ray domain, which could become an important discriminator should this source be established as a VHE source in future observations. Modelling 1ES 0120+340 with the \ model assuming acceleration on a single shock did not yield a satisfactory result.

sources.summary: The paper reports month-scale flux and spectral-index changes in 1ES 0120+340 and finds that both epochs fit a single power-law model only with excess absorption.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0120+340 This source was observed twice in a month, and was about 20 weaker during the second observation. The best fits are shown in Fig. 4. In both epochs, a good fit is obtained with the single power--law model, but with a free absorption well in excess of the galactic value, at more than 99.9 confidence (see Fig. 5). As shown in Fig. 5 (and also indicated by the different ratio of MECS/LECS countrates), the spectral index has varied significantly, so it was not possible to sum the observations to enhance the S/N ratio.

gmw_00005545.name: M1423
gmw_00005545.overview: M1423 is a brightest cluster galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.545 [arXiv:1504.00598]. It features a luminous, extended central ultraviolet source with a compact nuclear object, and its infrared-estimated star formation rate indicates it is an obscured starburst galaxy [arXiv:1504.00598].

sources.summary: The paper identifies M1423 as a luminous UV BCG with extended central UV light, a compact nuclear object, and an infrared star-formation rate exceeding the unobscured UV rate.

quotes.quote: M1423 has a bright but extended central source sitting in an elliptical distribution of fainter UV light. It is also a luminous UV source in the observer frame, with rest-frame far UV detections of 22.0 0.2 and 23.1 0.6 AB mag (Table table:GALEX_NVSS ). Note that for a BCG at z=0.545 , the NUV and FUV bandpasses of GALEX cover mean rest-frame wavelengths of 147 and 98 nm respectively. In the F275W image, rest-frame far-UV ( 180 nm), which is the bluest image CLASH obtained for this cluster, there is an elongated (0.3-0.5 arcsecond) nuclear object located precisely with the BCG, oriented about 35 degrees W of N. Its IR estimated SFR rate of 46 3 2012ApJ...747...29R is 3 times the unobscured UV rate in Table 2, an IR excess typical of obscured starburst galaxies.

gmw_00005546.name: R1347
gmw_00005546.overview: R1347 is an active brightest cluster galaxy situated within a highly luminous X-ray cluster [arXiv:1504.00598]. The galaxy features a cool core with evidence of a merger, alongside a bright star-forming region located east-southeast of its nucleus [arXiv:1504.00598].

sources.summary: The paper describes R1347 as a well-known active BCG in a very luminous X-ray cluster, with a cool core, merger evidence, and modest UV star-forming structure near its nucleus.

quotes.quote: R1347 is a very well-known active BCG in what was once the most luminous X-ray cluster known 1995A&A...299L...9S and has a cool core with evidence for a merger 2010ApJ...716..739M . It does not show a spectacular UV filament system, at least not as spectacular as other CLASH BCGs in this category. But it does have a very bright SF region just to the ESE of the nucleus, and a faint filament on the opposite side of the nucleus.

gmw_00005547.name: A 4038 11
gmw_00005547.overview: A 4038 11, also known as IC5358, is a cD and brightest cluster radio galaxy located in the A4038 cluster [arXiv:1109.3261, arXiv:1808.04057]. It is offset from the cluster's X-ray peak and is connected by a diffuse radio bridge to a nearby radio relic [arXiv:1109.3261, arXiv:1808.04057].

sources.summary: The paper identifies A 4038 11 with the cD galaxy in A4038, subtracts its radio contribution, and discusses the cD as a possible but unconfirmed progenitor of the relic plasma.

quotes.quote: The southern cross marks a radio source identified with the cD galaxy in A4038 (A 4038 11 , SL01). In Fig. 2 (left), shown in grey-scale is the DSS R band image of the A4038 field. The cD galaxy can be clearly seen in the R band image.

The radio images of A4038 relic indicate a diffuse bridge connecting the relic to the cD. The bridge cannot be an active jet considering that it has a steep spectral index and is not detectable at higher frequencies (young relativistic plasma in the jet would have a flat spectrum). The cD galaxy could be the progenitor of the relic but the evidence is not sufficient to rule out other progenitors.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC5358 as the brightest cluster radio galaxy offset from the X-ray peak, linking its past activity to the compressed radio relic and bridge structure.

quotes.quote: The BCG in the cluster (IC5358) is a radio galaxy located at an offset of 10 kpc from the peak in X-rays.

The presence of the Bridge (region 14) region supports a scenario where there is a connection between the relic and the BCG. The relic may have originated from the cocoon of the past activity of the BCG. There is evidence for the disturbance at the cluster centre from the detection of turbulent broadening of spectral lines in X-rays. Turbulent broadening in line widths of 1300 km s ^ 1 at the cluster core has been reported 2011MNRAS.410.1797S , which implies that it does not have a cool core. This supports the possibility of the disturbance at the core that displaced the BCG away from the X-ray peak and led to the compression of the radio plasma.

gmw_00005548.name: RA 09h22m09.27s DEC 74d 59'48.99''
aliases_external: compact 4.7 GHz source in A786 relic
gmw_00005548.overview: RA 09h22m09.27s DEC 74d 59'48.99'' is a galaxy with a 2MASS counterpart that is located in the central region of a radio relic [arXiv:1109.3261]. It hosts a compact 4.7 GHz radio source with surrounding extended emission and may be the progenitor active galactic nucleus of a fossil radio-galaxy lobe [arXiv:1109.3261].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a compact 4.7 GHz source with a 2MASS counterpart near the A786 relic center and argues it could be the progenitor AGN of a fossil radio-galaxy lobe.

quotes.quote: A compact source is detected in the central region of the relic (RA 09h22m09.27s DEC 74d 59'48.99'' , J2000) with extended emission of linear extent 350 kpc surrounding it. A counterpart to this source is detected in 2MASS (total magnitudes in J, H and K _s bands are 13.976, 13.584 and 12.701, respectively), however no information on the redshift of it is available. From the K _s- z correlation deb02 , the K _s band magnitude implies a redshift of 0.1 (redshift of A786 is 0.128).

The source detected at 4.7 GHz in the central region of the relic has a peak flux density of 0.34 0.06 and a total (including the surrounding extended emission) flux density of 7.2 0.3 . Since the full extent of the relic is not detected at 4.7 GHz we do not report total flux density of the relic at 4.7 GHz. The central compact source is not detected at lower frequencies, therefore no correction due to this source is made to the flux densities of the relic reported at lower frequencies.
The compact radio source with extended features surrounding it, detected at 4.7 GHz could be the progenitor AGN and the relic, a fossil lobe associated with it.

gmw_00005549.name: UGC 6697
gmw_00005549.overview: UGC 6697 is a starburst spiral or irregular galaxy in the Abell 1367 cluster that is undergoing edge-on ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1707.06241, arXiv:2202.03443]. This interaction has created a long, ionized-gas tail containing star-forming H II regions [arXiv:1707.06241, arXiv:1801.03601] and an asymmetric H I distribution [arXiv:0706.3197].

sources.summary: The paper maps UGC 6697 as an infalling spiral whose ICM interaction creates a 100 kpc ionized-gas tail with stripped components and HII-region knots.

quotes.quote: We present the MUSE observations of UGC 6697, a giant (M _* 10^ 10 M_ ) spiral galaxy infalling in the nearby cluster Abell 1367. During its high velocity transit through the intracluster medium (ICM), the hydrodynamical interactions with the ICM produce a 100 kpc tail of ionized gas that we map with a mosaic of five MUSE pointings up to 60 kpc from the galaxy. CGCG 97087N, a small companion that lies at few arcminutes in projection from UGC 6697, is also suffering from the hydrodynamic action of the ICM of the cluster. Along the whole extent of the tail we detect diffuse H emission and, to a lesser extent, H , [ OIII ] 5007 , and [ OI ] 6300 . By comparing the kinematics and distribution of gas and stars (as traced by the CaII triplet) for both galaxies, we separate the ionized gas, as traced by the H line, in a component still bound to the galaxy and a component that is stripped.

In the tail of UGC 6697 we identify numerous bright compact knots with line ratios typical of HII regions. These are distributed along the only streams of stripped gas retaining low velocity dispersions ( 35 km s^ 1 ). Despite being in the stripped gas, their physical properties do not differentiate from normal HII regions in galactic disks.

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 6697 as a starburst galaxy near the A1367 shock region, with radio emission, an estimated star-formation rate of 5.1 M_ yr^ 1, and gas stripping that may seed the radio relic.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, we note there are three starburst galaxies (CGCG 097-073, 097-079, and 097-087/ UGC 6697) with H and radio tails ( 1987A&A...186L...1G ; 1995A&A...304..325G ; 2017ApJ...839...65Y ) close to the shock region. Two of them are brighter than NGC 3842 at 1.4 GHz (12.3 mJy from NVSS ): UGC 6697 (53.9 mJy), CGCG 097-073 (21.5 mJy), CGCG 097-079 (5.0 mJy). We estimate their star formation rate (SFR) from the GALEX FUV and WISE 22 m data with the relation derived by 2011ApJ...741..124H : UGC 6697 --- 5.1 M_ yr^ 1 , CGCG 097-073 --- 1.0 M_ yr^ 1 , CGCG 097-079 --- 2.1 M_ yr^ 1 .

All three also have X-ray tails opposite to their direction of motion as marked in Fig.  fig:multi (A). The ram pressure stripping could strip the gas from the galaxies, including the relativistic electrons. Star formation also can proceed in the stripped gas in the intracluster space e.g. 2010ApJ...708..946S .

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG097-087 as a spiral/irregular A1367 member undergoing edge-on ram-pressure stripping, interacting with CGCG097-087N, and showing complex gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: CGCG097-087 is the second brightest spiral/Irr member of A1367, transiting almost edge-on through the ICM towards the cluster center. Its smaller companion (CGCG097-087N) lies at 20 arcsec in the NE direction from CGCG097-087 and recent Subaru observations Yagi17 were highly suggestive of galaxy-galaxy interactions due to double gaseous tails connecting the two galaxies.

CGCC097-087 and CGCG097-087N have a stellar mass of (M_*/ M_ ) = 10.13 and (M_*/ M_ ) = 8.78 respectively, computed from g- and i- band photometry following the colour calibrations by Zibetti09 . The analysis of this galaxy pair using the high sensitivity and spatial resolution of MUSE gave us unique new information about the hydrodynamic processes driving the stripping and the interaction of the two systems, and most importantly about the physics in the tails. Consolandi17 found that CGCG097-087 shows complex gas kinematics due to the superposition of gas with different velocity components along the line of sight. Specifically, the ionised gas is split in a component still bound to the galaxy and in a stripped component that is consistent with a RPS event seen edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper treats CGCG 097087 (UGC 06697) as an edge-on long-tailed galaxy whose asymmetric H i profile and displaced gas indicate ram-pressure disturbance.

quotes.quote: CGCG 097087 In Fig. 87.global we present the global H i profile of CGCG 097087 (UGC 06697) taken from the image cube which has a spatial and spectral resolution of 21 ^ and 86 km s ^ 1 respectively. The profile has a wide velocity width of 600 km s ^ 1 ranging from 6435 to 7030 km s ^ 1 , and is very much asymmetric with respect to the systemic velocity of 6725 km s ^ 1 . There is more gas on the red-shifted side of the systemic velocity, the mass being 2.9 10 ^9 M _ compared with 1.7 10 ^9 M _ on the blue-shifted side.

The direction of the radio tail in CGCG 097087 (UGC 06697) also suggests that this edge-on galaxy is moving towards the south-east. The H i observations show that while the gas associated with the higher brightness region of the optical galaxy exhibits solid-body rotation, most of the H i gas is pushed towards the north-west and has an almost constant velocity of 6940 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 097-087 as a well-studied Abell 1367 galaxy whose Subaru H-alpha data reveal a tail extending beyond 150 kpc and signs of ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: CGCG 097-087 (UGC 6697) is also the best-studied galaxy in 1367 e.g., Gavazzi1987b,Gavazzi1989,Boselli1994,Gavazzi1995,Gavazzi1998,Gavazzi2001b,Scott2010 . Gavazzi2001b presented \ image and spectra of the galaxy and a part of the tail with a detailed investigation. In the Subaru \ data (Figure fig:097087 , we found that the tail of CGCG 097-087 extends more than > 150 kpc from the core, more than twice as long as previously known in \ Gavazzi1995 and in other wavelengths Sun2005,Scott2010 .

The two parents near the cluster's velocity are CGCG 097-087 and CGCG 097-092. As the tail of CGCG 097-087 points opposite to the direction to the cluster center (Figure fig:FC1 ), it is natural to think that CGCG 097-087 would be entering the cluster directly to the center on the tangential plane.

sources.summary: The paper describes 087 as a strongly disturbed cluster galaxy with a long HI tail, two HI maxima, star-forming clumps in stripped gas, warped disk gas, and possible ram-pressure plus tidal effects.

quotes.quote: 087 This is one of the best studied galaxies in the cluster, with our previous VLA D--array observations (Paper I) revealing a long diffuse 70 kpc \ tail which has \ and radio continuum counterparts black bosel94,gava95,gava01a,yagi17 . As noted in Section results the VLA C--array black (Figure 97087 ) only maps the high column density regions in the black VLA D--array map, but resolves its \ maxium into two distinct maxima SE and NW of the optical centre. The principal \ maximum ( 11:43:49.6 + 19:57:58) is projected 10.9 arcsec (4.5 kpc) SE of the optical centre and Spitzer 4.5 m maxima. The secondary \ maximum ( 11:43:47.4 +19:58:28) lies 71 arcsec (29 kpc) NW of the optical centre and beyond the optical disk. There are two bright star forming clumps at this position, with 4.5 m and \ counterparts, possibly tracing in--situ star formation within the stripped gas.

gmw_00005550.name: FDF 2484
gmw_00005550.overview: FDF 2484 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.65 [arXiv:astro-ph/0408072]. Hubble Space Telescope residual images and Fourier analysis reveal that the galaxy possesses a well-developed, two-armed spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0408072].

sources.summary: The paper treats FDF 2484 as one of three FDF spiral galaxies at redshift z 0.65, with HST residual images showing well developed two-armed spiral structure confirmed by Fourier analysis.

quotes.quote: We have examined the ACS image of each high quality object in the redshift range z = 0.5 - 1 and found three galaxies showing discernible spiral structure: FDF 2484 at redshift z 0.65 , FDF 2174 at z 0.68 , and FDF 4922 at z 0.97 , respectively. The HST images of the galaxies are reproduced in the upper panels of Fig. hstimag . As can be seen from the residual images in the lower panels all galaxies show well developed two-armed spirals. The m = 2 arm mode was confirmed by a Fourier analysis of the arm pattern as described in the next section (Fig. fourcomp ).

gmw_00005551.name: FDF 2174
gmw_00005551.overview: FDF 2174 is a high-redshift spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.68 [arXiv:astro-ph/0408072]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals that the galaxy possesses a well-developed, two-armed spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0408072].

sources.summary: The paper treats FDF 2174 as one of three FDF spiral galaxies at redshift z 0.68, with HST residual images showing well developed two-armed spiral structure confirmed by Fourier analysis.

quotes.quote: We have examined the ACS image of each high quality object in the redshift range z = 0.5 - 1 and found three galaxies showing discernible spiral structure: FDF 2484 at redshift z 0.65 , FDF 2174 at z 0.68 , and FDF 4922 at z 0.97 , respectively. The HST images of the galaxies are reproduced in the upper panels of Fig. hstimag . As can be seen from the residual images in the lower panels all galaxies show well developed two-armed spirals. The m = 2 arm mode was confirmed by a Fourier analysis of the arm pattern as described in the next section (Fig. fourcomp ).

gmw_00005552.name: FDF 4922
gmw_00005552.overview: FDF 4922 is a high-redshift spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.97 [arXiv:astro-ph/0408072]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals that the galaxy possesses a well-developed, two-armed spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0408072].

sources.summary: The paper treats FDF 4922 as one of three FDF spiral galaxies at redshift z 0.97, with HST residual images showing well developed two-armed spiral structure confirmed by Fourier analysis.

quotes.quote: We have examined the ACS image of each high quality object in the redshift range z = 0.5 - 1 and found three galaxies showing discernible spiral structure: FDF 2484 at redshift z 0.65 , FDF 2174 at z 0.68 , and FDF 4922 at z 0.97 , respectively. The HST images of the galaxies are reproduced in the upper panels of Fig. hstimag . As can be seen from the residual images in the lower panels all galaxies show well developed two-armed spirals. The m = 2 arm mode was confirmed by a Fourier analysis of the arm pattern as described in the next section (Fig. fourcomp ).

gmw_00005553.name: IC 4329 A
gmw_00005553.overview: IC 4329 A is an optical Seyfert 1 active galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0160 [arXiv:1609.01291]. Its nuclear region displays a featureless infrared continuum that lacks polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission, and its nucleus has no detected HCN or HCO+ molecular line emission [arXiv:1609.01291].

sources.summary: IC 4329 A is presented as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with PAH-free nuclear infrared continuum; ALMA detected continuum but not HCN/HCO+ line emission.

quotes.quote: IC 4329 A (z=0.0160) is an optical Seyfert 1 galaxy in the 12 m Seyfert galaxy sample rus93 . The nuclear 3--4 m ( L band) spectrum taken with a 1 6-wide slit is dominated by a PAH-free featureless continuum, with one of the highest observed fluxes for this class of object iw04 . The 11.3 m PAH emission is also not detected in the nuclear region hon10 . The detection of molecular rotational J-transition emission lines in the (sub)millimeter wavelength range toward the IC 4329 A nucleus has not been reported in the published literature.

Continuum emission at 250--260 GHz (observed frame) is clearly detected at the nuclear regions in all sources. For IC 4329 A, neither HCN J=3--2 nor HCO ^ + J=3--2 emission lines are clearly detected (Figure 4).

gmw_00005554.name: NGC 5626
gmw_00005554.overview: NGC 5626 is a massive dust-lane elliptical [arXiv:1711.08981] or dusty lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1707.06237] classified as a fast rotator [arXiv:1711.08981]. Its interstellar matter resides in a regular, well-settled disk [arXiv:1707.06237], and the galaxy exhibits a strong stellar kinematic twist without being barred or currently interacting [arXiv:1711.08981].

sources.summary: The paper maps dust and ionized gas in NGC 5626, finding a smooth attenuation curve and interstellar matter in a regular, well-settled disk.

quotes.quote: Using MUSE science verification observations of NGC 5626, we map the interstellar matter in this dusty lenticular. We use the resolving power of MUSE to measure the optical attenuation with a spectral resolution of 6.25 , at physical scales of 0.1-1 kpc. The integrated attenuation curve of NGC 5626 shows a smooth, slightly steeper than Milky Way and SMC attenuation curves.

We model and interpret the variations in the attenuation curves of each spatial resolution element of NGC 5626. We find that the amount and distribution of dust along the line-of-sight is highly degenerate with any variation in the intrinsic extinction law. Our analysis shows that the interstellar matter in NGC 5626 resides in a regular and well-settled disk.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5626 as a massive dust-lane elliptical and rare fast rotator, highlighting its virial mass, strong outer kinematic twist, and unusual misalignment.

quotes.quote: We present a kinematic analysis of the dust-lane elliptical NGC 5626 based on MUSE observations. These data allow to robustly classify this galaxy as a fast rotator and to infer a virial mass of 10^ 11.7 M_ , making it one of the most massive fast rotators known. In addition, the depth and extent of the MUSE data reveal a strong kinematic twist in the stellar velocity field (by up to 45 degrees beyond 1.5R_e ).

Among the fast rotators, only a few have a kinematic misalignment as large as the one we observe in NGC 5626, in particular beyond R_e . In fact, many of the fast rotators that exhibit a misalignment between the kinematic and photometric major axis are either barred or interacting. This makes NGC 5626 even more special as a fast rotator since it is neither barred nor currently interacting.

gmw_00005555.name: 3C 076.1
gmw_00005555.overview: 3C 076.1 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:0908.4391, arXiv:2010.11195] featuring twin jets [arXiv:0908.4391], an edge-darkened morphology [arXiv:0811.1907], and two diffuse emission-line regions [arXiv:2010.11195]. It contains a dust ellipse aligned with its minor axis, suggesting a prolate galaxy with a settled dust disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0502075].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C76.1 as an edge-darkened radio source with warm spots, nested emission peaks, possible jet precession, and a wide wing centered on the host galaxy.

quotes.quote: 3C76.1: This source has a clear edge-darkened morphology although a pair of warm-spots are present at the lobe ends mac83 . The source lacks the classic bright pair of jets; instead, it has a nested pair of emission peaks on either side of the core. The S-shaped morphology of the main axis suggests that the jet may have precessed over a small angle. The source has a wide wing that is centred on the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0300+16 as an FR I radio galaxy with twin jets, a faint core and halo, and an unresolved VLBA core missing some arcsecond-scale flux.

quotes.quote: 0300+16 (3C 76.1) -- On kiloparsec scales, 3C 76.1 is classified as an FR I radio galaxy; it was imaged at 6 cm by , and at 20 cm by . The source has twin jets with a faint ( 8 mJy) radio core surrounded by an extended low brightness radio halo. In our VLBA image, we found an unresolved core with the total flux density S_5=6.1 mJy. Some arcsecond core flux is lost on the parsec scale (S _ VLBA /S _ 60 ).

sources.summary: 3C 076.1 is described as an FR I radio source with two diffuse emission-line regions, regular central ELR rotation, and near-instrumental velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: 3C 076.1: FR I, no optical classification, z=0.032, 1 = 0.64 kpc (see Fig. 3c076.1 ). Two diffuse emission line regions emerge from the compact central source, extending 4 kpc on each side. The central ELR shows regular rotation with an amplitude of 300 . The velocity dispersion is, except on the nucleus, consistent with the MUSE spectral resolution.

sources.summary: 3C 76.1 is singled out as the only sample dust ellipse aligned with the galaxy minor axis, suggesting a possible prolate galaxy with a settled dust disk.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, there is one notable exception at 90 (3C 76.1, see Section s:discussion for further discussion).

3C 76.1 contains the only dust ellipse in the sample which is aligned with the minor axis of the galaxy instead of the major axis. Thus, this galaxy could be prolate with a settled dust disk in the stable plane perpendicular to the long-axis.

gmw_00005556.name: 3C 258
gmw_00005556.overview: The radio source 3C 258 is a compact steep-spectrum source with a compact, double radio structure [arXiv:0712.0595]. Although historically identified as a low-redshift galaxy, it is actually a compact quasar at a redshift of approximately 1.54 [arXiv:2010.11195].

sources.summary: The paper rejects the old low-redshift galaxy identification for 3C 258 and instead associates the radio source with a compact QSO at about z 1.54.

quotes.quote: The radio source 3C 258 was identified as a galaxy at redshift z=0.165 wyndham66 . This value was challenged by dey94 who noted the likely presence of a background quasar, but, due to poor seeing of the observations, no firm claims can be made. The NICMOS/HST images by floyd08 are consistent with the higher redshift as they show a very compact host galaxy, much fainter than expected for an object at z=0.165.

3C 258: As already mentioned above, we confirm that the identification of this radio source with a galaxy at redshift z=0.165 is incorrect. We confirm the suggestion of dey94 and floyd08 that it is instead a QSO at z 1.54 , based on the presence of a single broad emission line = 7116 , likely Mg II at = 2798 \ in rest frame.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 258 as a disputed-redshift compact steep-spectrum radio source whose NICMOS data favor a higher redshift and reveal a compact faint host plus a bright nucleus.

quotes.quote: 3C 258; z=0.165 This galaxy is a well-known compact steep spectrum (CSS) radio source, with a compact, double radio structure seen on scales of a few tenths of an arcsecond along PA 33^ , although its arcminute scale structure is aligned along a nearly north-south direction . Its redshift has been quoted as z=0.165 , but this value is in dispute as note that spectra taken at Lick show a likely background quasar, based on the observation of a single emission line at 7111 which would give z=1.54 if due to Mg II 2798 . However, due to poor seeing at the time the spectra were taken, no firm claims can be made as to the origin of this line. Our NICMOS data are consistent with a significantly higher redshift, as they show a very compact host galaxy that is much fainter than one would expect for an object at z=0.165.

gmw_00005557.name: G1
gmw_00005557.overview: G1 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2310.19884] located at a redshift of z=0.680 [arXiv:astro-ph/0312429]. It serves as the brightest cluster galaxy in its field [arXiv:2310.19884], with its spectrum displaying characteristic calcium and magnesium absorption lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0312429].

sources.summary: The paper locates and models the BCG in SDSS J1004+4112, estimating its velocity dispersion, mass, effective radius, ellipticity, and Sersic-lens contribution.

quotes.quote: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) is located at (7.114", 4.409"), and was measured to have a velocity dispersion of 352 13 km s ^ 1 inada08 . Using this measurement and the relations described in , we estimate the dynamical mass M_ dyn and effective radius R_e of the BCG to be 10^ 12.2 M_ and 10^ 1.2 kpc, respectively.

In addition, because the arrival time of the central QSO image E will be affected by the depth of the gravitational potential of the BCG, we decide to also explicitly include the BCG in another analysis for comparison. In this case, we model the BCG as an Elliptic Sersic Lens keeton01 with mass density: _S( ) = _ cen 2n- 1 3 R_e ^ 1 n where _ cen is the central mass density, n is the Sersic index, and is the elliptical coordinate position, defined as = x, y q with q the axis ratio. Since the BCG is an elliptical galaxy, we adopt n = 4 for a de Vaucouleurs model. Using the measured ellipticity e = 0.30 0.05 oguri10 , we fix e = 0.35 for our axis ratio, such that q = 1 - e = 0.65 .

The central density can be solved for: _ cen = M_ tot _0^ 2n- 1 3 R_e ^ 1 n d ^ 1 where M_ tot is the total mass in the Sersic lens. We approximate M_ tot as the dynamical mass M_ dyn of the BCG, and find that _ cen 5.95 10^5 M _ pc ^ 2 (124.17 g cm ^ 2 ).

sources.summary: G1 is described as the brightest galaxy near the quasar configuration center, with absorption features confirming its spectrum and a derived redshift of z=0.680.

quotes.quote: Galaxies = Spectrum of the galaxy G1 taken with LRIS on Keck I. The break, Ca 2 H K absorption lines, and Mg absorption line are consistent with redshift z=0.680 ( z=0.6799 0.0001 from the Ca 2 H line). The G-band also appears in the spectrum.

gmw_00005558.name: J092347.12 + 040254.5
gmw_00005558.overview: J092347.12 + 040254.5 is a broad-absorption line quasar at a redshift of approximately 6.6 [arXiv:2404.12443, arXiv:1810.11926]. The host galaxy features an ionized outflow on a 210 pc scale, a cold neutral outflow, and a bright, extended carbon-II halo [arXiv:2404.12443].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes black-hole feedback in the z=6.6 Lo-BAL quasar J0923+0402, finding ionized and neutral outflows plus an extended [CII] halo in its host.

quotes.quote: We perform the first multi-scale and multi-phase characterisation of black-hole driven outflows in the z 6.6 quasar J0923+0402 and assess how these winds impact the cold gas reservoir. We employ the SimBAL spectral synthesis to fit broad absorption line (BAL) features and find a powerful ionized outflow on 210 pc scale, with a kinetic power 2-100 of the quasar luminosity. ALMA observations of [ CII ] emission allow us to study the morphology and kinematics of the cold gas. We detect high-velocity [ CII ] emission, likely associated with a cold neutral outflow at 0.5-2 kpc scale in the host-galaxy, and a bright extended [ CII ] halo with a size of 15 kpc. For the first time at such an early epoch, we accurately constrain the outflow energetics in both the ionized and the atomic neutral gas phases.

Here we report on \ z=6.626 , Bischetti22 , which is the highest redshift low-ionization broad absorption line (Lo-BAL) quasar discovered so far. This object was initially classified as a low-luminosity, dust-reddened quasar based on SUBARU/HSC and WISE photometry Matsuoka18b , but revealed to be a brighter quasar with little extinction Wang19UKIRT, Kato20 . Indeed, \ has a bolometric luminosity log( L_ Bol / erg \ s^ 1 )=47.5 and a black-hole mass log( M_ BH / M_ )=9.4 Yang21,Mazzucchelli23 , which implies that it is accreting close to the Eddington limit ( L_ Bol /L_ Edd 1 , where L_ Edd is the Eddington luminosity).

sources.summary: J0923 + 0402 is treated as a WISE-detected SHELLQs quasar whose SED is fit without dust extinction and whose WISE detection is attributed to UV brightness.

quotes.quote: We found that four SHELLQs quasars - J120505.09 000027.9 (J1205 0000), J092347.12 + 040254.5 (J0923 + 0402), J114632.66 015438.2 (J1146 0154), and J023858.09 031845.4 (J0238 0318) - have WISE counterparts in the W1 and W2 bands (S/N = 2--15), and regarded these four quasars as red quasar candidates for further analysis. Figure fig:optical_spectrum displays their optical spectra, obtained with the Subaru/Faint Object Camera and Spectrograph (FOCAS; 2002PASJ...54..819K ) or the Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC)/Optical System for Imaging and low-Intermediate-Resolution Integrated Spectroscopy (OSIRIS; 2000SPIE.4008..623C ) 2016ApJ...828...26M, 2018ApJS..237....5M, 2019ApJ...883..183M . We note that two of the four candidates, J1205 0000 and J1146 0154, have broad absorption line (BAL) features.

J0923 + 0402: this is an i band dropout source, and we fitted the SED models to the HSC y band, UKIDSS J, H, K band fluxes and the decomposed W1, W2 band fluxes. The SED is reproduced by a quasar template without dust extinction, so J0923 + 0402 is a normal extinction-free quasar. This quasar is detected by WISE simply due to its brightness; it is much brighter in the UV than the other three candidates (see Table table:candidates ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0923+0402 as a newly discovered z=6.61 BAL quasar, notes its independent discovery, and includes it in the sample used for the BAL fraction estimate.

quotes.quote: J0923+0402 is a BAL quasar. It was independently discovered by .

Four newly discovered quasars in our sample that show strong blue-shifted \ absorptions, indicating that they are BAL quasars, J1316+1028 at z=6.35 , J0923+0402 at z=6.61 , J0910--0414 at z=6.63 , and J0839+3900 at z=6.905 .

gmw_00005559.name: J114632.66 015438.2
gmw_00005559.overview: J114632.66 015438.2 is a high-redshift broad-absorption line quasar candidate detected by WISE [arXiv:2007.08685]. It is identified as an i-band dropout source that may potentially be a red quasar [arXiv:2007.08685].

sources.summary: J1146 0154 is a WISE-detected BAL quasar candidate whose red-quasar interpretation is considered plausible but rejected from the red-quasar sample because of uncertain WISE decomposition.

quotes.quote: We found that four SHELLQs quasars - J120505.09 000027.9 (J1205 0000), J092347.12 + 040254.5 (J0923 + 0402), J114632.66 015438.2 (J1146 0154), and J023858.09 031845.4 (J0238 0318) - have WISE counterparts in the W1 and W2 bands (S/N = 2--15), and regarded these four quasars as red quasar candidates for further analysis.
