quotes.quote: The galaxy pair consists of a grand-design galaxy NGC 7753, and a small companion NGC 7752. NGC 7753 is a SAB galaxy with the radii R _ 25 1 arcmin (corresponding to 20 kpc), which is defined by the isophote at the brightness of 25 mag/arcsec ^2 in the B-band. A tidal bridge connecting two galaxies is found in optical images and active star formation is lying in two galaxies and the bridge Laurikainen1993 .

We have found that the extinction in NGC 7552 and the bulge region of NGC 7753 is (H ) 1.0, higher than that in the bridge and out disks of NGC 7753 ((H ) 0.4 -- 0.8). The BPT diagram indicates the pure star formation activity in NGC 7752 and AGN activity in the nucleus of NGC 7753. The oxygen abundances estimated from the N2 metallicity indicator yield the similar O/H ratio from 8.5 to 8.8 in most H ii regions. The slope of the abundance gradient in the inner disk of NGC 7753 is less than 0.1 dex R_ 25 ^ 1 along with a much shallower slope in the outer disk and tidal bridge, indicating that tidal forces during encounters can flatten the metallicity distributions. The total SFRs are 8.6, 4.7 and 0.5 M_ yr^ 1 for NGC 7753, NGC 7752 and the bridge region, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC7753 as Arp86’s main barred spiral, rich in H I but depleted at its center, with star-forming disk regions and a LINER-like nuclear radio source.

quotes.quote: It is an archetypal example of interacting galaxies consisting of a grand-design barred spiral galaxy, NGC7753, with a small companion, NGC7752, towards the end of one of the spiral arms, much like the extensively studied M51 system. NGC7753 has been classifed as SAB(rs)bc in the NASA Extragalactic Database (NED) and as SB()bc by , while NGC7752 is classified as I0 by NED and as Irr galaxy by . The optical diameter of NGC7753 is 3.3 ^ and that of NGC7752 is 0.8 ^ .

The disk of NGC7753 is rich in H i , though the centre shows depletion in the H i column density, possibly due to ongoing intense star formation. The gas in the disk of this galaxy shows signs of rotation, with the south-eastern side approaching us. The low-resolution images of NGC7753 show that it is dominated largely by diffuse disk emission, although its structure appears somewhat different at the different frequencies, which may be at least partly due to different mixtures of thermal and non-thermal emission.

gmw_00005597.name: NGC7752
gmw_00005597.overview: NGC 7752 is a companion galaxy in the Arp 86 system that is connected to NGC 7753 by a tidal bridge of neutral hydrogen and radio-continuum emission [arXiv:0904.4908]. The galaxy is currently undergoing a galaxy-wide starburst [arXiv:1404.7037] with star-formation-dominated radio emission [arXiv:0904.4908].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7752 as Arp 86's companion galaxy, dominated by pure star formation, with higher SFR surface density and younger stellar populations than the main galaxy NGC 7753.

quotes.quote: The companion galaxy NGC 7752 is currently experiencing a galaxy-wide starburst with higher surface density of star formation rate than the main galaxy NGC 7753, which can be explained that the companion galaxy is more susceptible to the effects of interaction than the primary.

We found that the companion galaxy NGC 7752 is currently experiencing a galaxy-wide starburst with log(SFR M_ yr^ 1 ) = -0.6 -- -0.3 and _ SFR = 0.5 -- 1.2 M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 1 for their H ii regions. Most of the H ii regions in NGC 7753 have log(SFR M_ yr^ 1 ) lower than -1.0 except for the nucleus and two star-forming sources, which have similar SFRs to NGC 7752. However, the SFR in the nucleus of NGC 7753 may be overestimated due to the effect of the AGN. The star formation in the bridge is of log(SFR M_ yr^ 1 ) 2.5, and the lowest _ SFR in the interacting system as experted. In order to further explore the star formation activities in Arp 86, we presented the ratio map of its 8.0 m and 3.6 m images in the right penal of Figure fig6 .

We also have found that the _ SFR of H ii regions are one order of magnitude higher in NGC 7752 than in the main galaxy NGC 7753, and the stellar population of NGC 7752 is also as young as 10 -- 100 Myr, predicting that the companion galaxy is more susceptible to the effects of the interaction than the primary.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC7752 as the smaller Arp86 companion, connected to NGC7753 by H I and radio-continuum bridges, with high velocity dispersion and star-formation dominated radio emission.

quotes.quote: The galaxy to the north east is NGC7753 and that to the south west is NGCC7752. The total H i map reveals a very disturbed morphology, the presence of a tidal bridge between the two galaxies and H i in form of tidal tails and debris around the system. The bulk of the gas follows an arc like structure starting from the north-western edge of the disk of NGC7753, following the optical bridge to the companion galaxy NGC7752.

The gas in the bridge connecting the two galaxies is approaching us, with the velocities in NGC7752 extending up to 4800 km s ^ 1 . The regions with high velocity dispersions of > 50 km s ^ 1 are seen towards NGC7752, the galaxy 2MASX J23470758+2926531 and towards the eastern end of the `south-eastern extension'. Radio continuum emission from NGC7752 seems to be starformation dominated and shows it to be well resolved at the highest resolution (7 3 arcsec ^2 ) image.

gmw_00005598.name: 2MASX J23470758+2926531
gmw_00005598.overview: The galaxy 2MASX J23470758+2926531 is a compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1404.7037] embedded in the tidal debris of the Arp 86 system [arXiv:0904.4908]. It is a tidal dwarf candidate [arXiv:0904.4908] with star formation properties and a redshift similar to those of its neighboring system [arXiv:1404.7037].

sources.summary: The paper detects 2MASX J23470758+2926531 in H I within Arp86, measures its stellar and gas masses, and argues its properties are consistent with a tidal dwarf candidate.

quotes.quote: Embedded in the tidal debris to the east, we detect a small galaxy, listed as `2MASX J23470758+2926531' in NED, which did not have any previous spectroscopic data. Our observations find this galaxy to be a part of this system. In the eastern side, gas is seen towards 2MASX J23470758+2926531 over a range of velocities extending from 4963 to 5181 km s ^ 1 .

We estimate the stellar mass of this galaxy using its K band magnitude and K J colours, listed in NED. The H i mass, estimated from the spectrum (Fig. 9) is 4.5 10 ^ 8 M _ . The properties are consistent with that of a TDG.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J23470758+2926531 as a compact dwarf near Arp 86 with similar redshift, abundance, and star-formation properties, arguing it may be an NGC 7753 companion.

quotes.quote: Beside NGC 7753 and 7752, the galaxy 2MASX J23470758+2926531 may be a part of the Arp 86 system. This galaxy is visible in the GALEX UV images Smith2010 , along with many optical images. Sengupta2009 estimated the stellar mass and H ii mass of this galaxy, and found they are 2.9 10^9 M_ and 4.5 10^8 M_ , respectively. HI column density image shows an connection between NGC 7752 and 2MASX J23470758+2926531 Sengupta2009 . We also obtained the spectra of 2MASX J23470758+2926531, and found its redshift z = 0.0166 0.0003 , similar to Arp 86 (z 0.0162).

We also have observed the spectra of the galaxy 2MASX J23470758+2926531, and have found that this galaxy has similar redshift to Arp 86, and its abundance and star formation properties are similar to NGC 7753, indicating it may be a part of the Arp 86 system.

gmw_00005599.name: WISEJ022051.24+250927.6
gmw_00005599.overview: WISEJ022051.24+250927.6 is a gamma-ray blazar candidate located at a redshift of z = 0.4818 [arXiv:1404.5211]. The active galaxy features both broad and narrow emission lines in its optical spectrum, and it is associated with radio, optical, and X-ray counterparts [arXiv:1404.5211].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEJ022051.24+250927.6 as a gamma-ray blazar candidate with radio, optical, and X-ray counterparts, broad and narrow emission lines, and redshift z = 0.4818.

quotes.quote: WISEJ022051.24+250927.6 This source lies in the positional uncertainty region at 95 level of confidence of the Fermi UGS 2FGLJ0221.2+2516 as reported in the 2FGL catalog nolan12 , and it is associated with the NVSS 1998AJ....115.1693C radio source NVSSJ022051+250926 with a 1'' angular separation. The USNO-B 2003AJ....125..984M optical counterpart, at 0.1'' , features magnitudes B1=18.74 mag, R1=18.82 mag, B2=19.80 mag, R2=19.51 mag and I2=18.10 mag. 2013ApJS..209....9P showed that this source is positionally consistent ( 4.7'' ) with the X-ray Swift source SWXRTJ022051.5+250930, featuring an unabsorbed flux 1.3 10 ^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

On the basis of its position in the two dimensional WISE IR color space - that is the [ 3.4 ] [ 4.6 ] vs [ 4.6 ] [ 12 ] color plane - this source has been selected by the same authors as ray blazar candidate. The spectrum of WISEJ022051.24+250927.6 presented in Fig. fig:spectra1 a clearly shows strong emission lines that we identify as broad Mg ii ( EW = 30.8 0.7 ), narrow [ Ne v ] ( EW = 1.8 0.3 ), narrow [ O ii ] ( EW = 1.7 0.3 ), narrow [ Ne iii ] ( EW = 1.1 0.4 ) and narrow [ O iii ] ( EW = 15.8 0.3 ), yielding a redshift z = 0.4818 0.0002 .

gmw_00005600.name: WISEJ050558.78+611335.9
gmw_00005600.overview: WISEJ050558.78+611335.9 is a BZB-type blazar candidate associated with a Fermi gamma-ray source [arXiv:1404.5211]. The object is linked to radio, optical, and infrared counterparts, and its featureless optical spectrum confirms its classification [arXiv:1404.5211].

sources.summary: The paper confirms WISEJ050558.78+611335.9 as a BZB-type blazar candidate associated with a Fermi AGU, radio detections, optical and infrared counterparts, and a featureless optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: WISEJ050558.78+611335.9 The Fermi AGU 2FGLJ0505.9+6116 has been associated with the gamma-ray blazar candidate WISEJ050558.78+611335.9 by 2013arXiv1303.3585M . The WISE source is associated with the radio sources NVSSJ050558+611336 ( 0.5'' ) and WENSS 1997A&AS..124..259R WN0501.4+6109 ( 2.3'' ). The closest source in the USNO-B catalog ( 0.4'' from WISE source, 0.1'' from NVSS source ) features magnitudes R1=18.71 mag, B2=20.73 mag, R2=18.67 mag and I2=17.30 mag, while the closest counterpart in the SDSS 2012A&A...548A..66P,2013arXiv1307.7735A survey is SDSSJ050558.78+611335.8 ( 0.1'' from WISE source, 0.4'' from NVSS source ) with magnitudes u=21.66 mag, g=20.58 mag, =19.65 mag, i=19.02 mag and z=18.58 mag.

According to the source position in the three dimensional WISE IR color space, this source has been selected by 2013arXiv1303.3585M as ray BZB candidate , and its featureless spectrum shown in Figure fig:spectra1 b confirms this classification of the WISE source. The similarly featureless spectrum shown by 2013ApJ...764..135S features comparable fluxes but a different spectral shape, which is not unexpected due to the blazar strong optical variability 2009ApJ...705...46B .
